# Thread: CT and extreme shivering
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-extreme-shivering.28422/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Goswin de Boer — 2023-12-15T16:07:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-extreme-shivering.28422/post-329253>

I've searched the forum for posts about shivering, but I still have some uncertainty. So please allow me...

I'm doing cold showers every morning for the last three years already. Since october I added the cold plunge in a tub in our garden. I submerge myself up to the chin, hands submerged as well, and try to let the cold in. And I can ... sort of.

I've been able to handle 5-6 mins @ 32F. Lately the water is at 45F, and I manage 30 minutes of that. No problems with my breathing at all, some shivering gradually building up, but I can still turn the shivers off for a while. They always come back though.

But then, when I step out and get dressed, my shivers get almost out of control. I can hardly walk straight, and for the next 45 mins to an hour I just shake like mad. And then it gradually stops. I read a meal before CT is recommended, so I will do that tomorrow. But I'm uncertain about the severity of the shivers.

My questions are:
\- are the shivers here to stay, or do they gradually go away?
(I didn't have them as much, building up to 30 mins)
\- do shivers signal I'm not adapted all that well, or can they be a good sign?
\- are all shivers the same, or do my severe shivers signal something else than normal shivers (like I had coming out of the pool as a kid)?

Thanks for helping out.

### Goswin de Boer — 2023-12-19T15:56:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-extreme-shivering.28422/post-329358>

Little update: a meal beforehand does help a little. The shivers are still severe, and I can't hold them off once I get out of the tub. But I can at least walk normally and have a decent conversation. So that's a little progress

The more body fat the less shivering.... in your case the less body fat the more shivering.

Have you increased your DHA ?

Preloading your semiconductors with DHA allows for increased cell signalling.

This implies that The colder it is the more omega 3’s are need for cell membrane signalling.

This is also why northern cold waters are saturated with Fish that are high in Omega 3's ,this Is not the case with warm water fish around the equator.

DHA becomes magnetically drawn to tissues with high mitochondrial densities which are magnetic.

The more electrons in cell membranes the better you handle cold.

DHA facilitates this.

### kris90 — 2023-12-21T22:02:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-extreme-shivering.28422/post-329392>

> [Goswin de Boer said:](/goto/post?id=329388)
>
> And would that also go for us? That would mean that we 'd also have to consume omega-6 prior to becoming cold adapted... Correct my reasoning when / where faulty.

Yes and no. I believe his point is that when a mammal's inflammation levels peak (which is usually in Fall), they are in that state of being ready to "den", (i.e. inflammation causes fatigue and "low energy"), at which point all the reversal (i.e. benefits of CT) of said inflammation begins once they enter hibernation (i.e. sleep, fasting/ketosis, cold/grounding).

Since we don't hibernate, we should never purposely get ourselves into an inflammatory state. Just cut carbs out in the Fall, and begin embracing CT while fueling on a ketogenic diet. I think the point he makes is that mammals use this pathway to reverse their self-induced inflammatory state, and humans can do the same thing to reverse ours which is self-induced but not for our own good (i.e. blue light, EMFs, circadian mismatches, etc).

### coho3131 — 2023-12-31T02:07:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-extreme-shivering.28422/post-329662>

It depends on the temperature of the water and how much free time I have.

Today in a local farm pond the water temperature was 40 degrees today and I did about 3 minutes in the water in shorts only, then walked home in 38 degree air temperature (28 degreee windchill) without drying off for 10 minutes in only shorts and sandals, then stayed outside for about 10 minutes more to dry off by evaporation. I came inside and started to feel at baseline about 15 minutes later. I did not take a shower until much later after doing some outdoor chores in shorts only.

In the Fall when the water was in the 50s I would stay in the water much longer and even swim a little bit.

### Saichi — 2023-12-31T03:23:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-extreme-shivering.28422/post-329668>

I wear neoprene gloves and booties when I do CT in city banyas. I don't wear them when I'm doing ocean CT since swimming keeps my fingers and toes warm but I do wear a neoprene cap to prevent surfer's ear when big waves take me under. Years ago I used to do mild banya CT of 52 deg and I didn't need gloves or booties to stay comfortable. I always wear gloves booties indoors now but I'm doing 35 to 50 minutes in 43 to 48 deg sometimes twice a day.

### coho3131 — 2023-12-31T17:06:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-extreme-shivering.28422/post-329681>

Last winter I wore neoprene socks and gloves when I needed to go through the ice on the pond.

This year I decided that if I needed artificial protection from the cold to prevent pain, then that water was too cold for my health.

So, now I just stay in until the pain gets significant, and then I get out.

Going barefoot on the mud/sand/rock bottom is actually less cold for me than wearing sandals because the bottom of my foot sinks in a little and gets insulated.

Paced breathing and visualization also have delayed the onset of pain.

### kris90 — 2024-01-03T17:13:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-extreme-shivering.28422/post-329769>

Neoprene gloves and footwear are game changers for sure. I'm doing anywhere's from 3-10 mins daily in 30-32 F water now and seems to be more than enough. 10 mins works best for a good shiver if I let myself evaporate and stay outside for a few mins. Interestingly, I can immediately halt the shivering if I follow breathing pattern: 5 second deep nasal inhale, hold for a couple seconds, and 10-15 second nasal exhale, hold for a couple seconds and repeat.

### mvivier — 2024-01-30T00:12:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-extreme-shivering.28422/post-330724>

[Andrew Huberman, a neuroscientist and tenured professor at Stanford University, has discussed the benefits of **deliberate cold exposure** in his podcast](https://www.hubermanlab.com/newsletter/the-science-and-use-of-cold-exposure-for-health-and-performance)[1](https://www.hubermanlab.com/newsletter/the-science-and-use-of-cold-exposure-for-health-and-performance). [He explains that shivering is a natural response to cold exposure and can have a significant impact on fat loss](https://www.hubermanlab.readablepods.com/blog/fat-loss-and-cold-exposure/)[2](https://www.hubermanlab.readablepods.com/blog/fat-loss-and-cold-exposure/). [Shivering and small movements can trigger the release of epinephrine and norepinephrine in the brain and body, which can make us feel alert and increase our level of energy and focus](https://www.hubermanlab.com/newsletter/the-science-and-use-of-cold-exposure-for-health-and-performance)[1](https://www.hubermanlab.com/newsletter/the-science-and-use-of-cold-exposure-for-health-and-performance).

In addition, Dr. [Huberman suggests that deliberate cold exposure can help build resilience and grit by forcing oneself to embrace the stress of cold exposure as a meaningful self-directed challenge](https://www.hubermanlab.com/newsletter/the-science-and-use-of-cold-exposure-for-health-and-performance)[1](https://www.hubermanlab.com/newsletter/the-science-and-use-of-cold-exposure-for-health-and-performance). Not sure if this thought has been approved by Dr. Jack. Does anyone have a link to the "Two Vikings" podcast he references?

# Thread: Human brain and body mass.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/human-brain-and-body-mass.28423/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JanSz — 2023-12-15T16:53:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/human-brain-and-body-mass.28423/post-329255>

In 18th century and before average weight of man was around 140 pounds and woman 120 pounds.
Their brains were having certain weight.
Now in 2023 average weight of people almost doubled.
By before I mean up too many thousands of years or much more.
I am assuming that their brains today have a weight same as previously.
How biologically important it is that the same brain now have to manage almost twice the weight?
\===========================================

The increase in body weight does have significant implications for brain function.
Here are some key points based on recent research:

1.  [**Decreased Brain Activity and Blood Flow**: As a person’s weight increases, all regions of the brain show decreased activity and blood flow](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2020/08/200805110127.htm)[1](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2020/08/200805110127.htm). [This is concerning because low cerebral blood flow is the number one brain imaging predictor that a person will develop Alzheimer’s disease](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2020/08/200805110127.htm)[1](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2020/08/200805110127.htm).
2.  [**Increased Risk of Mental Illness**: Higher body weight is associated with an increased risk of Alzheimer’s disease and many other psychiatric and cognitive conditions](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2020/08/200805110127.htm)[1](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2020/08/200805110127.htm).
3.  [**Reduced Cognitive Function**: Excess body weight can affect cognitive function, potentially due to inflammation](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/excess-weight-may-accelerate-brain-aging)[2](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/excess-weight-may-accelerate-brain-aging). [It impacts brain health from childhood well into adulthood, affecting everything from executive function skills to substantially raising dementia risk](https://www.heart.org/en/news/2022/01/12/obesity-harms-brain-health-throughout-life-yet-scientists-dont-know-why)[3](https://www.heart.org/en/news/2022/01/12/obesity-harms-brain-health-throughout-life-yet-scientists-dont-know-why).
4.  [**Brain Atrophy**: Obesity has been linked to a decrease in brain parenchymal volume, with areas particularly vulnerable to obesity-related atrophy including the hippocampus, cingulate gyrus, and frontal lobes](https://practicalneurology.com/articles/2013-july-aug/the-effects-of-obesity-on-brain-structure-and-size)[4](https://practicalneurology.com/articles/2013-july-aug/the-effects-of-obesity-on-brain-structure-and-size).
5.  [**Decline in Executive Function**: A body mass index (BMI) of 30 or higher has been linked with a decline in executive function over a ten-year period](https://practicalneurology.com/articles/2013-july-aug/the-effects-of-obesity-on-brain-structure-and-size)[4](https://practicalneurology.com/articles/2013-july-aug/the-effects-of-obesity-on-brain-structure-and-size).

In summary, while the brain’s weight may not have changed significantly, the increase in body weight can have profound effects on brain function and health.

///////////////////////////

# Thread: Want to move to El Salvador for my health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-move-to-el-salvador-for-my-health.28424/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### juliemarie — 2023-12-15T17:11:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-move-to-el-salvador-for-my-health.28424/post-329257>

Good morning!

My name is Julie and I have decided to move to El Salvador in the fall. I am actually open to all Central American countries but I want to go to El Salvador first, it seems like the best option for people like us  I have alopecia universalis, hashimoto’s, and autoimmune ovarian disease (premature ovarian insufficiency) so my health depends on this move. I am so hopeful that I will get better in the tropics. I’m considering using the turn-key ES package for members but there are a few options in there that I won’t need so not sure how that would work out.

I’m a little nervous because my income is not very high (about $30,000 a year) and I barely make it by in San Diego so I have no savings. I will start saving now and sell my car in August so I will have a little bit of money once I get to ES until I find a job (if I don’t find a remote job before I move). So I could potentially be coming down to ES with only $8,000 or so to my name. My health is worth it and I’m excited for a new adventure. If I don’t have a remote job by the time I get down there I am wondering if there are jobs/income options for immigrants like us. I have tons of work experience in many different industries so I’m confident that I could be an asset to anyone needing an employee for their business. For reference I have tons of experience with office administration, jewelry making, massage, sugaring hair removal (100% natural body waxing), personal assisting, and more. I have thought that I could potentially have a successful sugaring hair removal business down there because of beach life and a large natural/holistic community. That’s my job currently at a local shop here in San Diego but it could be exciting to see what happens if I started my own sugaring business. Just brainstorming  I just want to make sure that I’m not being irresponsible by moving down there and expecting to survive with the amount of money that I will have to start. I will make anything work, I’m a survivor and determined to make this happen no matter what; I know I just need to get down there. I have already looked on websites where you can volunteer in exchange for room and board so I know that's a potential option for me as well. I just want to make sure I’m not naive and getting myself into a bad situation by coming down there with less than $10,000 to my name.

I’m probably going to book a consult with Dr. Kruse to discuss improving my health conditions (yay!) and am so excited for what my life could look like in El Salvador! I'm a San Diego native so beach and surf culture is in my blood. I’d love to connect with any members who are there, especially any members who have healed from alopecia. It’s devastating but I have faith I can get better.

Very excited and can’t wait to connect!

### juliemarie — 2023-12-16T00:50:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-move-to-el-salvador-for-my-health.28424/post-329290>

> [alipankey said:](/goto/post?id=329288)
>
> Hi Julie, I am in a very similar situation healthwise and financially, except I don't have a car to hock, so I'd be coming with zero buffer--which is terrifying. No idea how you are surviving in San Diego at our income level--wow! I have also been looking at going to ES for my health; the sunshine in Florida did nothing for me. What is the "turn-key ES package for members" that you mention? I don't think I can afford really anything, so I am curious that you mentioned this.

It is an option that’s in the Optimal Klub’s dashboard you should see the button to “learn more” when you log into your dashboard. But I think it’s moreso geared towards people who want to buy property and start a business in ES so I’m no longer thinking it’s something I should consider.

Yes let me tell you it’s been crazy trying to survive here in San Diego on this income. I am ready for a change. Are you trying to move to ES soon?

Feel free to PM me!

### JanSz — 2023-12-16T16:38:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-move-to-el-salvador-for-my-health.28424/post-329303>

> [juliemarie said:](/goto/post?id=329257)
>
> I’m a little nervous because my income is not very high (about **$30,000** a year) and I barely make it by in San Diego so I have no savings.

The current median household income for San Diego is **$91,003.**
San Diego is a city in the U.S. state of California located alongside the Pacific Ocean in Southern California.
\=======================

San Diego California how the government is supporting very low income and sick people there?

[**The County of San Diego Health and Human Services Agency (HHSA) provides vital health, housing, and social services to more than 3.3 million residents across 18 cities,** 18 federally recognized tribal reservations, 16 major naval and military installations, and the unincorporated areas of the County](https://bing.com/search?q=san+diego+california+government+support+low+income+sick+people)[1](https://bing.com/search?q=san+diego+california+government+support+low+income+sick+people).
The HHSA administers several programs that help families and individuals with low income and high medical expenses.
Each of these programs has its own eligibility rules.
[Please refer to the program website for more information](https://www.sandiegocounty.gov/content/sdc/hhsa/programs/ssp/access/access_assistance_programs.html)[2](https://www.sandiegocounty.gov/content/sdc/hhsa/programs/ssp/access/access_assistance_programs.html).
Some of these benefits include:

-   CalWORKs
-   CalFresh
-   Medi-Cal
-   Cash Assistance Program for Immigrants (CAPI)
-   General Relief
-   County Medical Services (CMS)

**[If you would like to apply for any of these programs, please click on the program link above and refer to the “How to Apply” section](https://www.sandiegocounty.gov/content/sdc/hhsa/programs/ssp/access/access_assistance_programs.html)**[2](https://www.sandiegocounty.gov/content/sdc/hhsa/programs/ssp/access/access_assistance_programs.html). I hope this helps!

### JanSz — 2023-12-16T16:55:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-move-to-el-salvador-for-my-health.28424/post-329305>

I live in NJ, immigrated (legally) to USA in 1971.
Eventually I was helping some other people whom I knew from Poland.

One woman in particular stuck in my mind.
She is healthy able to work but since little childhood her psychology does not allow her to do any work for which she would get paid.
When she got to USA her mindset has not changed.
After rather short investigation she left New Jersy and moved to Pennsylvania. (for me, out of sight out of mind).
Pennsylvania is a heaven for this type of people (according to her).
Currently she has (provided by government) apartment in high-rise in Allentown and is given some amount of money for food and daily expenses.
Her health insurance is better than mine. Everything fully paid.
All for complaining at the right time why she feels sick and not able to work.
......
Your main problem [@juliemarie](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29251/) maybe that you have been trained to work for living.
**I am sorry hearing that you do not feel well.**
///////////////

### JanSz — 2023-12-16T17:16:34+0200

[@juliemarie](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29251/)
Since you must work for living
I noted that there are three 105 old in San Diego.
Likely many more 100 year olds.
Helping this type of people maybe a good learning experience for you.
Some may rub into you (in the nice way).
Just pick the healthiest ones, so you may learn useful info.

### Evee — 2023-12-26T21:46:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-move-to-el-salvador-for-my-health.28424/post-329494>

> [alipankey said:](/goto/post?id=329472)
>
> Hi Evee,
> Were things fairly expensive or reasonable for your 6 weeks in Mexico? I was considering both Mexico and ES, but probably simply need to choose whatever is cheaper.... I'll send you a PM

I would say reasonable! If you stick in purely tourist areas, you can end up paying American prices for sure, but my Airbnb was cheap by American standards, probably about 35 a night plus fees, but it didn’t have hot water, not that I needed it, because it would get to be 90 something degrees in my apartment so I was just take a shower then. So in Mexico, I would say you could end up saving a lot of money or spending American level money depending on what you rent, where you eat etc… Overall, I was never fully out or far from from a tourist area, just not in resort territory/prices. I always tried to be mindful that despite the fact I feel broke in the US a 16:1 exchange rate made me de facto well off relative in Mexico so just be aware and use common sense

# Thread: "ALL THINGS BLUE LIGHT...." Broken Link
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-blue-light-broken-link.28427/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joseph LeBlanc — 2023-12-18T17:57:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-blue-light-broken-link.28427/post-329339>

Dr. Kruse posted a link to a fantastic study on polychromatic light suppressing melatonin on the last post of the thread titled "ALL THINGS BLUE LIGHT...."

But, the link that was posted doesn't seem to be working for some reason.

Here is a working link: [https://jdc.jefferson.edu/neurologyfp/82/](https://jdc.jefferson.edu/neurologyfp/82/)

And here is a direct link to the PDF: [https://jdc.jefferson.edu/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1081&context=neurologyfp](https://jdc.jefferson.edu/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1081&context=neurologyfp)

### Jack Kruse — 2023-12-18T19:28:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-blue-light-broken-link.28427/post-329345>

THIS IS NOT A MOUSE STUDY

Artificial man-made Blue Light "Enhances" Melatonin Suppression….via melanopsin damage………Imagine that?

I'm sorry, but we are still relatively close to the starting line when it comes to the amount of research into blue light and circadian rhythm so we kind of have to read what we have access to and do our best to be DIRECTIONALLY accurate in what we take from them and how they apply to humans.

This paper, however, is one of the best you'll find anywhere.

They tested 24 humans. Some of whom, I'm told, actually look like mice.

They determined...

"Each fluence-response curve demonstrated that increasing corneal irradiances of light-evoked progressively increased nocturnal melatonin suppression. A comparison of these fluence-response curves supports the hypothesis that polychromatic fluorescent light is more potent for melatonin regulation when enriched in the short wavelength spectrum."

Download this 30-page PDF beauty here: [https://jdc.jefferson.edu/neurologyfp/](https://jdc.jefferson.edu/neurologyfp/)

# Thread: MALES AND AUTISM LINK;  IS IT RELATED TO LIGHT TOO?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/males-and-autism-link-is-it-related-to-light-too.28428/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2023-12-18T19:09:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/males-and-autism-link-is-it-related-to-light-too.28428/post-329343>

You just got a blog on the C terminus alterations that are involved in transgenerational epigenetics. Soon in the 2023 Xmas List blog, you'll get more info.

But right now, I want to add this entry to QE 45 and 62 blogs.

Diseases affect men and women differently; specifically, males are more prone than females to viral, bacterial, fungal and parasitic diseases. Whereas estrogens seem to enhance immune responses, androgens weaken immunocompetence

Male fetuses and neonates show increased immune vulnerability compared to females, which results in a higher risk of perinatal infections. These differences could partially be due to sex steroids differential modulation of vitamin D metabolism since calcitriol, the most active vitamin D metabolite, regulates immune responses and transcriptionally induces the antimicrobial peptide cathelicidin in the human placenta.

Calcitriol availability depends on C terminus SNPs CYP27B1 and CYP24A1 expression. Remember, these are P450 cytochromes, and that means they are heme proteins subject to blue light destruction. These cytochromes are involved in its synthesis and degradation, respectively. However, the effects of testosterone on these enzymes and the final biological outcome of the calcitriol-dependent immune-target cathelicidin in the placenta have been ignored by centralized science

In a recent from Mexico City they showed that testosterone significantly inhibited CYP27B1 while stimulated CYP24A1 gene expression in cultured trophoblasts. These effects were accompanied by CREB activation through cAMP–independent and androgen receptor-dependent mechanisms. Male placental cotyledons showed reduced basal CYP27B1 and cathelicidin gene expression compared to females (_P_ < 0.05).

Testosterone concentration was higher in the cord blood of male neonates (_P_ = 0.007), whereas cathelicidin levels were lower in females (_P_ = 0.002).

These results suggest that male placentas produce less cathelicidin due to decreased calcitriol bioavailability. We propose that the observed sex-dependent differences in placental vitamin D metabolism contribute to fetal responses to infections, and migration issues and could partially explain the increased male fetuses' immune vulnerability. Moreover, gestational hyperandrogenemia could adversely affect placental vitamin D metabolism independently of fetal sex. This implies girls born to mothers with PCOS-like diseases likely have higher risks of female autism.

CITES

-   S.L. Klein

    ### [The effects of hormones on sex differences in infection: from genes to behavior](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0149763400000270)​

    ### Neurosci. Biobehav. Rev.​

    (2000)
-   V.L. Clifton

    ### [Review: sex and the human placenta: mediating differential strategies of fetal growth and survival](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0143400409003737)​

    ### Placenta​

    (2010)
-   J. Challis _et al._

    ### [Fetal sex and preterm birth](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0143400412004420)​

    ### Placenta​

    (2013)
-   L.J. Vatten _et al._

    ### [Offspring sex and pregnancy outcome by length of gestation](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0378378203001695)​

    ### Early Hum. Dev.​

    (2004)
-   R.L. Goldenberg _et al._

    ### [The Alabama preterm birth study: intrauterine infection and placental histologic findings in preterm births of males and females less than 32 weeks](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0002937806006624)​

    ### Am. J. Obstet. Gynecol.​

    (2006)

• One hundred six massively obese patients, who each lost at least 45 kg, were treated as outpatients with the rice/reduction diet, exercise, and motivational enhancement under daily supervision. Average weight loss was 63.9 kg. Forty-three patients achieved normal weight. Men lost weight at a...

 jamanetwork.com

**63.9kg=140.87 pounds
800 kilocalories (kcal) is equivalent to 800,000 calories.
This is because in the context of nutrition and food, the terms “calories” and “kilocalories” are used interchangeably**

### JanSz — 2023-12-20T20:17:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/treatment-of-massive-obesity-with-rice.28430/post-329372>

[https://eurekamag.com/research/000/563/000563533.php](https://eurekamag.com/research/000/563/000563533.php)

### JanSz — 2023-12-20T20:21:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/treatment-of-massive-obesity-with-rice.28430/post-329373>

### [Rice diet - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet)

 en.wikipedia.org

### Rice diet​

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

The **Rice Diet** started as a radical treatment for [malignant hypertension](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malignant_hypertension) before the advent of [antihypertensive drugs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antihypertensive_drugs); the original diet included strict dietary restriction and hospitalization for monitoring. Some contemporary versions have been greatly relaxed, and have been described as [fad diets](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Food_faddism).

### Origin and original form\[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Rice_diet&action=edit&section=1)\]​

The Rice Diet Program was founded in **1939 by [Walter Kempner](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Walter_Kempner&action=edit&redlink=1) \[[de](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Walter_Kempner_\(Mediziner,_1903\))\] (1903-1997),** a German physician and refugee from the Nazis, who was at that time associated with [**Duke University**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Duke_University)**.**[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-Estes-1) [\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-Klemmer-2)
Kempner had many patients with [malignant hypertension](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malignant_hypertension) with kidney failure, and there were no good treatments for those patients.
He believed that the kidney had two functions, one excretory and the other metabolic, and "he theorized that if the protein and electrolyte load on the kidney was reduced to a minimum, the kidney might better perform its more essential metabolic role.
The details of his reasoning are obscure, but he began to treat patients with malignant hypertension with a **diet composed of nothing but rice and fruit, and amazingly, they rapidly improved.**"[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-Estes-1)
Kempner's implementation was very strict, but also careful - patients were hospitalized for several weeks at the beginning of treatment.
The initial treatment was stopping all medication and putting the patient on a diet consisting of **"white rice, sugar, fruit, fruit juices, vitamins and iron, and provided about 2000 calories, 20 grams of protein, and 700–1000 ml of liquid as fruit juices. Sodium content was extremely low, about 150 milligrams per day, and chloride content about 200 milligrams per day." If results were good, after several months small amounts of lean meat and vegetables were added to the diet.**
Kempner obtained remarkable results, and he was invited to present them at a meeting of the [New York Academy of Medicine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_Academy_of_Medicine) in 1946. His presentation survives and "presents clear and unambiguous evidence, including blood pressure charts, retinal photographs, chest radiographs, electrocardiograms and laboratory results, documenting the benefits of his diet."[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-Estes-1)[\[A\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-Results-3)
Kempner described his diet as "a monotonous and tasteless diet which would never become popular.... **Kempner's only defense of its use was the fact that “it works,**” and that the diet was preferable to the alternative of certain death"[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-Estes-1)

#### Controversy\[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Rice_diet&action=edit&section=2)\]​

Kempner admitted in statements before his death that he whipped patients who avoided his rice diet.
In 1993, a former patient Sharon Ryan sued him.[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-Los_Angeles_Times-4)
Ryan accused Kempner of keeping her as a "virtual sex slave" for nearly two decades.[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-Los_Angeles_Times-4)
According to the lawsuit, Kempner "persuaded Ryan to drop out of college, moved her into a home he owned, hired her to work for the clinic, and maintained a sexual relationship with Ryan by isolating her from the outside world".[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-Associated_Press-5)

The lawsuit ended with a [confidential settlement](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Settlement_\(litigation\)).[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-6) In 1997, the _Raleigh News & Observer_ reported that, by 1975, Duke University Medical Center (now [Duke University Hospital](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Duke_University_Hospital)), knew that Kempner had used a riding crop on several patients and reprimanded him, though he continued to be associated with the university.[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-Associated_Press-5)
None of these charges were proven in court.
Dr Kempner denied the accusations of having a sexual relationship with this patient. [\[6\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-7)
" Dr Kempner had whipped this patient on several occasions, and had also whipped several other patients in an effort to motivate them.
In all instances, the patients had either themselves suggested or had consented in advance to this punishment for breaking the diet" [\[7\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-8)
These events received a great deal of sensationalist media attention.

### Contemporary forms\[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Rice_diet&action=edit&section=3)\]​

Kempner retired from the Duke Faculty in 1974, but consulted until 1992.
The commercialization of drugs to treat hypertension reduced both demand for the program and the need to make it strict in order to prevent death.
In 2002 the program became independent of Duke University, and in 2013 the Rice House Healthcare Program opened in Durham, North Carolina.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-Estes-1) The Rice House Healthcare Program is an inpatient facility where people are put on a diet akin to the original diet and are monitored.[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-9)
The rice diet has influenced some contemporary advocates of the [plant-based diet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plant-based_diet).
For example, physician [John A. McDougall](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_A._McDougall) has commented regarding the research of Walter Kempner that "all who have followed in his footsteps, including [**Nathan Pritikin**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nathan_Pritikin), [Dean Ornish](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dean_Ornish), Neal Barnard, [Caldwell Esselstyn](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caldwell_Esselstyn), and myself, owe homage to this man and his work."[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-10)
The rice diet has been popularized in a modulated form through several modern books. Judy Moscovitz in her book _The Rice Diet Report_, allows fruit, vegetables and various carbohydrates.[\[10\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-11)
Kitty and Robert Rosati authors of _The Rice Diet Solution_ describe their diet as a "low-sodium, good-carb, detox diet". It is based on the consumption of carbohydrates such as [whole grains](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Whole_grain), fruits, [vegetables](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vegetable), and beans.[\[11\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-12)

### Criticism\[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Rice_diet&action=edit&section=4)\]​

The modern version of the rice diet has been categorized as a fad diet with possible disadvantages including a boring food choice, [flatulence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flatulence), and the risk of feeling too hungry.[\[12\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-fad-13)
Most people cannot follow the rice diet over extended periods, as it is too restrictive. [Loren Cordain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Loren_Cordain) has commented that "the tenets of the Rice Diet are inconsistent with the best science of the 21st century".[\[13\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-14)
Nutritionist Yvette Quantz has suggested that although the rice diet has some good short-term benefits in the long term it does not provide "enough calories or protein for most people to sustain."[\[14\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-15)

### Notes\[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Rice_diet&action=edit&section=5)\]​

1.  **[^](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_ref-Results_3-0)** "In Kempner’s original cohort of 192 people, 25 patients died.
2.  Of the remaining 167, 60 patients did not substantially improve their blood pressure values.
3.  However, 107 patients showed significant improvement (from 200/112 mmHg to 149/96 mmHg) with the diet. Heart size decreased in 66 of 72 patients.
4.  Serum cholesterol was reduced in 73 of 82 patients.
5.  Retinopathy was reduced or disappeared completely in 21 of 33 patients.
6.  We must keep these results in context with the times, **during which the life expectancy of anyone with malignant hypertension was 6 months."**[**\[2\]**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_diet#cite_note-Klemmer-2)

### References​

I am going to the yucatan for 3 months (jan, feb, mar 2024) to heal my chronic fatigue/lyme and I have a few questions.

I will watch the sunrise and sunset in swimshorts on the beach and will stay as long in the sun as possible, which most likely will be around 2 hours in the morning and 2 in the evening. When the sun becomes too hot, I plan to stay in cenotes the rest of the day every day. My questions are:

\- Is it ok to spend as much time in the cenote, or would it be better to only spend an hour or two and spend the rest of the day outside in the shade (to better increase vit D to increase my immune system and kill the lyme)? Or is more time spend in cenote better for healing even if it means less time in the shade (which would be a filter between the sun but still increasing vit D a little bit)?

\- Can I drink the water in the cenote? If so I plan to fill up 2 liters of water per day.

\- are the differences between cenotes in terms of healing?

Thanks

> However, I was wondering if a high altitude place could yield even better results than the yucatan in jan, feb and march? Would someone know of a high altitude place?
>
> I was looking at the Andes Mountains, but it seems all places in the Andes have a rain season in jan, feb and march. This means not much sunny days and I would think this would be important to heal.

You could try the Highlands of Panama or El Salvador. Brunate.sv is an option but there are other places.

# Thread: If information is energy, and a loss of information is a loss of energy.

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7

### Johan Lindøm — 2023-12-22T06:32:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-information-is-energy-and-a-loss-of-information-is-a-loss-of-energy.28435/post-329403>

If information is energy, and a loss of information is a loss of energy. If I carve my main name into a rock with a hammer and a nail. Lets say I have two identical rocks, one with the information, my name, and one without information. Then in my imagination I make both rocks disappear, evaporate in some ideal process. Was there any difference in information loss? Did more energy go lost in the rock with carvings?

### Jack Kruse — 2023-12-22T17:19:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-information-is-energy-and-a-loss-of-information-is-a-loss-of-energy.28435/post-329422>

Landauer principle answers this.

Lets say I have two identical rocks, one with the information, my name, and one without information. Then in my imagination I make both rocks disappear, evaporate in some ideal process. Was there any difference in information loss? Did more energy go lost in the rock with carvings?

That’s a mix of all kinds of energy and information and you would have to define each, before I even understand you

Having two identical rocks is already an issue. Theoretically identical maybe.

Israel has one of the highest ratios of dietary polyunsaturated to saturated fat in the world.
The consumption of ω-6 polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFAs) in Israel is approximately 8% higher than in the USA and 10–12% higher than in most European countries.
This high intake of PUFAs, **particularly omega-6s**, is a distinctive feature of the Israeli diet.

# Thread: NAZI connections to politicians
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nazi-connections-to-politicians.28443/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JanSz — 2023-12-25T22:31:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nazi-connections-to-politicians.28443/post-329467>

After WWII Germans word criminals emigrated to Argentina with help from the Vatican.
…

Yes, it’s true that after World War II, many high-ranking Nazi officials managed to escape Europe with the help of a network of escape routes called “ratlines”.
[These routes were established by the Catholic Church, particularly the Vatican, and were used to help these officials escape](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/)[1](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/).
[They were provided with fake passports, identity papers, and travel documents](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/)[1](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/).
[The Vatican’s Secretariat of State, which used its diplomatic connections and network of Catholic priests to facilitate the escape of Nazi officials](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/)[1](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/).
[Many of these priests were part of the Roman Catholic organization called the Order of Malta, which had close ties to the Vatican](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/)[1](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/).
[Some of the high-ranking Nazis who escaped through the ratlines include Adolf Eichmann, who was responsible for the deportation of millions of Jews to concentration camps](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/)[1](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/)[, Josef Mengele, the notorious Nazi doctor who conducted horrific experiments on concentration camp inmates](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/)[1](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/)[, and Franz Stangl, the commandant of the Treblinka extermination camp, where over 800,000 Jews were murdered](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/)[1](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/).
[All of them managed to find refuge in Argentina](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/)[1](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/).

[Powerful Vatican priests aided people who they apparently thought were Catholic refugees, to escape from oppressive post-war communist regimes, many of whom turned out to be some of Germany’s worst war criminals](https://theculturetrip.com/south-america/argentina/articles/a-brief-history-of-nazi-migration-to-argentina)[2](https://theculturetrip.com/south-america/argentina/articles/a-brief-history-of-nazi-migration-to-argentina).
[The Red Cross also helped thousands of Nazi war criminals and collaborators to escape after the second world war](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2011/may/25/nazis-escaped-on-red-cross-documents)[3](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2011/may/25/nazis-escaped-on-red-cross-documents).

///////////////////////////////

### JanSz — 2023-12-26T19:14:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nazi-connections-to-politicians.28443/post-329481>

After WWII Germans word criminals emigrated to Argentina with help from the Vatican.
How that affected Catholic Church then and today?

Germans war criminals emigrated to Argentina with help from Vatican.

Effect on Catholic Church due to emigration of Germans war criminals to Argentina

[After World War II, it’s documented that many Nazis and war criminals did indeed escape to South America, particularly Argentina, often with the help of Catholic clergy](https://www.dw.com/en/the-ratlines-what-did-the-vatican-know-about-nazi-escape-routes/a-52555068)[1](https://www.dw.com/en/the-ratlines-what-did-the-vatican-know-about-nazi-escape-routes/a-52555068)[2](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/).
[This network of escape routes, known as “ratlines,” was established by the Catholic Church, particularly the Vatican](https://www.dw.com/en/the-ratlines-what-did-the-vatican-know-about-nazi-escape-routes/a-52555068)[2](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/).
[They were provided with fake passports, identity papers, and travel documents](https://www.dw.com/en/the-ratlines-what-did-the-vatican-know-about-nazi-escape-routes/a-52555068)[2](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/).
The involvement of the Catholic Church in these actions has been a subject of controversy and debate.
[Some argue that the Church’s assistance was based on a hoped-for revival of European Christianity and fear of the Soviet Union](https://www.dw.com/en/the-ratlines-what-did-the-vatican-know-about-nazi-escape-routes/a-52555068)[2](https://churchandstate.org.uk/2023/04/the-vaticans-role-in-the-ratlines-examining-the-catholic-churchs-involvement-in-nazi-escapes/).
[Others suggest that the Church was aiding people who they thought were Catholic refugees escaping from oppressive post-war communist regimes](https://www.dw.com/en/the-ratlines-what-did-the-vatican-know-about-nazi-escape-routes/a-52555068)[3](https://theculturetrip.com/south-america/argentina/articles/a-brief-history-of-nazi-migration-to-argentina).
The impact of these actions on the Catholic Church has been significant.
[The Church’s role in aiding Nazi war criminals has been criticized and has led to calls for greater transparency](https://time.com/6270677/catholic-church-holocaust-documents/)[4](https://time.com/6270677/catholic-church-holocaust-documents/)[5](https://www.commentary.org/articles/kevin-madigan/how-the-catholic-church-sheltered-nazi-war-criminals/).
[Recently unsealed documents from Vatican City archives have shed new light on the Catholic Church’s action—and inaction—during the Holocaust](https://time.com/6270677/catholic-church-holocaust-documents/)[4](https://time.com/6270677/catholic-church-holocaust-documents/).
[These documents reveal a complicated mix of actions and views among European Catholics and church leadership; there was both silence and aid, support for Jews and their Nazi tormenters, antisemitism and empathy](https://time.com/6270677/catholic-church-holocaust-documents/)[4](https://time.com/6270677/catholic-church-holocaust-documents/).
[However, it’s important to note that the interpretation of these events and the extent of the Church’s involvement varies among scholars and researchers](https://www.catholicnewsagency.com/news/24741/claims-of-papal-help-for-nazi-war-criminals-verifiably-false)[6](https://www.catholicnewsagency.com/news/24741/claims-of-papal-help-for-nazi-war-criminals-verifiably-false).
The issue continues to be a subject of ongoing historical investigation and debate.

///

### JanSz — 2023-12-26T19:21:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nazi-connections-to-politicians.28443/post-329482>

Present Catholic Pope Francis is from Argentina. Is he affected by that history?
Pope Francis and Argentina's Nazi history
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Pope Francis, born Jorge Mario Bergoglio, is indeed from Argentina.
[His tenure as the Provincial Superior of the Society of Jesus in Argentina from 1973 to 1979 coincided with the period of Argentina’s brutal military dictatorship](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2013/mar/19/pope-francis-argentina-1970s)[1](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2013/mar/19/pope-francis-argentina-1970s)[2](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2013/mar/14/pope-francis-argentina-military-junta).
There have been questions about his role during this time.
[Some critics have suggested that he did not do enough to oppose the dictatorship, while others have defended him, stating that he worked behind the scenes to save lives](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2013/mar/19/pope-francis-argentina-1970s)[1](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2013/mar/19/pope-francis-argentina-1970s)[2](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2013/mar/14/pope-francis-argentina-military-junta).
[For instance, Adolfo Pérez Esquivel, the 1980 Nobel Peace Prize winner and a victim of the dictatorship, has stated that there is no evidence linking Bergoglio to the dictatorship](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2013/mar/19/pope-francis-argentina-1970s)[1](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2013/mar/19/pope-francis-argentina-1970s).
[Pope Francis has testified in court about his actions during this period, but he has never been formally investigated](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2013/mar/19/pope-francis-argentina-1970s)[3](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-21794798).
[There is no evidence that he was in collusion with the regime](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2013/mar/19/pope-francis-argentina-1970s)[3](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-21794798).
[However, the actions of the Roman Catholic Church during the “Dirty War” are still being called into question](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-21794798)[3](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-21794798).
I
n conclusion, while Pope Francis’s actions during the dictatorship continue to be a subject of debate, there is no clear evidence linking him directly to the events of that time.
His papacy, however, is certainly influenced by the broader history of the Catholic Church’s actions during that period.
The Church’s past, including its role in the escape of Nazi war criminals to Argentina, is a part of its history that continues to be examined and understood.

This history inevitably forms part of the context in which Pope Francis leads the Catholic Church today.

//

### JanSz — 2023-12-26T19:28:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nazi-connections-to-politicians.28443/post-329483>

Saint Francis whose name current Catholic Pope took, had some activities and believes that paralel current communistic ideas. How may that have affected Pope's Francis views and ideas?

Saint Francis and communism
Pope Francis views on communism

[Pope Francis, whose papal name was inspired by Saint Francis of Assisi, has indeed shown a deep concern for the poor and marginalized, much like Saint Francis himself](https://www.grunge.com/949015/inside-pope-pius-xiis-decree-against-communism/)[1](https://www.grunge.com/949015/inside-pope-pius-xiis-decree-against-communism/).
[This has led some to draw parallels between his teachings and certain aspects of communism](https://www.bbc.com/news/magazine-33024951)[2](https://www.bbc.com/news/magazine-33024951)[3](https://www.foxnews.com/world/pope-francis-mixing-marxist-concepts-catholic-church-ideological-exploitation).
Saint Francis of Assisi was known for his devotion to poverty and his belief in living a simple life, which are principles that can be found in some interpretations of communism.
[However, it’s important to note that these principles are also deeply rooted in Christian teachings about charity, community, and the equal worth of all individuals](https://www.grunge.com/949015/inside-pope-pius-xiis-decree-against-communism/)[1](https://www.grunge.com/949015/inside-pope-pius-xiis-decree-against-communism/).
[Pope Francis has been vocal in his critique of unbridled capitalism and economic inequality](https://www.bbc.com/news/magazine-33024951)[2](https://www.bbc.com/news/magazine-33024951).
[He has stressed the Christian duty to combat poverty through love and charity](https://www.grunge.com/949015/inside-pope-pius-xiis-decree-against-communism/)[4](https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/world/the-pope-is-a-communist-christians-react-to-pope-s-call-for-them-to-donate-to-charity-to-end-poverty/ss-AA1kvVug).
[However, he has also made it clear that his views should not be mistaken for an endorsement of communism](https://www.acton.org/pub/commentary/2016/12/07/does-pope-francis-really-believe-communists-think)[5](https://www.acton.org/pub/commentary/2016/12/07/does-pope-francis-really-believe-communists-think)[6](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/pope-francis-defends-himself-against-communism-claims/).
[In fact, the Catholic Church has historically been critical of both communism and unbridled capitalism, viewing both as systems that can lead to exploitation and a disregard for the dignity of the individual](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decree_against_Communism)[7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decree_against_Communism)[8](https://www.americamagazine.org/faith/2019/07/23/catholic-case-communism).
In conclusion, while there are some parallels between the teachings of Saint Francis, the views of Pope Francis, and certain aspects of communism, it’s important to understand these within the broader context of Christian social teaching.
Pope Francis’s views are influenced by a range of factors, including his own personal experiences, his understanding of the Gospel, and the social teachings of the Catholic Church.
His focus on poverty and economic inequality is consistent with a long tradition of Catholic social thought that emphasizes the dignity of the individual, the importance of community, and a preferential option for the poor.

///

### JanSz — 2023-12-31T03:53:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nazi-connections-to-politicians.28443/post-329669>

### [Why Argentina's Government Welcomed Nazis After WWII](https://www.thoughtco.com/why-did-argentina-accept-nazi-criminals-2136579)

Learn why thousands of Nazis and wartime collaborators went to Argentina and were welcomed there after WWII.

 www.thoughtco.com

### Why Argentina Accepted Nazi War Criminals After World War II​

-   [Important Argentines Were Sympathetic](https://www.thoughtco.com/why-did-argentina-accept-nazi-criminals-2136579#toc-important-argentines-were-sympathetic)
-   [Connection to Europe](https://www.thoughtco.com/why-did-argentina-accept-nazi-criminals-2136579#toc-connection-to-europe)
-   [Financial Incentive](https://www.thoughtco.com/why-did-argentina-accept-nazi-criminals-2136579#toc-financial-incentive)
-   [The Nazi Role in Perón's "Third Way"](https://www.thoughtco.com/why-did-argentina-accept-nazi-criminals-2136579#toc-the-nazi-role-in-perns-third-way)
-   [Americans and British Didn't Want to Give Them to Communist Countries](https://www.thoughtco.com/why-did-argentina-accept-nazi-criminals-2136579#toc-americans-and-british-didnt-want-to-give-them-to-communist-countries)
-   [Legacy of Argentina's Nazis](https://www.thoughtco.com/why-did-argentina-accept-nazi-criminals-2136579#toc-legacy-of-argentinas-nazis)
-   [Additional References](https://www.thoughtco.com/why-did-argentina-accept-nazi-criminals-2136579#toc-additional-references)

By
[Christopher Minster](https://www.thoughtco.com/christopher-minster-2136113)
Updated on April 04, 2021
After World War Two, thousands of Nazis and wartime collaborators from France, Croatia, Belgium and other parts of Europe were looking for a new home: preferably as far away from [the Nuremberg Trials](https://www.thoughtco.com/the-nuremberg-trials-1779316) as possible. Argentina welcomed hundreds if not thousands of them: the [Juan Domingo Perón](https://www.thoughtco.com/biography-of-juan-peron-2136581) regime went to great lengths to get them there, sending agents to Europe to ease their passage, providing travel documents, and in many cases covering expenses.

Even those accused of the most heinous crimes, such as [Ante Pavelic](https://www.thoughtco.com/latin-american-history-4133296) (whose Croatian regime murdered hundreds of thousands of Serbs, Jews and Romani people), [Dr. Josef Mengele](https://www.thoughtco.com/20th-century-people-and-events-4133272) (whose cruel experiments are the stuff of nightmares) and [Adolf Eichmann](https://www.thoughtco.com/the-eichmann-trial-1779368) ([Adolf Hitler's](https://www.thoughtco.com/adolf-hitler-biography-1221627) architect of the Holocaust) were welcomed with open arms. It begs the question: Why on Earth would Argentina want these men? The answers may surprise you.

## Important Argentines Were Sympathetic​

Argentine President Juan Peron. Hulton Deutsch/Getty Images
During [World War Two](https://www.thoughtco.com/overview-of-world-war-ii-105520), Argentina clearly favored the Axis because of close cultural ties with Germany, Spain, and Italy. This is not surprising, as most Argentines were of Spanish, Italian, or German descent.

Nazi Germany nurtured this sympathy, promising important trade concessions after the war. Argentina was full of Nazi spies and Argentine officers and diplomats held important positions in Axis Europe. Perón's government was a big fan of the fascist trappings of Nazi Germany: spiffy uniforms, parades, rallies, and vicious anti-Semitism.

Many influential Argentines, including wealthy businessmen and members of the government, were openly supportive of the Axis cause, none more so than Perón himself, who had served as military attaché to [Benito Mussolini](https://www.thoughtco.com/benito-mussolini-1779829)'s Italian army in the late 1930s. Although Argentina would eventually declare war on the Axis powers (a month before the war ended), it was partly a ploy to get Argentine agents in place to help defeated Nazis escape after the war.

## Connection to Europe​

It's not like World War II ended one day in 1945 and suddenly everyone realized how horrible the Nazis had been. Even after Germany was defeated, there were many powerful men in Europe who had favored the Nazi cause and continued to do so.

Spain was still ruled by the fascist [Francisco Franco](https://www.thoughtco.com/francisco-franco-biography-1221852) and had been a _de facto_ member of the Axis alliance; many Nazis would find safe if temporary, haven there. Switzerland had remained neutral during the war, but many important leaders had been outspoken in their support of Germany. These men retained their positions after the war and were in a position to help out. Swiss bankers, out of greed or sympathy, helped the former Nazis move and launder funds. The Catholic Church was extremely helpful as several high-ranking church officials (including Pope Pius XII) actively aided in the Nazis' escape.

## Financial Incentive​

There was a financial incentive for Argentina to accept these men. Wealthy Germans and Argentine businessmen of German descent were willing to pay the way for escaping Nazis. Nazi leaders plundered untold millions from the Jews they murdered and some of that money accompanied them to Argentina. Some of the smarter Nazi officers and collaborators saw the writing on the wall as early as 1943 and began squirreling away gold, money, valuables, paintings and more, often in Switzerland. Ante Pavelic and his cabal of close advisors were in possession of several chests full of gold, jewelry and art they had stolen from their Jewish and Serbian victims: this eased their passage to Argentina considerably. They even paid off British officers to let them through Allied lines.

## The Nazi Role in Perón's "Third Way"​

By 1945, as the Allies were mopping up the last remnants of the Axis, it was clear that the next great conflict would come between the capitalist USA and the communist USSR. Some people, including Perón and some of his advisors, predicted that World War III would break out as soon as 1948.

In this upcoming "inevitable" conflict, third parties such as Argentina could tip the balance one way or the other. Perón envisioned nothing less than Argentina taking its place as a crucially important diplomatic third party in the war, emerging as a superpower and leader of a new world order. The Nazi war criminals and collaborators may have been butchers, but there is no doubt that they were rabidly anti-communist. Perón thought these men would come in useful in the "upcoming" conflict between the USA and the USSR. As time passed and the [Cold War](https://www.thoughtco.com/introduction-to-the-cold-war-in-europe-1221198) dragged on, these Nazis would eventually be seen as the bloodthirsty dinosaurs they were.

## Americans and British Didn't Want to Give Them to Communist Countries​

After the war, communist regimes were created in Poland, Yugoslavia, and other parts of Eastern Europe. These new nations requested the extradition of many war criminals in allied prisons. A handful of them, such as the Ustashi General Vladimir Kren, were eventually sent back, tried, and executed. Many more were allowed to go to Argentina instead because the Allies were reluctant to hand them over to their new communist rivals where the outcome of their war trials would inevitably result in their executions.

The Catholic Church also lobbied heavily in favor of these individuals not being repatriated. The allies did not want to try these men themselves (only 22 defendants were tried at the first of the infamous Nuremberg Trials and all told, 199 defendants were tried of which 161 were convicted and 37 were sentenced to death), nor did they want to send them to the communist nations that were requesting them, so they turned a blind eye to the ratlines carrying them by the boatload to Argentina.1

## Legacy of Argentina's Nazis​

In the end, these Nazis had little lasting impact on Argentina. Argentina was not the only place in South America that accepted Nazis and collaborators as many eventually found their way to Brazil, Chile, Paraguay, and other parts of the continent. Many Nazis scattered after Peron's government fell in 1955, fearing that the new administration, hostile as it was to Peron and all of his policies, might send them back to Europe.

Most of the Nazis who went to Argentina lived out their lives quietly, fearing repercussions if they were too vocal or visible. This was particularly true after 1960, when Adolf Eichmann, architect of the program of Jewish genocide, was snatched off a street in [Buenos Aires](https://www.thoughtco.com/the-history-of-buenos-aires-2136353) by a team of Mossad agents and whisked off to Israel where he was tried and executed. Other wanted war criminals were too cautious to be found: Josef Mengele drowned in Brazil in 1979 after having been the object of a massive manhunt for decades.

Nazi war criminal Adolph Eichmann stands in a protective glass booth flanked by Israeli police during his trial June 22, 1961 in Jerusalem. Handout/Getty Images
Over time, the presence of so many World War II criminals became something of an embarrassment for Argentina. By the 1990s, most of these aging men were living openly under their own names. A handful of them was eventually tracked down and sent back to Europe for trials, such as Josef Schwammberger and Franz Stangl. Others, such as Dinko Sakic and Erich Priebke, gave ill-advised interviews, which brought them to the attention of the public. Both were extradited (to Croatia and Italy respectively), tried, and convicted.

As for the rest of the Argentine Nazis, most assimilated into Argentina's sizable German community and were smart enough to never talk about their past. Some of these men were even quite successful financially, such as Herbert Kuhlmann, a former commander of the [Hitler youth](https://www.thoughtco.com/hitler-youth-and-indoctrination-1221066) who became a prominent businessman.

## Additional References​

# Thread: Headed to El Salvador in 3 days
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/headed-to-el-salvador-in-3-days.28445/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6

### maus — 2023-12-26T16:24:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/headed-to-el-salvador-in-3-days.28445/post-329476>

Hi all,

I have been a lurker on the forum during the past six months or so, trying to soak it all in.

I am 54 years old with Crohn's disease which for now seems to be in remission. I live in Massachusetts, and I have a 21 year old son. I am a widower as my wife passed away from ovarian cancer about 3 weeks ago.

We just decided to plan a father/son trip down to El Salvador to get out of the winter darkness for a little bit. It will be our first trip there.

We are arriving on the 29th and will be in ES for a little over a week. I have airplane tickets but have not yet found a place to stay. I would be grateful for any suggestions of where to stay and what to do while we are down there (besides walking on the beach at sunrise).

All the best for you.
Please work on your psychological side.
After I lost my mother who lived with us her last 25 years,
without me realizing that initially, it affected my health greatly.
I was 65yo when my mom died at 95.
Be procreative in your search for a breakout of your difficulties.

After you done with your trip and are back home,
please describe how the trip affected your Crohn's.

Published: 30 October 2023

C15:0 activates AMPK and inhibits mTOR, both of which are core components of the human longevity pathway.

“geroscience hypothesis”

[Pentadecanoic acid (15:0) is a straight-chain saturated fatty acid that is found in foods such as **beef products, dairy and egg products, lamb, veal, game products, sausages and luncheon meats, American Indian/Alaska Native foods, restaurant foods, and fast foods**](https://www.careomnia.com/nutrition-tool-nutrient?nutrientID=170) [1](https://www.careomnia.com/nutrition-tool-nutrient?nutrientID=170).
[Pentadecanoic acid has several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory properties and the ability to regulate blood sugar levels](https://www.careomnia.com/nutrition-tool-nutrient?nutrientID=170) [1](https://www.careomnia.com/nutrition-tool-nutrient?nutrientID=170)[2](https://www.consumerlab.com/answers/what-is-pentadecanoic-acid-and-does-it-have-health-benefits/pentadecanoic-acid/).
If you’re looking to incorporate more pentadecanoic acid into your diet, here are some foods that are rich in this nutrient:

1.  [**Whipped Butter (With Salt)**: 801 mg per 100g](https://www.careomnia.com/nutrition-tool-nutrient?nutrientID=170) [1](https://www.careomnia.com/nutrition-tool-nutrient?nutrientID=170).
2.  [**Sunflower Oil (High Oleic, 70% & Over)**: 800 mg per 100g](https://www.careomnia.com/nutrition-tool-nutrient?nutrientID=170) [1](https://www.careomnia.com/nutrition-tool-nutrient?nutrientID=170).
3.  [**Butter (Salted)**: 753 mg per 100g](https://www.careomnia.com/nutrition-tool-nutrient?nutrientID=170) [1](https://www.careomnia.com/nutrition-tool-nutrient?nutrientID=170).

Hi Jack,
Have been on Patreon since 2019 with a small break in between and enjoying Optimal Klubs for 8 months.
Have always been opened to new ideas. Have listened to you speak highly about Infrared Saunas and now you have done an about face. I don't think I'm the only one in the audience that would be surprised by this sudden turnaround, what seems to be over the last 6 months. I noted you quoted a company called SaunaSpace and without sounding condescending this is pretty old tech. If we are talking strictly IR, this is not in the range of visible light, as SS is. As a distributor for the no.1 in IR therapy in the marketplace and IR heater tech (if quantity sold globally was to be the metric) then the patented tech we represent is so powerful, that we have to recommend to customers within our experiential centers that the first sessions are no longer than 30 min and at a lower setting, otherwise the dose they get is too much for them. This tech is the only tech we know of willing to provide independant studies of the output of the IR heaters (99% emmisivity). We regularly over the last 8 years of representing the brand have had customers who think they understand IR, speechless at how they feel after using them. Even brand ambassadors of our competition can not understand how they can sweat so much with such low heat (and no visible light). The reason i write this is not to promote the brand, but to have an opportunity to share with you, that my offer on instagram during your live feed a couple of weeks ago to donate our largest IR sauna (value $20k) to your new longevity center in El Salvador, was for no other reason than as a thank you. A thank you for what you are doing for people and personally for my family. Somewhat surprised to receive a block on instagram @agua.loco

All the very best for 2024!!

# Thread: Fighting with frequency.......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fighting-with-frequency.28449/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2023-12-27T16:37:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fighting-with-frequency.28449/post-329515>

Hedy Lemarr was a Hollywood actress in the 1940s and 1950s, and she was considered "the most beautiful woman in the world" during her time. She began her acting career in Austria and became notorious for being the first woman to simulate an orgasm on screen in 1933. It was during this time that she also got married to Friedrich Mandl, a Vienna-based arms dealer with ties to Mussolini and later Hitler. The marriage did not last long. In her own words, she wrote, "I knew very soon that I could never be an actress while I was his wife... He was the absolute monarch in his marriage... I was like a doll. I was like a thing, some object of art which had to be guarded and imprisoned, having no mind, no life of its own." According to her autobiography, she disguised herself as one of the maids and managed to flee to Paris. Others say she convinced her husband to wear all of her jewelry for a dinner party and then disappeared afterwards. She eventually booked a liner to New York, where she met the head of MGM, who was impressed enough to offer her a $500-a-week contract to work as an actress. In 1938, she arrived in Hollywood and went on to star in several movies, working with the likes of Clark Gable and James Stewart. Beyond her acting career, Lamarr was also a scientist and co-patented spread-spectrum technology during World War II to prevent the Nazis from jamming navy torpedoes. However, her invention was rejected in 1942 and wouldn't be implemented until the Cold War in 1962. She was far ahead of her time.

During World War II, she teamed up with composer George Antheil to invent a frequency-hopping spread spectrum technology. The concept was sparked by Lamarr's desire to create a secure communication system that could prevent the Axis powers from jamming Allied torpedoes. She and Antheil used the principles of a player piano to develop a device that could rapidly switch between 88 frequencies, mirroring the 88 keys of a piano. They received a patent for their invention in 1942, but the U.S. Navy didn't implement the technology until the 1960s, under a different form.

Remarkably, this invention laid the groundwork for modern Wi-Fi, GPS, and Bluetooth technologies. Despite her contributions, Lamarr's technological prowess remained largely overshadowed by her on-screen persona for many years, only gaining widespread recognition decades later.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-12-27T16:41:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fighting-with-frequency.28449/post-329516>

Hedy Lamarr was from Austria and here is her memorial in Vienna. The 88 rods are for the frequencies in her patented Spread Spectrum invention. The balls are information packets encoded on those frequencies. If you stand in the right spot, you can see her face in the art work.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-12-27T16:42:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fighting-with-frequency.28449/post-329517>

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-12T02:48:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-excessive-use-of-nitric-oxide-something-to-avoid-and-not-to-celebrate.28450/post-331353>

Did you know the human body has over 60,000 miles of blood vessels? That’s enough to wrap around the planet more than twice! Blood vessels are the channels that carry blood throughout your body. They include arteries, veins, and capillaries. Arteries deliver oxygen-rich blood from your heart to your organs and tissues. Veins return oxygen-poor blood to your heart. Capillaries are the smallest and most numerous blood vessels, where the exchange of oxygen, nutrients, and waste products occurs between the blood and the cells. Blood vessels are essential for maintaining your health and homeostasis because they carry the WiFI connection that connect the sun to our colony of mitochondria.

# Thread: Cat allergy and how I dealt with it.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cat-allergy-and-how-i-dealt-with-it.28453/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lukassss — 2023-12-28T11:19:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cat-allergy-and-how-i-dealt-with-it.28453/post-329536>

I spent my entire childhood with cats. There were 6 cats in my family home.
When I left my family home 20 years ago, returning to my family home and any other place where cats live became a problem for me.

Staying in rooms where there were cats or petting a cat caused me: initially, a severe runny nose, watery and itchy eyes. After spending the night in such a house, I felt very bad.

That's why for me every Christmas and Easter are 4 days of fighting for life...

I have tested many solutions to this problem.
\- antihistamines worked only in the initial phase. I did NOT take medications, I did not consider them to be a solution.
\- I tested various supplements in high doses - 0 effects
\- CT gave an amazing effect. Having a stuffy nose, runny nose and watery eyes, within 30 seconds of entering the icy water my nose was clear and my allergy symptoms disappeared. This method has helped me for the last 3 years. It had a short duration of action, like antihistamines.

This year for Christmas I fasted for 6 days (I didn't eat anything, only water).

**FASTING EFFECT**
I slept for 3 nights in a room with a cat, I could pet it. I had no allergy symptoms.
I will add that I started fasting 2 days before arriving at the house where there were cats. After 2 days I was already in ketosis.

Christmas without food and without allergies

# Thread: Saturated fats = low deuterium diets.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturated-fats-low-deuterium-diets.28454/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2023-12-28T15:26:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturated-fats-low-deuterium-diets.28454/post-329541>

In 1968, Ancel Keys & Ivan Frantz began a randomized control trial involving 9000+ people to study the effects of replacing saturated fat with vegetable oils high in linoleic acid. The study concluded in 1973, but the complete results remained unpublished during Keys' lifetime.

In 2013, the raw data was discovered at the estate of Ivan Frantz. Analysis of the data showed that while replacing saturated fats with vegetable oils did reduce serum cholesterol by 14% on average, it did not reduce the risk of death from coronary heart disease or other causes.

In fact, cardiovascular mortality increased when saturated fats were replaced.

According to the paper, "There was a 22% higher risk of death for each 30 mg/dL (0.78 mmol/L) reduction in serum cholesterol."

Imagine that.

### [Records Found in Dusty Basement Undermine Decades of Dietary Advice](https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/records-found-in-dusty-basement-undermine-decades-of-dietary-advice/)

Raw data from a 40-year-old study raises new questions about fats

 www.scientificamerican.com

### Jack Kruse — 2023-12-28T15:29:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturated-fats-low-deuterium-diets.28454/post-329542>

### [Re-evaluation of the traditional diet-heart hypothesis: analysis of recovered data from Minnesota Coronary Experiment (1968-73)](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4836695/)

Objective To examine the traditional diet-heart hypothesis through recovery and analysis of previously unpublished data from the Minnesota Coronary Experiment (MCE) and to put findings in the context of existing diet-heart randomized controlled ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### Jack Kruse — 2023-12-28T15:29:10+0200

The Long Read: In 1972, a British scientist sounded the alarm that sugar – and not fat – was the greatest danger to our health. But his findings were ridiculed and his reputation ruined. How did the world’s top nutrition scientists get it so wrong for so long?

 www.theguardian.com
