# Thread: gynecologist in El Salvador?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gynecologist-in-el-salvador.28455/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evee — 2023-12-28T19:25:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gynecologist-in-el-salvador.28455/post-329553>

Planning on traveling to El Salvador and I have a unique medical situation where I might need emergency gynecologic surgery (explanation at the end if you want to understand why, but skip if you don't want awful medical details.)
So I'm trying to live life and improve my health in spite of this, and planning to go to ES, but feel I need to know where my closest hospitals and surgeons are just in case. I can find some on google, but alway appreciate an insight if someone has personal experience with a doctor. Has anyone been to the gynecologist in El Salvador? [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) Any gyno doc friends in ES you recommend?

Of course, I'll be getting travel insurance for emergencies

Details/long story: prev post here [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-itragenic-harm.28357/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-itragenic-harm.28357/)

But to recap, I had what's called risk reducing surgery as I'm thought to be high risk for uterine cancer due to a germline defect. Surgery done March 2022: robotic, lap, hysterectomy/salpingectomy vaginal assist at age 40. Still have my ovaries and I did not actually have cancer. I thought I was helping myself at the time, but 10 weeks post op, the surgery site came apart during sex (yes, it was horrific and traumatizing)

I had emergency repair surgery at a public hospital in France July 2022.

I seemed to be fine since then, some pain with deep penetration during sex, but nothing that ruins it. I but presented to a gyno in Mexico due to abdominal pressure and he did what no doc in the US or France ever did, which was give me an ultrasound and the surgical site (top of the vagina) is overly thin in about the middle third. (only 1.9mm thick basically keeping my internal organs in my body) Maybe this thin part was missed during the first repair, but the bottom line is the Doc in Mexico feels I'm very likely to have another tear, especially being a sexually active person.

In the USA, we don't measure cuff thickness and most docs agree that I'm high risk for another tear but there's no standard of care for what to do now. I can watch and wait and see if I have another emergency. I can justify a repair surgery to insurance based on pain with sex, but the thought of messing up the 2/3 of the surgery site that is healed correctly to fix the third that isn't, also doesn't feel like a great option. (Though there may be an argument to be made for a controlled surgery with a skill urogyn VS waiting to end up in another ER.)

At the moment, I'm just trying to get back to the sun and get as much red light on my body as possible. There's structural problem with the surgical site, so unfortunately red light alone will not fix that, though I understand light is a foundation of health in general so that why I'm trying to figure out how to safely get to ES (second choice back to Mexico where I already have found a good gyno in case I have an emergency.) If I do any kind of preventative surgery in the USA where I have insurance, it will only be in the summer months. So that's the long story, if you're curious why I need a gyno surgeon on speed dial. Any advice in general welcome, esp if you have experience in risk/benefit of a repair surgery to attempt to pre-empt an issue

### Jack Kruse — 2023-12-28T23:05:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gynecologist-in-el-salvador.28455/post-329558>

My advice is have a GYN on speed dial in Houston, Dallas, or Atlanta. If you pop you need to get on a plane. Those are the three cities airline fly to most often from here.

### Evee — 2023-12-29T02:22:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gynecologist-in-el-salvador.28455/post-329564>

# Thread: Sperti use in winter outdoors
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sperti-use-in-winter-outdoors.28456/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kendall Toerner — 2023-12-29T01:06:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sperti-use-in-winter-outdoors.28456/post-329562>

I purchased a Sperti UV lamp to help with winter in Colorado Springs, and was going to purchase a Firedragon by EMR-TEK to balance the UV with IR.

It then occurred to me to just use the Sperti outside, getting CT as an added benefit.

As this is quite the environmental mismatch (but a fun variation of Sherpa quantum effects), and albeit necessary for my surfaces, does anyone know if the IR during winter at such a latitude is able to balance such strong UV? Especially on cloudy days with indirect sunlight. I'm basically thinking of using just the sun's IR with the Sperti, or adding in a Firedragon to also use outdoors alongside the sun.

My intuition as a photographer is that the sun is so bright, and half of it is IR light, so the sun should be enough. Curious to hear any habits of others supplementing with UV during winter. Not an option to travel or live other places during this next year of my life unfortunately.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-12-29T03:30:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sperti-use-in-winter-outdoors.28456/post-329566>

Especially on cloudy days with indirect sunlight. I'm basically thinking of using just the sun's IR with the Sperti, or adding in a Firedragon to also use outdoors alongside the sun.
>
> My intuition as a photographer is that the sun is so bright, and half of it is IR light, so the sun should be enough. Curious to hear any habits of others supplementing with UV during winter. Not an option to travel or live other places during this next year of my life unfortunately.

Hey Kendall, Makes sense to me that outside would make more sense. my curiosity was piqued by your being a photographer. Can you use a digital camera as a spectrograph? I remember having a pictograph of the light for each exposure but to be honest i never thought much about it without my current interest. Can you take a pic and find out the intensity/ratios of different spectrums? Thanks

### Kendall Toerner — 2023-12-29T16:36:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sperti-use-in-winter-outdoors.28456/post-329579>

> [Marko Pollo said:](/goto/post?id=329566)
>
> Hey Kendall, Makes sense to me that outside would make more sense. my curiosity was piqued by your being a photographer. Can you use a digital camera as a spectrograph? I remember having a pictograph of the light for each exposure but to be honest i never thought much about it without my current interest. Can you take a pic and find out the intensity/ratios of different spectrums? Thanks

Hey Marko, very interesting idea with the DSLR. The histogram is primarily for measuring brightness levels of a color throughout the photo. Unfortunately these don't correspond to wavelengths, and it's likely even if they did, several filters inside the camera would need to be removed to get a proper reading beyond the visible spectrum. I'm unsure if consumer camera lenses would also negatively affect the accuracy of the wavelengths output.

I'm curious about finding a good spectrograph to test my environment as well. If you come across one to rent or one that's fairly inexpensive to buy with decent results let me know.

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-01-03T02:18:48+0200

> µW vs mW (factor of 1000) . . . /cm2 vs /m2 (factor of 10,000)

Yes but mW is larger than µW. His device is measuring in µW/microwatts which is 1000x less than mW/milliwatts. UV in tropics is 375 milliwatts, not even remotely close to the 31 microwatts he measured (0.031 milliwatts)

Tropics UV compared to his measurement is 10,000x higher (albeit a mix of UVA and UVB, with UVB around 5%). That still leaves it at 500x higher UVB if his is an instantaneous measurement and not a measurement over time.

> Tropics UV compared to his measurement is 10,000x higher (albeit a mix of UVA and UVB, with UVB around 5%). That still leaves it at 500x higher UVB if his is an instantaneous measurement and not a measurement over time.

I've had this meter for around five years and the manufacturer confirmed with me the UVA and UVB probes are designed for accurate readings of solar light.

I would never buy a bulb today with mercury in it, much less spend $600 on a Sperti, but I also never use any bulb I haven't measured myself to have a flicker rate below 1%.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sperti-use-in-winter-outdoors.28456/post-329770>

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-01-03T21:30:45+0200

Ah shit I didn’t convert properly from my original source, UV index of 15 is 40 µW/cm^2 for both UVA and UVB combined.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-01-01T20:21:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-ddw-water-more-ez.28457/post-329707>

Solvation is kinetic in nature where water molecules have to move about to rip apart a solute substance particle by particle and disperse it throughout the bulk fluid.

Lower mass = higher velocity = faster solvation

# Thread: Using mirrors to boost sunlight in winter

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kendall Toerner — 2023-12-29T18:20:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-mirrors-to-boost-sunlight-in-winter.28458/post-329583>

Has anyone here tried using mirrors to amplify sun/UV exposure in winter (or summer if desired)?

For example, a rotating platform with 6 tall mirrors all calibrated/angled to reflect light to the center. Rotate the platform to face the sun as it moves (calibrated to work during a specific hour of day/sun elevation)

Can any reflective material efficiently reflect IR light? Would this effectively create a man-made lighting situation as the frequencies are modified from the reflection?

### Marko Pollo — 2023-12-29T18:43:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-mirrors-to-boost-sunlight-in-winter.28458/post-329586>

I have a solar oven that uses reflective panels and it works well. I would be wary of anything that might intensify the sunlight too much like ants under a magnifying glass. Matte white paint might hold in and reflect light while also diffusing it to remove hotspots. A small pod that blocks the wind, retains some heat and intensifies light lives in my head.

### rtd — 2023-12-29T19:39:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-mirrors-to-boost-sunlight-in-winter.28458/post-329589>

Big TVs from late 90s early 00s had a fresnel lens. That lens could start fires, melt pennies, etc.

### coho3131 — 2023-12-31T01:57:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-mirrors-to-boost-sunlight-in-winter.28458/post-329661>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=329657)
>
> Honest question - why? Yes, the type of glass would be a factor but is sun light reflected off water nnEMF? If not then why would light reflected off other shinny surfaced be?

This is my understanding.

Glass and metal absorb important amounts of the native EMF in unnatural ways, and the reflection is nnEMF.

Sunlight reflected off of bodies of water, or ice and snow (frozen water), is similar to sunlight reflected off of clouds (water and ice crystals).

Even on a day with no clouds, the sunlight is reflecting off of water vapor.

We are adapted to sunlight reflecting off of water and other surfaces in Nature, but not to sunlight reflecting off of artificial surfaces.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-water-is-everybody-drinking.28459/post-329591>

We used a BErkey filter for the last years to filter out fluoride and dirt, but maybe that’s not optimal, especially regarding deuterium, for daily use.

I am aware that „mountain spring water“ is best, but I cannot find anything about the whereabouts of certain mountain springwaters. Lake arrowhead seems to be a blend of southern Californian wells. I don’t think there can be much mountain to it.

Are you here in the forum getting the Fiji water? Topo Chico? I hear that’s what the people up on the hill are drinking.

What’s a good brand? Preferably not breaking the bank.

### Daulatwant — 2025-01-20T22:15:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-water-is-everybody-drinking.28459/post-345488>

People in the US and Canada living east of the rocky mountains have potentially very low deuterium water sources. The rain is easily 135-140ppm, in a large area. Idaho, Montana and Wyoming seem to be the best situated for Low Deuterium. But I saw data on springs of 135ppm even in California. Certainly there is a big area of the USA where snow could be getting close to 130ppm according to such graphs.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

δ2H - number for the deviation from VSMOW standard which is 155.76 ppm

2Hppm = (1000+ δ2H )/6.420135 for example (1000 -158.3 ) / 6.420135 = 131.1ppm
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

### Brent Patrick — 2025-07-31T00:42:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-water-is-everybody-drinking.28459/post-357235>

Pellegrino is no issue ,naturally occurring spring waters contain Calcium Fluoride but even local source spring water has to be tested for Deuterium levels and may not be a good alternative .Store bought waters and municipal water contain sodium Fluoride and other deviations of that.

Sodium fluoride dissolves easily in water while calcium fluoride does not due to being an inorganic compound and is practically insoluble in water.

**Sodium fluoride is readily soluble in water .**

_**

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ways-to-deplete-deuterium.20366/page-8#post-228308](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ways-to-deplete-deuterium.20366/page-8#post-228308)

**_

### AlbertW — 2025-08-06T23:06:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-water-is-everybody-drinking.28459/post-357696>

I see that mineral waters in the US have very low calcium, magnesium, etc.

Is there benefit to drinking European waters like Gerolsteiner for the high mineral contents? I can find Gerolsteiner sparkling water in the US, but it seems the European brands never sell still water in the US.

### Daulatwant — 2025-08-07T16:06:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-water-is-everybody-drinking.28459/post-357739>

For me adding minerals to water like sodium, potassium, magnesium was very useful. But relying on added salts in the long term became it's own problem.

Eating plenty of fresh meat, adding salt to taste and drinking water to thirst should be all that is needed for most people.

The benefits or downsides of supplemental minerals or electrolytes are very individual and situational. Something that worked good one day may make things worse another day or if done too frequently.

### Rudolf — 2026-02-04T20:14:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-water-is-everybody-drinking.28459/post-366366>

What is available in **Italy** and **Germany** is **_Acqua Plose_**, more northern than _St Pellegrino_ and _Acqua Panna_ (not because only altitude-wise but raining wise, the other two are really south of the alps) and altitude of the spring is 1800m over sea level. Another choice is _lauretana_, with 1000 m over sea level. Those are my new choices.
Cheers!

# Thread: New Member - Here is my Story!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-here-is-my-story.28463/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jonesy — 2023-12-31T00:43:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-here-is-my-story.28463/post-329654>

**History**

-   It was in 2016 when I felt numbness in my right cheek, chin and tip of my nose. This only lasted an hour or so, but it prompted me to go to emergency and get checked. They did an ECG and some other tests and everything was OK. This prompted them to refer me to a neurologist. The neurologist ordered an MRI along with some other tests and everything was normal.
-   Around 2017 I was going through a very stressful time in my life. Some of the first symptoms I was experiencing was a heightened startle response and very poor sleep.
-   Anxiousness was becoming a significant issue. I wouldn't call it anxiety, I just suddenly developed an anxious feeling in my body all the time. This was about 2016/2017. It is hard to explain…..I just felt anxious. This is when I was starting to really struggle with sleeping. Normally I was quite a good sleeper unless I had something to be excited about, but this was different as I wasn't sleeping well on a regular basis. At this point, the only symptoms I had was the incident with the numbness on my face, which as mentioned only lasted an hour or so, and poor sleep, anxiousness, and a very heightened startle response. After this, the symptoms you see below slowly started to creep in.

**Symptoms**

-   My muscles have a strange rippling in them when they are under tension or when flexed. This is something that I think offers one of the biggest clues....whenever a muscle is under tension, it ripples. If I lay back and extend my quad, you will see my muscles ripple. They did a genetic test for rippling muscle disease (CAV 3), but it was negative. Most of the physicians are not sure what to make of it and appear to shrug their shoulders.
-   All symptoms below have been slowly progressive
-   Significant Clonus
-   Significant Muscle Spasticity
-   Tone
-   Hyper reflexes
-   Stiff Rigid gait
-   Decreased dexterity in my left hand was one of my very early symptoms starting about 5 years ago in 2018. It then moved to my legs and I am now seeing decreased dexterity in my right hand.
-   I have no balance, agility, dexterity and coordination. I have always been very athletic and am now not able to run, play hockey, mountain bike, play golf, do jumping jacks, walk down stairs properly, etc. When walking in the dark my balance is even worse.
-   Fasciculations and twitching started in 2018 before any movement issues. They started with the fingers twitching in my left hand. Today, they are significant and the fasciculations will randomly move throughout my body. Sometimes it is my right bicep for a day or so, then that goes away and it is my left triceps, then chest, abs, and even the bottom of my feet. I don't think there is a muscle in my body that hasn't twitched. They are, however, constant in my calves daily. Some of the fasciculations can be quite significant and are very visible to the eye.
-   When I am in a heightened emotional state my rigidity and spasticity it at maximum. It can make it very hard to walk. If I see someone that I haven't seen in a while and I know they are looking at how I walk, my legs will completely tense up. When I see a doctor my legs are much worse than normal because I know they are staring at my legs and studying how I walk. Knowing this makes my walking much worse. The mind-body connection is strong. When I am extremely excited, my kneecaps will start bouncing up and down and my ankle clonus will basically start on its own. I am very sensitive to heightened emotional situations.......good or bad.
-   Cold affects me significantly. If I walk out on a cold winter day, I completely tense up and can barely walk. I tried a cold bath recently and my entire body was shaking uncontrollably to the point where I don't think I would have been able to stand if I didn't add warm water first.
-   I have developed a significant startle response to sudden noise when I am in a relaxed state. This is one of the early symptoms I started experiencing back in 2016/2017.
-   I am also sensitive to touch or movement. While in bed at night, if my wife moves, it can cause my body to tense up momentarily. Even my own movement will result in my body tensing up for a few seconds before relaxing.
-   Myoclonic Jerks - I will sometimes get a needle-like sensation in my foot which will cause a myoclonic jerk in my leg. Lately, I have been getting a myoclonic jerk in my legs when tired. Sometimes even my own thoughts can cause a myoclonic jerk. These myoclonic jerks happen while awake, not while falling asleep which is common.
-   Speech - some days I seem to be mumbling on my words and having issues with my speech. Some days it just takes more effort to properly annunciate my words. On other days I am fine.
-   I can't rapidly tap my hands, or fingers or shuffle my feet. I can't do anything fast.

**Strength:**

-   **And here is probably the most complex mystery to the neurologists:**
-   At age 54, after having symptoms slowly start 6-7 years ago, I have no muscle atrophy and have very nicely shaped muscle belly’s according to the neurologist. In fact, I am able to gain strength in the gym during weight training. I am now lifting weights that I have not lifted since university in my 20's when I was hardcore into weight training. I'm as strong as I have ever been. I have actually tracked my reps and poundage over the last 3-4 years and I continue to beat previous strength records. For example, I can do over 50 diamond push-ups, incline bench press 215 lbs for 16 reps on my 4th set, shoulder press 185 lbs for 15 reps on my 4th set, bicep curl 30 lbs per arm for 30 reps on my 3rd set. I don’t say this to brag and have been holding back talking about the weights I have been lifting because I am too humble, but I feel it is a very important part of the picture and that the neurologists need to be aware that I am throwing around some serious weights for a 54 year old. I even went so far as to take a picture of myself in the mirror with my shirt off so as to show them my muscle tone. That was very awkward for me to do, but I think it opened their eyes up.
-   I have no problem with stamina. I can ride the elliptical/stepper for 30-60 mins at a fairly strong resistance with no problem. About 1.5 years ago the neurologists ran some lung tests and my lung capacity was at 104% of normal.
-   I actually feel great and energetic! Other than the physical symptoms listed above, I feel completely normal and am very fit!

**

Neurologist Visits:**

-   I have seen 6 neurologists in Calgary, 1 in Edmonton and 1 in New York.
-   The bottom line is they don't really know what I have and aren't willing to give a firm diagnosis.
-   The neurologist said they see increasing signs of denervation on the EMG test. I then ask “denervation leads to muscle weakness and atrophy correct?” To which he answers “yes”. I then say “yet I have no muscle atrophy at all and in fact am continuing to get stronger”. His reply is “yes, that is why this is such a mystery”. I asked if there are conditions other than motor neuron disease that cause denervation. He said there are many, but these should have been revealed on the nerve conduction study test….which keeps coming back normal.
-   Some of the potential diagnosis are: Stiff Person Syndrome, Hereditary Spastic Paraplegia, Some sort of Autoimmune process, PLS and ALS. Although, they say ALS does NOT appear to be compatible because at the very least I should be weak by now after this many years, but the opposite is happening and I am getting stronger.
-   My neurologist in New York at first thought probably PLS, but now after seeing him for over a year and seeing my strength he now thinks it is some sort of virally mediated autoimmune condition. He also said not to get too hung up on the tests that are showing denervation.
-   My main neurologist is now in the process of referring me to the Mayo Clinic. Since I am in Canada, this is quite a cumbersome process and could take some time.

**Medical Tests:**

-   I have had so many tests that I can’t list them all or have forgotten, but here are some:
-   Multiple EMG tests – didn’t show denervation at first, but does now.
-   Nerve Conduction - normal
-   Multiple MRI’s – basically normal
-   I also had an upright MRI in New York where they looked at my Cerebrospinal Fluid Flow and my Cranial Cervical Junction. I have reduced CSF flow, a slight cerebellum tonsil and ligament/alignment issues.
-   CT Scan (From neck down – normal)
-   2 Lumbar Punctures which tested for many different issues. All negative except for slightly elevated protein.
-   PET Scan - showed some hypometabolism in bi-lateral cortices - Can be seen in ALS, but the neurologist said it is non-specific and could be from a concussion.
-   Muscle Biopsy - showed some signs suggestive of denervation
-   Numerous blood tests for a variety of other things such as tropical viruses etc.
-   I have Gene C9orf72 mutation in the intermediate range. My score was 20. 2-19 = Normal, 20-30 = Intermediate, Over 30 = Pathogenic. Although some criteria suggest normal results are 2-29. My SOD 1 was negative. I had an appointment with a Geneticist and they do not suspect ALS based on this result.
-   Genetic test for Hereditary Spastic Paraplegia – negative
-   Isaacs Syndrome - negative
-   Had a Whole Exome Sequencing genetic test completed with no findings. Currently waiting for results from Whole Genome Sequencing test.
-   Stiff Person Syndrome GAD-65 came back negative. They tested this a few times.

**Functional medicine tests:**

-   I have a binder of tests from the numerous functional medicine doctors that I have seen, but for the sake of not making my journal longer than it already is, I will only mention a few:
-   I have tested positive for CIRS (Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome)
-   I have a strong positive test result for lyme disease through Armin Labs, however, the follow-up test I did a year later was barely positive. This test was run through one of my functional medicine doctors who then put me through an antibiotic protocol which didn't appear to have much impact on my symptoms. My neurologists dismissed this test because they ran their own test through Alberta Health Care and it came back negative.....apparently they miss 50% of positive tests. I have spent a significant portion of my life in the forest from a very young age, so exposure wood ticks is significant.
-   I have tested positive for the multi-susceptible gene for CIRS and Lyme
-   My Leptin test result was low at 2.2ng/ml

**Medications Tried:**

-   Clonazopane - very small dose - no benefit
-   Baclofen - max dose - no benefit
-   Levodop/Carbidop - no benefit
-   They also tried IVIG 3 days per month for two 6 months trials. Possible very minor benefit.
-   My family doctor decided to try Sertraline just to see if it would help. It had no effect. In fact, I felt better once I quit taking it.

**Emotional:**

-   I don't spontaneously burst out laughing or crying for no reason, but I do find I don't need much of a trigger anymore for me to tear up. The same goes for smiling or laughing, it doesn’t take much. I find I am far more emotional than I used to be. Watching TV or emotional situations can bring me to tears far easier than they used to. I also find I smile or laugh more frequently and maybe more than I should. Pseudobulbar affect or just extreme emotions? I honestly feel like my nervous system is a complete mess causing me to be overly emotional.

**Stomach Issues:**

-   I have had stomach issues for at least 10 years. I have always been bloated, even in my youth. I used to alternate between diarrhea and constipation. I finally got tired of it and went to see a functional medicine practitioner and he tested for SIBO which was positive. He put me on herbs and that has cleared up the explosive diarrhea.
-   For many years (20 or more) I had significant heartburn and acid reflux. I took a lot of Zantac to combat this. Now, if I eat a clean diet, I have no heartburn.
-   I do not have celiac, but do have a major gluten intolerance. If I have pastries, you can almost guarantee diarrhea for a few days. I used to react negatively to dairy and eggs also, but seem to be a little better than I used to be.

**Other Symptoms:**

-   I am highly stressed and have been since about 2017. The symptoms get worse with stress and heightened emotions.
-   Tested positive for EBV and CMV
-   I used to get a strange buzzing/vibration feeling in my body. Primarily through my abdomen and chest.
-   My legs feel like they are stuck in mud, heavy and in slow motion
-   Urinary Urgency is worse than it has ever been. Not sure if this is because of my low ADH? Although, strangely enough, I almost never get up to pee at night.
-   Vertigo happens once in a while, but not often.
-   Symptoms I sometimes get: Numbness in the face and heel
-   Intermittent sore throat with redness. Intermittent raspy voice
-   Hair Thinning - This is not expected of me as my brothers, my dad and both grandpas have full heads of hair. Just a few years ago my new barber commented how I have such thick hair, and now he comments on how it is thinning.
-   Slight Vitiligo on my forehead. I never had this before. It becomes more obvious when I am in the sun.
-   I have noticed an increase in Cherry Angiomas all over my body.
-   My hands really slow down and lose dexterity when they are cold. This was one of my earlier symptoms a few years ago.
-   My thumb will sometimes stick when in a "pinching" position, but not when grabbing a doorknob or shaking hands
-   Typing on the keyboard is better some days than other days.
-   I can get major cramping in my foot and toes, especially at night. My fingers will also cramp often.
-   I feel like I am walking around drunk sometimes, but if I do have a couple of beers, my symptoms are very exacerbated.
-   When I work out my legs get extremely shaky.
-   During the night in bed, I am unable to roll from my side to my back without having to tense my legs when they straighten out. This wakes me at night. This started in 2028 and is extremely frustrating due to the lack of sleep it is causing. This lack of proper deep sleep is a HUGE concern for me.
-   Lower back “goes out” about once every month or so and is extremely painful. My neck also “goes out” about every 2 months.
-   Interestingly, I almost never get sick. I didn't have a cold or even a sniffle for 5-6 years until I finally got covid in November 2022. I can feel "run down" with extreme fatigue for a day or so, but haven't had any cold symptoms for a few years.
-   My muscles are way more defined and have never looked this way before. Not sure why this is as it should be the opposite as you get older.
-   If I step on something sharp I have an extreme reaction and can almost be brought to my knees.

**Anxiety:**

-   I have always been somewhat of an anxious person who worried a lot, but about 5 years ago I was more anxious than I have ever been. I had some extra stressors going on in my life at the time. That is when my sleep significantly deteriorated and my stomach issues started to get worse.
-   My anxiety peaked during the Christmas 2020 break when I experienced, for the first time in my life, a couple of panic attacks. My legs stiffened up so much with anxiety that I couldn't walk into the back of the ER and they wheeled me around in a wheelchair. I had so much shaking that 2 separate doses of Ativan had no effect. Since that Christmas season, my anxiety had settled a fair amount.
-   My family doctor tried Sertraline (eventually up to 200 mg per day) with no effect on my anxiety. In fact, I felt better when I finally stopped taking it.

**Physical Changes:**

-   My calves are always tight
-   Morning stiffness
-   Feels like I am walking in mud
-   I can't tap, shuffle feet, dance, or do anything fast

**Joint & Mobility Changes:**

-   Very reduced joint mobility. My joints aren't really sore per se, but the range of motion is significantly reduced in my hips, wrists and ankles.
-   For example, picking up a pill is difficult
-   Constantly walking on tip-toes
-   Walking downstairs is very difficult
-   My legs often go stiff and tense causing me to fall backwards if I don't grab something.
-   I have developed joint mobility and flexibility issues.
-   Can't touch thumb to pinky finger
-   If I trip or get off balance, I stiffen up and can't stick my leg out to brace my fall and I just go down. Have broken a rib because of this.

**General Comments:**

-   I feel as though I am in constant fight or flight - need to calm down my nervous system. The QEEG plus other tests basically show I have limbic system impairment and am in constant fight or flight. One of the many functional medicine doctors I have seen said my Amygdala is twice the normal size.
-   When I am excited and stiffen up is that a huge shot of adrenaline in my system?

So, what is everyone missing? Everyone says I am a “case study”, or a “one in a million”. I have spent over $200,000 of my own money trying to figure this out. I have read that CIRS can cause these “ALS like symptoms”, but that conventional medicine doctors will not recognize CIRS. Perhaps that is what I have? Or perhaps I have a structural issue in my spine, which is one of the first areas they looked at based on my symptoms but didn’t see anything. Have they missed something?

Obviously, ALS is my biggest fear, but as mentioned earlier, my strength, length of time with symptoms and no weakness make this seem very unlikely.

Thanks for putting up with the long read!

Ryan

### Jack Kruse — 2023-12-31T04:32:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-here-is-my-story.28463/post-329670>

Welcome

1\. This does not sound like CIRP at all because you don't have many organ systems involved
2\. This isn't SPS either; the nerve signals connecting the muscles to the brain and spinal cord aren't blocked like in MS.
3\. neuro lyme can cause this, but it is an unusual way to present, and your symptoms are not reflective of it
4\. This sounds like an early ALS variant to me, but the chronicity you described here does not fit ALS at all.
5\. If you had macrocytic anemia, these symptoms make sense, but with 200K in labs, I cannot imagine they'd miss that simple diagnosis of B12 deficiency or subacute combined degeneration. Stage 1 is decreased levels of vitamin B12 in the blood. Stage 2 is low concentration of vitamin B12 in the cell and metabolic abnormalities. Stage 3 is increased levels of homocysteine and MMA and decreased DNA synthesis resulting in neuropsychiatric symptoms. Stage 4 is macrocytic anemia.
6\. autoimmune conditions like lupus, RA, PMR, POTS, and scleroderma can cause these symptoms but it would seem a 200K work up would have found all this.
7\. Functional movement disorder is also a possibility.
8\. Viral diseases (MONONUCLEOSIS or norovirus) can cause this but not over a ten year span.
9\. Topologic effect related to nnEMF

### coho3131 — 2024-01-05T17:02:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-here-is-my-story.28463/post-329863>

> [Jonesy said:](/goto/post?id=329654)
>
> **History**
>
> -   It was in 2016 when I felt numbness in my right cheek, chin and tip of my nose. This only lasted an hour or so, but it prompted me to go to emergency and get checked. They did an ECG and some other tests and everything was OK. This prompted them to refer me to a neurologist. The neurologist ordered an MRI along with some other tests and everything was normal.
> -   Around 2017 I was going through a very stressful time in my life. Some of the first symptoms I was experiencing was a heightened startle response and very poor sleep.
> -   Anxiousness was becoming a significant issue. I wouldn't call it anxiety, I just suddenly developed an anxious feeling in my body all the time. This was about 2016/2017. It is hard to explain…..I just felt anxious. This is when I was starting to really struggle with sleeping. Normally I was quite a good sleeper unless I had something to be excited about, but this was different as I wasn't sleeping well on a regular basis. At this point, the only symptoms I had was the incident with the numbness on my face, which as mentioned only lasted an hour or so, and poor sleep, anxiousness, and a very heightened startle response. After this, the symptoms you see below slowly started to creep in.
>
> **Symptoms**
>
> -   My muscles have a strange rippling in them when they are under tension or when flexed. This is something that I think offers one of the biggest clues....whenever a muscle is under tension, it ripples. If I lay back and extend my quad, you will see my muscles ripple. They did a genetic test for rippling muscle disease (CAV 3), but it was negative. Most of the physicians are not sure what to make of it and appear to shrug their shoulders.
> -   All symptoms below have been slowly progressive
> -   Significant Clonus
> -   Significant Muscle Spasticity
> -   Tone
> -   Hyper reflexes
> -   Stiff Rigid gait
> -   Decreased dexterity in my left hand was one of my very early symptoms starting about 5 years ago in 2018. It then moved to my legs and I am now seeing decreased dexterity in my right hand.
> -   I have no balance, agility, dexterity and coordination. I have always been very athletic and am now not able to run, play hockey, mountain bike, play golf, do jumping jacks, walk down stairs properly, etc. When walking in the dark my balance is even worse.
> -   Fasciculations and twitching started in 2018 before any movement issues. They started with the fingers twitching in my left hand. Today, they are significant and the fasciculations will randomly move throughout my body. Sometimes it is my right bicep for a day or so, then that goes away and it is my left triceps, then chest, abs, and even the bottom of my feet. I don't think there is a muscle in my body that hasn't twitched. They are, however, constant in my calves daily. Some of the fasciculations can be quite significant and are very visible to the eye.
> -   When I am in a heightened emotional state my rigidity and spasticity it at maximum. It can make it very hard to walk. If I see someone that I haven't seen in a while and I know they are looking at how I walk, my legs will completely tense up. When I see a doctor my legs are much worse than normal because I know they are staring at my legs and studying how I walk. Knowing this makes my walking much worse. The mind-body connection is strong. When I am extremely excited, my kneecaps will start bouncing up and down and my ankle clonus will basically start on its own. I am very sensitive to heightened emotional situations.......good or bad.
> -   Cold affects me significantly. If I walk out on a cold winter day, I completely tense up and can barely walk. I tried a cold bath recently and my entire body was shaking uncontrollably to the point where I don't think I would have been able to stand if I didn't add warm water first.
> -   I have developed a significant startle response to sudden noise when I am in a relaxed state. This is one of the early symptoms I started experiencing back in 2016/2017.
> -   I am also sensitive to touch or movement. While in bed at night, if my wife moves, it can cause my body to tense up momentarily. Even my own movement will result in my body tensing up for a few seconds before relaxing.
> -   Myoclonic Jerks - I will sometimes get a needle-like sensation in my foot which will cause a myoclonic jerk in my leg. Lately, I have been getting a myoclonic jerk in my legs when tired. Sometimes even my own thoughts can cause a myoclonic jerk. These myoclonic jerks happen while awake, not while falling asleep which is common.
> -   Speech - some days I seem to be mumbling on my words and having issues with my speech. Some days it just takes more effort to properly annunciate my words. On other days I am fine.
> -   I can't rapidly tap my hands, or fingers or shuffle my feet. I can't do anything fast.
>
> **Strength:**
>
>
> -   **And here is probably the most complex mystery to the neurologists:**
> -   At age 54, after having symptoms slowly start 6-7 years ago, I have no muscle atrophy and have very nicely shaped muscle belly’s according to the neurologist. In fact, I am able to gain strength in the gym during weight training. I am now lifting weights that I have not lifted since university in my 20's when I was hardcore into weight training. I'm as strong as I have ever been. I have actually tracked my reps and poundage over the last 3-4 years and I continue to beat previous strength records. For example, I can do over 50 diamond push-ups, incline bench press 215 lbs for 16 reps on my 4th set, shoulder press 185 lbs for 15 reps on my 4th set, bicep curl 30 lbs per arm for 30 reps on my 3rd set. I don’t say this to brag and have been holding back talking about the weights I have been lifting because I am too humble, but I feel it is a very important part of the picture and that the neurologists need to be aware that I am throwing around some serious weights for a 54 year old. I even went so far as to take a picture of myself in the mirror with my shirt off so as to show them my muscle tone. That was very awkward for me to do, but I think it opened their eyes up.
> -   I have no problem with stamina. I can ride the elliptical/stepper for 30-60 mins at a fairly strong resistance with no problem. About 1.5 years ago the neurologists ran some lung tests and my lung capacity was at 104% of normal.
> -   I actually feel great and energetic! Other than the physical symptoms listed above, I feel completely normal and am very fit!
>
> **
>
> Neurologist Visits:**
>
> -   I have seen 6 neurologists in Calgary, 1 in Edmonton and 1 in New York.
> -   The bottom line is they don't really know what I have and aren't willing to give a firm diagnosis.
> -   The neurologist said they see increasing signs of denervation on the EMG test. I then ask “denervation leads to muscle weakness and atrophy correct?” To which he answers “yes”. I then say “yet I have no muscle atrophy at all and in fact am continuing to get stronger”. His reply is “yes, that is why this is such a mystery”. I asked if there are conditions other than motor neuron disease that cause denervation. He said there are many, but these should have been revealed on the nerve conduction study test….which keeps coming back normal.
> -   Some of the potential diagnosis are: Stiff Person Syndrome, Hereditary Spastic Paraplegia, Some sort of Autoimmune process, PLS and ALS. Although they say ALS does appear to be compatible because at the very least I should be weak by now after this many years, not getting stronger.
> -   My neurologist in New York at first thought probably PLS, but now after seeing him for over a year and seeing my strength he now thinks it is some sort of virally mediated autoimmune condition. He also said not to get too hung up on the tests that are showing denervation.
> -   My main neurologist is now in the process of referring me to the Mayo Clinic. Since I am in Canada, this is quite a cumbersome process and could take some time.
>
> **Medical Tests:**
>
> -   I have had so many tests that I can’t list them all or have forgotten, but here are some:
> -   Multiple EMG tests – didn’t show denervation at first, but does now.
> -   Nerve Conduction - normal
> -   Multiple MRI’s – basically normal
> -   I also had an upright MRI in New York where they looked at my Cerebrospinal Fluid Flow and my Cranial Cervical Junction. I have reduced CSF flow, a slight cerebellum tonsil and ligament/alignment issues.
> -   CT Scan (From neck down – normal)
> -   2 Lumbar Punctures which tested for many different issues. All negative except for slightly elevated protein.
> -   PET Scan - showed some hypometabolism in bi-lateral cortices - Can be seen in ALS, but the neurologist said it is non-specific and could be from a concussion.
> -   Muscle Biopsy - showed some signs suggestive of denervation
> -   Numerous blood tests for a variety of other things such as tropical viruses etc.
> -   I have Gene C9orf72 mutation in the intermediate range. My score was 20. 2-19 = Normal, 20-30 = Intermediate, Over 30 = Pathogenic. Although some criteria suggest normal results are 2-29. My SOD 1 was negative. I had an appointment with a Geneticist and they do not suspect ALS based on this result.
> -   Genetic test for Hereditary Spastic Paraplegia – negative
> -   Isaacs Syndrome - negative
> -   Had a Whole Exome Sequencing genetic test completed with no findings. Currently waiting for results from Whole Genome Sequencing test.
> -   Stiff Person Syndrome GAD-65 came back negative. They tested this a few times.
>
> **Functional medicine tests:**
>
> -   I have a binder of tests from the numerous functional medicine doctors that I have seen, but for the sake of not making my journal longer than it already is, I will only mention a few:
> -   I have tested positive for CIRS (Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome)
> -   I have a strong positive test result for lyme disease through Armin Labs, however, the follow-up test I did a year later was barely positive. This test was run through one of my functional medicine doctors who then put me through an antibiotic protocol which didn't appear to have much impact on my symptoms. My neurologists dismissed this test because they ran their own test through Alberta Health Care and it came back negative.....apparently they miss 50% of positive tests. I have spent a significant portion of my life in the forest from a very young age, so exposure wood ticks is significant.
> -   I have tested positive for the multi-susceptible gene for CIRS and Lyme
> -   My Leptin test result was low at 2.2ng/ml
>
>
> **Medications Tried:**
>
> -   Clonazopane - very small dose - no benefit
> -   Baclofen - max dose - no benefit
> -   Levodop/Carbidop - no benefit
> -   They also tried IVIG 3 days per month for two 6 months trials. Possible very minor benefit.
> -   My family doctor decided to try Sertraline just to see if it would help. It had no effect. In fact, I felt better once I quit taking it.
>
> **Emotional:**
>
>
> -   I don't spontaneously burst out laughing or crying for no reason, but I do find I don't need much of a trigger anymore for me to tear up. The same goes for smiling or laughing, it doesn’t take much. I find I am far more emotional than I used to be. Watching TV or emotional situations can bring me to tears far easier than they used to. I also find I smile or laugh more frequently and maybe more than I should. Pseudobulbar affect or just extreme emotions? I honestly feel like my nervous system is a complete mess causing me to be overly emotional.
>
> **Stomach Issues:**
>
>
> -   I have had stomach issues for at least 10 years. I have always been bloated, even in my youth. I used to alternate between diarrhea and constipation. I finally got tired of it and went to see a functional medicine practitioner and he tested for SIBO which was positive. He put me on herbs and that has cleared up the explosive diarrhea.
> -   For many years (20 or more) I had significant heartburn and acid reflux. I took a lot of Zantac to combat this. Now, if I eat a clean diet, I have no heartburn.
> -   I do not have celiac, but do have a major gluten intolerance. If I have pastries, you can almost guarantee diarrhea for a few days. I used to react negatively to dairy and eggs also, but seem to be a little better than I used to be.
>
> **Other Symptoms:**
>
>
> -   I am highly stressed and have been since about 2017. The symptoms get worse with stress and heightened emotions.
> -   Tested positive for EBV and CMV
> -   I used to get a strange buzzing/vibration feeling in my body. Primarily through my abdomen and chest.
> -   My legs feel like they are stuck in mud, heavy and in slow motion
> -   Urinary Urgency is worse than it has ever been. Not sure if this is because of my low ADH? Although, strangely enough, I almost never get up to pee at night.
> -   Vertigo happens once in a while, but not often.
> -   Symptoms I sometimes get: Numbness in the face and heel
> -   Intermittent sore throat with redness. Intermittent raspy voice
> -   Hair Thinning - This is not expected of me as my brothers, my dad and both grandpas have full heads of hair. Just a few years ago my new barber commented how I have such thick hair, and now he comments on how it is thinning.
> -   Slight Vitiligo on my forehead. I never had this before. It becomes more obvious when I am in the sun.
> -   I have noticed an increase in Cherry Angiomas all over my body.
> -   My hands really slow down and lose dexterity when they are cold. This was one of my earlier symptoms a few years ago.
> -   My thumb will sometimes stick when in a "pinching" position, but not when grabbing a doorknob or shaking hands
> -   Typing on the keyboard is better some days than other days.
> -   I can get major cramping in my foot and toes, especially at night. My fingers will also cramp often.
> -   I feel like I am walking around drunk sometimes, but if I do have a couple of beers, my symptoms are very exacerbated.
> -   When I work out my legs get extremely shaky.
> -   During the night in bed, I am unable to roll from my side to my back without having to tense my legs when they straighten out. This wakes me at night. This started in 2028 and is extremely frustrating due to the lack of sleep it is causing. This lack of proper deep sleep is a HUGE concern for me.
> -   Lower back “goes out” about once every month or so and is extremely painful. My neck also “goes out” about every 2 months.
> -   Interestingly, I almost never get sick. I didn't have a cold or even a sniffle for 5-6 years until I finally got covid in November 2022. I can feel "run down" with extreme fatigue for a day or so, but haven't had any cold symptoms for a few years.
> -   My muscles are way more defined and have never looked this way before. Not sure why this is as it should be the opposite as you get older.
> -   If I step on something sharp I have an extreme reaction and can almost be brought to my knees.
>
> **Anxiety:**
>
>
> -   I have always been somewhat of an anxious person who worried a lot, but about 5 years ago I was more anxious than I have ever been. I had some extra stressors going on in my life at the time. That is when my sleep significantly deteriorated and my stomach issues started to get worse.
> -   My anxiety peaked during the Christmas 2020 break when I experienced, for the first time in my life, a couple of panic attacks. My legs stiffened up so much with anxiety that I couldn't walk into the back of the ER and they wheeled me around in a wheelchair. I had so much shaking that 2 separate doses of Ativan had no effect. Since that Christmas season, my anxiety had settled a fair amount.
> -   My family doctor tried Sertraline (eventually up to 200 mg per day) with no effect on my anxiety. In fact, I felt better when I finally stopped taking it.
>
> **Physical Changes:**
>
>
> -   My calves are always tight
> -   Morning stiffness
> -   Feels like I am walking in mud
> -   I can't tap, shuffle feet, dance, or do anything fast
>
> **Joint & Mobility Changes:**
>
>
> -   Very reduced joint mobility. My joints aren't really sore per se, but the range of motion is significantly reduced in my hips, wrists and ankles.
> -   For example, picking up a pill is difficult
> -   Constantly walking on tip-toes
> -   Walking downstairs is very difficult
> -   My legs often go stiff and tense causing me to fall backwards if I don't grab something.
> -   I have developed joint mobility and flexibility issues.
> -   Can't touch thumb to pinky finger
> -   If I trip or get off balance, I stiffen up and can't stick my leg out to brace my fall and I just go down. Have broken a rib because of this.
>
> **General Comments:**
>
>
> -   I feel as though I am in constant fight or flight - need to calm down my nervous system. The QEEG plus other tests basically show I have limbic system impairment and am in constant fight or flight. One of the many functional medicine doctors I have seen said my Amygdala is twice the normal size.
> -   When I am excited and stiffen up is that a huge shot of adrenaline in my system?
>
>
>
> So, what is everyone missing? Everyone says I am a “case study”, or a “one in a million”. I have spent over $200,000 of my own money trying to figure this out. I have read that CIRS can cause these “ALS like symptoms”, but that conventional medicine doctors will not recognize CIRS. Perhaps that is what I have? Or perhaps I have a structural issue in my spine, which is one of the first areas they looked at based on my symptoms but didn’t see anything. Have they missed something?
>
> Obviously, ALS is my biggest fear, but as mentioned earlier, my strength, length of time with symptoms and no weakness make this seem very unlikely.
>
> Thanks for putting up with the long read!
>
> Ryan

Welcome.

I'm sorry if I missed these details in your report.

Have you noticed any improvements after optimizing your environment with regard to light, water, and magnetism?

Have you tried any Graded Exposure Techniques for cold water or cold air?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-05T19:01:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-here-is-my-story.28463/post-329867>

Years ago my wife had a bunch of bizarre (and painful) symptoms, the Dr’s wanted to do tests that were not covered by insurance and we could not afford. I did my research thing and one option for us was aspartame poisoning (she drank a lot of diet drinks). She cut out all drinks and food containing aspartame and within 2 weeks was symptom free. I share to ask - have you looked at (measured) your environment and other exposures (other than ticks) for a possible cause? Mold, chemicals and Jacks #9 nnEMF. In home originating EMF if not the cause could exacerbate the symptoms.

### Jonesy — 2024-01-06T23:17:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-here-is-my-story.28463/post-329905>

Here is a continuation of my story with more details about the functional medicine testing I have had.

**CIRS Tests**

-   My functional medicine doctors have told me the tests they have completed show that I have mold at the cellular level. I have so many tests I can’t remember which ones specifically indicate this.
-   I failed the VCS test multiple times. Apparently if you fail there is a 98.5% chance you have CIRS
-   My nasal swab for MARCONS has tested positive multiple times
-   I have had the HLA DR genetic test and it showed that I have the “multi-susceptible” gene for mold and lyme.
-   C4a high: 11,759 ng/ml
-   C3a normal: 216
-   MSH low: 25pg/ml
-   MMP9 high: 414 ng/ml
-   TGF Beta-1 high: was 2,112 for first test then went up to 3,616
-   VEGF high at 460
-   ADH Low: <0.8 pg/ml
-   Genie Test: did not indicate CIRS
-   Neuroquant MRI – points more to mold brain than lyme
-   Leptin: 2.2 ng/ml (not really a CIRS test)

**Mold Testing in House**

-   I have had my house tested multiple times
-   Comments from Inspector:
-   ERMI - For patients with CIRS, in general, an ERMI score of 2 or less is considered safe. An ERMI score of 2.1 was determined for the swab sample collected within the Jones residence.
-   HERTSMI-2 - The calculated HERTSMI2 score for the swab sample was found to be 8. A score less than 10 is necessary for CIRS patients.
-   Actinomycetes are apparently a new and evolving discovery of a cause for CIRS. I recently had my house tested and it has some findings, but nothing alarming. However, when they did a skin swab on me they found high results. They said this could be why I did not respond to he CIRS protocol I went through, because the cause was not my house, but was on my skin! My doctor said it requires a special shampoo then repeating then Ritchie Shoemaker protocol steps for CIRS. They said there is a blood test I can take that will add more clarity regarding actinomycetes. I haven’t done this yet as money is an issue at the moment.

Is mold in my home a concern or not? Two of my functional medicine doctors say no, and one says yes. Although I sense he is overly cautious about everything.

I have already gone through the whole Shoemaker CIRS protocol with no change in symptoms. I need to decide if it is worth it to spend the money and go through it again and get rid of the actinomycetes this time.

I have also received IV’s for the Patricia Kane protocol for those that are familiar with It. It gets very expensive.

So is mold a concern or not and is that what is causing my symptoms? I’m not sure. Neil Nathan in his book Toxic states that if a neurologist says that you have atypical MS, Parkinsons ALS like symptoms it is a tip-off to look for mold toxicity, lyme or bartonella. Well, I am extremely atypical. That is what every neurologist says.

Even after all this treatment my mold levels stayed high. My functional medicine doctor says I am having trouble achieving autophagy. He said to get in touch with Dr. Courtney Hunt, which I did. Her thing is to get you into Ketosis, which then makes it easier to fast. She is also a huge promoter of sunrise, sunshine and sunsets. I got myself into ketosis and did some fasting last summer and felt really good. I went from around 183 lbs down to 169.5 lbs. I let myself slide out of keto in the fall and since then, whenever I try to get back into it, the cramping in my toes is almost unbearable…especially at night. Feet, calves, and fingers cramp also, but nothing compared to my toes.

**Lyme Tests**

My Armin Labs test in January 2020 below. I then did the same test in May 2021 and the score came back as 1SI, 2 SI and 1 SI. Go figure!

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

-   I have positive lyme tests from Viibrant Wellness
-   My Igenex lyme test came back as Indeterminate. When this happens, they recommend what is called ‘test 488’ for confirmation. I then did test 488 which came back positive.

So I did go through the whole lyme antibiotic protocol with 3-4 different antibiotics and then Rocephin via IV. None of this seemed to change my symptoms. There are some days my symptoms seemed improved, but nothing significant.

**Structural Issue?**

-   Could my issues be caused from something structural? I have had multiple MRI’s on neck and spine and the neurologists don’t see anything significant that may cause this. They see disc degeneration.
-   When I went to New York to see a neurologist last fall, he brought me to Long Island for an upright MRI.
-   Craniocervical Junction MRI:
-   Right transverse ligament sprain.
-   Right alar ligament sprain.
-   Attention to the C 1 / 2 articulation demonstrates the body of C2 to extend slightly more anteriorly on the right compared to the left (clockwise rotation), with asymmetric placement of the odontoid process relative to the lateral masses of C1 measuring approximately 1.2 mm on the left and 3.3 mm on the right. Configuration indicates a degree of C 1 / 2 rotation.
-   Slight right inferior cerebellar tonsillar displacement measuring approximately 1-2 mm associated with borderline ectopia without evidence of hydrocephalus or syringohdromyelia.
-   Cerebrospinal Fluid Flow Study
-   In both caudocranial and craniocaudal directions, decreased flow is noted anterior to the lower cervical spinal cord.
-   My New York neurologist then brought me to a chiropractor to review the findings. Even though the neurologist didn’t think these findings could cause spasticity, the chiropractor said they could. So who is correct? I have since had multiple Nucca and Atlas Orthogonal treatments on my CCJ back in Calgary with no change in symptoms.
-   I do have chronic lower back pain, as well as frequent neck pain.
-   Due to my continued strength, yet still having spasticity, I keep hoping that everyone is missing something and that this is something structural.

**Heavy Metals**

I have tested high in heavy metals and went through 2 rounds of chelation therapy. This did nothing but make me tired. The wanted me to go for and 3rd round….and then probably a 4th, but I said no.

**Cholesterol**

My cholesterol is very high and my doctor wants me on meds. I refuse. I’m not exactly sure why it is so high as I am a fit lean guy on a healthy diet.

**Vitamin B12**

-   Dr. Kruse mentioned a potential vitamin B12 deficiency in his reply to my initial post. I will need to ask my doctor if they tested for this as I can’t remember, but 2 summers ago I was taking vitamin B12 injections subcutaneously and I may have felt some improvements. This is something I would like to explore further.
-   I did test positive for SIBO back around 2017 when all these symptoms started. That combined with the stomach issues I have had makes me wonder sometimes if I have absorption issues and have a lack of B12, magnesium, etc.
-   I used to take Zantac like candy for years and have since learned that it can cause vitamin B12 malabsorption issues among other concerns. Haven’t taken it in about 5 years and no longer have acid reflux as long as I keep my diet clean.
-   Homocysteine is high at 10.4
-   MCV is high in multiple tests

EBV – this keeps popping up as positive on many of my functional medicine tests.

**Sunshine**

I wish I knew about Jack Kruse and the benefits of sunshine 6 or so years ago when my symptoms started. I would have saved some of the ridiculous amount of money I have spent and went straight to him. I would still like to set up a consult and will do this in the future. I have Jack’s Epi-Paleo RX book and read it often. I now get outside and get as much sunshine as I can. Last summer I never used sunscreen and had a fantastic tan without ever getting a burn. Interestingly enough, I had basal cell carcinoma about 7 years ago and that is when I started avoiding the sun and using loads of sunscreen. Then shortly after…..my symptoms started. Is there a connection? Possibly! If only I knew better back then. The problem is that because I live in Canada it is so cold out that you can’t get out in the winter and lay in the snow in the back yard with only your shorts on.

**Supplements**

Well this is ridiculous, but one of the functional medicine doctors had me taking around 50 supplements per day.

**Strength**

Well, I am very uncomfortable posting these pics, but I do feel as though it is part of my story that needs to be shared and can hopefully help the neurologists think outside the box. I mentioned in my opening post that I am strong as I have ever been in my life. These pics were taken this past summer about one more before I turned 54. I'm not sure if I have ever been this fit in my life. When the neurologists see these pics and I explain the weights that I am lifting, it really gives them pause. Especially since I have had these symptoms started 6-7 years ago. Now I realize there are alot of 54 years olds that are lifting much heavier weights than myself, but I use myself from 3-4 years ago as a baseline, and I continue to track what I can lift and I continue to get stronger. All the scary motor neuron and neuro muscular type diseases come with muscle weakness and muscle wasting....especially after this many years. So what are they missing?

### Jonesy — 2024-01-06T23:25:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-here-is-my-story.28463/post-329907>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=329670)
>
> Welcome
>
> 1\. This does not sound like CIRP at all because you don't have many organ systems involved
> 2\. This isn't SPS either; the nerve signals connecting the muscles to the brain and spinal cord aren't blocked like in MS.
> 3\. neuro lyme can cause this, but it is an unusual way to present, and your symptoms are not reflective of it
> 4\. This sounds like an early ALS variant to me, but the chronicity you described here does not fit ALS at all.
> 5\. If you had macrocytic anemia, these symptoms make sense, but with 200K in labs, I cannot imagine they'd miss that simple diagnosis of B12 deficiency or subacute combined degeneration. Stage 1 is decreased levels of vitamin B12 in the blood. Stage 2 is low concentration of vitamin B12 in the cell and metabolic abnormalities. Stage 3 is increased levels of homocysteine and MMA and decreased DNA synthesis resulting in neuropsychiatric symptoms. Stage 4 is macrocytic anemia.
> 6\. autoimmune conditions like lupus, RA, PMR, POTS, and scleroderma can cause these symptoms but it would seem a 200K work up would have found all this.
> 7\. Functional movement disorder is also a possibility.
> 8\. Viral diseases (MONONUCLEOSIS or norovirus) can cause this but not over a ten year span.
> 9\. Topologic effect related to nnEMF

Hi Dr. Kruse,

Thanks very much for your reply and for some possible explanations to my story! I have added another post below which adds some insight into my story.

I have read your book Epi-Paleo RX and will continue to devour the loads of information on this website, as well as follow you on the various social media platforms to learn as much as possible.

Ryan

> [coho3131 said:](/goto/post?id=329863)

> Welcome.
>
> I'm sorry if I missed these details in your report.
>
> Have you noticed any improvements after optimizing your environment with regard to light, water, and magnetism?
>
> Have you tried any Graded Exposure Techniques for cold water or cold air?

Hi there,

I am starting to wear blue light blocking glasses at night.

I do have cold baths once in a while, but need to increase my frequency.

I haven't noticed any change in symptoms, but do notice a positive change in mood after.

In regards to magnetism, I really don't know anything about it. I have heard it mentioned, but need to do some research and understand what it is all about. Feel free to post any links that may be a good starting point for someone just learning.

### Jonesy — 2024-01-06T23:36:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-here-is-my-story.28463/post-329909>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=329867)
>
> Years ago my wife had a bunch of bizarre (and painful) symptoms, the Dr’s wanted to do tests that were not covered by insurance and we could not afford. I did my research thing and one option for us was aspartame poisoning (she drank a lot of diet drinks). She cut out all drinks and food containing aspartame and within 2 weeks was symptom free. I share to ask - have you looked at (measured) your environment and other exposures (other than ticks) for a possible cause? Mold, chemicals and Jacks #9 nnEMF. In home originating EMF if not the cause could exacerbate the symptoms.

I just added another post after my initial post. It will explain a little more regarding what I have gone through.

I used to chew alot of gum, which of course has aspartame, but I quit chewing it a few years ago for that very reason. Zantac is something I regret taking. I had taken it for years before I found out how bad it is for you.

In regards to nnEMF, this is an area I really need to look into and see what I can mitigate.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-01-07T14:50:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-here-is-my-story.28463/post-329919>

The gum-chewing thing is interesting. Aspartame destroys endogenous melanin and could explain why you are sensitive to mold. People who cannot create endogenous melanin are the ones who get the symptoms. They usually get the wrong advice from the mold mitigators. It is a lack of alpha MSH disease.
