### nichole — 2024-03-21T04:01:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-here-is-my-story.28463/post-332716>

> [Jonesy said:](/goto/post?id=329654)
>
> **History**
>
> -   It was in 2016 when I felt numbness in my right cheek, chin and tip of my nose. This only lasted an hour or so, but it prompted me to go to emergency and get checked. They did an ECG and some other tests and everything was OK. This prompted them to refer me to a neurologist. The neurologist ordered an MRI along with some other tests and everything was normal.
> -   Around 2017 I was going through a very stressful time in my life. Some of the first symptoms I was experiencing was a heightened startle response and very poor sleep.
> -   Anxiousness was becoming a significant issue. I wouldn't call it anxiety, I just suddenly developed an anxious feeling in my body all the time. This was about 2016/2017. It is hard to explain…..I just felt anxious. This is when I was starting to really struggle with sleeping. Normally I was quite a good sleeper unless I had something to be excited about, but this was different as I wasn't sleeping well on a regular basis. At this point, the only symptoms I had was the incident with the numbness on my face, which as mentioned only lasted an hour or so, and poor sleep, anxiousness, and a very heightened startle response. After this, the symptoms you see below slowly started to creep in.
>
> **Symptoms**
>
> -   My muscles have a strange rippling in them when they are under tension or when flexed. This is something that I think offers one of the biggest clues....whenever a muscle is under tension, it ripples. If I lay back and extend my quad, you will see my muscles ripple. They did a genetic test for rippling muscle disease (CAV 3), but it was negative. Most of the physicians are not sure what to make of it and appear to shrug their shoulders.
> -   All symptoms below have been slowly progressive
> -   Significant Clonus
> -   Significant Muscle Spasticity
> -   Tone
> -   Hyper reflexes
> -   Stiff Rigid gait
> -   Decreased dexterity in my left hand was one of my very early symptoms starting about 5 years ago in 2018. It then moved to my legs and I am now seeing decreased dexterity in my right hand.
> -   I have no balance, agility, dexterity and coordination. I have always been very athletic and am now not able to run, play hockey, mountain bike, play golf, do jumping jacks, walk down stairs properly, etc. When walking in the dark my balance is even worse.
> -   Fasciculations and twitching started in 2018 before any movement issues. They started with the fingers twitching in my left hand. Today, they are significant and the fasciculations will randomly move throughout my body. Sometimes it is my right bicep for a day or so, then that goes away and it is my left triceps, then chest, abs, and even the bottom of my feet. I don't think there is a muscle in my body that hasn't twitched. They are, however, constant in my calves daily. Some of the fasciculations can be quite significant and are very visible to the eye.
> -   When I am in a heightened emotional state my rigidity and spasticity it at maximum. It can make it very hard to walk. If I see someone that I haven't seen in a while and I know they are looking at how I walk, my legs will completely tense up. When I see a doctor my legs are much worse than normal because I know they are staring at my legs and studying how I walk. Knowing this makes my walking much worse. The mind-body connection is strong. When I am extremely excited, my kneecaps will start bouncing up and down and my ankle clonus will basically start on its own. I am very sensitive to heightened emotional situations.......good or bad.
> -   Cold affects me significantly. If I walk out on a cold winter day, I completely tense up and can barely walk. I tried a cold bath recently and my entire body was shaking uncontrollably to the point where I don't think I would have been able to stand if I didn't add warm water first.
> -   I have developed a significant startle response to sudden noise when I am in a relaxed state. This is one of the early symptoms I started experiencing back in 2016/2017.
> -   I am also sensitive to touch or movement. While in bed at night, if my wife moves, it can cause my body to tense up momentarily. Even my own movement will result in my body tensing up for a few seconds before relaxing.
> -   Myoclonic Jerks - I will sometimes get a needle-like sensation in my foot which will cause a myoclonic jerk in my leg. Lately, I have been getting a myoclonic jerk in my legs when tired. Sometimes even my own thoughts can cause a myoclonic jerk. These myoclonic jerks happen while awake, not while falling asleep which is common.
> -   Speech - some days I seem to be mumbling on my words and having issues with my speech. Some days it just takes more effort to properly annunciate my words. On other days I am fine.
> -   I can't rapidly tap my hands, or fingers or shuffle my feet. I can't do anything fast.
>
> **Strength:**
>
>
> -   **And here is probably the most complex mystery to the neurologists:**
> -   At age 54, after having symptoms slowly start 6-7 years ago, I have no muscle atrophy and have very nicely shaped muscle belly’s according to the neurologist. In fact, I am able to gain strength in the gym during weight training. I am now lifting weights that I have not lifted since university in my 20's when I was hardcore into weight training. I'm as strong as I have ever been. I have actually tracked my reps and poundage over the last 3-4 years and I continue to beat previous strength records. For example, I can do over 50 diamond push-ups, incline bench press 215 lbs for 16 reps on my 4th set, shoulder press 185 lbs for 15 reps on my 4th set, bicep curl 30 lbs per arm for 30 reps on my 3rd set. I don’t say this to brag and have been holding back talking about the weights I have been lifting because I am too humble, but I feel it is a very important part of the picture and that the neurologists need to be aware that I am throwing around some serious weights for a 54 year old. I even went so far as to take a picture of myself in the mirror with my shirt off so as to show them my muscle tone. That was very awkward for me to do, but I think it opened their eyes up.
> -   I have no problem with stamina. I can ride the elliptical/stepper for 30-60 mins at a fairly strong resistance with no problem. About 1.5 years ago the neurologists ran some lung tests and my lung capacity was at 104% of normal.
> -   I actually feel great and energetic! Other than the physical symptoms listed above, I feel completely normal and am very fit!
>
> **
>
> Neurologist Visits:**
>
> -   I have seen 6 neurologists in Calgary, 1 in Edmonton and 1 in New York.
> -   The bottom line is they don't really know what I have and aren't willing to give a firm diagnosis.
> -   The neurologist said they see increasing signs of denervation on the EMG test. I then ask “denervation leads to muscle weakness and atrophy correct?” To which he answers “yes”. I then say “yet I have no muscle atrophy at all and in fact am continuing to get stronger”. His reply is “yes, that is why this is such a mystery”. I asked if there are conditions other than motor neuron disease that cause denervation. He said there are many, but these should have been revealed on the nerve conduction study test….which keeps coming back normal.
> -   Some of the potential diagnosis are: Stiff Person Syndrome, Hereditary Spastic Paraplegia, Some sort of Autoimmune process, PLS and ALS. Although, they say ALS does NOT appear to be compatible because at the very least I should be weak by now after this many years, but the opposite is happening and I am getting stronger.
> -   My neurologist in New York at first thought probably PLS, but now after seeing him for over a year and seeing my strength he now thinks it is some sort of virally mediated autoimmune condition. He also said not to get too hung up on the tests that are showing denervation.
> -   My main neurologist is now in the process of referring me to the Mayo Clinic. Since I am in Canada, this is quite a cumbersome process and could take some time.
>
> **Medical Tests:**
>
> -   I have had so many tests that I can’t list them all or have forgotten, but here are some:
> -   Multiple EMG tests – didn’t show denervation at first, but does now.
> -   Nerve Conduction - normal
> -   Multiple MRI’s – basically normal
> -   I also had an upright MRI in New York where they looked at my Cerebrospinal Fluid Flow and my Cranial Cervical Junction. I have reduced CSF flow, a slight cerebellum tonsil and ligament/alignment issues.
> -   CT Scan (From neck down – normal)
> -   2 Lumbar Punctures which tested for many different issues. All negative except for slightly elevated protein.
> -   PET Scan - showed some hypometabolism in bi-lateral cortices - Can be seen in ALS, but the neurologist said it is non-specific and could be from a concussion.
> -   Muscle Biopsy - showed some signs suggestive of denervation
> -   Numerous blood tests for a variety of other things such as tropical viruses etc.
> -   I have Gene C9orf72 mutation in the intermediate range. My score was 20. 2-19 = Normal, 20-30 = Intermediate, Over 30 = Pathogenic. Although some criteria suggest normal results are 2-29. My SOD 1 was negative. I had an appointment with a Geneticist and they do not suspect ALS based on this result.
> -   Genetic test for Hereditary Spastic Paraplegia – negative
> -   Isaacs Syndrome - negative
> -   Had a Whole Exome Sequencing genetic test completed with no findings. Currently waiting for results from Whole Genome Sequencing test.
> -   Stiff Person Syndrome GAD-65 came back negative. They tested this a few times.
>
> **Functional medicine tests:**
>
> -   I have a binder of tests from the numerous functional medicine doctors that I have seen, but for the sake of not making my journal longer than it already is, I will only mention a few:
> -   I have tested positive for CIRS (Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome)
> -   I have a strong positive test result for lyme disease through Armin Labs, however, the follow-up test I did a year later was barely positive. This test was run through one of my functional medicine doctors who then put me through an antibiotic protocol which didn't appear to have much impact on my symptoms. My neurologists dismissed this test because they ran their own test through Alberta Health Care and it came back negative.....apparently they miss 50% of positive tests. I have spent a significant portion of my life in the forest from a very young age, so exposure wood ticks is significant.
> -   I have tested positive for the multi-susceptible gene for CIRS and Lyme
> -   My Leptin test result was low at 2.2ng/ml
>
>
> **Medications Tried:**
>
> -   Clonazopane - very small dose - no benefit
> -   Baclofen - max dose - no benefit
> -   Levodop/Carbidop - no benefit
> -   They also tried IVIG 3 days per month for two 6 months trials. Possible very minor benefit.
> -   My family doctor decided to try Sertraline just to see if it would help. It had no effect. In fact, I felt better once I quit taking it.
>
> **Emotional:**
>
>
> -   I don't spontaneously burst out laughing or crying for no reason, but I do find I don't need much of a trigger anymore for me to tear up. The same goes for smiling or laughing, it doesn’t take much. I find I am far more emotional than I used to be. Watching TV or emotional situations can bring me to tears far easier than they used to. I also find I smile or laugh more frequently and maybe more than I should. Pseudobulbar affect or just extreme emotions? I honestly feel like my nervous system is a complete mess causing me to be overly emotional.
>
> **Stomach Issues:**
>
>
> -   I have had stomach issues for at least 10 years. I have always been bloated, even in my youth. I used to alternate between diarrhea and constipation. I finally got tired of it and went to see a functional medicine practitioner and he tested for SIBO which was positive. He put me on herbs and that has cleared up the explosive diarrhea.
> -   For many years (20 or more) I had significant heartburn and acid reflux. I took a lot of Zantac to combat this. Now, if I eat a clean diet, I have no heartburn.
> -   I do not have celiac, but do have a major gluten intolerance. If I have pastries, you can almost guarantee diarrhea for a few days. I used to react negatively to dairy and eggs also, but seem to be a little better than I used to be.
>
> **Other Symptoms:**
>
>
> -   I am highly stressed and have been since about 2017. The symptoms get worse with stress and heightened emotions.
> -   Tested positive for EBV and CMV
> -   I used to get a strange buzzing/vibration feeling in my body. Primarily through my abdomen and chest.
> -   My legs feel like they are stuck in mud, heavy and in slow motion
> -   Urinary Urgency is worse than it has ever been. Not sure if this is because of my low ADH? Although, strangely enough, I almost never get up to pee at night.
> -   Vertigo happens once in a while, but not often.
> -   Symptoms I sometimes get: Numbness in the face and heel
> -   Intermittent sore throat with redness. Intermittent raspy voice
> -   Hair Thinning - This is not expected of me as my brothers, my dad and both grandpas have full heads of hair. Just a few years ago my new barber commented how I have such thick hair, and now he comments on how it is thinning.
> -   Slight Vitiligo on my forehead. I never had this before. It becomes more obvious when I am in the sun.
> -   I have noticed an increase in Cherry Angiomas all over my body.
> -   My hands really slow down and lose dexterity when they are cold. This was one of my earlier symptoms a few years ago.
> -   My thumb will sometimes stick when in a "pinching" position, but not when grabbing a doorknob or shaking hands
> -   Typing on the keyboard is better some days than other days.
> -   I can get major cramping in my foot and toes, especially at night. My fingers will also cramp often.
> -   I feel like I am walking around drunk sometimes, but if I do have a couple of beers, my symptoms are very exacerbated.
> -   When I work out my legs get extremely shaky.
> -   During the night in bed, I am unable to roll from my side to my back without having to tense my legs when they straighten out. This wakes me at night. This started in 2028 and is extremely frustrating due to the lack of sleep it is causing. This lack of proper deep sleep is a HUGE concern for me.
> -   Lower back “goes out” about once every month or so and is extremely painful. My neck also “goes out” about every 2 months.
> -   Interestingly, I almost never get sick. I didn't have a cold or even a sniffle for 5-6 years until I finally got covid in November 2022. I can feel "run down" with extreme fatigue for a day or so, but haven't had any cold symptoms for a few years.
> -   My muscles are way more defined and have never looked this way before. Not sure why this is as it should be the opposite as you get older.
> -   If I step on something sharp I have an extreme reaction and can almost be brought to my knees.
>
> **Anxiety:**
>
>
> -   I have always been somewhat of an anxious person who worried a lot, but about 5 years ago I was more anxious than I have ever been. I had some extra stressors going on in my life at the time. That is when my sleep significantly deteriorated and my stomach issues started to get worse.
> -   My anxiety peaked during the Christmas 2020 break when I experienced, for the first time in my life, a couple of panic attacks. My legs stiffened up so much with anxiety that I couldn't walk into the back of the ER and they wheeled me around in a wheelchair. I had so much shaking that 2 separate doses of Ativan had no effect. Since that Christmas season, my anxiety had settled a fair amount.
> -   My family doctor tried Sertraline (eventually up to 200 mg per day) with no effect on my anxiety. In fact, I felt better when I finally stopped taking it.
>
> **Physical Changes:**
>
>
> -   My calves are always tight
> -   Morning stiffness
> -   Feels like I am walking in mud
> -   I can't tap, shuffle feet, dance, or do anything fast
>
> **Joint & Mobility Changes:**
>
>
> -   Very reduced joint mobility. My joints aren't really sore per se, but the range of motion is significantly reduced in my hips, wrists and ankles.
> -   For example, picking up a pill is difficult
> -   Constantly walking on tip-toes
> -   Walking downstairs is very difficult
> -   My legs often go stiff and tense causing me to fall backwards if I don't grab something.
> -   I have developed joint mobility and flexibility issues.
> -   Can't touch thumb to pinky finger
> -   If I trip or get off balance, I stiffen up and can't stick my leg out to brace my fall and I just go down. Have broken a rib because of this.
>
> **General Comments:**
>
>
> -   I feel as though I am in constant fight or flight - need to calm down my nervous system. The QEEG plus other tests basically show I have limbic system impairment and am in constant fight or flight. One of the many functional medicine doctors I have seen said my Amygdala is twice the normal size.
> -   When I am excited and stiffen up is that a huge shot of adrenaline in my system?
>
>
>
> So, what is everyone missing? Everyone says I am a “case study”, or a “one in a million”. I have spent over $200,000 of my own money trying to figure this out. I have read that CIRS can cause these “ALS like symptoms”, but that conventional medicine doctors will not recognize CIRS. Perhaps that is what I have? Or perhaps I have a structural issue in my spine, which is one of the first areas they looked at based on my symptoms but didn’t see anything. Have they missed something?
>
> Obviously, ALS is my biggest fear, but as mentioned earlier, my strength, length of time with symptoms and no weakness make this seem very unlikely.
>
> Thanks for putting up with the long read!
>
> Ryan

im so sorry your going thru this. i have many of your symptoms. i was diagnosed with lupus, cirs and dysautonomia. i was sick and just surviving for 18 years and bedridden for 5 of that. finally i started improving the last 2 years. i was finally able to get my first full time job last year. your body can definitely heal, you just have to do what it needs. ive incorporated much of what dr kruses protocol is but also im working with mary ruddick. she's an ancestral nutritionist and absolutely amazing. so knowledgeable. she gets all of the really sick people who have searched the globe for the right doctor but No doctor knows hows to fix them. and then she swoops in and peoples chronic conditions start reversing. i love her. u can check out her you tube interviews. see what you think. ive been working with her for almost a year now. and ill never leave her. she's spot on with her advice. i do love jack kruse also. im going to join his membership program so i learn more and reverse everything. You got this! your body can absolutely heal.

# Thread: Apteryx - a journal from two-thousand and twenty-four. Let's go
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apteryx-a-journal-from-two-thousand-and-twenty-four-lets-go.28464/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Apteryx — 2024-01-01T11:06:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apteryx-a-journal-from-two-thousand-and-twenty-four-lets-go.28464/post-329693>

The first day of the year, and where I'm located (on the hemisphere containing Antarctica) the days arrive earlier than almost anywhere else.
So what did I do today:
Grounding in the morning. Tall grass - hands and feet right in it, the sun's rising and shining.
I climb up a lemonwood tree and then an oak tree - the largest oak here is not all that large, fifteen to twenty metres tall. Still quite young, really, under thirty years old.
Lots of red ... far red ... near-infrared ...
I eat a few things from the garden and ponder the last few months that have passed since I commenced my first regular outdoors employment - taking care of a forest-garden. Reflecting with gratitude. Tummy is feeling settled.

Many things to do. One step at a time, because I lack the wings. If you don't count those of a soaring mind of course. Melanin in the feathers? The light is delightful.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: The BioCharger™ NG  - Any thoughts or experience?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biocharger%E2%84%A2-ng-any-thoughts-or-experience.28466/>

### Sara Pugh — 2024-01-01T22:25:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biocharger%E2%84%A2-ng-any-thoughts-or-experience.28466/post-329717>

Dear all
I am not a big fan of ;large plug in the wall devices and prefer the natural way if all possible with quantum biology. but I am a light and bulb person if I must use electricity- but I am not closed to the idea of machines. Not PEMF though

I was asked about this device recently BioCharger [https://biocharger.com/](https://biocharger.com/?fbclid=IwAR0F9GgE-Zxs4IWbj0Xh6XVeoK1w8gygovF6t4fvNs3z4zfdNDSYmuTd56g). “The BioCharger™ NG produces a customizable sweep of digitally pulsed electromagnetic fields that bathes the whole body in a rich field of bio-compatible frequencies, generating bio-photon light via plasma gas spectrum tubes.” This is from one of my clients and she said Tony Robbins has it at his events , this put me off a bit- Has any one tried this or have any thoughts on it ? I have looked at the spec and some of the actual physics is beyond me at the moment. Im skeptical as it is PEMF, has a Wifi element at the Hz at which it operates is not explained in a way I am convinced. I have some understanding of how toroid waves are key to biology but not sure if I am convinced about this machine. So if its BS Im happy to put the word out

Sara

### Brent Patrick — 2024-01-02T03:23:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-biocharger%E2%84%A2-ng-any-thoughts-or-experience.28466/post-329723>

This device coped alot of heat (no pun intended) several years ago here in OZ our resident alternative wellness "expert" Peter Evans was promoting it to cure COVID and many other ills but had no proof of work to back up his claims.

I cannot comment on the device in of itself!

### [TGA fines Pete Evans more than $25,000 after coronavirus claims](https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-04-24/pete-evans-fined-25000-by-tga-over-coronavirus-biocharger-claims/12183050)

Celebrity chef Pete Evans is issued two infringement notices by the Therapeutic Goods Administration over claims he made about the BioCharger "hybrid subtle energy revitalisation platform".

 www.abc.net.au

As you already know Sara nothing beats the Sun for all its transitional qualities

In review of the BioCharger device again, I looked at it many years ago -> here's a link to its specs -> [https://biocharger.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Product-Spec-1.pdf](https://biocharger.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/Product-Spec-1.pdf)

Please notice that they do not inform you on the polarity of the electrical charged ion emitted from the device.

What we know from nature is that air can conduct charge. Depending on the polarity of that charge will determine what biological influence it will have onto and into your body.

For example: In Boulder Colorado (and surrounding areas) where the dry air blows above 60 miles an hour, a greater positively charge builds up in the air. Generally speaking, its not a good thing. Boulder Colorado like many places like it are high altitude deserts.

However, in a tropical environment where the moist air blows regularly as in El Salvador, a greater negative charge builds up in the air. Generally speaking this is a good thing for all living things like plants and humans.

May I recommend you study what this "polarity" means and does to your human cells.

I have written up many papers on the subject. Here's one for your reference -> "Melanosomes - Our Photo-Electro-Magnetic Power Plants" [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/page-10#post-329692](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/page-10#post-329692)

Grandpa John

# Thread: What drives stability thermodynamically?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-drives-stability-thermodynamically.28471/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-01-03T23:50:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-drives-stability-thermodynamically.28471/post-329778>

To better understand the concepts of entropy and enthalpy in this application, think of a 10-year-old trying to construct a doghouse out of a giant pile of Legos. Even with limited types of building blocks, there would be many possible design outcomes.

Enthalpy is a measure of how sturdy each design is, and entropy is a measure of the number of possible designs with similar strengths.

The first promotes ordered configurations, like those that might be found in instruction booklets. The latter captures the unavoidable chaos that would occur as the child puts more time and energy into the increasingly confusing construction effort. Both measure the amount of energy and heat that end up being absorbed into the final product.

Stefano Curtarolo, Disordered enthalpy–entropy descriptor for high-entropy ceramics discovery, _Nature_ (2024). [DOI: 10.1038/s41586-023-06786-y](https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/s41586-023-06786-y). [www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06786-y](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06786-y)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-01-04T04:36:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-drives-stability-thermodynamically.28471/post-329782>

## THINGS FOOD GURUS STILL ARE INGORANT ON?​

## ​

## **mtFAS is an essential pathway in ALL mammalian cells because it determines the stability of the inner mitochondrial membrane. The enthalpy is controlled by mitochondrial redox power.**​

-   Sackmann U.
-   Zensen R.
-   Röhlen D.
-   Jahnke U.
-   Weiss H.

The acyl-carrier protein in Neurospora crassa mitochondria is a subunit of NADH:ubiquinone reductase (complex I).
_Eur. J. Biochem._ 1991; 200: 463-469

### Jack Kruse — 2024-01-04T04:37:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-drives-stability-thermodynamically.28471/post-329783>

We now have proof that mitochondrial lipid by products of metabolism cause genetic variation. This is not what Medel or Darwin told us.

Mitochondria are often thought of as predominantly catabolic organelles, breaking down carbon sources for use as fuel through fatty acid oxidation (FAO) and the citric acid (TCA) cycle. Ultimately, these catabolic pathways produce reduced co-factors (NADH and FADH2) that feed electrons to the mitochondrial electron transport chain (ETC), where they drive cellular respiration and ATP production. Alongside these famous energy-producing metabolic processes, mitochondria also harbor a plethora of anabolic biochemical pathways (or steps within larger pathways), including those that build non-essential amino acids, sphingolipids, cholesterol, and steroid hormones. However, one of the most overlooked biosynthetic pathways in mammalian mitochondria is the mitochondrial fatty acid synthesis (mtFAS) pathway. I wonder why that is?

I bet the food gurus won't lke the reason why. Mitochondria not foods cause genetic alterations. It turn out defective mitochondrial respiration is can be entirely driven by ETC assembly defects

The mtFAS is part of the optical signaling pathway that drives mTOR biology. Mitochondrial fatty acid synthesis is an essential pathway that **does not contribute** to synthesis of cellular lipids. It is a molecular photoswitch created by mitochondrial respiration that has a frequenvy map associated with it bio photon production. What do these biomolecules do? They tell muscle and bone how to act based on the light information they contain. The enzymes of mtFAS are ubiquitously expressed in mammalian tissues, with the highest expression in skeletal muscle and heart (Triepels et al., 1999).

The mtFAS pathway is required for production of the eight-carbon lipid lipoic acid (LA),
LA is a required co-factor for several mitochondrial metabolic enzymes involved in the TCA cycle, amino acid catabolism, and purine biosynthesis: pyruvate dehydrogenase (PDH), ⍺-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase (OGDH), branched-chain ketoacid dehydrogenase (BCKDH), 2-oxoadipate dehydrogenase (OADH), and the H protein of the glycine cleavage system (GCSH). mtFAS takes place in the mitochondrial matrix and builds fatty acyl chains on a soluble scaffold protein called the mitochondrial acyl carrier protein (ACP) and is highly conserved throughout the eukaryotic kingdom.

New research interest in mtFAS has resurged with two major events: the finding that other lipid products of mtFAS have important cellular functions in ETC assembly and iron-sulfur (FeS) cluster biogenesis and the discovery of the first humans with disease-causing variants in mtFAS pathway genes.

LA deficiency has also been shown in a number of patients with defects in the FeS cluster biosynthesis pathway, as FeS clusters are required for the stability and catalytic activity of the enzyme LIAS. Impairments in the LA synthesis pathway often present with severe neurological symptoms, including seizures, hypotonia, feeding difficulties, and developmental delays.

It was not until 2016 that Heimer et al. described the first human patients with disease-causing variants in the mtFAS pathway itself (_MECR_)

### JanSz — 2024-01-04T18:03:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-drives-stability-thermodynamically.28471/post-329813>

**Lipoic acid (LA), also known as α-lipoic acid, alpha-lipoic acid (ALA)**, and thioctic acid is an organosulfur compound derived from octanoic acid.

**Omega-3**
\===================
Jack Kruse said:

We now have proof that mitochondrial lipid by products of metabolism **cause genetic variation.** This is not what Medel or Darwin told us.

Mitochondria are often thought of as predominantly catabolic organelles, breaking down carbon sources for use as fuel through fatty acid oxidation (FAO) and the citric acid (TCA) cycle. Ultimately, these catabolic pathways produce reduced co-factors (NADH and FADH2) that feed electrons to the mitochondrial electron transport chain (ETC), where they drive cellular respiration and ATP production. Alongside these famous energy-producing metabolic processes, mitochondria also harbor a plethora of anabolic biochemical pathways (or steps within larger pathways), including those that build non-essential amino acids, sphingolipids, cholesterol, and steroid hormones. However, one of the most overlooked biosynthetic pathways in mammalian mitochondria is the mitochondrial fatty acid synthesis (mtFAS) pathway. I wonder why that is?

I bet the food gurus won't lke the reason why. Mitochondria not foods cause genetic alterations. It turn out defective mitochondrial respiration is can be entirely driven by ETC assembly defects

The mtFAS is part of the optical signaling pathway that drives mTOR biology. Mitochondrial fatty acid synthesis is an essential pathway that **does not contribute** to synthesis of cellular lipids. It is a molecular photoswitch created by mitochondrial respiration that has a frequenvy map associated with it bio photon production. What do these biomolecules do? They tell muscle and bone how to act based on the light information they contain. The enzymes of mtFAS are ubiquitously expressed in mammalian tissues, with the highest expression in skeletal muscle and heart (Triepels et al., 1999).

**The mtFAS pathway is required for production of the eight-carbon lipid lipoic acid (LA),
LA** is a required co-factor for several mitochondrial metabolic enzymes involved in the TCA cycle, amino acid catabolism, and purine biosynthesis: pyruvate dehydrogenase (PDH), ⍺-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase (OGDH), branched-chain ketoacid dehydrogenase (BCKDH), 2-oxoadipate dehydrogenase (OADH), and the H protein of the glycine cleavage system (GCSH). mtFAS takes place in the mitochondrial matrix and builds fatty acyl chains on a soluble scaffold protein called the mitochondrial acyl carrier protein (ACP) and is highly conserved throughout the eukaryotic kingdom.

New research interest in mtFAS has resurged with two major events: the finding that other lipid products of mtFAS have important cellular functions in ETC assembly and iron-sulfur (FeS) cluster biogenesis and the discovery of the first humans with disease-causing variants in mtFAS pathway genes.

**LA deficiency** has also been shown in a number of patients with defects in the FeS cluster biosynthesis pathway, as FeS clusters are required for the stability and catalytic activity of the enzyme LIAS. Impairments in the **LA** synthesis pathway often present with severe neurological symptoms, including seizures, hypotonia, feeding difficulties, and developmental delays.

It was not until 2016 that Heimer et al. described the first human patients with disease-causing variants in the mtFAS pathway itself (_MECR_)

I'm wondering- for those of you whose health has suffered due to a toxic work or home environment (or both) as well as getting away from the poor environment- has anybody tried sleeping outdoors for any length of time to see how it affects their health? I ask because I have seen anecdotal accounts of people going extended camping or creating a sleeping space in a rural/semi-rural garden and reporting benefits, even if they do not understand why.

Curious if anyone has tried this??

### Chlo_e — 2024-01-04T19:09:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-outdoors.28473/post-329817>

> [markthesunbather said:](/goto/post?id=329812)
>
> Glad to see this thread. I'd been wondering about this ever since 2007 when a guy I met opted to sleep on the back porch of a place I was living at. He claimed he did so for health reasons; never had the chance to ask him more about it but it stuck with me.

I got recommended some books a couple years ago- a trilogy by a British author called Raynor Winn. In short, her husband is diagnosed with corticobasal degeneration (similar pathology to Parkinson's) at the same time they get evicted from their house due to a good-faith financial investment gone wrong. Anyway they decide to walk the South West Coast path for 4 months even though he is sick. He gets better whilst doing it. Then he deteriorates when he returns to 'normal' living and they go to Scotland and spend about 5-6 months hiking and camping until they eventually return to Cornwall in the south. His DAT scan improves and he's still alive and hiking even though this was about 7-8 years ago. Since then I've been trying to find similar studies and stories.

It's interesting that your neighbour was already doing this- I've noticed others reporting the same. Although it's not easy to find a lot of stories because most people don't want to sleep outdoors for more than a few nights if at all!

### Evee — 2024-01-05T00:09:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-outdoors.28473/post-329832>

> Hi all! Happy New Year!

> I'm wondering- for those of you whose health has suffered due to a toxic work or home environment (or both) as well as getting away from the poor environment- has anybody tried sleeping outdoors for any length of time to see how it affects their health? Either camping or setting up a sleeping space in their garden?
> I ask because I have seen anecdotal accounts of people going extended camping or creating a sleeping space in a rural/semi-rural garden and reporting benefits, even if they do not understand why.
>
> Curious if anyone has tried this??

Just a fun anecdote about sleeping outside: I slept in my yard in NJ one night this summer. It's not perfect environment by any means (have measured with a Trifled) but it's also far from worst, have seen much, much worse. Anyway, I was sleeping under a pyramid shaped outdoor furniture thing and I dreamed about 2 things that happened the next day. Nothing significant, but just made me feel minorly psychic. Maybe coincidence, maybe being more connected to nature outside, maybe the pyramid, maybe a combo? I don't know but it was interesting so I thought I share and ask if anyone feels more psychically attuned (sleeping or awake) outside...?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-05T16:46:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-outdoors.28473/post-329858>

Earths magnetic field extends way out into space and is DC. The primary (stronger by proximity) magnetic fields in buildings with AC electricity are AC. Physical contact with the ground outside, away from nnEMF (man made AC electric fields) allows for DC grounding. Our bodies are electrically and magnetically DC, I believe this is the #1 reason why sleeping outside is healthy, being away from 60Hz (50Hz) AC.

Also wondering how standard synthetic camping tents would fair, if this would diminish the benefits of sleeping outdoors vs natural materials to sleep on?

I noticed how well I slept and repeated it many times with same result. I believe the magnetic field drops off real fast as we move away from the source, so not only do we want to avoid NN emf, we want to bask in Native emf, especially at night. I concluded this reading Becker but I could be wrong.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-05T19:20:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-outdoors.28473/post-329869>

> [Chlo\_e said:](/goto/post?id=329864)
>
> Do you know if being indoors removes our DC connection? Or is it the presence of AC and nnEMF? Or a bit of both?
>
> Also wondering how standard synthetic camping tents would fair, if this would diminish the benefits of sleeping outdoors vs natural materials to sleep on?

A bit of both, but the DC magnetic and DC electric connections are slightly different. I would say the DC magnetic is masked by the AC magnetic but to have the benefit of DC electric we need physical ground contact outside away from AC electric fields. A rubber based or other non conductive material (like synthetic shoes when walking) when camping would reduce the potential advantages of being outside on the ground. Our ancestors would sleep on natural materials (leafs, moss, animal skins etc) on the ground.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-07T21:58:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-outdoors.28473/post-329947>

> [Chlo\_e said:](/goto/post?id=329931)
>
> Thanks for sharing.
>
> I wonder whether the Faraday tents would be a useful tool when camping to mitigate some of these risks, or whether they would just act as antenna, any thoughts?

In the video the main problem was not RF or electric fields it was magnetic. A Faraday tent would do nothing for the magnetic fields IN the ground. The whole point of camping and grounding in nature is getting away from nnEMF and connecting WITH nature. So either find a nnEMF clean piece of dirt to lie on Or just stay home.

That said . . . IF you have no choice where you camp and are in a RV park situation with power and WiFi provided or the people around you with satellite dishes and WiFi etc. then, just like at home with neighbors, some RF shielding material might be a good idea. RF canopies are great for RF but if there is a 60Hz AC electric field present then they can exacerbate your electric field exposure. Measure and make informed decisions.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-09T19:53:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-outdoors.28473/post-330021>

> [Chlo\_e said:](/goto/post?id=329964)
>
> I"ve read that grounding the canopy directly into clean earth can offset the electrical field exposure and should be done regardless of whether the electric field measurements are low?

As with most things in life there are different schools of thought. My partner is an electrician/electrical engineer and we do not recommend grounding a canopy but turning off the electric fields. Grounding makes you part of the circuit and outside (away from nnEMF) this is great BUT if you are inside and ground, even to an outside ground you can be exposed to additional nnEMF. A 50ft wire out the window to ground can act as an antenna. Always turn off what you can and then distance and/or shield what you have no control over.

Where in the UK are you?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/geographical-nnemf-protection.28474/post-329809>

I'm wondering if anyone has any relevant information as I begin searching for info on protection from nnEMF via geographical features and how helpful it could be. I'm currently in the process of finding an optimal place to live, as many others here likely are.

The idea I'm pondering is to live somewhere outside of a town or small city, far enough and within geographical features that could protect from the nnEMF. Traveling away from the city for activities outdoors, and periodically traveling to the city when needed to avoid complete social isolation.

In the past few months I have tried living in New Zealand, Martinique, and Colorado Springs. Most recently, Colorado Springs is very appealing for many personal reasons but is a nnEMF nightmare with the military bases and of course the 5G like everywhere else.

Could living in a pocket outside of a place like Colorado Springs as shown below mitigate a large amount of the nnEMF? Does anyone have any blog links or resources relating to this? I'm most concerned about the military bases' radar:

### Saichi — 2024-01-05T02:27:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/geographical-nnemf-protection.28474/post-329842>

Everyone here has to measure for themselves with a Safe and Sound Pro II. Even isolated places can have moderate to high levels with invisible towers respect to line of sight especially in forested areas.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-05T08:15:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/geographical-nnemf-protection.28474/post-329852>

nnEMF is made up of electric fields, magnetic fields as well as radio frequency (RF). As important as it is to avoid/minimize RF from cell towers, radar etc., for most people their greatest exposure to nnEMF is inside their home, from the wires in their walls and all the appliances and devices plugged into them, PLUS all the unnatural (blue) light. I can’t imagine finding the “ideal” place to live surrounded by military bases. I would recommend maximizing your natural EMF (sun light) to be your priority and find a “cave” as far south as possible. Have you looked into underground and/or dome houses?

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-01-05T23:53:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/geographical-nnemf-protection.28474/post-329877>

Agreed, I use a Cemprotec 34 right now.

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=329852)
>
> nnEMF is made up of electric fields, magnetic fields as well as radio frequency (RF). As important as it is to avoid/minimize RF from cell towers, radar etc., for most people their greatest exposure to nnEMF is inside their home, from the wires in their walls and all the appliances and devices plugged into them, PLUS all the unnatural (blue) light. I can’t imagine finding the “ideal” place to live surrounded by military bases. I would recommend maximizing your natural EMF (sun light) to be your priority and find a “cave” as far south as possible. Have you looked into underground and/or dome houses?

Absolutely, already on top of all of that.

The main last variable I'm unable to control is what surrounds where I live.

Trying to achieve good sunlight strength, optimal magnetic flux, minimize high altitude for long durations, avoid deserts, airports, military, 5G town centers, power lines, etc. Then there's the whole mold situation so a non hermetically sealed home is ideal in a location optimal for mitochondria with plenty of moisture and conducive to life. And be close enough to others my age, avoiding social isolation. Pretty tough to combine all those variables and also find a place I enjoy living with activities and things to do in nature. All at an affordable price.

Love the idea of a "cave"/underground/dome/eco house. Thank you for bringing this up. Pretty ideal for energy usage as well.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-with-people-at-night.28477/post-329834>

My choices are mine to honour myself with..
At that point nobody in my family minds me changing out lights and modifying access to stimulants after dark..
But they do mind the change in culture.
If I am to lead my family a little stronger down this path, the major resistance has been to \*talking\* and \*sharing\* in that space at night in which they'd have once 'checked out'.
it seems the appeal of a person that you're with can't compete anymore with diffuse electronic pseudo-connection. sad.
Looking for ideaas on how to add some real quality to that time, into the evening, so that the benefit ratio increases. Wish me luck!! hahah!

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-01-06T01:19:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-journal-for-aaron.28479/post-329878>

I'm creating this for a close friend because he is busy in med school and unable to dedicate much time to anything else.

Aaron F is a male, 27 years old, 5'9" Northern India descent. Lives in northern Louisiana.

His symptoms:
\-Inability to maintain ketosis as his heart rate skyrockets, has gone to ER trying to get into ketosis (via carnivore)
\-Easily dips into deep ketosis all the time if he doesn't have enough carbs
\-He is convinced the issue is hereditary and he has some kind of kidney related salt wasting issue/disorder/gene
\-Thinks carbs are necessary for many people with more equitorial genes
\-Chronically skinny his entire life, unable to gain muscle or fat, even with mass gainer powders
\-Already graying hairs at 27 yrs old
\-Has had off and on issues since med school with pain in his hands almost like neuropathy
\-Had a rare accessory spleen removed about 6 months ago, abdomen is still so unhealed from surgery that he can barely walk or sit up for long periods
\-Red light therapy and cold both cause his abdomen pain to drastically worsen. Similar situation w/red/cold prior when he had pelvic pain he believes mainly was caused by the accessory spleen
\-Brain function is great with minimal brain fog

My initial thoughts are that he is getting too much nnEMF to his head. He is at quite a low weight for his height and is desparate to understand how to fix the issue. He has followed Jack Kruse for years now but I believe he has had trouble fully implementing environmental changes due to finances and life situation/school.

The main question here is how could someone be unable to get into ketosis (usually getting past keto flu and using salt + getting plenty of animal fat solves the issue). His heart rate goes up to 140+ if he doesnt eat insane amounts of salt (15+g) in ketosis.

I'm personally very fascinated by this, as it is my personal belief that his genetic expression is being modified by his environment and preventing optimal functioning.

# Thread: Varying Cold Tolerance

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-01-07T18:46:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/varying-cold-tolerance.28486/post-329941>

One thing I have noticed about myself, is that I can tolerate cold very well in the morning (can walk outside at dawn with nothing but shorts in 30-40 degree F temps) and be completely unphased

But, as the day progresses, especially entering evening time, I become very intolerant of cold (I feel a strong urge to bundle up and turn on the heater, even in temps as high as 60 degF).

This is not the pattern 100% of the time. There are some mornings here and there where I am very cold intolerant. But, I would say roughly 80-90% of my days, I experience what I laid out above

Anybody know why this variance in cold tolerance occurs or happen to experience the same phenomenon?

### Brent Patrick — 2024-01-08T08:15:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/varying-cold-tolerance.28486/post-329963>

The same happens to me at the end of the day ,I recall Jack mentioning this several years ago on a monthly webinar,It had to do with the mitochrondria becoming fatigued due to the daily workload they undertake. Basically your battery becomes drained as the day progresses and your mitochrondria cannot repel cold as well as earlier in the day.
Hence why sleep is so important for running your autophagy and apoptosis programs.

### kris90 — 2024-01-08T17:05:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/varying-cold-tolerance.28486/post-329975>

I feel like CT for me, is the dominant circadian pacemaker, so by doing it every 24 hours (first thing upon rising), it's become my on switch, and I don't bother doing any additional CT sessions aside from just my daily walks (usually 2-3) or sitting outside up until bed time. Probably similar to if you have a set eating schedule/window, if you miss your usual meal, you get the ghrelin response. But if after a few days you continue to skip it, the ghrelin response goes away as your circadian feeding pattern adapts.

I do the same. Each morning a brief CT session and some outside breathing mobility work at around sunrise. Consistent for years now. Sometimes later i may do a cold outside walk or a quick ice plunge. Later in the day, I do feel the need of not wanting to go into the cold and tend to get a little cold as nightfall approaches. I think more, so it’s my body temperature dropping, and my melatonin production is starting to ramp up. As when it’s bedtime, it’s like I get hit with a tranquilizer and fall asleep within five minutes.

Also, most of my activity is in the a.m. and I don’t eat until one or two in the afternoon on my first meal. This set me up for a gradual relaxation mode going into the evening

# Thread: Google and Meta Patents for manipulating people through screens
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-and-meta-patents-for-manipulating-people-through-screens.28487/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### petrichor — 2024-01-07T20:52:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-and-meta-patents-for-manipulating-people-through-screens.28487/post-329944>

I heard Dr Kruse on a podcast recently refer to patents owned by Google and Meta that prove that they know how devices can be used to lower dopamine in users. I found the Google patent, but I am not having any luck finding the Meta patent. Does anyone know which patent he was refering to?

Here is Google's:

**Nervous system manipulation by electromagnetic fields from monitors**
[https://patents.google.com/patent/US6506148B2/en](https://patents.google.com/patent/US6506148B2/en)

> ### Abstract​
>
> Physiological effects have been observed in a human subject in response to stimulation of the skin with weak electromagnetic fields that are pulsed with certain frequencies near ½ Hz or 2.4 Hz, such as to excite a sensory resonance. Many computer monitors and TV tubes, when displaying pulsed images, emit pulsed electromagnetic fields of sufficient amplitudes to cause such excitation. It is therefore possible to manipulate the nervous system of a subject by pulsing images displayed on a nearby computer monitor or TV set. For the latter, the image pulsing may be imbedded in the program material, or it may be overlaid by modulating a video stream, either as an RF signal or as a video signal. The image displayed on a computer monitor may be pulsed effectively by a simple computer program. For certain monitors, pulsed electromagnetic fields capable of exciting sensory resonances in nearby subjects may be generated even as the displayed images are pulsed with subliminal intensity.

### NikkiYork — 2024-01-07T22:06:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-and-meta-patents-for-manipulating-people-through-screens.28487/post-329949>

That’s the expired patent of an individual. Not google‘s from what I see.

I guess the guy wanted to sell his tech and wasn‘t successful

### petrichor — 2024-01-08T20:44:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/google-and-meta-patents-for-manipulating-people-through-screens.28487/post-329989>

You're right. I made the Google association based on [a comment by Dr Kruse here](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/pfbid0wUWsyhzw7pKPCFyNzhZ4AdECEdxuxFpMnCqFPaZBCANKvYL2DbmdyYh3UEeV4h3ul?comment_id=2335004829897191&reply_comment_id=2335189329878741). It looks like [he may have gotten this](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-certain-are-you-that-things-are-well-in-school.17757/#post-191790) idea originally from Dave Wigington's Geoengineering watch website, but [the article is no longer available](http://www.geoengineeringwatch.org/us-patent-6506148-nervous-system-manipulation-by-electromagnetic-fields-from-monitors/), and I was not able to corroborate that claim doing my own research.

I do see that the patent holder has several patents: [https://patents.justia.com/inventor/hendricus-g-loos](https://patents.justia.com/inventor/hendricus-g-loos)
And appears to have been a real person: [https://obits.ocregister.com/us/obituaries/orangecounty/name/hendricus-loos-obituary?id=15581042](https://obits.ocregister.com/us/obituaries/orangecounty/name/hendricus-loos-obituary?id=15581042)

Beyond that, there's just a bunch of speculation on conspiracy and sci-fi/tech oriented websites.

More importantly though, the patent suggests that this technology is possible, and the fact that it is expired means any company can now make use of it without risk of legal infringement.

### jeremiasbtome — 2024-01-08T13:05:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-5-ldl-cholestrol-danger.28489/post-329965>

Got blood tests and my doctor said I had elevated ldl cholestrol at 3.5, is this bad

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-01-09T00:13:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-5-ldl-cholestrol-danger.28489/post-329996>

LDL is not inherently bad. A healthy individual can have a high LDL level

Specifically, sdLDL (small, dense LDL) is the one to watch out for that can be easily oxidized and create lots of issues.

LDL gets a bad rep bc of sdLDL. But, just bc LDL is high, doesn't necessarily mean sdLDL is as well

With much respect for what you have posted, I want to add a layer to it.

The sun will do WAY more for you than any supplement, except when there is a deficiency of a required nutrient, even in the presence of daily sun exposure. Let's take, say, a non-stop period causing low iron, hemoglobin, and ferritin (possibly B12). Malnutrition due to incorrect veganism, anorexia, or bulimia causes a whole host of deficiencies. Alcohol or medication (Tylenol) is chronically depleting NAC and B12. Diabetes causes severe vitamin B deficiency, especially B1, leading to peripheral neuropathy and possible amputation. Addison's disease causes chronic loss of sodium while sparing potassium, and for 60 years before pharma, 4-14 grams of salt was the accepted treatment.

Never separate a piece of the story from the whole or a part of the body from the whole.

"The sun will do WAY more for you than any supplement, as long as your house is in order."

READERS SUMMARY: 1. WHY DO MANY HAVE A PROBLEM WITH KETOSIS? 2. HOW DOES SEASONAL AFFECTIVE DISORDER EXPLAIN THIS PHENOMENA? 3. HOW DOES TECHNOLOGY SHORT CIRCUIT OUR MITOCHONDRIA? 4. DO YOU CONFUSE DYSFUNCTION WITH IMMATURITY? 5. CAN AN IMPERFECT HYPOTHESIS STILL BE DIRECTIONALLY...

### Sue-UK — 2024-01-10T12:11:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mushrooms-for-cancer.28490/post-330047>

> [giedremail said:](/goto/post?id=329970)
>
> Their research says it will. I just want to make sure I am not missing on any potential harm.
> Is there any research we can trust?

Cancer Research UK says "As far as we know, there is nothing in the mushrooms or compounds that would be harmful. It is not currently known how helpful they are in cancer care. "
([https://www.cancerresearchuk.org/ab...idual-therapies/mushrooms-in-cancer-treatment](https://www.cancerresearchuk.org/about-cancer/treatment/complementary-alternative-therapies/individual-therapies/mushrooms-in-cancer-treatment) ).

As to whether they are helpful - my interest is in a whole food, not a tincture or supplement, and how that affects how we deal with sunlight, and the effects on microbiome photon emission, either for signalling or as a source of light. Capsules and tinctures may lose some of the benefits of the whole food, as well as being expensive.

Another example is phycocyanin, (found in spirulina), which is interesting cancer researchers for use in chemo.

### [Phycocyanin: A Potential Drug for Cancer Treatment](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5687155/#:~:text=Introduction%3A%20Phycocyanin&text=Natural%20products%20derived%20from%20food,been%20fully%20studied%202%2C%203.)

Phycocyanin isolated from marine organisms has the characteristics of high efficiency and low toxicity, and it can be used as a functional food. It has been reported that phycocyanin has anti-oxidative function, anti-inflammatory activity, anti-cancer ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

But spirulina is farmed as a crop, and it contains a lot of vitamins and minerals, amino acids, chlorophyll etc and has had a lot of research, possibly because of its potential use as a food for space travel. Raw spirulina is now being incorporated in some of the alternative cancer therapies, but (for other reasons) I've used a minimally processed one, with third party testing for contaminants.

One of the toxins in the environment that affects how our bodies deal with light is aluminium - think of what happens when aluminium is heated in a microwave. I regularly detox from that using a silicon rich mineral water, which for me is a better bang for my buck than a supplement. There's lots of sources I think people are unaware of other than anti perspirants, which I think is more dangerous in a microwaved world. Then there's the potential estrogenic effects of artificial fragrances and how we are awash with them with everything from laundry products which finish up on our skin from clothing and bedding, to room sprays etc. Apart from any toxicity, I would suspect they signal year round summer .... but its enough to make me avoid them.

### giedremail — 2024-01-10T17:49:34+0200

### Sue-UK — 2024-01-11T12:48:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mushrooms-for-cancer.28490/post-330089>

> [giedremail said:](/goto/post?id=330053)
>
> Mushrooms absorb radiation, I was just thinking that maybe supplements would be checked for that.
> I saw analysis of snow from one of the EU countries, it was shocking. Aluminium highest. I wonder how much our foods affected...

I think what aluminium is found with makes a difference in food. I see aluminum as the anti-uv element on the periodic table, and that to protect life mother nature tied it up with silicon, and environmentally humans have messed with that, and by stripping silicon in processing foods, it may be another way processed food is a problem.

I haven't looked at the research but in his book Silica Water the secret of healthy blue zone longevity in the aluminum age Dennis Crouse refers to aluminum in relation to the germ line BRCA1 gene mutation, but even for non carriers he says that aluminum epigenetically lowers expression of BRCA1 resulting in reduced levels of BRCA1 preventing it from suppressing breast cancer.

I suspect aluminium interferes with everything from vitamin D production in the skin, (which would be especially bad news for northern latitudes) to microbiome light signalling, to cellular ELF UV light signalling, which may affect sleep and natural deuterium depletion. Jack has talked about it relation to its reflective properties, i.e. interfering with cellular ELF UV signalling and in answer to my question about whether aluminum could stop UV from slowing down time, he said “of course.” Time not slowing down means faster ageing, and aluminum is implicated in diseases of aging such as Alzheimers, Parkinson’s, heart disease, as well as autism and cancer.

For me I don’t think blood tests for levels of aluminum are as useful as an audit of current and previous exposure, because a test doesn’t show where it is in tissues. There’s been experiments done on urine levels before and after drinking silicon rich mineral waters which show that higher levels are being excreted from the tissues. Two of the main excretion routes are the urine and sweat, and the skin is a sink for aluminum. This could be because the body preferentially puts it there to keep it away from the bone marrow, or it could be that it's one of the areas with higher cell turnover to get rid of it, and it’s interesting that some dandruff shampoos contain aluminium, presumably to slow cell turnover …..

It's not just from things like antiperspirants, there’s aluminium foil, non stick pans, aluminium coffee makers. (Aluminum plus heat connection). It's also someone’s body burden from things like past or current sun cream use, cosmetics and hair care products, buffered aspirins, antacids and other meds, vaccinations, tap water, air pollution, colouring in sweets and processed foods, baking powders (so a lot of commercial baking products), certain pigments in tattoos which could finish up in the lymph, tobacco, cannabis… It can be stored for years in the body, depending on where it is dumped. Although it's doing damage on the way, I think the gut lining and skin’s turnover is much faster at getting rid of it than the internal organs and the bone. It damages the internal organs, which in turn then probably can’t detoxify it, and it could leave one tissue, get back into the blood, and finish up in another. It can affect fertility in both sexes, (in his book about aluminium Chris Exley showed a pic of aluminium in the sperm), and even if conception is successful, there’s multiple exposure opportunities from the moment of conception.

From Jack's comments, I don't think auditing my exposure regularly and doing something simple to get it out goes against the teaching.

Anything kind of advice from our mitochondriac family would be great, like locations for watching the sunrise/sunsets, places to eat, places to visit and explore etc.
I have been fascinated with the information that Uncle Jack has brought to our consciousness and am willing spread his message by any means possible. I am a doctor of Chiropractic and my goal is to make the transition to El Salvador and help Dr. Jack in any way possible. My goal is to introduce a unique style of Chiropractic to the people of El Salvador while also implementing Nature's rules. I am also very interested in learning from Dr. Jack in greater depth and with his blessing, possibly teaching doctors in the future would be wonderful.
I will be taking my adjusting table with me and will be more than happy to adjust as many black swans as possible and their families. I think this is a great way to introduce myself, build relationships and hopefully see some amazing things while down there.
I am very excited to start on this new journey and will be grateful to meet as many of you as possible. Hope to see you guys there.

Dr. Manny Barquero

# Thread: POMC and sweeteners POMC and gender
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pomc-and-sweeteners-pomc-and-gender.28493/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sara Pugh — 2024-01-09T19:37:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pomc-and-sweeteners-pomc-and-gender.28493/post-330020>

1.  Do low-calorie sweeteners (LCS) disrupt POMC regulation? This paper (it is mice who are nocturnal and in blue light) suggests that maternal LCS consumption results in POMC dysregulation and increased adiposity and glucose intolerance in male offsprings but not in female offsprings (NB: LCS here included aspartame and rebaudioside A (highly purified form of stevia)). [https://insight.jci.org/articles/view/156397](https://insight.jci.org/articles/view/156397?fbclid=IwAR14uvRKAgjkLVEh65bm61qWcQ1KdpoxavL1vWn9sfI4L3uuARp0yZiyX_Y)
2.  POMC regulation of energy balance and adiposity in males and females? This study suggests that males have less POMC neuronal activity and are less responsive to leptin and more responsive to insulin. This would make sense as leptin has a lot more to do in a female regading the complexity of a cycle. How else or how different is POMC signalling different in males and females as females are more sensitive to light [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9322714/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9322714/?fbclid=IwAR25NZnXQhgeGyDdlTapJCz-WQNuPK2e8o4EzTsr4btjUvrJQQx0MxJT2yU)

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-01-17T03:36:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pomc-and-sweeteners-pomc-and-gender.28493/post-330311>

Sweeteners are almost ubiquitous in sodas. You have to read every single label. ( Every thing I see in Panama with the exception of Canada Dry has acesulfameK and sucralose, and some have aspartame which is a potent BH4 disrupter. ). It's good in one way as I just buy water and pure juice). But you see a product read the label and think. Poison !

### JanSz — 2024-01-17T16:33:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pomc-and-sweeteners-pomc-and-gender.28493/post-330323>

> [Dr Rohen Kapur said:](/goto/post?id=330311)
>
> Sweeteners are almost ubiquitous in sodas. You have to read every single label. ( Every thing I see in Panama with the exception of Canada Dry has acesulfameK and sucralose, and some have aspartame which is a potent BH4 disrupter. ). It's good in one way as I just buy water and pure juice). But you see a product read the label and think. Poison !

**low-calorie sweeteners (LCS) do they contain fructose?**

Low-calorie sweeteners (LCS) can be made from various types of sugars, **and some of them do contain fructose.** However, it’s important to note that not all LCS contain fructose.
[For instance, sweeteners like **High Fructose Corn Syrup**, **Agave Nectar**, **Maple Syrup**, **Coconut Sugar**, **Dates**, and **Date Sugar** contain a high ratio of fructose to glucose](https://janeshealthykitchen.com/natural-sweeteners-best-and-worst/)[1](https://janeshealthykitchen.com/natural-sweeteners-best-and-worst/).
[On the other hand, there are sweeteners like **Stevia** and **Erythritol** that are considered safe and contain neither fructose nor glucose](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/healthy-natural-sweeteners)[2](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/healthy-natural-sweeteners).
[It’s also worth mentioning that **Tagatose**, a form of fructose that is around 90% sweeter than sucrose, is used in food production as a low-calorie sweetener](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/323469)[3](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/323469).

\=============================================

**Fructose raises uric acid in the blood.
What about Stevia, Erythriol and Tagatose?
Do they also raise Uric acid in the blood?**

Stevia, Erythritol, and Tagatose are all low-calorie sweeteners that are alternatives to fructose.
Here’s what research says about their effects on uric acid levels:

-   [**Stevia**: A 2018 clinical trial found that stevia supplements reduced uric acid levels in participants with chronic kidney disease](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/319837)[1](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/319837).
-   [Other sources suggest that stevia **may increase uric acid secretion** due to its diuretic properties, which could potentially lower uric acid levels in the blood](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/319837)[2](https://goutpatients.com/stevia-and-gout/).
-   [**Erythritol**: According to a pilot dose-ranging study, acute ingestion of erythritol did not affect blood lipid and uric acid concentrations](https://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/319837)[3](https://www.mdpi.com/2072-6643/15/2/458).
-   [**Tagatose**: Some sources suggest that high doses of Tagatose **may cause temporary rises in uric acid** blood levels](https://perfectketo.com/tagatose/)[4](https://perfectketo.com/tagatose/).
-   [However, another source states that Tagatose only increases blood uric acid levels by a small margin, not enough to lead to gout](https://naturalpedia.com/tagatose-sources-health-risks.html)[5](https://naturalpedia.com/tagatose-sources-health-risks.html).

\=============

# Thread: Kendall's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kendalls-journal.28495/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kendall Toerner — 2024-01-10T20:35:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kendalls-journal.28495/post-330062>

I wanted to post my experience so far to at least help other people who may have similar symptoms and issues to me.

**Background**
\-I'm 30 years old, Finnish and German descent, 6' and 160 lbs
\-Am living and have lived most of my life around 40th degree latitude
\-I've abused tech for many years and spent much of my teens reverse engineering my Xbox's software and hardware. My entire life has been poor circadian cycles staying up until 12-2 am and sleeping until 9-11 am
\-I've eaten mostly vegetarian my entire life until 2020 when I switched to mostly whole foods and lots of meat
\-I've been seed oil free since 2020 as well, alongside getting lots of mid day sun (not ideal but better than the prior years of my life)

**I owned a Tesla for 4 years** which is what I believe led to my issues, combined with buying and **living in a** **house in Seattle WA which ended up being high in mold** and more importantly **had a very strong magnetic field throughout the entire home**. As a tech abuser, I had the entire home also hooked up with smart lights, smart blinds, and other connected devices. I also was dealing with a stressful relationship on top of these stressors. I would often drive the Tesla hours on road trips around Washington State several times a week. Around 6+ hours a day, 2-3 days a week

**Symptoms**
\-My symptoms peaked suddenly after a single day of driving 11 hours in the Tesla in a loop around the majority of the Cascade mountain range
\-This coincided interestingly enough within a week after the fall equinox
\-I began feeling a sense of doom whenever inside the house I bought and moved into, even after mold eradication
\-Slowly got worsening pinpoint internal stabbing pain in my lower left abdomen at lower abdominal V
\-I then started developing a very annoying feeling of fullness along my entire left half of my abdomen that would come and go
\-Alongside the fullness, I felt a sharp pain at times inside my abdomen under the bottom of my left ribcage

**Attempts to solve**
\-Like everyone I've tried many supplements, homeopathic doctors, traditional doctors, with none having a clue about what's going on. I've received a hilarious number of incorrect diagnoses from so many "professionals"
\-I tried eating every diet under the sun. Lots of experiments. Lower PRAL, lower oxalates, lower lectins, lower fiber, higher polyphenols, etc
\-Animal based diet with fruit and absolutely no lectins was very helpful but not optimal
\-I spent lots of time outside and getting sun, which helped, but my circadian rhythm was still far from ideal until I learned more about it's importance
\-Found the true issue behind my symptoms through traveling twice for work. My symptoms greatly reduced within 2 days of leaving home and would come back a day or two after arrival.

**Findings**
\-Although animal based was helpful, I didn't see real results until going full carnivore
\-Luckily around the same time I began listening to and reading more of Jack's work
\-Dry skin and low iodine symptoms led me to fully believe that seafood must be included in a proper species appropriate human diet, which resolved both quickly
\-Unable to shake coffee this entire time, I tried going off of it and eventually learned that tapering is important to not shock gut motility too quickly, causing my symptoms to slightly come back
\-Dairy is the biggest contributor to recurrent symptoms, especially milk (although A2 and raw react less)
\-Methylene blue supplementation of 2-10 mg/day initially had massive effects on my mood and mental focus. It seems to have reversed the feeling of dread that has hung over me since living in my toxic environment. Stopped doing this daily after 2 months and don't notice much effect from it when taken now

**Current day**
\-I'm still dealing with a very minor version of my abdominal symptoms occasionally when I am stuck for too long in a low UV environment or eat the wrong foods
\-These physical symptoms also come back stronger in a dose dependent manner when eating products with lectins, but especially cakes/breads
\-Drinking water before or without a meal starts to trigger the upper abdominal pain, which is what I'm trying to figure out. I don't believe it to be related to GERD based on my testing and since I have no other symptoms
\-The lower abdominal pain is always accompanied by harder stool when it happens, but I'm unsure what the issue is internally as I have extremely low markers of gut inflammation and zero blood, both from high accuracy testing
\-I'm trying to get everything to fully heal up currently with as much grounding, sunlight, and connection to nature as possible
\-UV light has the strongest healing effect as I've abruptly changed environments via travel and also a Sperti UV lamp which both instantly hide symptoms
\-I eat fully carnivore, primarily high fat red meat, salmon, shrimp, and occasionally seaweed
\-Have had optimal circadian rhythm and minimal tech usage for about 2 years now
\-I don't burn from the sun even laying in one position for an hour mid day in the summer

My symptoms are currently 99% managed as I know the triggers and healing components

I'm now trying to get what I assume to be internal wounds to fully heal, so they don't get too much more recurring damage for the rest of my life. Hoping UV light will do this for me over time. Would love any input on this.

WiFi and Bluetooth are 2.4 GHz as is a microwave oven, AT&T (+Verizon?) “5G” is “C” band between 3 & 4 GHz. T-Mobile ”5G” is 600 MHz. It is my understanding that 90% of 5G is sub 6 GHz. For shielding think microwave over door, same idea as “smart meter” covers. If you are on the ground floor you could put metal screen on the outside of the building or on the fence between you and your neighbors. Not 100% but reduces RF significantly. Think of the RF source (cell tower, neighbors WiFi) as a light and the metal mesh as a shade, your bed should be in the shade. This is from HomeDepot, if used inside needs to be grounded.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-01-17T19:26:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kendalls-journal.28495/post-330331>

T-Mobile:

| Technology | Frequencies |
| --- | --- |
| 5GUC (Ultra Capacity 5G) |
-   Band n41 (2.5 GHz)
-   Band n258 (24 GHz)
-   Band n260 (39 GHz)
-   Band n261 (28 GHz)

 |
| 5G (Extended Range 5G) |

-   Band n71 (600 MHz)

 |

T-Mobile have a lot of double speed on their web site. Their main 5G (5th Generation) service is 600MHz. I don’t know where their “5GUC” service is even available, a few major city down town areas I expect. Their 5G in home internet service appears to be the same as everyone else’s with 2.4 GHz and/or 5GHz connection option. Everyone around you with WiFi is the same.

A general rule of thumb in home networking says that Wi-Fi routers operating on the 2.4 GHz band can reach up to 150 feet indoors and 300 feet outdoors. Older 802.11a routers that ran on 5 GHz bands reached approximately one-third of these distances. Newer 802.11n and 802.11ac routers that operate on both 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz bands reach greater distances.

Because it uses narrower wavelengths, a 5 GHz Wi-Fi connection is more susceptible to obstructions than 2.4 GHz connections, and so will usually have a slightly shorter effective range, typically, 10 to 15 feet shorter.

## Factors Influencing Range​

There are three main factors that influence your Wi-Fi range: the access point or router itself, the structure you're in, and the wireless standard you're using.

### Access Point or Router​

The Wi-Fi signal range of any given access point varies significantly from device to device. Factors that determine the range of an access point include the specific [802.11 protocol](https://www.lifewire.com/what-is-802-11-wireless-protocol-2378244) it runs, the strength of its device transmitter, and the nature of physical obstructions and radio interference in the surrounding area.

The distance at which someone can connect to an access point varies depending on antenna orientation. Smartphone users, in particular, may see their connection strength increase or decrease simply by turning the device at different angles. Furthermore, some access points use directional antennas that enable longer reach in areas the antenna is pointing but shorter reach in other areas.

Change the antenna that came with your router if you're not getting the signal strength you need.

### Type of Structure or Building​

Physical obstructions in homes, such as brick walls and metal frames or siding, can reduce the range of a Wi-Fi network by 25 percent or more.

A Wi-Fi signal weakens every time it encounters an obstruction, which happens a lot indoors, thanks to walls, floors, and even the electronic interference caused by appliances.

### Wireless Standard​

The wireless standard you're using has a direct effect on your wireless signal range and strength. The 802.11g protocol has an indoor range of 125 feet, while 802.11n has a range of 235 feet.

All this applies to everyone with neighbors, so . . . measure and shield accordingly . . . then measure again. AND remember your mobile phone is also sending out a signal that can reach a cell tower up to 1 or even 2 miles away . . . And so is everyone else’s phone. If you live around other people this is just a fact of modern life . . . Have fun.
