When did light bulbs become common in America?

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

In **1882** Edison helped form the Edison Electric Illuminating Company of New York, which brought electric light to parts of Manhattan. But progress was slow. Most Americans still lit their homes with gas light and candles for another fifty years. By _1925_ about _half of prosperous homes_ in the U.S. had electric power. Alternating circuit "won the game" over the **war of the currents** - a series of events surrounding the introduction of competing electric power transmission systems in the late 1880s and early 1890s.

However, the new infrastructure from DC to AC took money to make the change. New developments in California used the "new" AC design. But non-city folk did not, and "old city DC power" costs to change was not funded by government, but individuals - if they saw the investment profitable. Remember the Great Depression hit in 1929. These investments to convert to AC went to hell.

However, "new" housing development used AC. Thus the total average was a progression to AC generally speaking over time.

How did California mine gold mostly after 1930? ->
In a modern style of hydraulic mining first developed in California, a high-pressure hose directed a powerful stream or jet of water at gold-bearing gravel beds. The loosened gravel and gold would then pass over sluices, with the gold settling to the bottom where it was collected.

This is how the AC power grid became successful -> hydro-AC-generators.

Pictures of city DC power-grid:

See this- [https://byjus.com/neet/difference-between-c3-c4-and-cam-pathway/](https://byjus.com/neet/difference-between-c3-c4-and-cam-pathway/)
Also what I buy from them pork, the melted fat is grainy not smooth and solid at room temperature, no need for fridge.

### logan.spears33 — 2024-01-24T01:29:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-and-floaters.28535/post-330520>

Hello. I have myopia with a -.50/-.50 prescription glasses and severe floaters in both eyes and they bother me more in sunlight. Doc says it’s just natural breakdown of the eye and is normal but I am only 25 so seems as if some internal process is occurring. Is there any protocol to help this or do I just try and ignore it? Any help would be appreciated! Thanks

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-01-24T14:55:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-and-floaters.28535/post-330525>

Get more IR into your eyes. Get outdoors at sunrise and sunset as much as possible.

### jules3791 — 2024-01-24T16:28:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-and-floaters.28535/post-330530>

Being outdoors for sunrise and sunset for the last 2.5 years has done wonders for my eyesight...I am 51 years old. At 48, my eye doc said my eyes would decline rapidly from there. Much to his surprise, each time I've had an examination since, he finds that I've maintained in my right eye and have better eyesight in my left. I also do not wear any type of eye glasses, contacts or sunglasses when outdoors. I will use a hat for shade.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-02T15:34:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-and-floaters.28535/post-332124>

Blue Light is Giving You Eye Floaters More and more young people are reporting eye floaters. Why? Because Blue light accelerates vitreous degeneration in the eye! The vitreous has a lot of melanopsin it > This leads to vitreous opacity and increased floaters. "While blue light helps to maintain normal biological rhythms, excessive blue light radiation may induce fundus lesions." Studies show Vitreous floaters are higher among digital device users. Protect your vision. Limit screen time and wear blue light blockers. Study: [https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32244150/](https://t.co/3sWk9ZcWTb)

Hi guys, Hi Jack,

i was wondering if there is anybody who has experienced hypertrophy of nasal chonae and thus is mostly a mouth breather (at night); for me almost my whole life.

Any experiences in changing that? Hacks?

Alper

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-01-24T19:27:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypertrophy-of-nasal-conchae.28536/post-330541>

I've dealt with seemingly random sinus swelling my entire life.

The only trend I've been able to deduce is that it is stimulated by storms.

There hasn't been a pattern with what I eat, or whether outside vs inside.
But, one thing always holds true, if my sinuses swell, it is either storming, or a storm is right around the corner.

My guess is that it is related to atmospheric pressure.

I can say that -- Ever since I have been mindfully following Kruse's protocols, my sinus symptoms have gotten much less severe. I still get minor swelling here and there though.

### Alper — 2024-01-24T20:52:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypertrophy-of-nasal-conchae.28536/post-330545>

Thanks for your answers, guys.

I was wondering about the analogy of Jack with adipose people not having to much energy but beeing "leaky", loosing enery to the outside world and was wondering if someone who was been doing CT or isolated ice in your mouth (like Jack recommends for obstructive sleep apnea) works on the nasal conchae shrinking as well.

### Alper — 2024-01-25T13:25:37+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2024-01-25T15:21:05+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2024-01-25T19:14:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypertrophy-of-nasal-conchae.28536/post-330575>

Do you know that UV light has amazing effects in water confined in small spaces below 1.4 nm? The smaller an object is, the less strictly it is governed by the laws of classical physics and the more it is subject to quantum effects. So realize that deuterium has double the atomic mass and radius of Hydrogen atom. Is this why mitochondria and cells chose H+ over deuterium 3.8 billion years ago to run its hydrogen heat furnace thag drives life?

A tiny hydrogen atom is a quantum object—sometimes behaving like a particle, sometimes more like a wave. Deuterium, twice as heavy as hydrogen, is less subject to quantum effects. The consequence is that D2O is less destabilized than H2O when squeezed between two hydrophobic surfaces and the hydrogen bonds between water molecules get broken. Because of Einstein's E-mc^2 deuterium decreases energy flux when it is in water.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-01-25T20:57:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypertrophy-of-nasal-conchae.28536/post-330584>

I understand that bc deuterium is heavier, it takes more energy to move it around and that it can "clog things up" and can ruin semiconduction throughout the body.

But, isn't the body able to clear deuterium (thru urine, excrement or sweat) when it builds up? What would prevent the body's natural ability to get rid of excess deuterium?

If the body is healthy, shouldn't it easily be able to extract and use the protium it needs and just excrete the excess deuterium no problem?

### JanSz — 2024-01-25T21:31:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypertrophy-of-nasal-conchae.28536/post-330586>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=330562)
>
> In the orbital frontal gyri yes.

# Thread: AGING AND CANCER ARE MITOCHONDRIAL PROCESSES.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aging-and-cancer-are-mitochondrial-processes.28540/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-01-27T17:07:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aging-and-cancer-are-mitochondrial-processes.28540/post-330643>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Cancer requires cells to become pluripotent. The effect requires an inability to burn fat via the TCA cycle. Sunlight keeps the TCA functioning well.

Somatic cell reprogramming to iPSCs includes a transition in mitochondrial morphology from an elongated, filamentous, and branching network structure to a collection of small, punctate, and separate organelles.

Concurrent with this morphologic shift, metabolism skews from mainly OXPHOS in somatic cells used in this illustration towards mainly glycolytic metabolism in reprogrammed iPSCs. Additional changes that occur during reprogramming include a reduction in mtDNA copy number, alterations in TCA cycle metabolite levels, changes in Ca2+ handling and the production of Fe–S clusters, and a required time-coordinated oxidative burst.

Mitochondrial dysfunction can disrupt these critical metabolic transitions and may result in incomplete reprogramming, spontaneous differentiation, or cell death. Abbreviations: iPSC, induced pluripotent stem cell; mtDNA, mitochondrial DNA; OXPHOS, oxidative phosphorylation; TCA, tricarboxylic acid.

**HETEROPLASMY AND AGING ARE LINKED, BUT HERE IS WHERE LAB MEDICINE AND DECENTRALIZED MEDICINE DIFFER**
Cellular reprogramming does not always generate iPSCs with heteroplasmy rates identical to the somatic source cells in lab experiments. Mutant mtDNA copy number can be enriched or reduced during somatic cell reprogramming, prolonged iPSC culture, and iPSC differentiation. I believe this effect is tied to the light frequency in the lab.

In Nature, I believe the sun creates iPSCs that lower the heteroplasmy rate, keeping us from chronic disease, delaying aging, and helping explain human longevity compared to other primates.

Reactive oxygen species (ROS) are byproducts of normal cellular function, including the mitochondria’s electron transport chain, and are usually kept in low concentrations under normal cellular conditions. Melanin is critical in this protection because of how it deals with ROS. However, if ROS are allowed to accumulate chronically, these toxic molecules can precipitate oxidative damage to membranes, mitochondrial DNA, proteins, and lipids, as well as increase mitochondrial dysfunction by disrupting TCA efficiency. This opens the door to aging diseases like Alzheimer's disease and leads to more rapid aging via stem cell depletion.

Within the mitochondrial membranes, a non-specific pore is formed by the association of several membrane proteins referred to as the mitochondrial permeability transition (MPT) pore. This liberates parts and or peptides from the mitochondrial membrane, and these particles can then change cell signaling.

Patrushev M, Kasymov V, Patrusheva V, Ushakova T, Gogvadze V, Gaziev A. Mitochondrial permeability transition triggers the release of mtDNA fragments. Cell Mol Life Sci CMLS. 2004;61(24):3100–3.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-01-27T17:10:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aging-and-cancer-are-mitochondrial-processes.28540/post-330644>

Under pathological conditions, say in ALS or AD, the probability of pore opening and the amount of mtDNA released from the mitochondria increases significantly. MtDNA has also been shown to be found in extracellular vesicles released from the cell, and there is some evidence that this process increases under pathological conditions, particularly in neurological disorders. As mitochondrial damage and dysfunction increase during states of oxidative stress, mtDNA is released upon apoptosis via BAX/BAK signaling and mitochondrial membrane permeabilization.

1\. Tsilioni I, Theoharides TC. Extracellular vesicles are increased in the serum of children with autism spectrum disorder, contain mitochondrial DNA, and stimulate human microglia to secrete IL-1β. J Neuroinflammation. 2018;15(1):239.

2.Vaidya M, Sreerama S, Gaviria M, Sugaya K. Exposure to a pathological condition may be required for the cells to secrete exosomes containing mtDNA aberration. J Nucleic Acids. 2022;17(2022):e7960198.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-01-27T17:18:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aging-and-cancer-are-mitochondrial-processes.28540/post-330646>

The physical opening of the MPT, the release of an exosome, or the activation of BAK.BAX apoptosis is a physical cipher in which the mtDNA's biophoton signature has shifted. The chromophores in the cell capture this signal. This assumes they are in good shape.

Cellular 'vision' is based on how the organism built its non-visual photoreceptor system to operate. Because of this arrangement, you should realize that the system cells use requires a considerably complex response. That is what the biophoton system from mitochondrial metabolism is all about. Namely the ability of the cells to make out several light sources individually to give a proper physiological response.
Biochemical guidance is not chemical in nature. This offends the centralized paradigm in biology and medicine. This is why they are impotent to come up with a cure or a decent treatment for Alzheimer's disease or in ALS.
What are biochemical substrates at their core? They are solar signals. They are an intermediate vessel for carrying photonic data. I’ve repeatedly said this in my posts.

Stimulus sensation is the key mechanism built into the mammal body plan. Our mortal constraint forces us to prioritize tactile-haptic sense to protect the body. Our centralized scientific mistake was universalizing that local bias over the whole of the organism. Stimulus sensation does not depend on the matter in cells, but matter depends on the cell's ability to handle the tactile-haptic sense. The circadian mechanism in mammals is this system's control arm. Light is visible, but photons are tactile. Melanin captures the tactile stimulus for the non-visual photoreceptor system.

This is how chronic disease and aging occur.

### JanSz — 2024-01-27T19:12:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aging-and-cancer-are-mitochondrial-processes.28540/post-330651>

Stimulus sensation does not depend on the matter in cells, but matter depends on the cell's ability to handle the **tactile-haptic sense.**
\===================
Haptic sensing relates to any **sense of touch** and is a combination of two sensing abilities.
One part of haptic sensing is tactile sensing, which is the detection of force on the skin surface, whereas the other part of haptic sensing is kinesthetic, meaning the sensing of body movement and muscle strength.

\----------------------------
**tactile-haptic sense**
The terms “haptic” and “tactile” are often used interchangeably, but they have distinct meanings.
Haptic refers to the sense of touch and the perception of physical sensations through the skin.
It encompasses the intricate interplay between the human body and its environment, allowing us to explore and interact with the world around us.
Tactile, on the other hand, is a broader term encompassing all aspects related to the sense of touch.
[It encompasses both haptic experiences and the overall perception of touch, including not only physical sensations but also emotional and psychological responses elicited by tactile stimuli](https://thecontentauthority.com/blog/haptic-vs-tactile).
\========================

# Thread: Do you know Ernesto prieto gratacos?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-know-ernesto-prieto-gratacos.28542/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BrunoB — 2024-01-28T20:09:42+0200

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-04T19:48:22+0200

> And then he says that you should eat broths, not eat suddenly after fasting for more than 2 days.
> Health depends on many factors, obviously, not just one or two.

The Germans had this classic book about fasting that everybody read in the 80ies and 90s. That’s what my “knowledge” is based on. Or what I remember from it, hahaha.

There you have one week of getting solid food out of your system which includes a bunch of enemas. And smoothly transitioning to what is eventually going to be filtered broth and diluted fruit juice during the fast.

Then comes fasting, however long, but it does include drinking water, and depending on your situation, broth or juice diluted. And then the same week is taken to smoothly go back to regular food.

To me that makes sense, but like I said, I only tried it once and I could not stand up from my bed in the morning on the second day of fasting. I had to crawl on my knees to the fridge and eat a slice of orange and then my circulation kicked back on. Hahahaha.

If I was you, I would just try out a fast that makes sense to you and doesn’t involve too crazy techniques. I’m talking about those starving gurus who occasionally die. That can’t be good.

However, fasting has been done in forever and if you try it in moderation and with some sort of information about, what can go wrong?

Worst is that you end up like me not being able to do it and then you brake the fast, just like I did.

Like I said, in Germany it was a total hype in the 90s and all my friends did it. It was like going to yoga. People went to retreats or did it by themselves.

Some liked it, some didn’t. Just try it out reasonably.

# Thread: LUPUS is a combo disease.  Genetic and mtDNA are present simultaneously
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lupus-is-a-combo-disease-genetic-and-mtdna-are-present-simultaneously.28544/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-01-29T03:47:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lupus-is-a-combo-disease-genetic-and-mtdna-are-present-simultaneously.28544/post-330694>

Lupus causes severe inflammation throughout the body and can seriously impact the lives of those affected. Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Infection Biology in Berlin have now discovered a mechanism that can trigger lupus in children. This mechanism regulates the amount of a specific immune receptor that normally recognizes the genetic material of pathogens. If this mechanism is disrupted, receptors accumulate in the immune cells, leading to the recognition of the body's own genetic material. As a result, the immune system turns against its own body and causes the systemic inflammation of lupus.

The innate immune system responds to invading pathogens within minutes. It is an initial line of defense that keeps pathogens at bay until the more specific adaptive immune system kicks in. However, this speed comes at a price: the innate immune system’s response is so strong and unspecific that it must be well controlled to prevent it from turning against the body itself.

Centralized researchers are working better to understand these control mechanisms of the innate immune system. They focus on an immune receptor called Toll-like receptor 7, which can recognize the genetic material of viruses and bacteria and then trigger an immune response against the invaders.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

## Immune receptor BORC is out of balance.​

For the immune system to react quickly, a certain number of these receptors must be present in the immune cells. Cells maintain this balance by constantly producing and degrading receptors. The researchers showed that BORC is required to degrade Toll-like receptor 7 within the cell. In addition, BORC needs another protein, UNC93B1, to perform the degradation process correctly.

If there is an error in this process, the receptor is not degraded and accumulates in the immune cells.

From earlier experiments in mice carried out a few years ago at the University of Berkeley in California, we already knew that too many of these receptors are a problem. A greater amount of receptor biases toward recognizing the body’s own genetic material. This leads to an immune response against self, triggering the autoimmune disease lupus. Until recently, neither BORC nor UNC93B1 have been associated with lupus in humans.

Fabian Hauck teaches, researches, and treats patients at the Ludwig Maximilian University Hospital in Munich, specializing in congenital immune disorders such as lupus. He became aware of this research because one of his patients had a mutation in the gene for a previously unnoticed protein: UNC93B1. It was precisely this protein that the researchers had identified. They were able to confirm that the mutation in UNC93B1 was the cause of this patient’s lupus. Now we have proof it can happen in humans.

### xxx — 2024-10-07T05:41:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lupus-is-a-combo-disease-genetic-and-mtdna-are-present-simultaneously.28544/post-340529>

I've MTHFR Gene mutation - C677T. Following the recommended diet has helped me tremendously. I'm wondering if diet can affect/improve UNC93B1. So far, I have not seen anything regarding this, will keep my eyes open though.

# Thread: Sensory Processing Disorder
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensory-processing-disorder.28545/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TaylorF — 2024-01-29T18:12:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensory-processing-disorder.28545/post-330710>

I am a special education teacher.

What are your thoughts on sensory processing issues?
What is the root cause of the problem?
Hacks/supplements that I could recommend to families with kids who struggle with this?
I believe there is a huge tie with glutamate/GABA regulation and how the balance is maintained in the brain and body.
Some websites say to eliminate dairy, gluten, etc. but there has to be more. Nutrient deficiencies?
One of the struggles is sensitivity to light, so recommending sunlight will have to be a slow process for a lot of these kids.

Any thoughts greatly appreciated!

### JanSz — 2024-01-30T00:21:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensory-processing-disorder.28545/post-330726>

I think that Gabapentin is used too often.
Possibly also in too high doses.

Gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) is a neurotransmitter, a chemical messenger in your brain. It slows down your brain by blocking specific signals in your central nervous system (your brain and spinal cord). [GABA is known for producing a calming effect](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/articles/22857-gamma-aminobutyric-acid-gaba)[1](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/articles/22857-gamma-aminobutyric-acid-gaba).

GABA is the most common inhibitory neurotransmitter in your central nervous system. [Inhibitory neurotransmitters prevent or block chemical messages and decrease the stimulation of nerve cells in your brain](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/articles/22857-gamma-aminobutyric-acid-gaba)[1](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/articles/22857-gamma-aminobutyric-acid-gaba)

### coho3131 — 2024-01-30T17:17:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensory-processing-disorder.28545/post-330747>

> [TaylorF said:](/goto/post?id=330710)
>
> I am a special education teacher.
>

> One of the struggles is sensitivity to light, so recommending sunlight will have to be a slow process for a lot of these kids.
>
> Any thoughts greatly appreciated!

Dear Freezers,

do you have experience with cools vests? I don't have room for the amounts of ice necessary in my freezer. I used to have just one big block of ice, which I put on my chest and back with a small backpack (double-bagged). I alternated between front and back. I do exercise every 45 min, when I work sitting (e.g. 50 kettlebell swings). So, I need to be able to remove the ice within a couple of seconds.

I am flirting with a simple cooling vest, but am not sure if the normal cooling packs have enough capacity.

So, what is your take on cooling vests.

Live long and prosper
Sascha

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-01T16:57:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/t-cell-immunity-stimulation-with-the-sun.28550/post-330819>

T cells, it turns out, are intrinsically photosensitive, and when they manage to catch some rays, they react by moving more quickly, potentially enhancing their ability to do their work, which is to travel to infection sites and mount orchestrate an immune response

1\. [https://gumc.georgetown.edu/news-re...efit-it-energizes-infection-fighting-t-cells/](https://gumc.georgetown.edu/news-release/sunlight-offers-surprise-benefit-it-energizes-infection-fighting-t-cells/) (2016)
2\. [https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/6223071/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/6223071/) (1983) Yet we keep believing dermatologists bullshit

Full spectrum sunlight creates the ROS that is hydrogen peroxide in T cells, which makes the cells move. The blue red balance is key to the H202 creation. And we know that an immune response also uses hydrogen peroxide to make T cells move to the damage. The UV light from the pathogen is the GPS of the infection site. The UV light in the sun limits the H202 production so autoimmunity does not happen

Hi All.. So I have read that- _Remember with seafood try to combine with mono-saturated fats because saturated fats like butter can hinder DHA absorption into the brain_.

In the Epi Paleo Rx it states -

## Fats: (in order of Optimal)​

**1\. Spring and summer:** [Coconut oil](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001EO5Q64/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B001EO5Q64&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20), ghee, palm oil, duck fat, beef tallow, bacon fat, duck fat, pastured butter if there are no medical issue precluding its use, olive or avocado oils for salads, macadamia nut oils for mayonaise, raw cream if there are no contraindications\* **_When you eat seafood try to use MUFA’s as the added fat._

2\. Fall and Winter:** ghee, pastured butter, duck fat, beef tallow, bacon fat, non hydrogenated lard, raw cream\* Stick to animal fats in colder months. **_When you eat seafood try to use MUFA’s as the added fat.\*_**

But my question is in winter, what mufas are ideal? Since they are mostly listed as spring - summer seasonal fats? What do yall do ? Or simpy up your sat fat in winter with no seafood protein as the Epi Paleo Rx states, and just not add any extra fat with seafood?

that tells our body to shut down, lover bod temperature, get ready to hibernate.

### JanSz — 2024-03-05T15:32:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-fat-in-winter.28552/post-332228>

Work on escribing how excessive seed oils in foods and linoleic acid in them pushes mammals **and humans eating it** toward hibernation process.

Do not concentrate on hibernation process. I am interested in the time right before hibernation. Time when mammals are getting ready to hibernate. Usually end of summer mostly during fall when the actual hibernation happens in the winter.
\--------------

During the pre-hibernation period, mammals undergo significant dietary and physiological changes to prepare for the long winter months. [This period is characterized by increased food intake, often shifting towards a diet rich in fats](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00360-012-0689-0)[1](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00360-012-0689-0)[2](https://www.open.edu/openlearn/nature-environment/natural-history/surviving-the-winter/content-section-1.4.4)[3](https://mdpi-res.com/d_attachment/metabolites/metabolites-14-00134/article_deploy/metabolites-14-00134.pdf?version=1708520767).

Seed oils, which are high in linoleic acid, a type of polyunsaturated fatty acid (PUFA), can play a role in this process. [A study on garden dormice found that the fatty acid composition of a pre-hibernation diet can influence the depth and duration of metabolic suppression achieved during hibernation](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full)[4](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full). [**More specifically, a diet high in omega-6 PUFAs (like linoleic acid) relative to omega-3 PUFAs was found to be essential to maximize torpor expression**](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full)**[4](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full).**

[The study also found that the ratio of linoleic acid (omega-6 PUFA) and linolenic acid (omega-3 PUFA) in the pre-hibernation diet influenced stress-inducible cell signaling in the white adipose, brown adipose, and liver tissues of the dormice](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full)[4](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full). [Dormice fed a high linoleic acid diet regulated NFκB signaling in a protective manner in all tissues](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full)[4](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmolb.2020.00097/full).

\===========================

Overall
High fat diet and especially when that fat contains very high Linoleic Acid content
cannot be healthy for humans (that are mammals who do not hibernate).
\-----------------

Overall, before 1900 but more definitely before 1850
human's ratio
Linoleic Acid (O6) to Linolenic Acid (O3)
was around 1:1
some say 4:1
others study parts of human bodies and figuring or finding variations, but still within 1:1 to 4:1 range.
\--
Laboratories and studies of fatty acids came long after 1900.
Laboratories have never seen person with healthy fatty acids.
LabCorp, where I do my analysis assumes healthy ratio of LA/ALA =34
Most peoples I have seen has LA/ALA somewhat slightly over 50.
Maximal ratio I have seen was somewhat over 180.

////

### JanSz — 2024-03-05T18:49:06+0200

How to get out of torpor is another story, and it is more than just losing some body weight.

.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-02T21:35:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriacs-journal-what-would-a-wise-mitochondriac-do.28554/post-330879>

How good is your perception when it comes to healthcare?

Human perception and attention have significant limitations, especially if you cannot think well.

A. Always pay attention to what you're missing in science and in nature when you’re ill. It will give you clues to trends you’ve missed. Our brains have limited mitochondrial capacity because energy is a zero-sum game based on Kleiber's law. More often we will fail to notice important details or events that fall outside our focus. Being aware of our limitations helps us become more vigilant and attentive & curious about what we might be overlooking.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-02T21:37:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriacs-journal-what-would-a-wise-mitochondriac-do.28554/post-330880>

Do you favor multitasking because the modern world fosters the idea that it is something to aspire to?

Try to be careful and cautious with multitasking - Our brains are not built by nature to be efficient at multitasking, and attempting to do so can lead to decreased cognitive performance. Human executive function has signing thermodynamic limits. Most are unaware of this and this is why our species suffers an increased likelihood of making errors. This is the basis of the Dunning-Kruger effect. Recognizing the limitations of multitasking can help us prioritize and allocate our attention more effectively.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-02T21:37:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriacs-journal-what-would-a-wise-mitochondriac-do.28554/post-330881>

What do you expect from your doctors now?

Understand the power of expectations - Our expectations significantly shape our perceptions. The more you expect the more disappointed you’ll be if your expectations for someone or something falls short. They can lead us to see what we expect to see, even when the reality is different. Being aware of this bias can help us approach situations with a more open mind and consider alternative perspectives.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-02T21:38:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriacs-journal-what-would-a-wise-mitochondriac-do.28554/post-330882>

Do you realize everything on the internet and Dr. Google has no relation to your specific medical case (n=1)?

Context matters a great deal in science and in medicine because every variable has its own relativity. This makes our perceptions and judgments to become heavily influenced by the context in which they occur. Recognizing the impact of context can help us avoid making snap judgments and consider a broader range of information before forming conclusions in science & medicine.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-03T02:04:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondriacs-journal-what-would-a-wise-mitochondriac-do.28554/post-330900>

Mitochondria are versatile organelles that play central roles in energy metabolism and act as signaling hubs. Changes in the activation state of immune cells are accompanied by, and dependent on, profound alterations in cellular metabolism. Light stimulates mitochondria in cells, especially in immune cells. Cytochrome c oxidase (CcO) mediates the rate-limiting step in mitochondrial respiration.

It is remodeled during development (morphology/aging). In response to changes in oxygen availability in a cell, but within centralized science, very few studies have been done on CcO remodeling during active or chronic inflammation. Blue light causes this type of activation. This CcO complex is the only one within the electron transport chain that contains tissue-specific isoforms of several subunits.

The hypoxia-responsive transcription factor hypoxia-inducible factor-1α (HIF-1α) links directly to the circadian gene regulators. This linkage mediates the interchange of CcO subunits in response to the changing oxygen availability. Light changes oxygen tissues in cells, which is how nocturnal and diurnal animals vary their responses to light. Most people know about the effect of red light on cytochrome C oxidase, but few people know how blue light irradiation causes mitochondrial peptide release that causes a lot of activity. UV light is a brake on the immune response.

UV light is an immunomodulator of mammals' very active blue light stimulus-response. This is why blue light toxicity is the cause of autoimmunity, but it has to be paired with a lack of UV light to get full-blown effects. Consider the effect of blue light radiation on ROS production in our immune cells. Blue light irradiation at low doses (00 mJ cm−2) triggers the mitochondrial synthesis of (ROS) of hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) in T cells. Hydrogen peroxide is a compound that white blood cells release when they sense an infection to kill bacteria and to “call” T cells and other immune cells to mount an immune response. It changes the phenotype of macrophages as well.

Mitochondrial protein C15ORF48 is liberated by blue light irradiation. H2O2 activates a Src kinase/phospholipase C-γ1 (PLC-γ1) signaling pathway, leading to Ca2+ mobilization. Pharmacologic inhibition or genetic disruption of Lck kinase, PLC-γ1, or the T cell receptor complex inhibits light-evoked Ca2+ transients in cells. Expression of C15orf48 is a conserved response to inflammatory signals and occurs in many immune-related pathologies in mammals. Here is the key to remember with respect to mammals. Nocturnal ones and diurnal ones do not react the same in experiments. This is because of how the non-visual photoreceptor network wires differently due to the light they live under. That link is to HIF 1 to the circadian genes. HIF-1 activity is upregulated by one of the important POR2 circadian clock genes. Other target genes of HIF-1 are especially related to angiogenesis, cell proliferation/survival, and glucose/iron metabolism. There is growing evidence that metabolic responses to proinflammatory stimuli differ between human and mouse myeloid cells, suggesting that conclusions based on mouse cells should be extrapolated cautiously.

CITES
Mitochondrial protein C15ORF48 is a stress-independent inducer of autophagy that regulates oxidative stress and autoimmunity | Nature Communications

### [Mitochondrial protein C15ORF48 is a stress-independent inducer of autophagy that regulates oxidative stress and autoimmunity - Nature Communications](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-024-45206-1)

Stress-independent autophagy is less understood than stress-induced autophagy and is important for thymic self-tolerance. Here the authors show that a mitochondrial protein C15ORF48 is important for stress-independent autophagy and alters glutathione metabolism and C15orf48 knockout mice develop...

 www.nature.com

**What is the key lesson for a mitochondriac to know implicitly?**

The Newtonian world was a mechanical world where time was absolute; biological science stagnated because of this perspective. In 1905, we learned time was relative, and it was a game changer for biology that still needs to be fully recognized in 2024.

Newton's mechanical laws that describe objects with ‘simple location in space and time’ utterly failed to represent natural processes. Nature operates as a syncytium. Einstein opened the door that it is only possible to know and understand nature as an organism.

How does this operate?

Eukaryotes are electrodynamic organisms because of how melanin attunes them to the electric plasma in the cosmos. Melanin made water the leading act in this drama.

In the early 2000s, evidence became available that suggested cells are filled with an electromagnetic ﬁeld generated by water. Water is a quantum-coherent liquid crystal that is created in cells. Melanin and water set that stage. Water molecules comprise 70–90% of organisms, depending on their mitochondrial age. Combining melanin and water in eukaryotic cells is essential for complex life to become probable from chaos.

On Earth, water forms quantum coherent domains at ordinary temperatures and pressures. Within eukaryotic organisms, coherent domains stabilize as liquid crystalline water on a massive topological insulator (TI).

Cellular TI's are made from membranes and macromolecular surfaces, effectively aligning the entire body electrically to form a single uniaxial liquid crystal.

This liquid crystalline water makes living probable by enabling mitochondrial proteins and nucleic acids to act as quantum molecular machines that transform and transfer energy at close to 100% efﬁciency in eukaryotes.

It provides excitation energy to split water in animal photosynthesis, releasing oxygen, hydrogen, and a sea of electrons for the teaming millions of air-breathing species on our pale blue dot. Melanin and water joined forces to generate DC electricity for intercommunication and the redox chemistry that powers the entire biosphere.

When you have sex, this is what is happening deep in your loins. Few realize it.

I’d love to see if a few trips to sunny spots could help me recharge my battery, but I don’t want to waste the time, energy and/or money if a few weeks in a sunny locale won’t do much. Maybe there’s no easy answer, it’s try and test. As far as a permanent move, I know that’s not always necessary, but if it is, we need to move once with a family, not uprooting everyone multiple times.

Essentially, I understand what everyone is saying, that it’s on an individual level, that it’s common sense, that labs can help, that it’s getting back to nature that should be the focus. I’m reading a lot and am understanding the principles, and I still have a lot to learn. ### NikkiYork — 2024-02-19T22:09:04+0200

> [Alexandra said:](/goto/post?id=331657)

As far as a permanent move, I know that’s not always necessary, but if it is, we need to move once with a family, not uprooting everyone multiple times.
>
> Essentially, I understand what everyone is saying, that it’s on an individual level, that it’s common sense, that labs can help, that it’s getting back to nature that should be the focus. I’m reading a lot and am understanding the principles, and I still have a lot to learn. At this moment, I’m curious and wish to learn more from Dr. Kruse or other folks how to assess this and how long to plan on visiting somewhere in order to decide whether it’s a good fit….is it two weeks? Is it a summer? That kind of thing. Thanks for any thoughts!

In regards to moving.
I moved to New York without ever visiting and I knew nobody. I was only responsible for myself and I would only do such thing by myself. With a family? No way.

I moved without visiting, because my thought was that visiting would never feel like living in a place. I could hate the visit and then maybe not move.

I still believe that is true.

Jack tells people to visit El Salvador and then they would see, if they want to move.
Well, visiting El Salvador does not make me personally want to move. It’s cool there, but did I fall in love with it? Nope. It makes no difference. If my family moves there, it’s independently from liking or living it there. It is or it would be, because it’s the right thing to do.

I chose the United States as my destination, because I believe in the idea of the founding fathers and beyond. It’s the land of my dreams and the American dream is neither dead to me, nor is it a nightmare. It only has bad parts and that is the fault of cowards.

Unfortunately it’s a bad time in the US right now. To me it still is the best place on earth. If it wasn’t, I would not be here.

It’s not ideal and we tried moving to Hawaii, had a house and all. But it didn’t work out for various reasons, so that was a fail.
I am not ready to give up on my country, but I am ready to possibly have to go into exile.

My grandparents left their forever home over night with 5 dollars to their name, and with no possibility to go back. They revisited 50 or so years later. I can do that, I can leave over night and never return if needed. They fled when nobody could legally get out. I hope this won’t happen here, but I can do what they did. I have it in me.

That said, maybe the immigrants will have to fight for the USA and the OG Americans will need to leave?

I get the biohack of moving lower in latitude. I’ve profited from it twice already.

I will go house hunting in ES soon. I’m still working on the money part. That’s another factor. Our son would love to live where Jack is living (he’s a smart kid), but I ain’t no brain surgeon and neither is my husband

You have a non believer come to your new clinic in El Salvador

Is that a viable option?

When fed chair powell says he's not sure if he'll consider cbdc's seriously it's just in the discussion stage... they've already done 3 successful pilot test projects.
Very real chance this could happen before the 2024 election, all it takes is a big enough "emergency" hack attack/emp, middle east conflict stop terrorist money laundering
IE. Biden passed Executive order 14067 contemplates pursuing a cbdc and regulated all digital assets
Patriot act passed 45 days after 911
Tarp 700B (trouble assets relief program) was passed 18 days after lehmans brother collaspsed
Cares act 2.2Tril 15 days after covid was declared officialy pandemic on a voice vote
Several States have said no to CBDC but all they need to do is rename it with an emergency.
One of the reasons CBDC's failed in nigeria was lack of infrastructure. This wont be a problem in North America.

1\. Project hamilton MIT media lab and federal reserve of boston to replace cash 1.7Million(more 10x the current capacity) transactions per second. Joy Ito funded by epstein changed btc from whitepaper use for peer to peer digital cash (currency) to digital gold/store of value ,wealth preservation. Same author of white paper for Project Hamilton 2020-2022 Technology is a success just figuring out the "legalities"

2\. Project Cedar wholesale cbdc banks for larger volumes, crossborder transactions. Launched after fednow.

3\. Regulated Liability Network (the more boring the name the more deadly) Tracks all cbdc and non cbdc transactions, and tokenized assets. Meaning you could buy something and if your social credit score drops too low they could not allow to you to sell that token.

# Thread: How does your kids skin condition predict future brain function?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-your-kids-skin-condition-predict-future-brain-function.28561/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-04T17:26:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-your-kids-skin-condition-predict-future-brain-function.28561/post-330987>

Question asked to me this AM via DM by a concerned parent will be today's biophysics lesson: "Why is using sunblock on my kids skin afflicted with roscea bad advice. I was told this this week at the peditrician's office. They also tried to cousel us in taking the jab."

Answer: The answer is here in a Patreon blog I wrote years ago about that condition and how sunblock affects it. Also there are implications of this peditricians advice that is harmful to the future development of your kids CNS and PNS. That is a bit more complicated but here is the genesis of how that problem can manifest. Deuterium in humans is kept in the circulatory system and out of the cytosol and mitochondrial organelles by design because it acts as a photosensitizer for the endogenous production of light that assists RBCs in their task of oxygen and CO2 deliver to tissues.

Deuterium is essentially a photosensitizer for the creation of UVA, UVB, and UVC light for RBCs in our circulatory system. It requires surface level UVA light to begin to work. So when you block sunlight with clothing or sunblock you are blocking endogenous light production in the circulatory system for the needs of your RBCs. Moreover, this has collateral affects. It affects oxygen deliver to you colony of mtDNA but it also affects the circadian cycle of your RBCs and can lead to anemia and other blood dyscrasias. This is very easy to assess with an O2 Hb sat % monitor you can buy for 25 bucks on Amazon and a tubr of toxic sunblock or UV blocking shirt above the hand you put the monitir on.

The UV blocking effect in RBCs is registered in peroxiredoxins of the RBCs since adult human RBCs have no mitochondria. Peroxiredoxin-2 (PRDX2) in preventing hydrogen peroxide-induced oxidative stress in the red blood cell. Here is the thread with the full lesson.

[https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1754147699799040292](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1754147699799040292)

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-04T22:50:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blushield-active-emf-cancellation-device-or-fuel-to-the-fire.28562/post-331015>

From Blushield: “When coherent frequencies are available alongside the chaotic ones produced by many non-native EMFs, Blushield says our bodies will preferentially tune into the coherent ones. In other words, the broad spectrum of EMF frequencies in our environment are like channels on a tv – they’re all broadcasting simultaneously, but some channels are static-filled and chaotic and some are peaceful and coherent.”

Ok, so let’s say this device is putting out a “coherent field” (without adding to the electric field from the 60Hz AC circuit it is plugged into) and that a coherent field is healing and our bodies can tune into it. The 60Hz AC field and the 2.4 GHz RF from your cell phone, WiFi etc. are still there and we know they are unaffected by the Blushield Phi because your lights, TV, cell phone etc still work. The damage done to the body by nnEMF‘s is not because we are tuned into them and the damage goes away if we “tune out”, the damage is because they are damaging and we are exposed to them.

I was once told of a study where they exposed some lab critters to nnEMF. One group “A” had a “coherent field” (neutralizer/harmonizer) gizmo of some kind the other group “B” nothing. Group A ran around happy all day. Group B stayed as far away from the nnEMF as they could. Group A lived half as long as Group B . . .

Reduce exposure to harmful things if you want to reduce their harm. These devices do not cancel nnEMF they leave it unchanged.

# Thread: Bunkers, shelters, cabins, basements, exiles, prepping
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bunkers-shelters-cabins-basements-exiles-prepping.28563/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2024-02-04T22:19:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bunkers-shelters-cabins-basements-exiles-prepping.28563/post-331012>

Various forms and shapes of futures are imaginable. Agreeing to Jack, there is no cause and effect, but there are probablities, less and more probable.
Along with probabilities there are also ideals, ideal situations, and less ideal situations.

The latter less ideal futures require prepping in my opinion.

Top idea is exile and exile opportunity, in my opinion options are a good tactic. Clearly people have different strategies of how to maximize an outcome, and diversity is one of thoss strategies, so I would like to apply it to locations as well. As soon as we can get a place in El Salvador, we will.

I don't have the money or even grounds to buy a bunker like the rich are currently building them. The fact that they do is unsettling, if one assumes they didn't just get bored and don't know what else to do with their mullah.

A friend of mine just bought protective suits for herself, her partner, and her cat. And she suggested that I build a metal protective case of some sort in our house. And I said, dang, I was thinking the same thing, I thought about suits also. We compared pictures that we had in our heads, mine lead me to aluminized firefighter suits and she said she had a similar picture in her head. She would think aluminum could be the way. I had done some research and thought of copper mesh for a room.

She and I are quantum entangled in an odd way. We never met, but we have the same input all the time. I know, it's crazy, but whatever. I journaled my crazy diseases, so I might as well share that. She already lives in exile in South America.

Every Californian has a minimum of prepping done, because of the earthquakes, so we are good with that. If anything happenes, I'd prefer to not even be here, but I can't exclude the possibility.
Long story short: What cage or room can be buildt to protect from rays, radiation, frequency, basically waves of all sort? What do you think? The engineers among you, or the people who have an interest?

I found a German company that makes a copper mesh and it's used to shield against x-rays and such. I read in order to protect against waves, you cannot have a tiny hole in the cage, because all the waves come through a tiny hole. Like in a microwave. If the shield has a hole, it all comes through.
So if I would take one room in the house and completely cover it with copper mesh, have a door that can be closed and shape a copper cage, could that work?

Could I sleep with my family in such a room? I'm sorry that it sounds very crazy. I'm not scared, I'm just somebody who hates not bringing an umbrella and getting drenched. If something like that is not needed, it totally makes a cool experiment, so I could invest some time into it at no loss.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-04T23:22:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bunkers-shelters-cabins-basements-exiles-prepping.28563/post-331016>

A Faraday room, depending on how it is made will protect against certain Radio Frequencies. 100% coverage of walls, floor, ceiling, doors and windows is required. Being a metal box/cage it will attract electric fields so must be well grounded. It will not protect against magnetic fields.

You can have NO electric devices, no cell phone RF device etc inside the Faraday room or you will effectively turn it into a “microwave oven” with you inside the oven. If you have any contact with the metal, if you are not insulated from the metal, you make yourself part of the circuit and the electric frequencies attracted by the metal will be going through you as well including any ground affects, dirty electricity etc. AND potentially cut you off from the Schumann resonance.

Wearing a metallic suit will do the same thing, block RF but attract electric fields. Do more research before spending any money.

Plan and prep to carry your own water; don't plan on hiring people to do your tasks. Plan to help your neighbors. Don't live in fear of low probability but horrible shit you can't predict like earthquakes, nukes, solar flares etc. Live joyfully and fearlessly!

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-04T23:37:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mementos-of-truth.28564/post-331017>

There is a brain wash in effect that let’s people forget.

There is a human behavior called “hind site rationalization”. What it means is that people make sense of things that don’t make sense, just so that it fits their picture of what the truth is supposed to be.

Example: under hypnosis people were suggested to open a window, as soon as they were “woken up”. There was no reason for this. None.
Most people would go and open the window as suggested. Upon being asked why they did, they would explain that they were hot or needed air. They readily made up a reason. That’s what people do. And it is a lie of course.

Jack says the worst lies are the ones to yourself and I agree. That’s why I am hoping we can collect things in this threat, that we don’t want to forget. Even if society tells us to.

All my life I am repeating certain things to myself, so that I won’t forget them, or make reality smooth. It has helped me immensely with sticking to the truth.

I will start with a quote I just found on Instagram that I can absolutely verify.

“The Pandemic showed me that most of my neighbors are nazis who are simply awaiting instructions from a Führer & that's a lesson I can never unsee.”

My neighbors and housing managers called the police on me for not wearing a mask at the swimming pool while watching my swimming son. Gladly there was no police available because Santa Monica was looted and burned down, because of George Floyd.
Yeah, that happened also. The police did not stop a single person from looting. These guys just walked out of the stores with the goods. I have seen it with my own eyes. It is the truth.

# Thread: Genomic instability is linked to mitochondrial inflammation signaling
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/genomic-instability-is-linked-to-mitochondrial-inflammation-signaling.28569/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 41
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-06T04:27:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/genomic-instability-is-linked-to-mitochondrial-inflammation-signaling.28569/post-331073>

Genomic instability is linked to inflammation in senescent cells. In mammalian cells, p53 is the protector of the genome. How does p53 operate?

The p53 tumor suppressor functions as a tetrameric transcription factor to regulate hundreds of genes—many in a tissue-specific manner.

mtDNA controls the inflammatory response via ROS, proton motions, and bio-photon production. A mitochondria-regulated molecular circuit connects the p53 tumor suppressor complex and the cytoplasmic chromatin fragments (CCF). The molecular regulation of CCF formation is poorly understood by centralized science because it is an optical switch in cells.

This retrograde circuit is a driver of inflammation through the cGAS-STING pathway.

It turns out that p53 activation occurs when whole spectrum solar light suppresses the formation of cytoplasmic chromatin fragments (CCF) in cells by promoting DNA repair mechanisms. p53 suppression causes CCF accumulation in the cytoplasm, which leads to downstream inflammatory senescence-associated
secretory phenotype (SASP). That opens the door to chronic diseases.

The SASP contributes to chronic disease vulnerability and frailty in aged mammals.

“Senolytic” approaches that target and remove senescent cells are one area of intensive investigation yet to bear fruit for old humans.

The “senomorphic” approach" targets the SASP. There are many known regulators of the SASP, including mTOR, GATA4, p38MAPK and p53. Paradoxically, p53 is a master inducer of senescence and has been reported to be a suppressor of SASP. A molecular explanation of this paradox has been lacking in centralized science.

The SASP is driven by a mitochondria-nucleus retrograde signaling pathway that promotes the expulsion of cytoplasmic chromatin fragments (CCF) from the nucleus to the cytoplasm of senescent cells. Light, exogenous, endogenous, and biophotons from metabolism directly affect the CCF. The CCF is another optical switch that are sensed by the cGAS/STING pathway, which activates the master transcriptional regulator of the SASP, NFkB.

### JanSz — 2024-02-06T19:16:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/genomic-instability-is-linked-to-mitochondrial-inflammation-signaling.28569/post-331092>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=331073)
>
> The molecular regulation of CCF formation is poorly understood by centralized science **because it is an optical switch in cells.**

**how human bodies work using optical switches in their cells**

1.  **Optogenetics: Illuminating Cellular Behavior**:
    -   **Optogenetics** is a powerful technique that allows scientists to control the activity of specific cells using light. It involves introducing light-sensitive proteins (such as opsins) into target cells.
    -   **How It Works**: Opsins are light-gated ion channels or pumps that absorb light at specific wavelengths. [When exposed to light, these channels and pumps respond by opening or closing, regulating the flow of ions into or out of the cell](https://www.addgene.org/guides/optogenetics/)[1](https://www.addgene.org/guides/optogenetics/).
    -   **Application in Humans**: Optogenetics enables precise manipulation of neurons. [By activating or inhibiting specific neurons using light, researchers study brain function, treat neurological disorders, and explore potential therapies for conditions like epilepsy and depression](https://www.addgene.org/guides/optogenetics/)[2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Optogenetics).
2.  **Understanding Cellular Development**:
    -   Researchers at **Princeton University** have developed an innovative tool that combines light and modified proteins to explore cellular development within embryos.
    -   **ERK Pathway**: They focused on the **ERK pathway**, a chain of chemical signals preserved over billions of years of evolution. This pathway plays a crucial role in cell-to-cell communication and exists in nearly every multicellular organism.
    -   **Experimental Approach**: Using a technique called optogenetics, they activated proteins in developing zebrafish embryos with targeted pulses of light. By disrupting the normal sequence of activation, they traced cause-and-effect relationships in cellular behavior during development.
    -   **Application in Humans**: Understanding how cells differentiate into specific tissues (e.g., toenails vs. brain stems) is essential for comprehending diseases and disorders. [Dysregulation of cellular control mechanisms can lead to developmental disorders and even cancer](https://www.addgene.org/guides/optogenetics/)[3](https://engineering.princeton.edu/news/2019/12/04/optical-switch-illuminates-cells-development).
3.  **Photoactivatable Drugs and PDT**:
    -   **Photoactivatable drugs** become active only when exposed to specific light wavelengths. These drugs can selectively target cells or tissues.
    -   **Application in Humans**:
        -   **Photodynamic Therapy (PDT)**: Used in cancer treatment, PDT involves light-activated compounds (photosensitizers) that selectively destroy cancer cells.
        -   Photoactivatable drugs could also be used in antimicrobial therapy and managing age-related macular degeneration.
4.  **Cellular Imaging and Gene Expression Control**:
    -   Optical switches enable real-time imaging of cellular processes. Fluorescent proteins can be switched on or off using light, aiding in diagnostics and treatment monitoring.
    -   Light-controlled gene expression systems allow precise regulation of gene activity.
    -   **Applications in Humans**: Studying gene function, creating synthetic circuits, and potentially correcting genetic mutations.

In summary, optical switches provide a window into the intricate dance of cellular processes. From understanding development to treating diseases, these tools illuminate the hidden workings of our bodies, offering hope for improved health and well-being.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-06T20:15:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/genomic-instability-is-linked-to-mitochondrial-inflammation-signaling.28569/post-331098>

Why do we blame food for things that light causes? Because our scientists have no light controls in any of their experiments is the short answer.

How can seed oils, out of season diets, or nnEMF lead to the same diseases?

The sun shines on the exterior of all things. Darkness shines on the surface of all things once the sun sets. What happens when your missing either one? You ruin the optics of the ERK pathway. That is a basic biophysical pathway in cells. Cell membranes all made from fats and all fats have lipid rafts that are electromagnetic antenna. the sun interacts with them via its electric plasma cathode ray. That ray is unpolarized light. Once the light is assimilated it becomes polarized and then things get radically complex. The **MAPK/ERK pathway** is a chain of [proteins](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Proteins) in the [cell](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cell_\(biology\)) that communicates a light signal from a [receptor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Receptor_\(biochemistry\)) on the membrane surface of the cell and transmits this signal like a game of telephone all the way to the [DNA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DNA) in the nucleus of the cell.

The signal starts with the sun, and when a signaling molecule binds to the receptor on the cell surface and ends when the DNA in the nucleus expresses a protein and produces some change in the cell, such as [cell division](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cell_division). Cell division cannot occur without ultraweka UV light because mitosis needs that frequency of light for this process to occur. The pathway includes many proteins, such as [mitogen-activated protein kinases](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mitogen-activated_protein_kinase) (MAPKs), originally called [extracellular signal-regulated kinases](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Extracellular_signal-regulated_kinases) (ERKs), which communicate by adding phosphate groups to a neighboring protein ([phosphorylating](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Phosphorylation) it), thereby acting as an "on" or "off" switch. Phosphorylation always requires light to be present at some level for it to happen.

That last statement gets me in trouble with biochemists because they do not believe this. But they are wrong.
They believe "photophosphorylation" is light dependent process where as oxidative phosphorylation is light independent process. Sunlight energy is used as external source where as in oxidative phosphorylation energy liberated during ETS is used to generate ATP using ADP and inorganic phosphate. They believe oxidative phosphorylation is independent of light because biochemistry books say during oxidative phosphorylation, electrons derived from NADH and FADH2 combine with O2, and the energy released from these oxidation/ reduction reactions is used to drive the synthesis of ATP from ADP. What they forget is that NADH and FADH2 are capturing electrons and electrons become excited by light. So the light is present on these electrons. Biochemistry never accounts for this energy source. Interestingly enough, biochemistry does know that carbohydrate electrons enter at NADH and fats and protein electrons tend to come in at FADH2. They know there is a difference. But what they do not know is the absorption spectra of both cytochromes is radically different. One is a fluorophore protein and the other one responds best to blue light frequencies.

When one of the proteins in the pathway is mutated, let's say by a high deuterium diet because it is out of season or because you like a seed oil diet, the ERK pathways OPTICAL substrates can become stuck in the "on" or "off" position. This means there is a loss of fidelity in how the antenna in you lipid rafts operate. This electromagnetic pollution can happen in many ways but it is aa necessary step in the development of many chronic diseases like cancer and autoimmunity. In fact, components of the MAPK/ERK pathway were first discovered in cancer cells, and drugs that reverse the optical "on" or "off" switch are being investigated by centralized science as cancer treatments. Few will get to the truth because they do not yet realize that pathway is an optical pathway more so than a biochemical one.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

UV light has effects on mTOR and ERK antenna's in cell membranes.

The other thing people forget is that light radiation has hormetic effect. **Radiation hormesis, though still controversial in centralized science, is actually an old topic. Radiation hormesis to be effective, the light stress must be pulsed; it cannot be chronic. Diurnal variation is the pulse we are talking about. Moreover, light hormesis is most effective when synchronized with circadian time frame windows.

Most biological macromolecules contain nucleophilic centers, so in chemistry electrophiles are particularly reactive and toxic in a biological context. The majority of cellular electrophiles are generated from polyunsaturated fatty acids by a peroxidation chain reaction that is readily triggered by oxygen-centered radicals, but propagates without further input of reactive oxygen species (ROS). In the eye this happens in the Bazan loop but it is tightly coupled to be recycled so electrophiles never build up to destroy melanin in the RPE. But if the Bazan loop is broken then all hell breaks loose. This is why the carivore diet and an inside life with blue light and nnEMF is a form of suicide.

In people who live this way, the lifestyle creates HNE an electrophile from cell membranes. The formation of lipid-derived electrophiles such as 4-hydroxynon-2-enal (4-HNE) is believed to be relatively insensitive to the level of initiating ROS in mitochondria (not true if melanin is missing), but they depend mainly on the availability of peroxidation-susceptible fatty acids liberated from cell membranes.

Light is adjacent to cell membranes, food is not. People forget this constantly. Blue light and nnEMF cause massive release of the major electrophile in mammals, 4-HNE. The food gurus want to tell you PUFA's are the cause of 4-HNE but they are wrong. Light causes it. When opsins are liberated from non visual photoreceptors we see massive spikes in 4-HNE in mammals. This is consistent with numerous observations that life span is inversely correlated to membrane peroxidizability, and with the hypothesis that 4-HNE may constitute a mechanistic link between high susceptibility of membrane lipids to peroxidation and shortened life span.

Why is fish important in strong solar environments? Selenoenzymes can resist [electrophilic](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/biochemistry-genetics-and-molecular-biology/electrophilicity) stress induced by strong UVB light. What happens when you lack selenoproteins and you live an indoor life? This is where chronic diseases come from.

Another study showing a radiation hormesis effect on the cell level is reported in the 2011 publication [The low-dose ionizing radiation stimulates cell proliferation via activation of the MAPK/ERK pathway in rat cultured mesenchymal stem cells](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21436606): “Hormesis induced by low-dose ionizing radiation (LDIR) is often mirrored by its stimulation of cell proliferation. The mitogen-activated protein kinases (MAPK)/ extracellular-signal- regulated kinases (ERK) pathway is known to play important roles in cell growth.**

Hormesis occurs when both positive and negative feedback loops are yoked or connected to some coupler. In cells, it can be the level of hormones or antioxidants and the power of light in the cell codified by the redox potential. The redox potential is the electrical potential between cytochrome 1-oxygen in a cell. When you take exogenous glutathione you alter the redox balance and uncouple the endogenous system. Hormesis effects are lost or extinguished. How? Hormesis is a fine edge between positive and negative feedback loop reactions that ARE ONLY linked by the circadian mechanism in man. This is why people who say chronic fasting or chronic ketosis optimize endogenous systems are DEAD wrong. The same thing is true about taking supplements of things normally made in a cell. Glutathione and Vitamin E being examples here. Hormesis is built by Nature to be a temporary state of being/behavior to help cells cope with stressors. Hormesis only works when the positive feedback loop is properly coupled to the negative feedback control tied to light and dark cycles. When the coupling mechanism is not present, hormesis becomes impossible and extinction for both sides of the feedback loop occur and the labs show flatlining of the substance in question. This is why so many people's glutathiones drop to undetectable when they take exogenous glutathione.

In the brain and adrenal gland, Vitamin C has to be changed to the oxidized version called dehydroascorbate (DHA) to cross the blood-brain and cervical cord barrier to gain entrance to protect the tissues and maintain the levels of cortisol, norepinephrine, epinephrine, and dopamine. All of the amines are depleted by oxidative stress. Taking Vitamin E and glutathione does nothing to protect those stores. Decreased recycling of dehydroascorbate (DHA), the oxidized form of vitamin C) to vitamin C may further contribute to low plasma vitamin C levels post-EMF or organ damage due to the altered redox state of the colony of mitochondria in that organ.

### [Long-term exposure of INS-1 rat insulinoma cells to linoleic acid and glucose in vitro affects cell viability and function through mitochondrial-mediated pathways - Endocrine](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s12020-010-9432-3)

Obesity with excessive levels of circulating free fatty acids (FFAs) is tightly linked to the incidence of type 2 diabetes. Insulin resistance of peripheral tissues and pancreatic β-cell dysfunction are two major pathological changes in diabetes and both are facilitated by excessive levels of...

 link.springer.com

The results of this study indicate that **chronic **exposure to linoleic acid****\-induced β-cell dysfunction and apoptosis, which involved a mitochondrial-mediated signal pathway, and increased glucose levels enhanced linoleic acid-induced β-cell dysfunction.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-07T04:53:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/genomic-instability-is-linked-to-mitochondrial-inflammation-signaling.28569/post-331121>

Outdoor UVA vs Indoor UVA Authors suspect that outdoor UVB protects DNA from damage of outdoor UVA. Also, UVA seems to play a role re: melanin and a-MSH production Indoor UVA (as UVB gets blocked by windows) is isolated, and may cause harm.

UV light has effects on mTOR and ERK antenna's in cell membranes.

The other thing people forget is that light radiation has hormetic effect. **Radiation hormesis, though still controversial in centralized science, is actually an old topic. Radiation hormesis to be effective, the light stress must be pulsed; it cannot be chronic. Diurnal variation is the pulse we are talking about. Moreover, light hormesis is most effective when synchronized with circadian time frame windows.

Most biological macromolecules contain nucleophilic centers, so in chemistry electrophiles are particularly reactive and toxic in a biological context. The majority of cellular electrophiles are generated from polyunsaturated fatty acids by a peroxidation chain reaction that is readily triggered by oxygen-centered radicals, but propagates without further input of reactive oxygen species (ROS). In the eye this happens in the Bazan loop but it is tightly coupled to be recycled so electrophiles never build up to destroy melanin in the RPE. But if the Bazan loop is broken then all hell breaks loose. This is why the carivore diet and an inside life with blue light and nnEMF is a form of suicide.

In people who live this way, the lifestyle creates HNE an electrophile from cell membranes. The formation of lipid-derived electrophiles such as 4-hydroxynon-2-enal (4-HNE) is believed to be relatively insensitive to the level of initiating ROS in mitochondria (not true if melanin is missing), but they depend mainly on the availability of peroxidation-susceptible fatty acids liberated from cell membranes.

Light is adjacent to cell membranes, food is not. People forget this constantly. Blue light and nnEMF cause massive release of the major electrophile in mammals, 4-HNE. The food gurus want to tell you PUFA's are the cause of 4-HNE but they are wrong. Light causes it. When opsins are liberated from non visual photoreceptors we see massive spikes in 4-HNE in mammals. This is consistent with numerous observations that life span is inversely correlated to membrane peroxidizability, and with the hypothesis that 4-HNE may constitute a mechanistic link between high susceptibility of membrane lipids to peroxidation and shortened life span.

Why is fish important in strong solar environments? Selenoenzymes can resist [electrophilic](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/biochemistry-genetics-and-molecular-biology/electrophilicity) stress induced by strong UVB light. What happens when you lack selenoproteins and you live an indoor life? This is where chronic diseases come from.

Another study showing a radiation hormesis effect on the cell level is reported in the 2011 publication [The low-dose ionizing radiation stimulates cell proliferation via activation of the MAPK/ERK pathway in rat cultured mesenchymal stem cells](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21436606): “Hormesis induced by low-dose ionizing radiation (LDIR) is often mirrored by its stimulation of cell proliferation. The mitogen-activated protein kinases (MAPK)/ extracellular-signal- regulated kinases (ERK) pathway is known to play important roles in cell growth.**

Hormesis occurs when both positive and negative feedback loops are yoked or connected to some coupler. In cells, it can be the level of hormones or antioxidants and the power of light in the cell codified by the redox potential. The redox potential is the electrical potential between cytochrome 1-oxygen in a cell. When you take exogenous glutathione you alter the redox balance and uncouple the endogenous system. Hormesis effects are lost or extinguished. How? Hormesis is a fine edge between positive and negative feedback loop reactions that ARE ONLY linked by the circadian mechanism in man. This is why people who say chronic fasting or chronic ketosis optimize endogenous systems are DEAD wrong. The same thing is true about taking supplements of things normally made in a cell. Glutathione and Vitamin E being examples here. Hormesis is built by Nature to be a temporary state of being/behavior to help cells cope with stressors. Hormesis only works when the positive feedback loop is properly coupled to the negative feedback control tied to light and dark cycles. When the coupling mechanism is not present, hormesis becomes impossible and extinction for both sides of the feedback loop occur and the labs show flatlining of the substance in question. This is why so many people's glutathiones drop to undetectable when they take exogenous glutathione.

In the brain and adrenal gland, Vitamin C has to be changed to the oxidized version called dehydroascorbate (DHA) to cross the blood-brain and cervical cord barrier to gain entrance to protect the tissues and maintain the levels of cortisol, norepinephrine, epinephrine, and dopamine. All of the amines are depleted by oxidative stress. Taking Vitamin E and glutathione does nothing to protect those stores. Decreased recycling of dehydroascorbate (DHA), the oxidized form of vitamin C) to vitamin C may further contribute to low plasma vitamin C levels post-EMF or organ damage due to the altered redox state of the colony of mitochondria in that organ.
