# Thread: How the charge state of proteins leads to clotting.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-the-charge-state-of-proteins-leads-to-clotting.28582/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-08T02:01:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-the-charge-state-of-proteins-leads-to-clotting.28582/post-331190>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Negatively charged liquid nanoparticles (LNPs) can cause clots because of charge variation changes indiced in Factor XII Protease. Factor XII (FXII) autoactivates by contact with a variety of artificial or biologic negatively charged surfaces (contact activation), resulting in blood coagulation and activation of the inflammatory kallikrein-kinin and complement systems. Factor XII is a protein involved in the blood coagulation cascade--clotting. Factor XII, also known as Hageman factor, is a protein involved in the blood coagulation pathway. Under normal circumstances, Factor XII circulates in an inactive form in the blood. But when it comes into contact with negatively charged surfaces such as LNPs,, it can undergo a conformational change that activates it. This is referred to as autoactivation.

Because Factor XII can activate itself without a need for other clotting factors to initiate the process. Factor XII initiates a series of reactions that form a blood clot via the conversion of other clotting factors, like Factor XI and Factor IX, creating fibrin, which stabilizes the clot. The kallikrein-kinin system is a part of the inflammatory response. Activation of Factor XII creates kallikrein, an enzyme that produces bradykinin, a potent vasodilator, meaning it causes blood vessels to widen, increasing blood flow to the affected area. It also increases vascular permeability, allowing immune cells and other substances to enter the tissue more easily.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-08T02:04:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-the-charge-state-of-proteins-leads-to-clotting.28582/post-331191>

Vascular permeability of this nature can result in vasculitis, and damage and a remodeling of the inside of the blood vessels.

First, the charge on the inner lining (intima) of the blood vessels (arteries, veins, AND in the heart), not only changed from having a negative surface charge, but to more positive. Also, when inflamed, the intima was remodeled! It went from looking like this: (see gap below?)

This will happen with positively charged LNPs too. "A Positively Charged Surface Triggers Coagulation Activation Through Factor VII Activating Protease (FSAP)"

[https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/acsami.7b14281](https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/acsami.7b14281)

After three hours, coagulation was seen (clotting) on multiple positively charged surfaces using whole blood. Factor XIIa (activated Factor XII) and kallikrein are both enzymes involved in the coagulation cascade and the inflammatory response.

Factor XIIa is activated when Factor XII comes into contact with certain surfaces, while kallikrein is activated through various pathways, including the contact activation pathway. In this study, they found the pathway was activated by contact with positive charges. Pfizer measured the lipid nanoparticle to have a zeta potential of - 3 millivolts, which is almost close to zero, or what one would call neutral. RNA has a negative charge. DNA has a negative charge (high!). Cationic lipids in the LNP have a positive charge.

# Thread: I am looking for cancer advice to help my mother...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-looking-for-cancer-advice-to-help-my-mother.28584/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AlexanderWhite — 2024-02-08T18:59:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-looking-for-cancer-advice-to-help-my-mother.28584/post-331223>

Just a few days ago, my mother was diagnosed with stage 3 cancer in a lymph node near her pelvis, which was discovered during a one-month post-op checkup after a hysterectomy on Jan 10th. She previously had a small polyp on her kidney about a decade ago that was found early and removed with no side effects and no chemotreatments required afterward.

Apparently, this is aggressive, so the doctors are opting to start chemo treatments right away next Monday morning (before defining where else the cancer exists) and then ongoing treatments for the next 5 months once every three weeks.

She is a 65-year-old white German/Irish female in Ohio who is an overweight smoker and lives a generally sedentary lifestyle as an elementary school librarian with otherwise "healthy" biomarkers, according to her medical team (I guess we have very different standards for "healthy").

At this point, I am unsure what questions to ask and where to start, so I am looking for insights from this community.

What foods, tests, habits, medications, or supplements should we consider along this journey?

**For example:**
Should she remove meat and sugar from her diet?
Should she get a PET scan to identify where there may be other tumors?
Should she start RED light treatments at home?
What can we do to mitigate the discomfort of chemo?
What red flags should we watch out for from standard medicine now?

Any advice or direction towards resources is welcomed for consideration. There is SO much out there, and it's all very overwhelming.

Thank you

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-02-08T20:50:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-looking-for-cancer-advice-to-help-my-mother.28584/post-331230>

2 most important things her body needs:

\-Total darkness at night (to optimize melatonin, autophagy and apoptosis)
\-Maximal sunlight during the day (into eyes and onto skin, ideally the area of skin closest to the cancerous tissue)

**She needs to live every possible waking moment outdoors if she wants to reverse the root underlying issues.**

Realize that every moment spent inside of a hospital will only make matters worse (bc of blue light + other nnEMFs, and lack of IR + UV). She needs to limit time inside the hospital as much as possible.

I would also drop carbs like a hot potato. Especially this time of year in her location.

Carbs feed cancer and speed up its proliferation, especially when/where there is a lack of UV

Don't shrug off the part about darkness. It is VERY important to cancer reversal!

>
> Just a few days ago, my mother was diagnosed with stage 3 cancer in a lymph node near her pelvis, which was discovered during a one-month post-op checkup after a hysterectomy on Jan 10th. She previously had a small polyp on her kidney about a decade ago that was found early and removed with no side effects and no chemotreatments required afterward.
>
> Apparently, this is aggressive, so the doctors are opting to start chemo treatments right away next Monday morning (before defining where else the cancer exists) and then ongoing treatments for the next 5 months once every three weeks.
>
> She is a 65-year-old white German/Irish female in Ohio who is an overweight smoker and lives a generally sedentary lifestyle as an elementary school librarian with otherwise "healthy" biomarkers, according to her medical team (I guess we have very different standards for "healthy").
>
> At this point, I am unsure what questions to ask and where to start, so I am looking for insights from this community.
>
> What foods, tests, habits, medications, or supplements should we consider along this journey?
>
> **For example:**
> Should she remove meat and sugar from her diet?
> Should she get a PET scan to identify where there may be other tumors?
> Should she start RED light treatments at home?
> What can we do to mitigate the discomfort of chemo?
> What red flags should we watch out for from standard medicine now?
>
>
> Any advice or direction towards resources is welcomed for consideration. There is SO much out there, and it's all very overwhelming.
>
> Thank you

About radiation therapy.
There are gamma radiations.
There are proton radiations.
\----
When irradiating you want the least damage to healthy tissue.
So, when directing radiation, the stream of it is the smallest possibly.
but
Gamma goes always thru the whole body, first thru healthy part, then target, but then it still goes thru until it leaves body.

Proton radiation depth can be adjusted, so it ends at the target.

If possible, get proton radiation.

////

# Thread: Change of jobs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/change-of-jobs.28585/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### giedremail — 2024-02-08T19:36:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/change-of-jobs.28585/post-331226>

I am moving to another country and don't speak the language yet. I would love to do a job without PC.
Anyone has done that? Would love to hear your experiences. Thanks

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-09T02:18:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/change-of-jobs.28585/post-331248>

I moved to the US without speaking great English. I started working for a German company. Did not like it, only did it to survive.

And then I opened my own company repairing electronics and hired people who came from all over the place. I hired Mexicans, Russians, Chinese, Polish… Americans, no Germans though, hahaha (I’m German).

So there you go. Work for a US company! Or start one. Or a Swedish company. You are Swedish, right? Sorry, if it was another nationality, you know what I mean though.

People don’t even hire cleaning ladies who don’t speak English, why would that be different in another country? There needs to be some understanding.

In South American countries - in case that’s where you are going - they are generally not happy, if you take away the job of somebody local. That is something to consider as well. Possibly it’s like that everywhere. But you could hire locals as long as they understand you.

So it’s probably working for yourself/opening your own thing.

My experience is, that nobody is waiting for you to show up anywhere, and especially not, if you don’t qualify for something, like you lack a language ability. So you need to do it yourself, it‘s more fun anyways

### markthesunbather — 2024-03-13T20:21:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/change-of-jobs.28585/post-332519>

I'd love to do a job \*with\* a (Iris software installed) PC if it's (remote based) data entry work.

I am very fluent in English, and I'm probably going to wind up teaching it on one of these platforms like Verbling or italki if something else doesn't pan out soon.

# Thread: What will Apple's new VR glasses likely do to humans who use it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-will-apples-new-vr-glasses-likely-do-to-humans-who-use-it.28591/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-09T21:30:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-will-apples-new-vr-glasses-likely-do-to-humans-who-use-it.28591/post-331284>

CPC 73 says it all.

Cephalopods have been on earth from the cambrian explosion. What wisdom do they have for us?

Even cephalopods know what most humans do not. Light at night is not good. In fact, it is the fast track to chronic neolithic diseases like cancer.

[https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1755714067971379631](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1755714067971379631)

Cephalopods predate bivalaves on our pale blue dot. Oysters, clams, and mussels are bivalves that the census takers of our pale blue dot. All their relatives are not only featured for dinner at my house often but they form one of th most abundant components of the Earth's fossil record. Google Dave Jablonski sometime. They are common in ancient lakes, streams, oceans, and rivers everywhere on EARTH. Bivalves species fill the cabinets of paleontological collections world wide. They have been on the planet for 500 million years. 85 million years after the Cambrian explosion. Realized that cephalopods are older then they are.

Bivalves remain the most nutrient dense food stuff on Earth for mammals. Their evolutionary history makes them the ideal laboratory to test theories on how species diversity changes through time. If you eat a lot of them it will also lead to an extension of your own life by giving you time because of how many electrons they have. [http://www.vox.com/.../11352354/oysters-environment-farming](https://www.vox.com/2016/4/6/11352354/oysters-environment-farming?fbclid=IwAR209-A26p_-DM7Put-3uRLywvzFRuDEC44Du1szQXMtbF0d4KfRnOC-Jfg)

# Thread: The deuterium content of tilapia growing in southern China
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-deuterium-content-of-tilapia-growing-in-southern-china.28593/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### budaev — 2024-02-11T01:34:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-deuterium-content-of-tilapia-growing-in-southern-china.28593/post-331317>

Some low latitude tilapia fish have deuterium levels as high as 171 ppm.Does it mean that some low latitude fish and meat are not suitable for consumption, even in the local area.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Marko Pollo — 2024-02-11T18:29:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-deuterium-content-of-tilapia-growing-in-southern-china.28593/post-331331>

Think of possible water sources in Southern China or anywhere else:
1\. An underground cavern that has collected from evaporated precipitation or snow/ice melt over time could possibly have light water and should be tested.
2\. Rain water that evaporated from the local environment should be in the middle ground and should be tested.
3\. Water in rivers and local environment that is making its way to the sea slowly, evaporating constantly, leaving heavier water. Need to test to make assumptions.

These 3 water sources can vary greatly. Now think of the food the tilapia eat. Water sources, soil type, fertilization, plant variety all can affect D levels in the fish food and hence the fish.

My point is that D levels can vary greatly within a distinct geographic area and without testing individual samples, we are just speculating.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-11T20:25:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-deuterium-content-of-tilapia-growing-in-southern-china.28593/post-331338>

I’ve seen photos of tilapia farms in China . . . the workers were in full hazmat suits. I got the impression deuterium content is the least of your worries. I would never eat/drink anything knowingly that came from China.

# Thread: Kina's journal - in need of some encouragement and support
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kinas-journal-in-need-of-some-encouragement-and-support.28596/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KinaBibiDiop — 2024-02-11T17:37:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kinas-journal-in-need-of-some-encouragement-and-support.28596/post-331330>

Hello everyone.

Here is my story:

I am a 38 year old Mauritian woman. I was diagnosed with triple negative breast cancer in 2017 and since then I have been trying to heal my body and mind back to optimal health. The journey has been hard but I cannot give up.

I had to undergo many rounds of chemo (a combination of TAXOTERE, ADRIAMYCIN and CYCLOPHOSPHAMIDE). I still to this day suffer from alopecia. My hair is still very thin and sparse, same for eyelashes and eyebrows and body hair (had a full head of healthy curls, thick eyebrows and eyelashes prior to chemo). I also went through several rounds of radiation and I had several lumpectomies as well.

My twin sister got diagnosed with exact same cancer a year after me. My mother, who has now passed, had breast cancer twice and died of metastatic ovarian cancer (a lot of my mother's sisters and my female cousins have had either breast or ovarian cancer).

Throughout my healing journey I have tried many things :
\- GAPS diet (to heal the gut) - 8 months
\- carnivore diet - 1 year
\- the root cause protocol - 1 year
\- Jane McLelland's anti cancer protocol (using off-label drugs like metformin, doxycycline, aspirin, and statins to block cancer growing pathways) - 6 months
\- various detox protocols including frequent blood donations, daily coffee enemas - 2 years
\- the copper revolution protocol (copper and co-factors supplementation) - 2 years
\- supplementation (copper, zinc, diatomaceous earth, boron, magnesium, iodine, ....) to address deficiencies and to detox.....very hard sometimes - 2 years

I cannot say for sure if these protocols have helped me but I learnt a lot along the way and I continue to learn everyday. These protocols might have harmed my body too: at some point I lost so much weight with all the detox I was doing that I had to stop everything or else I think my body would have shut down...Also, using the off-label pharmaceutical drugs to prevent cancer recurrence must have definitely wreaked havoc in my body.

My current health issues/concerns/symptoms are:

\- bad sleep/trouble falling asleep/indomnia/night wakings
\- energy crashes daily after meals/fatigue/low energy
\- digestive issues (SEVERE CONSTIPATION - cannot poop for days in a row, only enema helps, bloating after eating, lots of burping)
\- dry skin/dark circles (lack of sleep? liver issues?)
\- thin hair, eyebrows, eyelashes (hasn't resolved since cancer treatment)
\- dull and tired eyes

The positive side is that I have a good appetite, good mental health, stable weight (63kg, 1m68).

I am motivated to heal more and more, I am hopeful as ever even after 7 years on this journey.

I am grateful to have found this community.

It's been 30 days since I have stopped all supplementation and detox protocols. I have been watching sunrises daily for a 30 days non-stop. Caught many sunsets as well. Have done CT every other day in the freezing cold sea water. I now eat an epi-paleo diet with oysters everyday. I stay outside in the sun during the day as much as possible and wear blue light blockers and turn off ALL artificial light after sunset and limit EMF during the day.

I cannot say for sure that I have seen benefits in those 30 days but I can feel something good is coming.

I would love to have some support and encouragement from you.

Thanks for reading.

### Brent Patrick — 2024-02-15T08:09:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-has-the-recipe-correct-why-seafood-trumps-fish-oil.28597/post-331465>

## 6\. Transfer to the Brain​

"Investigations into photo-transduction have not included any role for the DHA, electron-rich membrane structure surrounding rhodopsin and other opsins. Based on this non-classical behavior described above, it is plausible that a quantum field is responsible for energy transfer from the isomerization to the DHA surrounding rhodopsin. The idea of using quantum field theory in brain function is not new \[[32](https://www.mdpi.com/1099-4300/25/11/1520#B32-entropy-25-01520)\].
On the other hand, **the idea is that the isomerization energy surplus is released as heat, which is vibrational energy that is quantized.** With electronic/vibronic coherence already demonstrated, we have a novel and plausible mechanism for the quantized transfer of the isomerization energy to DHA"

## 9\. Summary and Conclusions: DHA as a Photon-Energy-Electron Transducer​

"In this paper, we have set out the limitations of the current model of photo-transduction and provided experimental evidence to support a previously unrecognized key role for DHA in the process. In this revision to the process, the quantum of energy from the photon-induced cis–trans isomerization of retinal is absorbed by a DHA π-electron. **Hyperpolarization** **extracts the energized electron,** which then transmits its information to the brain, **conserving the fidelity** of the original wavelength. **This hypothesis provides a clear rationale to support the observations of the extraordinary high density of DHA and the presence of very long-chain omega-3 PUFAs surrounding the opsins, which serve to absorb the energy, and DHA acts as the photon-energy transducer sending visual information to the brain for image reconstruction in our consciousness.**
In so doing, this thesis provides an explanation for the previously unresolved issues around the speed of information transfer and the purity of the conservation of a photon’s wavelength. It also explains the extraordinary concentration of the hexaenoic motive in the lipids adjacent to and attached to rhodopsin. This relationship has been conserved throughout the evolution of vertebrate species \[[41](https://www.mdpi.com/1099-4300/25/11/1520#B41-entropy-25-01520)\]. Our proposal that DHA acts as a **photon-electron transducer** for visual reception places DHA in an unassailable position at the center of visual signaling, and on a par with vitamin A"

### [Docosahexaenoic Acid Explains the Unexplained in Visual Transduction](https://www.mdpi.com/1099-4300/25/11/1520)

In George Wald’s Nobel Prize acceptance speech for “discoveries concerning the primary physiological and chemical visual processes in the eye”, he noted that events after the activation of rhodopsin are too slow to explain visual reception. Photoreceptor membrane phosphoglycerides contain...

 www.mdpi.com

Hyperpolarization is when the **membrane potential becomes more negative at a particular spot on the neuron's membrane.**

### Brent Patrick — 2024-02-17T05:03:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-has-the-recipe-correct-why-seafood-trumps-fish-oil.28597/post-331536>

"Despite the myriad influences of DHA on AD neuropathological mechanisms, **and the association of greater fish intake with a reduced risk of AD** at an epidemiological level, **manipulation of dietary DHA intake by fish oil supplementation has had little consistent effect to prevent or ameliorate AD** \[[30](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B30-ijms-22-11826)\]. More recently, the understanding that different dietary and plasma DHA forms have specific metabolic fates affecting distribution into tissues may help to explain why **fish oil supplementation does not provide the beneficial effects expected from pre-clinical and epidemiological evidence.** It has been argued that the brain has limited uptake of triglyceride (TG)-DHA found in fish oil supplements, **instead favoring DHA in lysophospholipid forms, such as lysophosphatidylcholine (LPC), derived from dietary phospholipid** (PL) \[[31](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B31-ijms-22-11826),[32](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B32-ijms-22-11826),[33](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B33-ijms-22-11826),[34](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B34-ijms-22-11826)\]. Lysophosphatidylcholine-DHA (LPC-DHA) enters the brain via the Mfsd2a transporter, a selective transporter of lysophospholipid-bound fatty acids \[[35](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B35-ijms-22-11826)\].

**Higher dietary N3 PUFA intake** has been associated with **fewer cerebral infarcts** \[[58](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B58-ijms-22-11826)\] as well as **a reduction in the risk of age-related decline in verbal fluency**, with particular benefit to those with hypertension \[[55](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B55-ijms-22-11826)\]. **DHA** therefore has **wide peripheral effects** that may also **indirectly reduce the risk of neurodegenerative disease"**

"Meta-analyses have found **no benefit of N3 supplementation to prevent cognitive decline** in cognitively normal elderly \[[89](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B89-ijms-22-11826)\] **or in the prevention and treatment of AD** \[[90](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B90-ijms-22-11826)\]. Post hoc analysis of cross-sectional studies by Cunnane et al. found no difference in blood DHA values between individuals with AD or cognitive decline and controls \[[91](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826#B91-ijms-22-11826)\]."

### [Why Have the Benefits of DHA Not Been Borne Out in the Treatment and Prevention of Alzheimer’s Disease? A Narrative Review Focused on DHA Metabolism and Adipose Tissue](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/22/21/11826)

Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA), an omega-3 fatty acid rich in seafood, is linked to Alzheimer’s Disease via strong epidemiological and pre-clinical evidence, yet fish oil or other DHA supplementation has not consistently shown benefit to the prevention or treatment of Alzheimer’s Disease...

 www.mdpi.com

### [Brain Gut 6: Epi-Paleo Rx](https://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/)

Readers Summary: How should we consider eating our diets? What is the Epi-Paleo Rx? Why your brain health is linked to ultimate health. This blog followed a very popular July 2012 Webinar that I put on for my members a few days ago. The webinar was over two hours long and had a lot of

### Brent Patrick — 2024-03-17T03:36:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-has-the-recipe-correct-why-seafood-trumps-fish-oil.28597/post-332604>

### Potential mechanisms of macular degeneration protection by fatty fish consumption.​

Fatty fish have a protective effect on AMD and **DHA/EPA fish oil supplements do not**. Perhaps this can be tempered with the notion that there are multiple pathways and that we do not know the ones in effect. It is assumed that there is some synergistic effect of the diet of fish and we just do no know if there is one particular component or the combination of different nutritional components that are important for this protective effect. In a [randomized controlled trial](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/neuroscience/randomized-controlled-trial), **Johnson et al. found that DHA promoted the accumulation of the [carotenoid](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/neuroscience/carotenoid) [lutein](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/pharmacology-toxicology-and-pharmaceutical-science/xanthophyll) in the blood and in the macula \[[49](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054#bib49)\]. Lutein, which is widely found in fruits and vegetables, was associated with a 37% reduction in the risk of developing late-stage AMD \[[50](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054#bib50)\].** Fish itself is also rich in nutrients such as [vitamin B6](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/neuroscience/pyridoxine), which inhibits the activation of hypoxia-inducible factor (HIF) and significantly inhibits [retinal neovascularization](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/pharmacology-toxicology-and-pharmaceutical-science/retina-neovascularization) \[[51](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054#bib51)\], and the nutrients in fish may have a synergistic effect in delaying the onset and progression of AMD.

Omega-6 LC-PUFA, which comes mainly from vegetable oils and animal fats, has been reported to promote inflammation. Omega-3 LC-PUFA, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties \[[47](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054#bib47)\], and **Omega-3 LC-PUFA can provide protection by replacing Omega-6 LC-PUFA \[[36](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054#bib36)\]

More Omega 3's not less!**

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1471489222000054)

### Brent Patrick — 2024-03-18T22:55:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-has-the-recipe-correct-why-seafood-trumps-fish-oil.28597/post-332644>

### Docosahexaenoic Acid and the Aging Brain​

[Walter J. Lukiw](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=Lukiw%20WJ%5BAuthor%5D)\* and [Nicolas G. Bazan](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=Bazan%20NG%5BAuthor%5D)

DHA is an absolute requirement for the development of the human central nervous system (CNS), and the continuous maintenance of brain cell function, illustrating the strong mechanistic link between an adequate supply of essential PUFA in the diet and the sustenance of cognitive health.

Changes in the redox balance of brain cells, modulated in part by bioavailable antioxidants, may further affect the kinetics of these DHA-processing systems ([19](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib19)–[23](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib23)). For example, **the overall bioavailabilty of DHA may be in part dependent on the redox state of brain cells,** and the supplementation of DHA with antioxidants such as antioxidant carotenoids has been shown to significantly improve cognitive abilities in elderly populations

. The chronic nature of AD suggests that brain survival factors are progressively diminished or exhausted over decades of life as self-perpetuating neuropathology slowly takes over and spreads throughout the neocortex ([38](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib38),[39](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib39)). Part of this pathology promotes an up-regulation in the expression of apoptotic factors coupled to decreases in the expression of antiapoptotic members of the Bcl-2 gene family ([6](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib6),[39](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib39)–[43](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib43)). Unlike cholesterol and HF-C dietary intake, **DHA and DHA-derived NPD1 decrease the rate of A_β_ peptide generation and shedding from brain cells and diminish A_β_ peptide-mediated proinflammatory, proapoptotic, and pathogenic consequences** ([6](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib6),[16](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib16),[19](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib19),[43](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/#bib43))

### [Docosahexaenoic Acid and the Aging Brain](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2666388/)

The dietary essential PUFA docosahexaenoic acid \[DHA; 22:6(n-3)\] is a critical contributor to cell structure and function in the nervous system, and deficits in DHA abundance are associated with cognitive decline during aging and in neurodegenerative ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### Brent Patrick — 2024-03-18T23:05:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-has-the-recipe-correct-why-seafood-trumps-fish-oil.28597/post-332645>

### Neuroprotectin D1-mediated anti-inflammatory and survival signaling in stroke, retinal degenerations, and Alzheimer's disease​

Bazan Nicolas G.

"The name “neuroprotectin D1” was suggested based upon its neuroprotective bioactivity in [BIR](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/biochemistry-genetics-and-molecular-biology/reperfusion) and oxidatively stressed retinal pigment epithelial (RPE) cells, as well as its potent ability to inactivate proapoptotic and proinflammatory signaling. **“D1” refers to it being the first identified mediator derived from DHA"**

Specific intercellular trafficking assures the retention/conservation of DHA ([17](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022227520306465#bib17)). **The liver takes up DHA and linolenic acid (18:3,n-3) from the diet and elongates and desaturates linolenic acid to DHA;** then DHA is esterified into phospholipids, secreted as lipoproteins ([18](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022227520306465#bib18)), and delivered to the brain through the neurovascular unit (blood brain barrier) and to the RPE or retina through the choriocapillaris or [microcirculation](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/biochemistry-genetics-and-molecular-biology/microcirculation), respectively.

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022227520306465](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022227520306465)

Few studies have examined dietary protein intake and sources, in combination with longitudinal changes in brain structure markers. Our study aimed to examine the association between dietary protein intake and different sources of dietary protein, with the longitudinal rate of change in brain...

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

Second, hippocampal atrophy has not only been described in pathological, but also in healthy ageing. A meta-analysis estimated the average yearly rate of hippocampal atrophy at **1.4% in healthy ageing, and 4.7% in AD** (Barnes et al., 2009)

Hippocampal volume in particular has been tied to **decline in multiple cognitive domains including episodic, semantic, working memory, and visuospatial ability**.

# Thread: Poll: What is your optimal CT water temperature, duration & frequency?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColinGorham — 2024-02-12T10:15:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-331357>

What is your optimal CT water temperature, duration & frequency? I read the full CT series 10 years ago. What is your current thinking and best practice?

I think we can all agree CT is best done outdoors, under the sun, grounded in a natural environment, and with clean water.

This winter I have been running CT for: 90 minutes @ 54-60 F, 1-2 times per week in a cold tub (remineralized reverse osmosis water) on the deck.

During the summer, I would CT for: 60-120 minutes @ 50-75 F, 1-2 times per week in the Ocean in San Diego.

### JanSz — 2024-02-12T17:03:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-331369>

Fantastic results. Congratulations.

### Mat.I — 2024-06-15T11:06:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335805>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=335778)
>
> I've never tried not warming up after. Do you not shiver a lot few minutes after you get out of the water and stay outside?

No when I get in the cold plunge it feels like my body reacts (warms) whilst in there after the first few minutes. When I get out no shivering at all just a little cold however I do some light movement and get warm again outside without drying off, this way you have used your body to create the heat rather than using external sources.. This is why some may experience after drop. Years ago when I was cold adapting I would dry off and go about my usual day however around 1 hour later I would experience some after drop shivers (not to intense, however enough to make you feel a bit uncomfortable)..

### Mat.I — 2024-06-16T12:28:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335822>

No, i get as much sun as possible. For example today me and my family were out from sunrise to sunset. We only came in to make some food for the kids then back out again. Normal days in the week i will do my morning sunrise routine then set up my work station (fold out desk, laptop and screen) outside in direct sun. When it rains I work under our covered area or in the office with the window open (however still get outside and ground in the rain every hour or so for bit of time. Every chance I get I will get any CT and sun. Example on cold days I will still drive with the windows all the way down and only wear shorts and Tshirt or singlet. My summer clothes are my winter clothes everyone jokes about that to me.. However there is intention behind what i'm doing. In the holidays when I take some time off i will be out everyday sunrise to sunset.

### Saichi — 2024-06-16T15:55:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335823>

Amazing, congrats for doing what we all should be doing. That much sun not surprised you don't shiver at all post-CT.

### Emmanuela — 2024-06-17T16:39:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335843>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=335822)

> No, i get as much sun as possible. For example today me and my family were out from sunrise to sunset. We only came in to make some food for the kids then back out again. Normal days in the week i will do my morning sunrise routine then set up my work station (fold out desk, laptop and screen) outside in direct sun. When it rains I work under our covered area or in the office with the window open (however still get outside and ground in the rain every hour or so for bit of time. Every chance I get I will get any CT and sun. Example on cold days I will still drive with the windows all the way down and only wear shorts and Tshirt or singlet. My summer clothes are my winter clothes everyone jokes about that to me.. However there is intention behind what i'm doing. In the holidays when I take some time off i will be out everyday sunrise to sunset.

I also work in direct sun and am currently working on the wifi problem. I know this is off topic, but what do you do for internet in your outdoor workstation?

### Mat.I — 2024-06-18T04:16:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335854>

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=335843)
>
> I also work in direct sun and am currently working on the wifi problem. I know this is off topic, but what do you do for internet in your outdoor workstation?

[@Emmanuela](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31113/) - No worries. Just use a long ethernet cable from your nearest wall point (if you are hardwired already) or from your Lan port on router with WIFI disabled. If you searched amazon I'm sure they will sell ones up to 50m or more in length. You can get outdoor UV resistant ones that would be a bit more durable, however they are a bit more expensive. Such as this -

[https://www.amazon.com.au/Abireiv-Resistant-Waterproof-Buried-able-Compatible/dp/B09M6MR2WL/ref=pd\_sbs\_d\_sccl\_2\_3/356-3005478-9653648?pd\_rd\_w=FfWVm&content-id=amzn1.sym.e316aa1d-6e77-4be9-996c-5cb8fd4d5414&pf\_rd\_p=e316aa1d-6e77-4be9-996c-5cb8fd4d5414&pf\_rd\_r=39E0F2YVA72RRZ0H9FK3&pd\_rd\_wg=LMRYG&pd\_rd\_r=84a4e144-1f83-4b41-a04a-a339bc3f70f2&pd\_rd\_i=B09M6MR2WL&th=1](https://www.amazon.com.au/Abireiv-Resistant-Waterproof-Buried-able-Compatible/dp/B09M6MR2WL/ref=pd_sbs_d_sccl_2_3/356-3005478-9653648?pd_rd_w=FfWVm&content-id=amzn1.sym.e316aa1d-6e77-4be9-996c-5cb8fd4d5414&pf_rd_p=e316aa1d-6e77-4be9-996c-5cb8fd4d5414&pf_rd_r=39E0F2YVA72RRZ0H9FK3&pd_rd_wg=LMRYG&pd_rd_r=84a4e144-1f83-4b41-a04a-a339bc3f70f2&pd_rd_i=B09M6MR2WL&th=1)

### Mat.I — 2024-06-18T11:21:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poll-what-is-your-optimal-ct-water-temperature-duration-frequency.28598/post-335861>

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=335857)
>
> Did you drill a hole in a window or wall to run the ethernet cable from inside to outside?

I have hardwired most the house, therefore I just plug in at the wall plate next to our sliding door to the outside so run the cable through the door track under the doormat then outside on the ground. I pack this away / set this up everyday that I'm outside. I will be changing out the existing cabling to a higher spec, when I do that I will include an outdoor wall plate for convenience.

Re: your cable run - It depends what is the closest point to your point of connection (router Lan port I assume) to where you are setup outside. It is quite a small cable so you could make a little hole in the bottom corner of the insect screen (if you have one) and run the cable out the window OR just go through the door to outside (dependent on your configuration of your house) OR if you are looking for something more permanent - you could hardwire in a IP rated outdoor ethernet wall plate jack on your external wall (they usually come with a hinged flap with a gasket). I would just run a cable outside and set it up each day as needed, that way it is simple.

### Emmanuela — 2024-06-18T14:57:56+0300

I would just run a cable outside and set it up each day as needed, that way it is simple.

# Thread: Astrophotography
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astrophotography.28601/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kendall Toerner — 2024-02-12T18:23:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astrophotography.28601/post-331373>

One of my greatest passions has been astrophotography. Unforuntately this does not align well with circadian rhythm practices.

What are everyone's thoughts on a way to do this the most optimally with minimal disruption? Given that the only real man-made influence on this hobby is the camera itself, I find it also a great way to connect with nature and stargaze since exposures take several minutes.

My thoughts are to do it between sleep cycles (in between deep sleep and REM sleep) around 12-1 am. Obviously I would need to be camping in the location I do it to avoid much disruption as well, and luckily astrophotography demands zero nnEMF light pollution (or moonlight).

I've read that UV-A minimally disrupts circadian rhythm, so maybe using an external display with the backlight off and shining a black light on it would allow me to see what I'm doing? (color is not very important for this)

Does anyone have any knowledge on what can cause the sleep "program" to end or mess with clock genes too much? I would assume minimal physical activity and no significant light exposure would be key here. But I don't know if being too "awake" from an hour of wakefullness/thinking while doing photography will mess up the subsequent REM cycles I experience after the astro session.

### JanSz — 2024-02-12T19:28:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astrophotography.28601/post-331376>

Your greatest passions has been astrophotography.
I think you should continue it.
Should ease your worries if you read this, Jacks post:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-journey-2019-and-beyond.23037/post-330614](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacks-journal-journey-2019-and-beyond.23037/post-330614)

### JanSz — 2024-02-13T16:06:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astrophotography.28601/post-331397>

> [Kendall Toerner said:](/goto/post?id=331373)
>
> One of my greatest passions has been astrophotography. Unforuntately this does not align well with circadian rhythm practices.

But do not worry, most important is to be happy.

If you were happy with what Jack said on Jan 26/2024
then
look again
Friday at 1:18 PM February 9/2024

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-moonlight-as-good-as-sunlight.28590/post-331273](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-moonlight-as-good-as-sunlight.28590/post-331273)

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=331273)
>
> Moonlight is toxic. It is reflected sunlight at night. The word "luna"tic comes from moon. In language LUNA = moon

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=331281)
>
> Light at night is how you get cancer quickly.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-13T20:35:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astrophotography.28601/post-331409>

Typical lunar illuminance (full moon) is around 0.05 to 0.1 lux at temperate latitudes during the summer.
Sky glow on a clear night (no moon away from town) approximately 0.001 lux, (good astrophotography conditions) - in town 0.007 lux +/-
All these measurements depend on location, time of year, distance from town/city lights etc. because sky glow (no moon) on an overcast night in a city can be as bright as full moon. (Sun light is 400,000 brighter than full moon.)
Having said all that what I understand Jack to have consistently told us over the years, is ANY light at night is not good/healthy. That especially includes nnEMF blue light (including reflected city lights) but even moon light (reflected sun light).
We all compromise in some way but ideally, after sunset, crawl into your cave and go to sleep - till dawn.

### JanSz — 2024-02-13T21:29:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/astrophotography.28601/post-331411>

Do not worry, be happy.
Some of my best nights were under moon light.

# Thread: Itchy arms
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itchy-arms.28602/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet

### Thomas Russsell — 2024-02-13T01:05:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itchy-arms.28602/post-331383>

Hello,
I am new to this forum, but am a long time reader and believer of Jack Kruse’s work. Almost 2 years ago, while my wife and I were building a wall I accidentally elbowed her, quite forcefully, just above her forehead. She immediately felt pain in the back of her skull where it meets her spine, and tingling down her shoulders and arms. She still experiences pain near the base of her skull some days, and most terribly, extreme itching in one or both arms almost every day. The only thing that calms the itch is ice packs on the arms. She has tried ozone shots to the cervical area and acupuncture, both expensive and did not cure her, though may have provided a bit of temporary relief. She just started going to a chiropractor, who thinks adjustments and more time are the answer. I am looking for any other suggestions that may help.
Thank you,
Tom.

### coho3131 — 2024-02-13T01:34:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itchy-arms.28602/post-331387>

Low redox and intracellular dehydration?

How many hours per day does she spend outside? How many hours in front of a screen? How much ALAN?

What is her fasting blood sugar?

Or better yet, does she have signs of leptin resistance?

I hope she feels better soon.

**Tom, I am sorry your wife has been in pain and experiencing extreme arm itching.**
**I like the questions coho3131 asked.** Also is your wife eating _**Epi-Paleo**_, low carb? Or keto or carnivore?

I personally suffer only mild upper arm itching (usually one arm or the other) when I am exposed to some fabrics or foods I am sensitive to...tough for me... got sensitive to a whole lot of things after a cancer health crisis 14 years ago.

May not be related... but I have also heard of people going Keto diet /low carb and Keto rashes

### Are You Itching on a Ketogenic Diet? – Dr. Berg on Keto Rash [**https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Od7LYWhcLFs**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Od7LYWhcLFs) ​

AND even better video...

### Ep:23 Keto Rash, Dr. Cywes You tube

**I hope and pray you get exactly the help you need.**

We turn the wifi off, have black-out curtains, and keep a temp of 62 degrees for sleeping, however, my wife works in a school with strong wifi and about 1,000 laptops, and is working on an online degree when she gets home, spending about 3 more hours online per evening for the past 2 years. Because of the temporary relief my wife experienced from ozone shots to the cervical area and a misalignment diagnosed by her chiropractor, we are leaning toward a physical diagnosis of Brachioradial pruritis. > Thank you...good ideas ...no fuzzy wall paper or murals, duct work pretty new but I think there was a coating on the metal when it was installed and I asked them to wipe it down well with rubbing alcohol to remove any possible coating...not at all sure if they honored my request. Time to have them cleaned I will ask if they can check for and remove any coating. Heat has been running a lot and been having low (25%) humidity inside home this winter.
> And yes, will work on dust.
> Again, thank you for those suggestions!

About humidifier on Air type heating system.
The problem with humidifiers is that they work on home water system.
That water, at least in my area is relatively hard and clogs the humidifier within less than two weeks. Even if it went first thru water softener.
A month ago, I removed my old humidifier, told them to install the best available but also told them to install Reverse Osmosis system that feeds water to humidifier.

.....

### Thomas Russsell — 2024-03-22T23:06:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/itchy-arms.28602/post-332767>

Sorry it has been a while since my last post; I was waiting to see if there was a continued change in my wife's symptoms. I am happy to say that since she has been going to the chiropractor, her symptoms have steadily improved. It has now been over a month since she has had to ice her arms to stop the itch!
JanSz, you will be happy to know that the chiropractor she has been seeing is, in fact, using the Palmer set-up and method.
Thanks to all for the much appreciated thoughtful and helpful ideas and suggestions.
Tom

# Thread: My wild journey : my EHS, losses, a domestic case, observations, El Zonte, a new beginning and more. Pt. 1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-wild-journey-my-ehs-losses-a-domestic-case-observations-el-zonte-a-new-beginning-and-more-pt-1.28608/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Leonaero_Light — 2024-02-15T21:33:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-wild-journey-my-ehs-losses-a-domestic-case-observations-el-zonte-a-new-beginning-and-more-pt-1.28608/post-331488>

Hello all,

It’s been a while since I ‘ve ‘met & greeted’ but I’m finally ready to share.

My journey actually starts by me having to leave Amsterdam Airport by car and seeing flight UA21 leaving without me.

I had planned to go to El Zonte, El Salvador to get some 13th latitude sun & UV, clear my head, check out job- and life options to possibly restart my masseuse practice there. Cause I am tired, weary and done with this latitude, this version of society and its scrambled head inhabitants. I want a completely new life. I had planned to write this whole extensive, detailed journey while laying on Pacific sand. I figured I’d be inspired to write when I’m the right environment. So this is the relatively short 52nd-latitude version of my journey.

I have been staying with my older brother in “5G” The Hague City (Den Haag) in the Netherlands / Holland since summer last year. It’s no NY or Amsterdam even, but there are still 600k idiotic users packed together, using technology. My brother will turn 51 this year, has had two TIA’s, has nerve pains in hands & feet, high blood pressure, diabetes type II and works at night as a taxi driver, driving a Tesla. And doesn’t get outside much.

I’ve bought him two Ra Optics glasses, but ofcourse that’s not enough. To me that’s an ending case in all honesty. Once Holland goes into phase 2 of the 5G roll out. That’ll probably happen next year. I’ve already been preparing myself for the worst, emotionally. But he’s not taking me with him. That’s for sure.

So I basically been running away for 7 - 8 months, renting out places in the south of the Netherlands/ Holland and Belgian/ Flemish Ardennes. Mainly to better my environment, health, biology and overall outlook on life. And get my head clear, my thinking up and my cell charge up. ‘Cause I have (some form of) EHS. But I feel like I’m running out of time. And money. At the same time my experiences and observations in these last couple of months have enriched me in ways I could’ve never imagined. I have used the power of fire, in- & outside, grounding in a.m. & p.m. light, grounding knee deep in the Northsea along the Belgian coast, even during a storm, cold exposure in- & outside, use of near & infrared light , been blocking blue light with several Ra Optics glasses, scarfs, cotton gloves, wooly hats and cotton clothing to protect my skin, neck & brain and of course being in nature, naked, barefoot and/or barechested. Everything has been an experience to write about. I actually found a surprisingly good friend in CT. Not right away but quicker than I expected. I have been in cold showers for the last 2 years,CT-ing 3 -4 times a week for the last 4 months at 8 - 12 C (46 - 54F)The last 3 weeks I was able to get in cold everyday straight. I did my first 75 min bath at 15 C (59F) a few days ago. Couldn’t get it any colder unfortunately. And been outside in 5-6 C (41 - 43F) water for 10 - 15 minutes. I remember sitting at the bottom of that natural pool, thinking, ‘Well this ain’t even that bad!” Of course, after 15 minutes, things got different. I haven’t missed a single sunrise or sunset in more than two years.

But renting all those houses, cabins and cottages has been a very costly endeavor. But I believed it was necessary to get better. And I have been in hell the last 8 years. Basically longer, but let me just stick to 8. And El Salvador then wasn’t as appealing as it is now. My mind wasn’t ready,

Before living with my brother I’ve had a terrible ending to my fam life, being forced to say goodbye to two ADHD, suïcidal kids and a depressed, anxiety disordered, down, stressed out, melatonin addicted, catatonic, non thinking, low dopamine, pale white, non supportive, medical paradigm addicted, ex partner. All three were and probably still are addicted to their phones too. With no respect for or understanding of my EHS. I basically had to fight my way out of there ending in a minor domestic case and a short stay in jail. That’s how far I had to take after being together for 28 years.

Which does not describe who I am. At all. I’ve been having arguments with grandmothers, friends, teachers, ADHD coaches, doctors, countless psychologists, mental coaches, Corona vaccine administers and psychiatrists, all eating from the same bullshit pie and spitting all that shit at me. And an ex partner who completely sided with all of them without being critical or thinking for herself. Those multiple stabs in the heart and back will probably never fully heal.

No one or no kid should be taking methylfenidate. Taking something exogenous that raises your blood pressure enormously probably isn’t the best way to better your health. I showed them side effect of Ritalin & Concerta but it’s like talking to a statue.

So I’ve cut most ties with family and I have been staying far away from so called ‘friends’. Even my best friend, who I’ve known for over 30 years.

I am a L0a1a haplotype, 45% Liberian & 45% Nigerian with 4% Scandinavian sprinkled on top. 6% unknown. Born in Surinam, 5/6th latitude, but been living in Holland since I was about 9 months.

I’ ll be turning 50 this year and have been able to stay away from doctors, prescriptions, medicine or faulty medical advice for almost 12 years. And of course, no Corona/SV-40 vaccine injection. Ofcourse no, are you crazy.

I think I’m doing ok..ish.

My earlier mentioned EHS, which I discovered on November 22nd 2020, that’s when I first heard of this dude named Jack Kruse, has been under control since 2021 and has been ‘evolving’ ever since. In a good way I think.

Evolving ‘cause it has become more than just ‘electro’ now. When I really think about it, it’s not. But it’s different for sure.

I can feel, detect and sense everything. And I literally mean EVERYTHING’. And it has not been irritating, annoying, painful or bothersome but feeIs more like sensing. Or having a compass even. It always feels very subtle. Sometimes very locally depending on the strenght of the field. Or size.

My fascia changes at very specific places, whenever I’m around nnEMF, for example tissues around my right adductor magnus, left IT/glute max, deep gut/upper pelvic region, right upper part of my semimembranosis (hamstrings) upper and lower thorax area, occipital ridge, right platysma, fascia running up my left triceps, shoulderblade & down to my left rhomboid and spine. But mostly the left part of my thoracolumbar fascial area. It’s never muscle but always fascial tissue. Changes are always felt across the whole fascial system but certain area just light up more.

### Leonaero_Light — 2024-02-15T21:40:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-wild-journey-my-ehs-losses-a-domestic-case-observations-el-zonte-a-new-beginning-and-more-pt-1.28608/post-331489>

### ​

Pt. 2 / 4
My wild journey : my EHS, losses, a domestic case, observations, El Zonte and more.

Here we go :
I feel every 4/5G tower, powerlines, trains, phones, electrical wiring or appliances, planes flying over, Tesla’s and GLC300’s driving behind me. Everything new built by the German Volkswagen & BMW Group feels like a disaster on wheels. Deleting any file on my phone is a change, even adjusting the volume.

But it goes further than that. I feel a distinct difference between a Bluetooth and a WIFI signal. Or when I enter a room where my phone is charging I know and feel that it’s at a 100%. Or the switch from 2% to 1% battery power. Winding, curling or changing the shape of a disconnected iphone cable, I sense those changes too.

I can tell you by placing my hand against a wall how the electrical wiring runs. If it’s a place not stuffed with nnEMF though. Dimmer switches are the worst to stand next to. Or bad wiring, dirty electricity. It does not bother me, it’s just there. On the outskirts of my senses.

But its gone crazier than that. I can feel a plant’s energy. Watering plants is a joyful moment as it induces deep breathing and it makes my eyesight clearer. I feel a warm tingle across ny ribcage, overall it just makes me feel good. I can tell by touch but also by intensely looking if a plant has energy in its roots, therefore knowing that it’s dead or not dead. So I have been sleeping with plants around my bed. No sex ;-) but i think we are enjoying each others company. I can feel a difference in energy between the roots, stems and leafs. If I open a window up more to let some more sun in or put them outside everything I just mentioned just get heightened.

The same goes for water. l feel lakes, pools and forests at a distance. I can sense ocean water at approximately 3 km/2 mi distance. I can feel trees or forests at a km/0,6 mi distance. I can even ground through trees or thick branches by holding /touching a tree trunk or standing on the roots of a tree barefoot. It’s almost the same surge of energy as standing on grass, barefoot. Especially when it’s raining.

But that’s not all. I can feel or sense bio energy coming from food. Every tomato, onion, egg, oyster. ounce/gram of kale, red cabbage, curcumine, red meat, animal fat, shrimp or fish. Just by holding it in my hand, or looking at it intensely, or even standing next to whatever it is, I can feel slight changes in my eyes, eyesocket, eyebrow, brow ridge, nose, chest and my deep gut. There are actual physical changes I detect. Red cabbage, kale especially fish have got the strongest signal or energy. So of course, those I eat the most.

Again, very very subtle but it’s definitely detectable.

I’m able to feel colours too. Hovering my hand over a white, blue and red piece of paper with my eyes closed I can tell you which is which. With the colour red having the strongest signal. I sense the difference between 100% cotton and 50% polyester/ 50% cotton. Or when a cotton shirt has 5% elastine.

But during these last couple of weeks it went even further than that. I can see and feel deuterium in food. When splitting egg yolk from eggwhite I can feel deuterium ‘burning’ on my fingertips. I boldy assume it’s deuterium. I get the same signal from all those packaged deuterium bombs in our local supermarkets. Oatmeal, potato chips, chocolate bars, cookies, some are way worse than others but they’re basically all the same. McDonalds is at the top of that list by the way.

I can look at and through a pack of eggs and determine how much energy is in the yolk and or how much deuterium is its surrounding white. So I know which eggs are best. Free roam with added vitamine D are horrible plus there is little to no energy in its yolk. Compared to normal free roam eggs. Which I eat almost daily.

Looking at a bowl of eggwhite ‘burns” fascia in and around my eyes.

But.. when eating oysters, that’s when the party starts, especially French Gillardeau’s. The bio energy makes me do stupid happy dances while holding empty shells like Spanish castanets. And no, you will never see me do that. I can tell you which oysters are best. To me. The French lead the race, Irish are usually good too, not all of them. Dutch ones taste horrible but they do the job. Makes a lot of sense…

The water inside an oyster also gives off a signal which resembles deuterium in some cases. I once had Spanish oysters which gave me the same signal as eggwhites. I ate a few of them and threw the box away. Never again.

But it goes even further than that. I can now distinguish local food from this latitude. from let’s say Morocco or Spain. 90% of all fruits and vegetables get imported from these countries.

I only have to look at a tomato and know it’s from Spain. Or at least not local. One day there selling you kale from Dutch soil, but will switch to kale from Spain the very next day. I wasn’t paying attention one day, I just grabbed a pack of kale and brought it home. But during my first bite or chew I felt changes in my (upper)gut immediately. Bad ones. All I should have done was be aware and pay attention to signals. Which obviously I didn’t. Never made that mistake again.

I stopped eating sugar in October. Ate a Dutch tomato in december. My eyes said ‘ok’ but my deep gut said ‘no Sir!’ I cant even try to explain how that mismatch feels. So I haven’t touched tomatoes since.

Just for jokes I walk by a tropical fruit & vegetable section in a supermarket just to feel and confirm all that awful and mismatching energy coming from those mangoes, kiwi, pineapples in January. Just to confirm in my tissues what I already know.

### Leonaero_Light — 2024-02-15T21:43:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-wild-journey-my-ehs-losses-a-domestic-case-observations-el-zonte-a-new-beginning-and-more-pt-1.28608/post-331492>

Pt.3 / 4

My most recent discovery is seeing, sensing and feeling people. I can look at a human being as a whole but I can also zone in on specific parts of the body. So I have been doing that with people’s heads, hearts , chest and brains. This all happens through clothing. The signals I get by doing this are by far the most interesting of them all, ‘cause I don’t exactly know what I’m looking at. Or receiving rather. Plus there is so much happening inside and I can’t be staring at people for 5 minutes. Magnetic fields, infrared light, other types of light people are sending out, ELF’s or some other form of energy I’m not aware of. What am I receiving ?

When I focus in on somebody’s brain, the signal I get from them is ‘good’, ‘bad’ or ‘distorted’. Good’ relaxes fascia in my eyes & socket, especially my left eye, brightens up my vision, implodes my gut and reenergizes or resets my whole abdomen which subsequently makes me roll my shoulders back and basically shifts my whole posture into a more relaxed state. Again this is all very subtle. ‘Bad’ tightens up my gut & eyes, making me want to pinch my eyes and look away. People with headphones on or looking at their phones will give me a scrambled signal. It makes me think of distortion or white noise. A very annoying signal.

Kids mostly send out a good signal , from babies to 9 / 10 year-olds. With adults you never know what you’re getting. A mom walking her kid(s) to school could give me very strong brainsignal while a father walking behind them is totally bad. Old people sometimes surprise me with a very strong brain signal, just strolling or walking their dog. Women usually have a stronger heart & brain signal than men. What I’ve been noticing too is that I’m able to sense the combined light or energy of a crowd. Whether it’s at the airport or at a food plaza, of course there’s nnEMF around, but it’s very manageable. It’s almost the same cloud of energy that hits me when I walk into a forest. But slighty different. I think that’s what the Dutch call ‘gezelligheid’ (cosiness, fun).

I’m leaving out a whole lot I would have wanted to talk about. A whole goddamn lot. About the correlation between the shape and size of an eye and human structure and the rotations in the spine. Or how a smaller eye correlates to your gait cycle. You can learn a lot about people’s health by this observing. And watching them stand ir walk. Maybe I will give you the extended version once I’m feeling inspired more. But I have been wallowing in sorrow, regret, pain, loss, loneliness & disappointment for many months and I feel like I’m just getting out of a hole. So this is basically what I was able to squeeze out of my brain and hand. I would have wanted to talk in depth about those countless podcasts I listened to, my experiences, especially my observations in the last 4 - 5 years and the millions of questions I have because of these observations. Or buying a spectrometer and having my eyes opened. Wow.

Or the people, strangers, not friends, people I didn’t know and introduced to Jack Kruse. Or the measurements of my TriMeter I was able to show folks. There are many, many, amazing moments in there.

Goddamn. I just HAVE to talk about a few.
While sitting barechested and grounded in grass in a public park enjoying the Dutch summersun last year at UV index 7,5 I had a little brown bird sitting in the grass just 1 meter (3 ft.) away from me, wings spread, obviously to get some sun. She hopped out of the bushes and literally squatted down like she came home from a hard day’s work and crashed on the couch. She turned her head and looked at me like: ‘oh shit dude, you here too huh ?’ , looked away and just relaxed. Again, wings spread with the shiny, purple, reflective part in her feathers exposed.

Both of us just being there for a good 3 minutes was just beautiful, with me glancing over every 20 seconds. 2 animals with a similar goal. All I could do was smile. And chuckle. Softly ofcourse. One of the funniest and at the same time most beautiful things I’ve ever experienced. Amazing.

Talks with strangers in that park. Eating oysters attracts all kinds of people. An Indian dude and a dude from Curacao starting talking to me. After 30 minutes I showing them measurements of my spectrometer and TriMeter. Again, oysters were there. 2 friends, IT guys being outside on sickleave, suffering from stomach and gut issues who I convinced to go barechested. Which eventually they did after I told them about he gut and the brain and skin surface. I threw a little POMC and melanin in there without knowing all the details or quantum processes. I politely asked them to turn there phones off which they did.

After that I had the pleasure of sharing my oysters with them, which they had never had. I had draped theirs with lemon, red onions, curcumine, ginger and some nutmeg. After the first ones, they both nodded and smiled, which in turn made me smile from ear to ear. After a lot of ‘hmmmm’s’ and ‘oooh’s’ we shared about 24 oysters between the 3 of us. It’s wonderful and very fullfilling to open eyes that have been shut for years. If you are willing and/or interested, I can sense that.

### Leonaero_Light — 2024-02-15T21:45:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-wild-journey-my-ehs-losses-a-domestic-case-observations-el-zonte-a-new-beginning-and-more-pt-1.28608/post-331493>

Pt. 4 / 4 (last part)

I have got a whole bunch of those stories and experiences. Like, real magical experiences, like me magnetically and energetically having a lemon stuck to my wrist for a second or my left leg being magnetically drawn to an active dishwasher, which is by far the most out of this world experience I’ve ever had. And it scared the shit out of me. That would be my first question to Jack.

My medical history, my future plans, there is so much. My analysis of Dutch general health, using figures provided by the Central Bureau of Statistics. It tells you a story that should scare all. But not when you’re blue light poisoned. And profiting from that. I want to tell that story someday.

Once I get my head clear, have a new place to call home and I’ve got my legs and feet sturdy up under me I would like to take down those holy and sacred cows which Holland is filled with. And they’ve got a whole lot of cows.

I don’t want to go back to my brother, ever, ‘cause that’s straight hell. I found a place to stay with a lost friend, a real friend who just became my soulmate. It’s not ideal, but of course I can’t complain. When I think about it, I’m ashamed I haven’t kept in touch with him.

He’s always had a special place in my heart. Not because he offered to let me stay at his place, but the warmth and energy he always exuded. And still does. He is a really smart dude, critical thinker, muslim of Turkish descent, doesn’t look his age, has a positive outlook on life and makes connections easily. But is still finding his way in life.?It’s fun to hear him connecting the dots when hearing something for the first time. I have never met anyone like him. He is interested in life, biology and in one of our of conversations about going back home, sun & grounding, my life, quantum biology and energy he casually dropped that he was born in Brunei, 4/5th latitude and spent the first 8 years of his life in Brunei. At the beach! That just shocked the shit of me and makes our consersations even better. I jumped up out of exitement and had to walk it off. The Turkish are generally, mostly J2 so I’m curious to know what those 8 years did to him, his mom anhis sisters and their mitochondria. It obviously gave him some form of extra energy compared to most I know. Or knew. That’s what I detect when I’m around him. No wonder I love him. With the knowledge I have about circadian rhytm, strong UV, apoptosis and autophagy and how melanine plays a role in transforming light to energy, I’m trying to explain to him (and myself!) why he is who he is. This is a dude for life now so I hope he’ll adapt and incorporate the things we talk about in his personal life. I think he will. He just joked that he’s basically blacker than I am cause he’s been born closer to the equator and been there for a much longer time. Good one, but there might be some thruth in there. This is the only dude that I would invite over if I ever get the chance to get settled in El Salvador.

So I just joined as a Gold Member and could definitely use some help. Or a hand. Or a place to stay abroad, with respect to nnEMF. And I need some new friends, preferably, energetic & non idiotic.

There was a validity problem with my passport so I can’t travel and come to El Salvador. Upon entry the Salvadoran government demands a passport to be valid for at least 6 months. Mine expires on June 18 of this year. Getting a new passport would initially take at least 6 weeks. Which really fucked with me. Really. Seeing that flight leave out of Amsterdam Airport kept me up at that night. Not long. But it did. Through perseverance I still managed to submit my passport application today, so fortunately I was able to cut those 6 weeks back to just 1. Hope is back.

Dear Jack, if you’re reading this, this is for you :

You’ve really given me a purpose and a direction. A forever seemingly vagus mind that lost its way finally knows what it wants. You took crazy away and replaced that with quantum science and facts

I’m still that little 7 year old melanine boy that deliberately broke his birthday toy cars just to see how they work. Only to use those little engines/batteries in his own Lego built helicopter. That little dude’s curiosity and intererest in general mechanics are still there. The kid’s got some grey in his beard now, his left eye has a slighty changed shape, his structure is also affected a bit, but the hunger has never left. You gave me an ok to be curious without boundaries. It’s like getting that splinter in your feet removed that’s been bothering you for 3 years or getting rid of a dead weight that you’ve been carrying around for way too long.

I’m eternally grateful, really,

I just wish I had the vocab to really express how I’m feeling inside.

You changed my whole outlook on human biology, human nature, nature and just life period. For the better. Which has severely altered all life choices I’m making. Nobody has ever done that for me. The shit I’ve been going through in the last 8 years, especially the last 2 years is nothing compared to what people and society must’ve dragged you through for the last 20 years. But you still persevered and landed on top. Only to have a good view of the shit they got themselves in. Chuckling, drinking wine and eating oysters in the sun.

Your pursuit of truth is inspiring and to me addictive. You’ve inspired me to do the same. And I already know how. I know I have like years and years of reading & learning but I’m willing. Reading is a problem right now, but we’ll discuss that some other time. You have answered a million questions for me , without you knowing and subsequently made me think of 2 million more. Like what happened with me and that lemon. Or what happened with me and that dishwasher.

Besides massages there’s nothing else I really place value in. Anymore. Valence electrons in groups 1 and 2, making use of the photoelectric effect just make sense. I see that. I want more of that. That’s why I became a member. I don’t feel alone anymore. I just feel by myself now. And at the same time part of something bigger, a community of thinkers. In all honesty I’ve shed many tears writing this all this down. But I have a feeling it’ll all be ok eventually.

I thank you for making my life more meaningful. From the bottom of my heart. Or mitochondria. You helped me to really accept myself for everything that I am. On all levels, even the ones I can’t explain. Yet. And I haven’t even jumped into bitcoin yet.

Thank you for being Jack Kruse, Jack Kruse.

If we ever get to meet in person I’m sure you’ll feel and see the gratitude in my eyes. I might even give you a big hug too.

Hope to hear from anyone soon.

I appreciate you all.

Love,

Leonaero
