### KinaBibiDiop — 2024-02-18T21:29:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-wild-journey-my-ehs-losses-a-domestic-case-observations-el-zonte-a-new-beginning-and-more-pt-1.28608/post-331602>

> [Leonaero\_Light said:](/goto/post?id=331488)
>
> Hello all,
>
> It’s been a while since I ‘ve ‘met & greeted’ but I’m finally ready to share.
>
> My journey actually starts by me having to leave Amsterdam Airport by car and seeing flight UA21 leaving without me.
>
> I had planned to go to El Zonte, El Salvador to get some 13th latitude sun & UV, clear my head, check out job- and life options to possibly restart my masseuse practice there. Cause I am tired, weary and done with this latitude, this version of society and its scrambled head inhabitants. I want a completely new life. I had planned to write this whole extensive, detailed journey while laying on Pacific sand. I figured I’d be inspired to write when I’m the right environment. So this is the relatively short 52nd-latitude version of my journey.
>
> I have been staying with my older brother in “5G” The Hague City (Den Haag) in the Netherlands / Holland since summer last year. It’s no NY or Amsterdam even, but there are still 600k idiotic users packed together, using technology. My brother will turn 51 this year, has had two TIA’s, has nerve pains in hands & feet, high blood pressure, diabetes type II and works at night as a taxi driver, driving a Tesla. And doesn’t get outside much.
>
> I’ve bought him two Ra Optics glasses, but ofcourse that’s not enough. To me that’s an ending case in all honesty. Once Holland goes into phase 2 of the 5G roll out. That’ll probably happen next year. I’ve already been preparing myself for the worst, emotionally. But he’s not taking me with him. That’s for sure.
>
> So I basically been running away for 7 - 8 months, renting out places in the south of the Netherlands/ Holland and Belgian/ Flemish Ardennes. Mainly to better my environment, health, biology and overall outlook on life. And get my head clear, my thinking up and my cell charge up. ‘Cause I have (some form of) EHS. But I feel like I’m running out of time. And money. At the same time my experiences and observations in these last couple of months have enriched me in ways I could’ve never imagined. I have used the power of fire, in- & outside, grounding in a.m. & p.m. light, grounding knee deep in the Northsea along the Belgian coast, even during a storm, cold exposure in- & outside, use of near & infrared light , been blocking blue light with several Ra Optics glasses, scarfs, cotton gloves, wooly hats and cotton clothing to protect my skin, neck & brain and of course being in nature, naked, barefoot and/or barechested. Everything has been an experience to write about. I actually found a surprisingly good friend in CT. Not right away but quicker than I expected. I have been in cold showers for the last 2 years,CT-ing 3 -4 times a week for the last 4 months at 8 - 12 C (46 - 54F)The last 3 weeks I was able to get in cold everyday straight. I did my first 75 min bath at 15 C (59F) a few days ago. Couldn’t get it any colder unfortunately. And been outside in 5-6 C (41 - 43F) water for 10 - 15 minutes. I remember sitting at the bottom of that natural pool, thinking, ‘Well this ain’t even that bad!” Of course, after 15 minutes, things got different. I haven’t missed a single sunrise or sunset in more than two years.
>
> But renting all those houses, cabins and cottages has been a very costly endeavor. But I believed it was necessary to get better. And I have been in hell the last 8 years. Basically longer, but let me just stick to 8. And El Salvador then wasn’t as appealing as it is now. My mind wasn’t ready,
>
> Before living with my brother I’ve had a terrible ending to my fam life, being forced to say goodbye to two ADHD, suïcidal kids and a depressed, anxiety disordered, down, stressed out, melatonin addicted, catatonic, non thinking, low dopamine, pale white, non supportive, medical paradigm addicted, ex partner. All three were and probably still are addicted to their phones too. With no respect for or understanding of my EHS. I basically had to fight my way out of there ending in a minor domestic case and a short stay in jail. That’s how far I had to take after being together for 28 years.
>
> Which does not describe who I am. At all. I’ve been having arguments with grandmothers, friends, teachers, ADHD coaches, doctors, countless psychologists, mental coaches, Corona vaccine administers and psychiatrists, all eating from the same bullshit pie and spitting all that shit at me. And an ex partner who completely sided with all of them without being critical or thinking for herself. Those multiple stabs in the heart and back will probably never fully heal.
>
> No one or no kid should be taking methylfenidate. Taking something exogenous that raises your blood pressure enormously probably isn’t the best way to better your health. I showed them side effect of Ritalin & Concerta but it’s like talking to a statue.
>
> So I’ve cut most ties with family and I have been staying far away from so called ‘friends’. Even my best friend, who I’ve known for over 30 years.
>
>
>
> I am a L0a1a haplotype, 45% Liberian & 45% Nigerian with 4% Scandinavian sprinkled on top. 6% unknown. Born in Surinam, 5/6th latitude, but been living in Holland since I was about 9 months.
>
> I’ ll be turning 50 this year and have been able to stay away from doctors, prescriptions, medicine or faulty medical advice for almost 12 years. And of course, no Corona/SV-40 vaccine injection. Ofcourse no, are you crazy.
>
> I think I’m doing ok..ish.
>
> My earlier mentioned EHS, which I discovered on November 22nd 2020, that’s when I first heard of this dude named Jack Kruse, has been under control since 2021 and has been ‘evolving’ ever since. In a good way I think.
>
> Evolving ‘cause it has become more than just ‘electro’ now. When I really think about it, it’s not. But it’s different for sure.
>
>
>
> I can feel, detect and sense everything. And I literally mean EVERYTHING’. And it has not been irritating, annoying, painful or bothersome but feeIs more like sensing. Or having a compass even. It always feels very subtle. Sometimes very locally depending on the strenght of the field. Or size.
>
> My fascia changes at very specific places, whenever I’m around nnEMF, for example tissues around my right adductor magnus, left IT/glute max, deep gut/upper pelvic region, right upper part of my semimembranosis (hamstrings) upper and lower thorax area, occipital ridge, right platysma, fascia running up my left triceps, shoulderblade & down to my left rhomboid and spine. But mostly the left part of my thoracolumbar fascial area. It’s never muscle but always fascial tissue. Changes are always felt across the whole fascial system but certain area just light up more.

Hi. I have sent you a couple of private messages. Are you receiving them?

# Thread: Plasmalogens
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasmalogens.28610/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Esg999 — 2024-02-15T23:51:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasmalogens.28610/post-331498>

Does anyone know Jack's opinion on Omega 3 Plasmalogens supplements? Supposedly have the fatty acid at the SN-2 position. Of course seafood is best, but kids wont eat it. Yes, I can force them and do but looking for a partial alternative.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-02-16T16:05:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasmalogens.28610/post-331515>

The big issue with oil supplements is they are very prone to oxidation. If you get the omega 3 naturally from seafood, it is much more resistant to oxidation

### coho3131 — 2024-02-16T19:11:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasmalogens.28610/post-331529>

I also think absorption for substances outside of their natural form is another issue.

Are they even making it past the gastric acid to get to the duodenum?

There are really no shortcuts with regard to DHA. Like Joseph said, just eat the DHA-rich seafood.

The methodology of RCTs for fish oil supplements are significantly weak also.

I think it is most likely a real waste of money for something that lacks evidence.

### JanSz — 2024-02-16T19:55:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasmalogens.28610/post-331531>

The problem with DHA is that there is no problem with DHA.
Most people have plenty of it.
If you do not believe in that, just test it.
\----
Just make sure that you test DHA and not some "indicators".
....

### Dr. Marcus Ettinger — 2024-02-17T06:59:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasmalogens.28610/post-331539>

> [Esg999 said:](/goto/post?id=331498)
>
> Does anyone know Jack's opinion on Omega 3 Plasmalogens supplements? Supposedly have the fatty acid at the SN-2 position. Of course seafood is best, but kids wont eat it. Yes, I can force them and do but looking for a partial alternative.

Data on plasmalogens has been known for a long time. I first read about them in the mid-2000s. There is no need to go crazy on expensive supplements. I tell my patients to eat grass-fed beef, heritage-breed pork, lamb, mussels, salmon, oyster, mackerel, and pastured eggs. 'EVERYTHING' you need is right there.

**For the kids**, it's grass-fed burgers, lamb kababs, heritage-breed pork chops, and pastured eggs (cooked however they like). Instead of tuna fish, if they eat that, try using canned salmon. It tastes very similar.

Everything we need already exists. We all need a guide sometimes to help us find what we are looking for.

# Thread: How do you know when you've reached a good level of optimal?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alexandra — 2024-02-16T19:33:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/post-331530>

Hi, All! From what I've read / heard on the powwows so far (Dec/Jan), it sounds like it's a process for each person looking at their data and figuring out what geo location and amount of light is best for them.

My question is: what is the best way to measure success/whether you've reached a good level of optimal where you can stay for a bit/a good geo location to relocate to? Is it purely through labs or are there other ways? Also, when checking out geographic locations, any thoughts on how do you know what's right/where to relocate? We're in the USA, family is here so we prefer to stay and optimize here if we can. Maybe I just haven't come to it yet but it's not clear to me what my / our metrics should be except, perhaps lab results and the way we feel. Thanks!

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-17T03:54:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/post-331535>

> [Alexandra said:](/goto/post?id=331530)
>
> Hi, All! From what I've read / heard on the powwows so far (Dec/Jan), it sounds like it's a process for each person looking at their data and figuring out what geo location and amount of light is best for them.
>
> My question is: what is the best way to measure success/whether you've reached a good level of optimal where you can stay for a bit/a good geo location to relocate to? Is it purely through labs or are there other ways? Also, when checking out geographic locations, any thoughts on how do you know what's right/where to relocate? We're in the USA, family is here so we prefer to stay and optimize here if we can. Maybe I just haven't come to it yet but it's not clear to me what my / our metrics should be except, perhaps lab results and the way we feel. Thanks!

Here is how I understand it:
There is not “good optimal” as you call it for all, and the guide is nature.

Since we developed and adjusted as humans pretty much all in North Africa around the equator, that’s a starting point for “optimal”, however us white people moved away from the equator and turned white. So we can actually live with less sun, but it’s no longer ideal or optimal.

Same with everything else. There was no artificial light on the planet, no electro-magnetic fields, no environmental stressors of all sorts. So all of that takes away from optimal, but different people have different issues and some are stronger than others.

While there are healthy centenarians who are smokers, it is unlikely to rightfully conclude that smoking is healthy. Those have a different history, environment, physique, it’s all different.

When people ask what you are asking, more often than not the idea behind it is: “I don’t have to change a lot, do I?”

And that is up to you. The sicker somebody is, the more radically they need to change should they want to get better - and not everybody does, believe it or not.

There doesn’t seem to be a great location to live in the US, so you can’t really ask, if there is an okIsh great one. If it’s good enough for you and your loved ones, then it is. It turns out that our “islands” of comfort are getting smaller and smaller and that is unsettling to say the least.

It all adds up, and humans are great at compensating. If you have good mitochondria from your mother and her mother you can worry less. If you already had a history of all sorts of ailments than you can completely ditch your concept of optimal and you need to hurry up making better choices.

Good signs are when: you are healthy and happy, have good hair, good skin, good teeth, good mental health, sexual health, gut health, good relationships with others, good connection to nature, good instincts… success… what else? You know where I am heading at. It’s actually common sense.

So then you can learn and improve from what you are learning.

If you have a deficit, well, then something is up and you need to start looking for changes.

Data is not even the best measurement.
Many of us have things wrong with them for all their life, that’s going to take a while to improve, no need to measure anything, just getting on it.

Of course not everybody can afford the 100percent best thing for them. But again more often than not, it’s not affordability that’s the issue, but some odd form of postponing. There is fear, unwillingness to change, being too comfortable, too lazy, not having discipline, being spoiled, lacking courage, inspiration or aspiration… lots of talent is wasted, time is wasted, opportunity is wasted, because humans have free will and they can override nature because of it. Many bad decisions are made out of disconnection from nature.

I would say most people here are sincerely trying to do the right things. And Jack preaches it really well: connect to nature.

### Dr. Marcus Ettinger — 2024-02-17T07:20:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/post-331540>

**I vote for testing and data as needed tools for optimization** - as well as - good signs are when you are healthy and happy, have good hair, good skin, good teeth, good mental health, sexual health, gut health, good relationships with others, good connection to nature, good instincts… success…

All the above is great, but guessing is the best you will do if you're not testing. Testing can give you clues as to where any potential outpoints may be and/or how things inside of you, or outside of you, or lifestyle is affecting you. Retesting gives you an additional feedback tool to know if your protocol(s) are working or if you need to pivot.

The first symptom 10% of those experiencing cardiac arrest feel is death. Data in the months beforehand may have saved a life and led them to a path to correct any outpoint contributing to their atherosclerosis. A bone density scan on a seventy-year-old woman who feels well but does not exercise may pick up on osteoporosis. This data could save her from a hip fracture or spinal compression fracture if acted upon.

Hello, and thanks for taking the time to reply! I feel very strange sharing private health info on here, but to make it succinct, I realized after having a couple of cervical spine-related issues come up last year that I've been ignoring my own health for a long time and need to make some changes. I've started this process, but as far as getting to a better latitude, that's where my uncertainty lies. I can test by visiting places, but think it would be pretty selfish of me to move my family around willy nilly. So I'm open to changes, but I want to make sure I make the best ones possible and not waste time being foolish. I come from a digital marketing and tech background, so I do appreciate metrics and common sense approaches combined. It makes a lot of sense what you're saying.

### NikkiYork — 2024-02-17T21:51:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/post-331564>

> [Alexandra said:](/goto/post?id=331550)
>
> Hello, and thanks for taking the time to reply! I feel very strange sharing private health info on here, but to make it succinct, I realized after having a couple of cervical spine-related issues come up last year that I've been ignoring my own health for a long time and need to make some changes. I've started this process, but as far as getting to a better latitude, that's where my uncertainty lies. I can test by visiting places, but think it would be pretty selfish of me to move my family around willy nilly. So I'm open to changes, but I want to make sure I make the best ones possible and not waste time being foolish. I come from a digital marketing and tech background, so I do appreciate metrics and common sense approaches combined. It makes a lot of sense what you're saying.

How is it selfish of you to move somewhere with your family, if that would mean you don’t die quicker than you need to?

But then again, how can you calculate when you will die, or if you would even die prematurely? What if you don’t die, and you get just awfully sick for a long time? Would that be enjoyable for your family?

And on top of that, is it even proven what Jack says? Because really, it hasn’t been studied as much as all the other stuff, and only because he can back up his theses, it doesn’t mean that other approaches could possibly also have a decent outcome.

So there it depends on the experiences you had with regular medicine. Or the experiences others had with it.

I’ve asked Ettlinger, what coach he is talking about, because I’ve only found bad ones. I’ve only found non-helpful doctors.
I’m doing it all myself at the moment and I’m evaluating the math as well.

How many years do I have? Given not only my history, but my mother’s and my mother’s mother. How many years does my husband have? How much are we messing up our child by sending him to unhealthy places, while trying to optimize his future career and options.

In the end it’s math with a ton of variables and probabilities.
Can humans do that math? I don’t think we can and I’ve always been pretty good in math.
How can people who can’t do any math make good decisions? And how does the spider make its net? Do humans have access to a type of knowledge that supersedes math? I would say yes.

The sun is a good start and from there it’s a process and I’m sure it’ll come to you in one way or the other.

Another plus, you’re a mother. Since men can’t be mothers they can’t even fathom the power of a mother and in our generation or century really, we are vastly underestimated and undervalued. That’s definitely something to remember, because certain forces in effect do not necessarily want us to.

### DrEttinger — 2024-02-17T22:30:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/post-331567>

> [NikkiYork said:](/goto/post?id=331562)
>
> What kind of coach would that be?
> Spoiler alert: I’ve paid a couple of thousand to all kinds of coaches who actually made things worse.
> Is there a good kind of coach?

As an example, myself, Dr. Kruse, Dr. Stillman... First, you interview the doctor/coach and see if their philosophy and practice align with you. The more educated you are, the more you will know if this person is right for you. If, after a bit of time, you find they don't align anymore, you pivot.

I have been practicing and researching for 35 years. I have 2 degrees. I still utilize others who can help me in areas I'm weak in.

Lastly, I have had bad coaches, too. That will never stop me from searching for better coaches.

### JanSz — 2024-02-17T23:02:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-know-when-youve-reached-a-good-level-of-optimal.28611/post-331568>

Just so you know.
Multitude of tests you are able to get by buying scripts with your own money.
Then the tests are done by the laboratories that most often would do doctor ordered tests.
When you would buy that kind of tests, the cost is much less than the same laboratory would charge your medical insurance.

I do not only the above
but also, other tests done by laboratories that do not want to deal with insurances.
\-----
Insisting that individual person is not supposed to order his/her own tests (because (he she) is too stupid, does not sound smart (to me).
/////////////////

Have you ever been able to interview a doctor? Not me, hahahaha.
Last time I asked a question at my annual, which was “what do you recommend for losing weight” and his answer was “any mixed nutrition is good, don’t do anything radical.” That cost $200 out of pocket.

My husband fought it, because it really was not a consultation, and we got it back. I use that doctor to order the blood tests my insurance is paying for. He is a nice guy, has not helped with anything so far.

You might be different, Dr. Ettinger, but that’s not common.

Also, I use the word doctor as a derogatory term these days. Mostly it’s just used to pretend authority.

I really respect Jack, that’s when I stopped calling him Dr.  he is the only medical person I know who makes sense to me as of now. I would t call him a coach though, but I guess that just wording.

I looked you up, Dr. Ettinger and you have a chiropractor Dr. So hm. I don’t believe in great benefits of chiropractic and I’m sorry to tell you that, because it’s your job.

I’ve done hair for the last 6 years and I don’t believe in any of that either. does that seem fair?

I need to look up Dr. Stillman, thanks for your answer. Much appreciated.

I'm sorry you have had such bad experiences. I've not had that, so I don't know what that frustration feels like. My DC degree is just part of what I do; it does not define me or how I practice. It's the degree, along with a BSc in human biology, that offered me the knowledge base I sought, holistic over allopathic.

I believe everyone has a right to know the person they are going to work with, whether it's the body, finance, or home. This is on the home page of my website."Please call me today to schedule your complimentary 15-minute phone consultation. Let’s see if you and I are a good fit. I truly want you to feel both excited and confident that I have an understanding of your situation and a plan that’s right for you. I look forward to hearing from you at 714-639-4360."

Since I see my patients often and share with them the knowledge and wisdom I have acquired over the years, I do use the word coach. I am a doctor who coaches his patients back to health and coaches them on how to maintain it over a lifetime.

"I'm convinced that about half of what separates the successful from the non-successful is pure perseverance."

# Thread: DHA & 'Cell Membranes'
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-cell-membranes.28614/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mike7.83 — 2024-02-18T00:08:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-cell-membranes.28614/post-331575>

There is constant mention of cell membranes in this forum but having read a lot of Gilbert Ling's work, he mentions that there are no cell membranes. What he did say was that cells are enclosed in multiple layers of structured water. A query that I have regarding 'essential' DHA. Have there been any studies performed on Mead acid for energy transfer in the brain and other tissues compared with DHA? DHA is docosahexaenoic acid 22: \[n-3\] 4,7,10,13,16,19 omega 3 polyunsaturated fatty acid. Mead acid aka eicosatrienoic acid 20: \[n-9\] 5,8,11 omega 9 polyunsaturated fatty acid. There seems to be a particular fetish for studying omega 3 & 6 polyunsaturated acids but very little meaningful study of omega 9 polyunsaturated fatty acids that humans can make in abundance. Is it possible that we do not need any omega 3s & 6s and can function fully on self-made as-needed omega 9s? Thanks.

# Thread: Healing PCOS, making homocysteine connections with blood clotting issues…
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-pcos-making-homocysteine-connections-with-blood-clotting-issues%E2%80%A6.28615/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Niki Stanton — 2024-02-18T16:07:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-pcos-making-homocysteine-connections-with-blood-clotting-issues%E2%80%A6.28615/post-331586>

When learning from Dr Jack and from Dr Masgutova (primary reflex integration- I believe there’s a link here- story for another day)- there are so many layers and connections that point to absolute truth in their teachings.

Dr Jack talked about pcos sufferers having a blue light in the retina issue- ding ding. As a child of divorced parents I would fall asleep to the tv every night in my room at my dads house. I’m sure the gardasil vaccine didn’t help matters at age 13.

I was fortunate to live in south Florida and be a very so my fitness level and sunlight counterbalanced my blue light abuse. I still struggle with tech addiction and have to constantly put myself on tech fasts and such.

Our firstborn was a surprise and has subclinical CP- just low enough muscle tone for me to notice with my subpar Occupational therapy degree- but not low enough for Docs to admit. Dr masgutovas interventions at a year allowed him to start walking by 15-16 months and has helped tremendously. All of her (additional, outside of reflex) advice has aligned 100% with Jacks teachings. She always tells clients to avoid blue light, be outside at least 2 hours a day, etc.

I posted about him in the kids section. He’s doing great and jacks specific interventions are helping a ton- mostly the emf avoidance, grounding, and seafood for him. We also use the nanovi device which was a huge healing boost for him- energy, endurance, cognition.

Our second born was cooking during the Zika virus scare. So since I wasn’t fully awakened he didn’t get sunlight in utero. I also took iron with him which I thought caused my nearly catastrophic 50% placental abruption during delivery. But now I’m thinking about homocysteine.

I had labs recently that showed levels out of range for it and have recently had horrible menstrual clotting. Then I had very strange blood clotting type issues though not exactly clotting. I had red splotches on my hands after gentle weight bearing and has red purple spots on my sit bones from sitting on a hard surface.

I started taking B12 and B6 and this last week of my cycle is feeling much better. I had to take a class in Orlando under blue light with minimal breaks and stayed in a condo building (won’t make that mistake again). Came home and broke out in a shingles rash which lit up when I drove under high powered transmission line. Sunlight and iodine paint completely cured it.

Anyway. Interesting connections. Blessed to live close to the shore but feeling a little panicky about the 5G poles that are sprouting up like weeds all around me. We live in a single family home on a quarter acre-ish and have our sights set on an acre and a quarter when finances line up. Not sure where else to go that won’t have the poles in-town.

Goes without saying but I’ll say it anyway- thank you for sharing this with us, Dr. Jack.

### Niki Stanton — 2024-02-18T16:46:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-pcos-making-homocysteine-connections-with-blood-clotting-issues%E2%80%A6.28615/post-331589>

I have to add that my second born (the Zika scare pregnancy) has a “birthmark” over his left eye- interesting yes? He also is still struggling to tan. So many freckles.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healing-pcos-making-homocysteine-connections-with-blood-clotting-issues%E2%80%A6.28615/post-331590>

>
> I had labs recently that showed levels out of range

# Thread: Is history repeating in the US?  Was Caeser's Rome a warning?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-history-repeating-in-the-us-was-caesers-rome-a-warning.28617/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-19T03:17:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-history-repeating-in-the-us-was-caesers-rome-a-warning.28617/post-331617>

Is history repeating in the US right now in real time? Was Caeser's Rome a warning?

It´s astounding how close our time is to the conditions that led to the rise of Julius Caesar in Rome - Rome was being fleeced by a greedy elite (called the Optimates) who were monopolizing land with usury and slave labor - These elites were migrating huge volumes of slaves to work the fields for low wages (sound familiar to WEF agenda or the open border plan everywhere?) - This drove down the price of labor and ended up pushing native Romans out of work and off their land - The elites would buy up these vacant family farms and move more slaves onto them - Today we have farmers under attack, and the Fed raising rates and BlackRock buying homes and now stacking BTC to buy it all when things in real estate really blow post US election.

These same elites would then give out loans to these broke Romans to help them "set up a new life in the city", which would put them into debt slavery even further - This is why car loans now go out to 96 months and home loans out to 50-75 years. That is a life of debt slavery. The city of Rome itself swole up with a huge disgruntled class of poor veterans, native Romans, and recently migrated slaves (sound familiar?) - These formed a jaded underclass while the elites lived in slave supported luxury - Roman soldiers would go abroad and risk their lives conquering territory for Rome in hope of "making it", only to get underpaid, and then come back to find their families replaced by slaves from the place they just conquered (sound familiar?) - Caesar was one of the elites who sided with the native Romans - known as a Populist - He used "the People" as a force to pushback against the elites and drive through reforms that limited debt slavery - Through decades of ruthless civil war he came out the winner, but was infamously assassinated by the elites for subverting their grip on Rome. History is repeating and few see it.

[https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1759072760066965979](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1759072760066965979)

Is this why some of us have risked moving out where freedom is sprouting?

According to Bobby Kennedy, BlackRock, Vanguard, and State Street will own 60% of America’s single-family homes by 2030. They also own 88% of the companies on the S&P 500. This is a massive problem if you are a US passport holder with fiat only in the bank and one zero in Bitcoin.

### ['They Can Outbid Your Children': RFK Jr. Warns That Corporations Are 'Trying To Buy Every Single-Family Home' In America — And They Are On Track To Own 60% Of Homes By 2030](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/outbid-children-rfk-jr-warns-164021972.html)

Housing affordability has been a pressing issue in America. While many people point to supply shortages, rising material costs and stagnant wages as drivers behind the crisis, Democratic presidential candidate Robert F. Kennedy Jr. believes there's another culprit — corporations. "The real...

If you really want an answer to what's happening, you need to start by asking yourself what happened in 1971 during the Nixon shock. # Thread: J's Optimal Journal

### doctorwu85 — 2024-02-19T16:53:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/js-optimal-journal.28619/post-331641>

New member here, excited to participate in the knowledge exchange.

I found out about Uncle Jack through the Mitochondriac Manifesto book and the Ben Greenfield podcast. My wife and I are both naturally very skeptical of "the system" in any iteration, (e.g. I was doing the paleo diet in 2010 when it was still not a mainstream idea), so the general anti-centralization message really resonated with us. One of the recent pre-Jack decentralized medicine ideas that really helped us was Klenner's Vitamin C protocol for pregnancy, which we believe facilitated a smooth pregnancy and home birth with zero issues.

My wife started doing the circadian rhythm stuff last year after seeing Sara Kleiner's posts on Instagram, which we now realize was basically repackaged Uncle Jack material. Just by blocking blue light at night and seeing the sun in the morning, she was able to lose 15 lbs over the course of a few months and get back to her pre-pregnancy weight with minimal change in her diet and exercise levels. We also started using a vortexer to structure all of our water last year, and she noticed that her recurring pain from an ACL reconstruction 10 years prior has totally disappeared. I also saw some slight health benefits from it.

My job requires me to be around EXTREMELY bright LED light after sundown for about half of the year, and I could never figure out why I slowly but steadily gained weight during this time, despite exercising almost every day and eating largely keto-carnivore. Once this period ended this year and my schedule became more normal, I started eating a regular breakfast, seeing the morning sun, and blocking blue light at night. I lost several pounds effortlessly and became a believer in the importance of light, water, and magnetism. Can't wait to implement it during the busy season this year.

Over the winter we replaced all of the lights in our house with incandescent bulbs, and we're very conscious of having minimal household lights on after sundown. We both wear VivaRays glasses every night to block blue light, get outside in the morning sunlight (not always exactly at sunrise, but within the hour after) and for a couple minutes at sundown, and try to time-restrict eating to 10 hours. We've ditched all sunscreen and sunglasses, and we take no prescription medications. We also have not taken any experimental medical interventions that have come out in the last couple years. I've dabbled with cold plunges, and I plan to make this a regular practice over the next few weeks. We're not perfect by any means, but I think we have the big items checked off to improve our redox.

Our most recent project has been to move about an hour outside the city to a more rural area. We've noticed the grounding is better and there's a much lower nnEMF load overall. In our previous home we'd pick up 10-12 wifi networks, whereas at the new home it's ours and one neighbor's, and the strength is much weaker/slower than in the city. We're at 34 latitude - not amazing for sun in the winter, but enough to get some benefits, and still works well in the summer. Nearly all of our food is local, organic, grass-fed meat. The baby eats what we eat, just as a paste instead of full chunks

We also had a building biology firm to come and measure our house for nnEMF. Based on their dirty electricity readings we bought a Satic PowerPerfect box that has helped reduce the house's dirty electricity fields. They said that we had very little RFs in our area, though I would like to get a better sense of wifi-type nnEMFs within the house before we go full hard-wired. Judging by phone reception just outside the neighborhood, there is not a lot of 5G in the area... yet.

I'm still trying to learn as much as possible. I'm in the middle of reading The Body Electric, with 4th Phase Of Water next on my list.

Here are some of the questions and issues we're having:

\- Our 1-year-old has eczema on parts of her legs. We are getting her out into the midday sun more and will soon be spending 6 weeks at a lower latitude (27), so we are confident the stronger sun will help. We had her in the direct midday sun for 30-40 minutes per day last summer and she did not develop this issue until the sun weakened in the winter. People used to joke about our baby's great tan, so we'll get back to that point and report back. I suspect it will clear itself up with stronger sun exposure.

\- Baby also has FPIES (type of food allergy) to a couple of foods, so we suspect some sort of gut imbalance. Otherwise she has been extremely healthy -- two fevers in her life, never an ear infection, etc. We are supplementing with liposomal Vitamin C and a gut health supplement our functional medicine doc recommended, and we're hoping she'll grow out of it. My wife makes all of her food using grass fed ruminants and organic/local vegetable ingredients, so I'm hoping continued nutritious food will strengthen her gut.

\- I am extremely allergic to most fish and oysters (FPIES-type vomiting reaction). I know Dr. Jack has said that this is a redox issue. I try to get as much DHA as possible from lamb, which we eat 2-3 times a week, but recognize this is probably not ideal. Not sure if there is a solution here besides improving overall redox and hoping for the allergy to lessen in severity. I also have a mild allergy to eggs, which I still eat occasionally but should probably eliminate due to the redox/inflammation effects. I attribute both of these to getting the full childhood schedule in the mid-80s.

\- We each regained a few pounds after stressful travel and some non-leptin friendly circadian and diet stuff over the holiday week, and we've plateaued off since then. Probably need to do the full leptin reset again to get going, which is already in progress.

\- Considering getting a Magnetico pad for the bed, as long as it's OK for the baby as well. I have read through some of the experiences on the forum but open to any advice about whether they make a noticeable difference.

\- Miron glass - Found out about this on the forum and have one on the way. Can't hurt to further energize water once in a while.

Short term goals:

\- Get 1-2 hours of direct sun every day. Due to the nature of my job over the next few months, this should not be difficult

\- Get 3-4 cold plunges in per week. I'm planning to buy one of the plunge buckets while we're at our temporary home. Is there any back of the envelope way to estimate heat loss given mass, the temperature of the water, and the time spent? Would be curious whether 3 minutes in 55 degree water or 10 minutes in 65 degree water is more effective, for example. I don't want to overthink it so I'll just do what feels correct, but always enjoy quantifying things when possible.

\- Tighten up diet. Have been enjoying an occasional baked good with breakfast lately. We don't drink caffeine, but we've also been having a few decaf coffees per week. Coffee beans are far from native to our area, so I recognize this is a sub-optimal seasonal/light mismatch. I am very sensitive to carbs so my default is to maintain ketosis-level carb intake (<40g) on most days. Will be giving up almost all coffee for 6 weeks and report back.

\- Work in more HIIT sprints. My cardio endurance has dropped as I've favored more outdoor garden work and landscape construction type projects to get into the sun.

\- Figure out how to rewire the house to hard-wire internet, since only one jack is active and it's far from my office. We have wifi on during the day but unplug at night.

\- Reduce computer screen exposure at night. I'm wearing blue blocking glasses and have a deep red / flicker reduction setting on Iris, but still not ideal. I've scaled back the latest computer-using time by about half an hour in the last week.

\- Buy a couple of EMF meters capable of assessing hazards other than dirty electricity. Still researching these on past forum posts.

Open to any feedback or suggestions that anyone has. We've been telling everyone who will listen about blue light, circadian practices, and light-water-magnetism, and have even gotten a couple friends to try them out. It takes a certain personality type to be interested in counter-culture medical and biological knowledge, but we'll keep trying to help our friends and family as much as possible.

Thanks for reading.

### JanSz — 2024-02-19T18:01:16+0200

All animals, birds, fish farmed are fed by extreme amounts of seed oils (Linoleic Acid LA Omega-6)
All organic anything including so called wild but raised by humans are fed by remains of seeds after (most) seed oil were taken out of it.
Farmed animals, birds, fish, when killed are really on their last legs, very sick. Usually just few days when they would die on their own if not slaughtered.
See discussions on why steak meat is tender.

To get rid of currently excessive LA, about 5-6 years of diligent effort is need, due to very long half life.

So, any carnivore diet is highly suspect, if one worries about health.
Jack talking about food, especially when lately, mentions mostly oysters.
I think that other than oysters, all kind wild seafood is the last place we have to go to.
If you have a gun and dear in your backyard, that maybe one way to get some meat that has normal LA levels.

//
Drink plain water.
//

Speaking only about ruminants and Linoleic Acid.
Ruminants have two stomachs. And comparing to other creatures that we eat; they are able to deal with LA the best if fed with it.
\===================================
Note that they (farm that you posted picture) are insisting on talking about combination of fatty acids, O3 or O6.
While LA is individual fatty acid.

Still the largest number they came up with is 15:1

But when one would do test and check individual
LA/ALA
Even laboratories think that 34:1 is acceptable.
But they never seen healthy person, before about 1850 or 1900.
That was the time when due to industrial revolution, machines extracting oil from seed came to existence.
Around 1945 and latter, my dad (in Poland) was feeding his animals on the farm with remains of seeds.
The seed oil was used to make margarines and similar.
Also, about sugar, we ate molasse from sugar beets.
In USA everything industrial happened 100 years earlier.

One of the girls here, having health problem, posted her numbers.
She has **180:1- LA/ALA**

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-23T08:31:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/js-optimal-journal.28619/post-331848>

Your WiFi (if you have) & your neighbors WiFi and your personal phones, due to proximity, are probably a greater source of RF than the cell tower. The 60Hz AC field from the wires in the walls and everything plugged into them is likely a more significant source of nnEMF than the RF. Check your body voltage in your sleep areas, at night turn off the circuits for a week and see how you feel: [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### doctorwu85 — 2024-02-23T15:15:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/js-optimal-journal.28619/post-331853>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=331848)
>
> Your WiFi (if you have) & your neighbors WiFi and your personal phones, due to proximity, are probably a greater source of RF than the cell tower. The 60Hz AC field from the wires in the walls and everything plugged into them is likely a more significant source of nnEMF than the RF. Check your body voltage in your sleep areas, at night turn off the circuits for a week and see how you feel: [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

Thank you Anthony, all great reminders. These are the sleep precautions we normally take, regardless of whether we're at home or traveling:

\- Unplug our unit's wifi at night (and anytime we're not actively using it during the day)
\- One phone is kept on airplane mode outside the room, the other is on airplane on the bedside table (2-3 feet away from body)
\- Unplug the salt lamp that's by the bed
\- No other electric devices in the room
\- We have two portable plugin dirty electricity filters, one in the bedroom and the other in the kitchen

I used a Greenwave meter in several of the outlets in the condo, including the bedroom, when we arrived. They were "high" but still not terrible (150-180), and none of them are close to our bodies while sleeping. Compare this with the time I got a 1500 (!) in a hotel room outlet next to the head of the bed.

I just got a body voltmeter in the mail, and did the sleep area measurement like you suggested, and it read 0.155V relative to the ground pin in the wall outlet. I took a reading in the kitchen and it was higher (0.50). I checked around the forum a bit and I couldn't find a scale of how bad different voltage levels are, if anyone has a guideline like that please feel free to share.

I also tested my body voltage outside while standing on the grass with the black pin in the ground and it read 0.00, which I think is what should happen. Though I'm not sure if there's a way to measure relative voltage if there's a current already in the ground (e.g. there are underground power lines but I'm standing on the same ground), but I might have that wrong. I might upgrade to one of your products if necessary once we get back home.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-23T19:07:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/js-optimal-journal.28619/post-331873>

The Building Biology Institute BV reading at night
<10 milli volts (mV) - no concern
10-100 mV - slight concern
109-1000 mV - moderate concern
\>1000 mV - extreme concern

If you are double grounded anywhere you will get a low or zero reading. For an accurate BV reading of the electric field being induced into your body, your only connection to ground must be the ground cord from the BV meter. Measuring ground currents is different and requires more expensive equipment.

A shield between you and the phone, even in airplane mode, is an added precaution.

# Thread: Oral Health for Children - An Quantum Biology Approach
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-health-for-children-an-quantum-biology-approach.28621/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### robtherhino — 2024-02-19T18:21:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-health-for-children-an-quantum-biology-approach.28621/post-331648>

Hi everyone,

TL;DR - what is a quantum approach to remineralising teeth for a 10 month old?
I've been suggested grounding, lots of sunlight, and for my wife to drink sole \[so-lay\] water. Anything else?

**Background**
Recently my 10 month old son started having a black spot and signs of demineralisation on his first two teeth.
For context, he had an anterior and posterior tounge-tie and some oral trauma from birth.
We got the tounge tie sorted around 3 or 4 months old, as well as guidance from an oral myofacial therapist to do a mouth massage daily.
Despite that, he has had a fairly consistent tounge thrust this whole time.

We've been to a conventional dentist and was told it was likely caused by lack of minerals around 6 weeks into development.
We should avoid citrus, brush twice a day, and use remineralising toothpaste.
It's also recommended to do fluoridation treatment when he is a bit older, which neither my wife nor I are keen on.

He is breast feeding and just started weaning.
I've started making raw milk kefir and feeding him bone broth / meat stock.
I've also spoken briefly with Kelly Bento \[soleil well\] who suggested the quantum practises in the TL;DR above.

Would appreciate any further guidance, reading, info, etc. on the subject.

### Niki Stanton — 2024-02-19T19:18:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-health-for-children-an-quantum-biology-approach.28621/post-331658>

> [robtherhino said:](/goto/post?id=331648)
>
> Hi everyone,
>
> TL;DR - what is a quantum approach to remineralising teeth for a 10 month old?
> I've been suggested grounding, lots of sunlight, and for my wife to drink sole \[so-lay\] water. Anything else?
>
> **Background**
> Recently my 10 month old son started having a black spot and signs of demineralisation on his first two teeth.
> For context, he had an anterior and posterior tounge-tie and some oral trauma from birth.
> We got the tounge tie sorted around 3 or 4 months old, as well as guidance from an oral myofacial therapist to do a mouth massage daily.
> Despite that, he has had a fairly consistent tounge thrust this whole time.
>
> We've been to a conventional dentist and was told it was likely caused by lack of minerals around 6 weeks into development.
> We should avoid citrus, brush twice a day, and use remineralising toothpaste.
> It's also recommended to do fluoridation treatment when he is a bit older, which neither my wife nor I are keen on.
>
> He is breast feeding and just started weaning.
> I've started making raw milk kefir and feeding him bone broth / meat stock.
> I've also spoken briefly with Kelly Bento \[soleil well\] who suggested the quantum practises in the TL;DR above.
>
> Would appreciate any further guidance, reading, info, etc. on the subject.

We had that issue. My kids are 6 and 9 now. What helped was in addition to sun water and magnetism, adding in supplemental vitamin K, making sure no mouth breathing at night. (If so, figure out why- chronic congestion? Mold exposure?) make sure emf is mitigated, no RF baby monitors, look into emf safe for low frequency emf in babies room. Avoid dairy even raw kefir until you have some variables knocked back. You won’t know if dairy is inflammatory causing nasal congestion or mouth breathing til you get other things sorted.

Current issue of the black staining might indicate nutritional status well before now.

If oral motor skills continue to be an issue, especially tongue thrust, find a highly skilled practitioner either in speech language pathologist that is also myofunctional trained, or possibly needs primary reflex training via MNRI therapist.

Avoid fluoride at all cost. Find a different dentist because it can be hard to hold your ground as a new parent. Best wishes to you on your journey.

2\. Check mouth breathing at night.

# Thread: Blue Light Therapy for Hyperbilirubinemia in Neonates
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-therapy-for-hyperbilirubinemia-in-neonates.28623/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-02-20T18:04:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-therapy-for-hyperbilirubinemia-in-neonates.28623/post-331707>

I understand this practice is used to break down elevated levels of bilirubin, but I would assume, based on what I understand from Kruse, that this practice could cause serious, irreversible damage to the eyes and also to brain development of preterm babies.

I have a friend that works in the NICU. He is the one who brought this to my attention, bc he knows my thoughts on blue light hazard. He doesn't believe blue light is a problem and says the neonates are perfectly fine. Also, explains how this practice has been used for decades.

He uses this as a major example as to why blue light is no biggie and that I "overreact" to its "dangers." I know better though.

Is this practice really necessary? I do know babies would be much better off in sunlight, but I also understand that American hospitals aren't going to start putting babies in the sun any time soon. So, is blasting them with this blue light the next best thing?

He also explained to me that babies sometimes soak in this intense blue for up to a week straight which blows my mind!

Anyone have any additional info regarding this practice? I am very curious bc I do plan to have kids soon

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-02-20T18:40:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-therapy-for-hyperbilirubinemia-in-neonates.28623/post-331708>

Ah, I realize now that eye protection is used for the infants when this is being done. That is good to know, but there is still the issue of their skin getting blasted with blue after sunset...

### jules3791 — 2024-02-20T21:51:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-therapy-for-hyperbilirubinemia-in-neonates.28623/post-331712>

I seem to recall that when my first born had jaundice, I was told (unsure by whom) to take him outside in the sun. That was almost 30 years ago.

### doctorwu85 — 2024-02-21T18:18:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-therapy-for-hyperbilirubinemia-in-neonates.28623/post-331739>

Yep, sun exposure will fix this unless it's an extreme case. Bilirubin lights are no good. Screencaps from "How To Raise A Healthy Child In Spite of Your Doctor" by Robert Mendelsohn, an excellent book for any parent:

# Thread: One meal a day and sleep
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-meal-a-day-and-sleep.28625/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jelle — 2024-02-21T10:09:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-meal-a-day-and-sleep.28625/post-331727>

Hi all,

I'm experimenting with one meal a day. I try to eat all my food for dinner. I noticed a few things:
\- Energylevels are great and recovery from exercise also is fantastic.
\- Sleep is a bit worse. I get awake earlier (instead of 8 hours of sleep I have 6,5 hours know). I think my cortisol is higher what causes worsening of my sleep.

Anybody has experience with this and has comments on my findings? It would be great if I have both: high energylevels troughout the day and perfect sleep.

Thanks!

### doctorwu85 — 2024-02-21T14:09:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-meal-a-day-and-sleep.28625/post-331728>

I did OMAD for a little while and had decent success, but had better success when I ate breakfast upon waking as recommended in the Leptin RX.

For me, the one thing was that I couldn’t eat the meal too late. If I eat within 4 hours of bedtime my sleep is noticeably worse. It might be worth trying to push the feeding window up to mid-afternoon and see if you still have the sleep issues.

### jules3791 — 2024-02-21T16:15:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-meal-a-day-and-sleep.28625/post-331736>

I agree with [@doctorwu85](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29980/), eating late does not bode well for sleep. I eat upon waking and depending on my lunch amount and timing then tells me if/when I eat dinner, preferably before 6pm. The earlier dinner is for me, the better.

### Alper — 2024-02-21T18:32:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-meal-a-day-and-sleep.28625/post-331740>

[@doctorwu85](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29980/) Correct me if i am wrong but Big ass breakfast in the leptin rx is meant for those who are leptin resistent, so if you arent leptin-resistent, BAB is not the best choice i gues.

[@jelle](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29277/) eating time depends on your sunset, according to your local situation. So if the sun sets at 7pm, you gotta eat before the sun sets.

### doctorwu85 — 2024-02-21T18:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-meal-a-day-and-sleep.28625/post-331744>

>
> [@doctorwu85](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29980/) Correct me if i am wrong but Big ass breakfast in the leptin rx is meant for those who are leptin resistent, so if you arent leptin-resistent, BAB is not the best choice i gues.

I'm saying that, subjectively, I felt better when I ate breakfast while doing the leptin reset, so I have continued to do it. Therefore, it is the best choice for me.

I don't eat a BAB, more just getting 40-50g of protein and some healthy fats.

Hypoxia is a cellular state that disrupts regular oxygen supply to the tissue (mitochondria), causing cellular dysfunction. Examples of this are altitude sickness at high elevations and clots in a blocked artery in an organ, causing an organ to fail and die. Apoptosis and autophagy allow cells to adapt over their lifespan to many situations. Hypoxia is directly toxic to mitochondrial energy production via a lack of beta-oxidation. When oxygen is in short supply, humans can shift to anaerobic energy production. Still, it is not as efficient as mitochondrial energy production in the human brain and heart because of how many mitochondria are found in these two organs.

When mitochondrial oxygen consumption shows in any human tissue, dissolved oxygen in the cell rises, and this sets the stage for excessive ROS production, which ruins optimal TCA function. This is when NAD+ is being lowered.

Due to pseudohypoxia, NAD+ levels drop, and this funnels back to how the aromatic amino acid tryptophan is catabolized in cells. This affects NAD+ creation and melatonin production at the mitochondrial level. Tryptophan has multiple metabolic pathways it can travel, and the pathway chosen will lead to the cell's fate. That case was made deeply in the Hypoxia series, and collateral effects were laid out in the Quantum Engineering series. Hypoxia one covered this in detail.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-22T17:12:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/post-331798>

Purines are a natural substance found in the body. They are also found in many foods such as liver, shellfish, and alcohol. They can also be formed in the body when DNA is broken down. When purines are broken down to uric acid in the blood, the body gets rid of it when you urinate or have a bowel movement. This removal process allows humans and birds to conserve mitochondrial water.

Parkinson's disease patients suffer from hypoxia of their pigmented neurons in the brain. This leads to melanin breakdown and lowered levels of endogenous dopamine. Uric acid, a metabolite of purine biochemistry protects neurons from melanin degeneration and dopamine loss. In primates, having a higher level of uric acid is protective of hypoxia in dopamine-producing neurons. It appears evolution knew what it was doing when it altered the uric acid pathways in the big-brained primate clade.

Parkinson's Disease (PD) patients have been found to have significantly lower levels of serum Uric Acid than controls (p = 0.000) in published research. This fact is not well-known in clinical medicine. In fact, in patients on L-dopa, the situation is even more dire and shows us why Big Pharma solutions often run contrary to nature's recipes. In patients with PD, serum UA levels were much lower in the groups under L-Dopa + dopamine agonist treatment. This means L-DOPA will accelerate PD disease.

NAD+ accepts two electrons and one proton to be reduced to NADH.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-22T17:33:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/post-331808>

NAD+ is reduced using 2 hydrogen atoms. The two hydrogen atoms can come from one reactant in lactic acid fermentation or from two (in a reaction of glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate to 1,3-bisphosphoglycerate. Never forget that the human brain runs on lactate and not on glucose as centralized science beliefs and repeats in textbooks.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-22T17:35:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nad-is-the-key-marker-for-aging-of-the-quantum-cell.28630/post-331809>

Phosphate, on its own, has one hydrogen at cytosolic pH because of the pKA. Thus in this reaction, there are two hydrogen atoms. One hydrogen atom is in the form of a hydride (a proton and two electrons) from the aldehyde group. This hydrogen atom is used to reduce NAD+ to NADH. A second hydrogen atom (in the form of a proton) comes from the phosphate. In the bisphosphoglycerate form, the proton dissociates from the phosphate group and is in solution.

The eventual fate of the electrons that were used to reduce NAD+ to NADH is the electron transport chain (in the mitochondria). In some cases, though, for example, muscle cells in an anaerobic environment or other species that don't have an electron transport chain, the electrons are used in fermentation of pyruvate to alcohol or lactic acid. When NADH donates its electrons in either case, it is then oxidized back to NAD+.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-22T17:40:44+0200

As you can see from the slide below, the reduction reaction transfers proton (H+) and hydride electrons (H- or 2e-) to NAD+. One electron goes to the nitrogen, and the other to the carbon, where the proton binds.

Today I give you a gift. It is my mitochondriac anniversary on April 2nd. I post this for my Mom. She used to call me at this time every year. Now i am calling you, my tribe. It is time to wake up.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154)

"The sage doesn't seek to be profound but is useful. A sage himself is a former fool who got tired of themselves."

This insightful quote paints a picture of wisdom born not from an innate desire for grandeur but from a humble journey of self-awareness and growth. A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. Their usefulness emerges from their centralized experience, mistakes made, lessons learned, and a quiet resolve to move beyond the foolishness of their centralized past. It’s a reminder that true depth often comes not from chasing profundity but from the simple, steady act of outgrowing one’s former self.

# Thread: Focal Dystonia - where to start?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/focal-dystonia-where-to-start.28631/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JPlessis — 2024-02-22T16:58:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/focal-dystonia-where-to-start.28631/post-331794>

Hello everyone,

My name is Jesse Plessis, I'm new here after listening to Dr. Kruse's episode of Tetragrammaton with Rick Rubin and Dr. Huberman, which was my first encounter with his ideas which Iinterested me very much.

I'm 36 years old, and in the autumn of 2020 I developed focal dystonia (as I was undergoing some pretty heavy life stresses, as seems to be the case often with this condition), which as you can imagine is very distressing for a pianist: my right hand cramps involuntarily when I try to play the piano (index finger pulls up, middle pulls toward the palm), and sometimes when I type, and my right knee, hip, lower back, scapula, shoulder, elbow, and jaw all have a baseline of co-contraction tension that is quite high. Experientially it feels like there are areas of my body I'm not in touch with, or can't feel, or have a confused relationship with.

I have tried neurofeedback on the advice of a neurologist (who took an EEG and some questionnaires and told me I had C-PTSD that she suspected was linked to the dystonia, which seems to follow logically from a pretty rough start on this planet), 8 months of weekly brainspotting therapy, 6 months of compassionate inquiry therapy, 5 months of hypnotherapy, and I have been retraining movement at the piano for two years with a student of Dorothy Taubmann (a New York pianist who was well known for her wise physical approach to piano playing).

I have also made some big lifestyle changes: reducing emotional stressors, exercising six days a week (including swimming, running, bodyweight, yoga, and kung-fu), eating a whole foods diet, and taking care of my sleep including yoga nidra several times weekly. I'm feeling much better in general, and I would definitely say that I'm not living with trauma anymore (at least not in a way that affects my daily life in an extremely negative way as before).

I have made some progress in dystonia recovery, and have regained a lot of my previous dexterity and reduced my performance anxiety quite a lot, but it is still present enough to encumber my relationship with music and my ability to be my best.

I will post this in another appropriate forum to have guidance and feedback from the other members on this site, but if you could supply any blog posts, protocols, readings, books, and other avenues that would be particularly relevant and best to start with I would really appreciate it.

Thank you very much for your cameraderie and support - I get a good vibe from the posts I've been reading. And take care of yourselves!
Jesse

My mom has had some knee pain that hasn't gone away. She's not sure if it's in her joint or the surrounding area, but walking downstairs is harder than upstairs. This all sort of happened after her shingles shot, so I'm thinking it's either a high viral load or inflammation from the shot. She doesn't recall really hurting it, but does walk a bunch each day and has never had any issues with her knees before.

### JanSz — 2024-02-22T23:56:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacks-for-knee-pain.28634/post-331840>

Around 1985 I was still working. 45yo at that time.
From time to time, I would get pain in one knee. Short lasting, in and gone.
After MRI doctor have not found any problems but suggested that there must be some small interferences there. Think of minute grain of sand in the ball bearing in the car. Floating around but getting caught sometimes.
Suggested that he could look it up and remove.
Afterward I myself suggested that he check my other knee.
No problem since.

# Thread: Low histamine and low purine seafood
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-histamine-and-low-purine-seafood.28635/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9

### healthy2015 — 2024-02-23T19:03:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-histamine-and-low-purine-seafood.28635/post-331871>

Can any one recommend low histamine and purine seafood.
I have been looking online but the information is confusing.
Thanks

### JanSz — 2024-02-24T02:35:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-histamine-and-low-purine-seafood.28635/post-331883>

100g of fresh, uncooked seafood:

1.  [**Cod**: Histamine: <50 ppm](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[1](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[2](https://www.coleparmer.com/tech-article/histamine-concentration-in-seafood)[3](https://www.globalseafood.org/advocate/detection-histamine-producing-bacteria-fish/)[, Purine: 63 mg](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[4](https://dr-barbara-hendel.com/en/nutrition/tables/purine-content-table)
2.  [**Salmon**: Histamine: <50 ppm](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[1](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[2](https://www.coleparmer.com/tech-article/histamine-concentration-in-seafood)[3](https://www.globalseafood.org/advocate/detection-histamine-producing-bacteria-fish/)[, Purine: 119.3 mg](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[5](https://www.dietaryfiberfood.com/purine-and-uric-acid/purines-uric-acid-fish-seafoods.php)
3.  [**Trout**: Histamine: <50 ppm](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[1](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[2](https://www.coleparmer.com/tech-article/histamine-concentration-in-seafood)[3](https://www.globalseafood.org/advocate/detection-histamine-producing-bacteria-fish/)[, Purine: 83 mg](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[4](https://dr-barbara-hendel.com/en/nutrition/tables/purine-content-table)
4.  [**Redfish**: Histamine: <50 ppm](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[1](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[2](https://www.coleparmer.com/tech-article/histamine-concentration-in-seafood)[3](https://www.globalseafood.org/advocate/detection-histamine-producing-bacteria-fish/)[, Purine: 100 mg](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[4](https://dr-barbara-hendel.com/en/nutrition/tables/purine-content-table)
5.  [**Zander**: Histamine: <50 ppm](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[1](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[2](https://www.coleparmer.com/tech-article/histamine-concentration-in-seafood)[3](https://www.globalseafood.org/advocate/detection-histamine-producing-bacteria-fish/)[, Purine: 46 mg](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[4](https://dr-barbara-hendel.com/en/nutrition/tables/purine-content-table)
6.  [**Sardines**: Histamine: <50 ppm](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[1](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[2](https://www.coleparmer.com/tech-article/histamine-concentration-in-seafood)[3](https://www.globalseafood.org/advocate/detection-histamine-producing-bacteria-fish/)[, Purine: 144 mg](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[4](https://dr-barbara-hendel.com/en/nutrition/tables/purine-content-table)
7.  [**Mackerel**: Histamine: <50 ppm](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[1](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[2](https://www.coleparmer.com/tech-article/histamine-concentration-in-seafood)[3](https://www.globalseafood.org/advocate/detection-histamine-producing-bacteria-fish/)[, Purine: 60 mg](https://mastcell360.com/the-best-low-histamine-meat-and-seafood-options-info-for-those-with-mast-cell-activation-syndrome-and-histamine-intolerance/)[4](https://dr-barbara-hendel.com/en/nutrition/tables/purine-content-table)
8. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/380nm-light-bulb.28637/post-331891>

I know using lamps/hacks is not recommended here, and there is no replacement for the sun, but thought I’d ask if anyone has experience with this bulb in combo with some IR/Red panel and natural sunlight (not for vit D, for melanin renovation). I know the wattage is low but could buy a few. It shows the spectrum and includes a lot of 380. Assuming they have too much flicker due to being LED?

### [UVA Grow Light: Ideal for Indoor Plant Growth Needs](https://forevergreenindoors.com/products/fgi-uva-385nm-led-bar-single)

Illuminate your indoor garden with the UVA Grow Light, built for durability and optimal plant growth. Order now and see the results thrive!

 forevergreenindoors.com

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-24T19:58:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/380nm-light-bulb.28637/post-331903>

A 30watt LED light puts out the equivalent light as a 240watt incandescent bulb. They vary but LED are about 8x more efficient. Not all LED’s flicker, just have to test.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-02-24T20:55:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/380nm-light-bulb.28637/post-331912>

Remember 42% of sunlight is IR and only about 5% UVA (depends on location/time of year etc). IF you want to try to duplicate full spectrum natural light - keep these ratio’s in mind. Some “full spectrum” LED grow, aquarium, reptile lights have IR and UV built in.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-25T04:15:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morphogenesis-links-to-mitochondria-via-light.28641/post-331935>

Morphogenesis links to mitochondria via light.

The DC electric current is the intermediary that does the morphogenesis and regeneration job in cells.

The TCA cycle is one level of energy generation in cells whose stochiometry cannot account for the ATP made in a cell. So what processes in man provide the rest? Semiconduction and melanin biology are two others. In total, I believe there 4 others system that help the TCA cycle keep the ATPase spinning.

When we lose metabolic water in our colony of mitochondria, we lose superconduction in nanotubes, time speeds up for life, and it first ages quickly and then dies. This is the key metric to TBI and many collagen vascular diseases. This is one of the cornerstone mechanisms of how all neolithic diseases today are created. When water is part of a semi-conductive and coherent energy transfer system, there is little or no free diffusion in such a system. It has a zero entropy. A biophysicist named Herbert Fröhlich pointed out in the 1960s that energy transfer by using water’s molecular resonance or coherent excitations has to occur through large distances, activating entire populations of similar molecules that are in different parts of the cell or different parts of the body, so long-range coordinating of function can happen instantaneously. Proton jump-conduction occurs along the long fibers of collagen and the water bound to it and belongs to a form of semi-conduction in the condensed matter called a liquid crystal. This allows for instantaneous action within the living organism. This proton conduction is much faster than the conduction of electrical signals by the nerves in the peripheral nervous system. The collagen fibril cytoarchitecture of the entire body has a much better intercommunication system than can be provided by the peripheral nervous system alone. That may help explain the observations of why lower animals, like a hydra, that do not have a nervous system are nonetheless sensitive and responsive.

Physics considered Fröhlich ideas radical in the 1960s because instrumentation was so poor it could not prove his theories. Most of his theories are true today because our testing technology is far superior. In QED, there is something called the [Bose-Einstein Condensate.](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9RDocoSWqPY) Fröhlich believed that living biological systems are “open” and very far from equilibrium and have considerable amounts of energy available, built into their design, that could cause non-linear changes in molecules and larger biological subsystems. He was the first biophysicist to believe that biology works at a distance because it too was far from equilibrium, and he felt it might work like the Bose-Einstein condensate principles in quantum electrodynamic theory (QED). He proposed long-range coherent action in living tissues in 1967 at the L’Institut de la Vie.

This idea was backed up fully by Becker, who confirmed that a body plan charge polarization that seemed to direct regeneration and healing was found in all the animals he tested. Water chemistry is key to this mechanism. It won't surprise me if it is holographic and light trapped in the living state by the delta psi on the mitochondrial membrane built by solar exposure controls everything.

[https://www.cell.com/current-biology/fulltext/S0960-9822(20)31073-3](https://www.cell.com/current-biology/fulltext/S0960-9822\(20\)31073-3)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-25T04:16:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morphogenesis-links-to-mitochondria-via-light.28641/post-331936>
