### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-25T15:04:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morphogenesis-links-to-mitochondria-via-light.28641/post-331939>

The TCA is like water.....it is not homogenous in all tissues. Water networks are not always H20, as the textbooks say.

Why must we see the sunrise? Sunlight must hit the membrane that houses Kreb's cycle to get it to spin correctly to innovate life. The TCA cycle has different carbon and hydrogen movements in different tissues. This means the TCA cycle is highly dynamic in different tissues, but this point is never amplified in biochemical texts. If the TCA does not spin properly, disease or death results. It is that simple. What does sunlight do to Kreb's cycle? Every sunrise, they all spin in a clockwise direction in unison to remove H+ and move carbon from the chemical intermediates to supply the mitochondrial matrix with its fuel source. Istopic LIGHT Hydrogen. Mitochondria are solar hydrogen furnaces.

Lipogenesis occurs in most tissues to maintain organelle membranes and growth. Still, it is most active in the liver and adipose during feeding to convert excess carbohydrates to lipids for long-term calorie storage. The circadian biology of the skin control liver TCA dynamics. Lipogenesis occurs in the cytosol through the condensation of consecutive units of acetyl-CoA, most of which is derived from mitochondrial PDH during carbohydrate metabolism. For mitochondrial acetyl-CoA to be trafficked to the cytosol, it is first condensed with OAA to generate citrate in the first step of the TCA cycle, and it is then actively transported by the citrate-isocitrate carrier (CiC). Cytosolic ATP–citrate lyase regenerates OAA and acetyl-CoA in the cytosol. These steps remove citrate from mitochondria and require continuous anaplerosis to maintain lipogenesis.

Interestingly, in vitro characterization of the CiC indicates that it couples citrate transport with malate antiport. Thus, cytosolic OAA could be converted to malate and exchanged back into the mitochondria with no further carbon transitions, obviating the need for other anaplerotic support. However, loss of CiC causes decreased TCA cycle pool size and lactate accumulation in cell models and humans, suggesting that citrate transport is critical to anaplerotic and cataplerotic homeostasis. It also explains why lack of AM sunlight leads to fatty liver states. Indeed, NAFLD subjects have elevated lipogenesis, coincident with increased hepatic anaplerosis and citrate synthase flux. Isotopic tracer studies indicate that citrate lyase is a significant source of OAA used by PEPCK-C. This pathway contributes to glycerol-3-phosphate for fatty acid esterification via the glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase pathway and is particularly important in adipose tissue, which lacks glycerol kinase.

Insulin is a solar hormone.

During obesity, type 2 diabetes (T2DM), and nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD), insulin fails to suppress cataplerotic PEPCK flux to gluconeogenesis. However, PEPCK-C is not overexpressed in all rodent models of diet-induced obesity or in humans with T2DM.m PEPCK seems to be deactivated by deuterium content in the diet or allowed access to the metabolic pathways of man by its high KIE

## ANAPLEROSIS AND CATAPLEROSIS differ in skeletal muscle​

Why are there so many Achilles tears in NFL players today? Most wear ungrounded shoes and never get solar exposure over their calves where the skin has POMC. Bulk water is a dipole, and dipoles have to remain with a neutral charge. Water becomes a liquid crystal when sunlight hits it. Crystals are capable of nonlinear functions. Liquid crystals elevate the ability to use certain nonlinear processes like second harmonic generation (SHG). SHG in collagen is an exciting science. In the 1970s, G.H. Bardelmeyer found this electric ability in the Achilles tendons of animals. He found the ability was tied to free proton water electricity from water and light. This is why Achilles' tears are always a sign of poor connections to the sun and Earth at some level. His results make sense in the recent observations made in experiments that proton superconduction occurs in nanospace-confined water below 1.6 nm. Water at this scale does not act like modern fluid flow dynamics believes. I cover this in the May 2017 webinar for the skeptics. The critical discovery of superfast quantum tunneling in chiral water polymers was vital. Collagen is also chiral. Around chiral collagen, water hydrates surround this molecule, and you can see how the body plan is built in animals and can be 100% light-activated and controlled.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-27T18:34:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-civilization-fail.28645/post-332002>

Anyone looking for proof that the medical establishment has gone mad need look no further than the University of California at San Francisco. Two weeks ago, an Adjunct Assistant Professor of Medical Education at Mayo Clinic College of Medicine and Science and a guest UCSF faculty member told a 300-person audience that white people are racist because they are genetically predisposed to psychopathy. Need more? How about Dr. Sherita Golden, a professor of medicine and vice president at Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine, who sent out a mass email last month with a list of identities to repent for, including: “white,” “able-bodied,” “heterosexual,” “cisgender,” “male,” “Christian,” “middle or owning-class,” “middle-aged,” and “English-speaking.”

Public outrage led Dr. Golden to apologize and delete her X account. Oh, and this past November, the American Psychiatric Association published Gender-Affirming Psychiatric Care. This ostensibly definitive textbook offered politicized polemics such as “scientific neutrality is a fallacy”, denounced the “mythical man-woman binary”, and used the words “oppress” 60 times, “power” 62 times, “victim” 63 times, “intersectional” 62 times, and “queer” 83 times.

Nearly 7,000 healthcare professionals signed an open letter calling the work unethical and unsafe. What is going on? Once a conservative-to-moderate profession, medicine in America has swung left – very left. And this politicization is alienating mainline America and undermining health care. The transition has come fast. Data from Stanford political scientists Adam Bonica and colleagues shows that over the past 20 years, medicine has gone from 60% Republican to 80% Democrat. This shift is still underway.

### [How America’s medical system has become totally woke](https://nypost.com/2024/02/17/opinion/americas-medical-system-has-become-totally-woke/)

In barely two generations, American medical care has gone from being staffed predominantly conservative practitioners to those who are mostly liberal.

 nypost.com

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-11T07:42:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-on-your-playlist.28649/post-334590>

A song can sounds like heaven.......yet it can burn us like hell, why?

Our brains are wired to associate certain songs with specific events, people, or experiences in our lives. These associations can evoke strong emotions, either positive or negative. For example, a song that was playing during a happy moment might evoke feelings of joy and nostalgia, while a song that was playing during a traumatic event might trigger feelings of anxiety or fear. Music has an incredible ability to tap into our emotions, memories, and experiences. When a song resonates with our emotional state, it can be euphoric, making us feel like we're in heaven. Conversely, if a song triggers painful memories or emotions, it can be like a burning fire, causing discomfort and distress.

Music can be incredibly vulnerable, exposing our deepest fears, desires, and vulnerabilities. When we listen to a song that speaks to these vulnerabilities, it can be both cathartic and uncomfortable, like walking through a fiery inferno. Music creates an intimacy with our Nature.
Music can create a sense of intimacy, connecting us to the artist, the lyrics, and the emotions conveyed. When this connection is strong, it can be exhilarating and uplifting, but when it's not what we expected or wanted, it can be painful and disappointing.

Music can be a powerful tool for catharsis, allowing us to process and release pent-up emotions. When we listen to a song that captures our emotions accurately, it can be a liberating experience, like a weight being lifted off our shoulders. Music can be a powerful trigger for memories, both good and bad. When we hear a song that's associated with a painful memory, it can be like reliving the experience all over again, which can be emotionally overwhelming. The context in which we listen to music can greatly influence how we perceive it. For example, listening to a happy song during a difficult time might provide comfort and solace, while listening to the same song during a happy moment might be annoying or overplayed

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-11T07:45:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-on-your-playlist.28649/post-334591>

Is there a hidden law of a probable outcome, as Sting wrote in his music?

1.  **The Butterfly Effect**: This concept, popularized by chaos theory, suggests that small changes in initial conditions can lead to drastically different outcomes. While not a direct prediction of a probable outcome, it highlights the sensitivity of complex systems to initial conditions.
2.  **The Monte Carlo method**: This numerical technique uses random sampling to estimate probabilities and predict outcomes. While not a deterministic "law," it's a powerful tool for modeling complex systems.
3.  **The concept of entropy**: In thermodynamics and information theory, entropy measures the uncertainty or disorder in a system. While not directly predicting outcomes, entropy can be used to understand the probability distributions of certain events.
4.  **The concept of emergence**: This idea suggests that complex systems can exhibit properties that are not predictable from the behavior of their individual components. Emergence can lead to unexpected outcomes, but it's not a direct prediction of a probable outcome.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-11T08:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-on-your-playlist.28649/post-334593>

When we look at someone's eyes, we can sense that they are looking into ours, and sometimes it feels like they can see into our very soul. This phenomenon is often referred to as "eye contact" or "gazing." It's as if our eyes are open doors, allowing others to peer into our inner world.

There are several reasons why this might happen:

1.  **Emotional resonance**: When we're deeply engaged with someone, our emotions can synchronize, creating a sense of empathy and connection. This emotional resonance can make us feel like the other person is "looking into" our eyes, even if they're not physically doing so.
2.  **Nonverbal communication**: Our eyes are incredibly expressive, conveying emotions like happiness, sadness, fear, or excitement. When we're in a situation where we're feeling vulnerable or exposed, it can feel like our eyes are "open doors" to others' perceptions.
3.  **Psychological intuition**: Some people may possess a natural ability to pick up on subtle cues from others, such as body language, tone of voice, or facial expressions. This intuition can lead them to feel like they're "looking into" someone's eyes, even if they're not consciously aware of it.
4.  **Attention and focus**: When we're fully engaged with someone, our attention and focus are directed towards them. This concentrated attention can create the illusion that our eyes are "open doors," allowing others to see into our inner world.
5.  **Synchronicity**: Sometimes, the timing and synchronization of eye contact can create a sense of connection or shared understanding. When we're looking at someone who's looking at us, it can feel like our eyes are "open doors" to a deeper level of understanding.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-11T08:18:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-on-your-playlist.28649/post-334594>

Mother Nature seems to allow life on Earth to swing on the spiral of our divinity and still be a human.

I'm touching on the idea that our existence on Earth is deeply connected to our spiritual nature, and that our humanity is an integral part of the natural world. It's as if we're part of a larger, intricate web of life that is infused with divinity. In many spiritual and philosophical traditions, the concept of "divinity" is not limited to a specific deity or supernatural being, but rather refers to the inherent, sacred quality of existence itself. This perspective suggests that everything in the universe, including human beings, is imbued with a spark of the divine light.

From this perspective, the idea of "swinging on the spiral arm of the galaxy to sample our divinity" is particularly evocative. It implies that our lives are part of a larger, cyclical pattern that is connected to the rhythms of the natural world. The spiral shape is often associated with growth, transformation, and evolution, suggesting that our journey as silly talking monkeys is characterized by a continuous process of unfolding and self-discovery.

The idea also highlights the idea that our humanity is not separate from nature, but rather an integral part of it. This perspective is often referred to as "ecosophy" or "deep ecology," which emphasizes the interconnectedness of all living beings and the importance of preserving the natural world.

By recognizing our place within the natural world and our connection to the divine, we can cultivate a deeper sense of reverence, respect, and responsibility for the planet and all its inhabitants. This understanding can inspire us to live more harmoniously with nature, to prioritize sustainability and environmental stewardship, and to strive for personal growth and spiritual development.

# Thread: Carrageenan is a problem in Franken foods
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carrageenan-is-a-problem-in-franken-foods.28650/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-02-29T00:40:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carrageenan-is-a-problem-in-franken-foods.28650/post-332035>

Lots of people have complained about carrageenan as a food additive. I've never found a good reason to be concerned about it, but this is concerning. In this model, carrageenan (CAR) primes white blood cells to overreact to infection (represented by LPS). "Our results demonstrate that activated leukocytes produce a large amount of TNF-a and that this plays an important role in the pathogenesis of liver injury in the CAR-pretreated endotoxin shock model." That could certainly explain the intestinal upset some report. "Carrageenan Primes Leukocytes to Enhance Lipopolysaccharide \[LPS\]-Induced Tumor Necrosis Factor Alpha Production" [http://doi.org/10.1128/IAI.67.7.3284-3289.1999](https://t.co/4pvNKmbirq)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-02T15:35:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eye-floaters-what-is-the-decentralized-perspective.28655/post-332125>

Blue Light is Giving You Eye Floaters More and more young people are reporting eye floaters. Why? Because Blue light accelerates vitreous degeneration in the eye! The vitreous has a lot of melanopsin it > This leads to vitreous opacity and increased floaters. "While blue light helps to maintain normal biological rhythms, excessive blue light radiation may induce fundus lesions." Studies show Vitreous floaters are higher among digital device users. Protect your vision. Limit screen time and wear blue light blockers. Study: [https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32244150/](https://t.co/3sWk9ZcWTb)

# Thread: Ladies your reproductive system ages at a different rate than you do.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-03T17:04:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332161>

### 1 in 6 people globally affected by infertility. The number is close to 2.5 out of six in Americans. I consider infertility in humans as I would consider a mutation in DNA or mutant disease. Today we know we can rescue fruit flies from their genetic fate. This implies that light maybe behind why humans are becoming infertile at record rates in a modern world that is blue lit, filled with RF and microwaves pulse radiation while blocking the healing rays of the sun in the visible spectrum.​

. The reproductive tract of women is one of the first organ systems to show hallmarks of ageing, in comparison to other organs.

Difficulty conceiving and infertility are treated as taboo topics in most nations and lead to significant psychological stress for those experiencing them ([Patel et al., 2018](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1568163721001252#bib0670)). Although assisted reproductive technologies (ART) such as [cryopreservation](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/biochemistry-genetics-and-molecular-biology/cryopreservation)(the freezing of oocytes, sperm, or embryos) and _in vitro_ fertilization exist, they are not ubiquitously available or successful and require substantial financial investment ([Katz et al., 2011](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1568163721001252#bib0455)). Furthermore, a fertilized oocyte derived from a woman of advanced age has a higher chance of resulting in miscarriage, and/or aneuploid offspring like [trisomy](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/biochemistry-genetics-and-molecular-biology/trisomy) of [chromosome 21](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/biochemistry-genetics-and-molecular-biology/chromosome-21), commonly known as Down syndrome ([Bittles et al., 2007](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1568163721001252#bib0105)).

With advancing age, a decline in mitochondrial number and function is observed in oocytes. Mitochondria are exclusively maternally inherited and therefore the original population present in the oocyte will give rise to all future mitochondria in the offspring ([Jansen and de Boer, 1998](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1568163721001252#bib0415)). Thus, elucidating the mechanisms underlying the loss of mitochondrial function with ageing and why oocytes age much earlier as compared to other organ systems, may lead to new therapeutic strategies to prolong oocyte fitness and fertility.

nnEMF ages your oocytes faster than it ages you.

Hard truth here.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T03:30:37+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T04:48:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332175>

Menarche and menopause are change states in the female life. All transion state require energy consumption. Thermodynamic principles stipulate that without the input of energy, a system is doomed to either drift toward increasing entropy or to remain in its initial state. For this reason, any type of change must consume energy. This is the proof of work requirement needed in all decentralized systems.

In other words, change costs energy.

But living cells and organisms constantly face the need for change, including energetically costly transitions from one cellular state to another, or from one position to another within the organism. Bearing adequate resources, all developing cells and subpopulations of adult cells must, at some point, transition from their relatively undifferentiated pluripotent identity to some “terminal” specialized identity in tissues. Therefore, understanding the core subcellular mechanisms that monitor a cell’s energetic status and that also energetically sustain their challenging life transitions, has the potential to uncover biological principles that are the basis of human health, or that falter in chronic diseases.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T04:55:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332176>

The cell’s software is the energy it can transform from the sun. This software program in mitochondria includes the program(s) executed by the hardware (DNA), which comprise biomechanical, bioelectrical, and transcriptional dynamics.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T04:58:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332177>

One of the hallmarks of multicellular organisms is that the activity of individual cells is harnessed toward broader organismal goals. This includes creating complex anatomical structures and morphologies (i.e., morphospace). Starting from the fertilized egg, all descendants of that cell must differentiate, migrate, and cooperate with each other to eventually reach the possible region with configurations that correspond to the normal species-specific anatomy. Yet, after differentiation, cells keep changing and adapting in response to environmental cues & stimuli. They are constantly actively monitoring their state, computing errors from their final position and function, and working to reduce those errors. These energy-consuming actions require reprogrammability in the organism.

While DNA genomes encode the specific hardware that each cell has access to (via the proteins specified by the DNA sequence and chromatin state), the behavior of each cell and the cell collective is driven by the physiological software that supervenes on the hardware. Note that the hardware-software analogy used here and in synthetic bioengineering and evolutionary developmental biology refers not to the anthropomorphic origin of some (but no longer all) software but rather refers to the fact that the exact same hardware (genomically determined cellular components) can give rise to numerous different outcomes. Just like the same physical computer hardware can run or execute vastly different software (text processing, video streaming, etc.).

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T05:01:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332178>

A cell’s hardware includes the proteins (semiconductor) encoded by the sequences of its genome that are epigenetically “open” and active or “closed” and repressed, plus the complex set of organelles, proteins, and molecules it contains at any given time.

1\. Zeng,H. What is a cell type and how to define it? Cell 2022; 185: 2739–55.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T05:07:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332179>

On the scale of individual cells, differentiation from stem cells to specific fates involves decisions that shape the hardware and the subsequent activity and responses, which is seen in transcriptional, translational, and biophysical states. For example, each active gene (POMC) comes at a substantial energetic cost (UV light exposure and photon capture).

The hardware of a differentiating cell is altered by actively repressing and silencing specific genes, while others are opened and transcribed. This gives rise to new proteins that must be energetically synthesized. Each peptide bond cost cells 4-5 ATP molecules per amino acid, while others are actively degraded or secreted.

Jewett, MC, Miller, ML, Chen Y et al. . Continued protein synthesis at low \[ATP\] and \[GTP\] enables cell adaptation during energy limitation. J Bacteriology 2009; 191: 1083 – 91.

The cell’s software is also reprogrammed by increasing or decreasing the resting plasma membrane potential where the TCA is housed, tweaking connections to surrounding cells, migrating, reconfiguring cytoplasmic constituents, and other non-random changes. Thus, as cell fate transitions change a cell’s molecular identity—as well as its role within the organism—it requires widespread energy input to reprogram both the cellular hardware and software. As a result, reprogramming and cell fate transitions may represent one of the most stressful and energetically costly events that a cell has to go through. Light drives everyone of these transition.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T05:09:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332180>

Importantly, the costs of stress and other processes are generally cumulative. This means that the energy required to power hardware/software reprogramming during cellular transitions must come on top of what is required at baseline to prevent entropy and sustain life.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T05:12:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332181>

But, living systems possess a finite capacity to transform energy. The interplay of limited energetic capacity with the competing demands of molecular processes leads to energy constraints both within cells and organisms. The limited energy budget (kleibers law or heteroplasmy state) means that not all cellular or physiological systems can be turned on at the same time.

Menopause turns off energy consumption in women to drive longevity. Men generally still have to generate sperm cells all their life post puberty and this is why they die sooner than females

Moreover, some transformations are also not compatible and must be separated in time (e.g., catabolic and anabolic pathways). Therefore, by necessity and to preserve efficiency in a given physiological context, one state must be dominant over other ones. Fertilty has high energy costs for women.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T05:20:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332182>

Aerobic mammalian cells derive most of their usable energy from the oxidative phosphorylation system within mitochondria. The OxPhos system pulls and reacts hydrocarbon-derived electrons with oxygen to build a transmembrane charge that powers ATP synthesis. For this reason, mitochondria seem to play a "permissive role" in sustaining life and enabling all psychobiological life transitions.

By producing ATP on demand—that is, when ATP is hydrolyzed into ADP and inorganic phosphate—mitochondria essentially “keep the lights on”, enabling life’s vital operations. But the stoiciometry of ATP never adds up to what life needs. Therefore we need to understand the other systems that the cell uses to drive phase transitions in life. Melanin biology is usuallly the wizard behind that curtain. But an important question is whether beyond its permissive role, energy transformation by mitochondrial OxPhos also plays an instructive role in shaping major cellular transitions, including differentiation from a stem-like precursor cell type to a terminally differentiated state. Or might melanin biology play that leading role?

In 2024 longevity researchers still believe that chemiosmosis can drive all ATP needs even though Gilbert Ling proved 70 yrs ago this is not true, and impossible In fact, Peter Mitchell's theory breaks the second law of thermodynamics by close to 500%. Cells cannot make enough ATP they consume.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T05:58:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332184>

In puberty and in menopause, naturally occurring transitional cells in the gonads of women upregulate a key transcriptional stress pathway called the integrated stress response (ISR). Thus, during normal development, transitional cells show evidence of biological stress in general and activation of ISR in particular.

The ISR, sometimes referred to as the mitochondrial ISR (ISRmt), is an evolutionarily conserved intracellular signaling network triggered in response to several environmental and cell-intrinsic stressors, including but not limited to nutrient deprivation, infections, misfolded proteins, and oxidative stresses

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T06:00:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332185>

Upon stress signaling, phosphorylation of the translation initiation factor 2 (eIF2) signals through CHOP \[CCAAT/enhancer binding protein (C/EBP) homology protein\] (Ddit3) and activating transcription factors Atf4 and Atf5 to upregulate the expression of several downstream genes Other stress signal transduction cascades may also converge on the ISR. Light stress is one.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T06:02:48+0200

How do we measure the stress response or heteroplasmy rate in people who have this problem?

One of the best-described ISR targets, growth differentiation factor 15 (GDF15).

GDF 15 is transcribed and secreted into the extracellular space, making its way into the bloodstream to exert broad systemic signaling effects. GDF15 concentration is elevated in the blood of patients with OxPhos defects linked to heteroplasmy increases in tissues.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T06:04:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332187>

How does the brain stay informed of energy deficits/heteroplasmy rate rises in tissues?

Another ISR target, fibroblast growth factor 21 (FGF21), is also systemically upregulated in human and animal mitochondrial OxPhos defects, where it contributes to systemic ISR signaling.

The ISR-GDF15 axis appears to act as a metabolic stress signaling network, which among other roles may exist to inform the brain of energetic/redox stress among somatic tissues

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T06:09:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332188>

ATP deficiency is an oxidative deficiency in cells related to TCA failure.. Mammals can stimulate their ISR response via a reductive stress. A reductive stress occurs when there is an increased NADH/NAD+ratio, which is a natural consequence of mitochondrial complex I deficiency. Thie lowered NAD+ ratio tells us their is lowered engine power due to a lack of solar exposure. Most reductive stress is caused by a lack of sun (Via low Vitamin D levels) and/or excessive use of light during the night.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T06:11:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-your-reproductive-system-ages-at-a-different-rate-than-you-do.28659/post-332189>

NADH donates its electron to complex I, oxidizing it back to NAD+. It was previously shown in myoblasts that oxidizing cytosolic NADH to NAD+ is sufficient to prevent ISR activation in complex I-deficient cells. Recent work also confirmed that selectively inducing NADH reductive stress, without OxPhos deficiency, is sufficient to activate the ISR in mammals. Sunlight can be used to regenerate NAD+. Optogenetic research has also used light to regenerate NAD+.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-04T06:15:14+0200

The cellular energy landscape required to sustain stressful and costly transitions involves not only the phosphorylation potential (ATP/ADP/AMP) that powers several enzymatic reactions but also the redox potential (NADH/NAD+) that powers and regulates perhaps an equal, if not larger, number of molecular transitions in a cell.

In mammals, GDF15 also appears to recruit the glucose-releasing and hyperglycemia-promoting hormone corticosterone, further mobilizing energetic resources to fuel energy-starved ISR-positive and GDF15-secreting cells and tissues. The hardness of money has to be costly in time and not in energy. Linking money to energy alone does nothing improve scarcity. Every improvement in energy production would allow man to create more money. When you make money costly in time, you create absolute scarcity because you cannot create more time. The same thing is true in biology. Linking life to mitochondrial energy does not create longevity. Every improvement in energy production in mammals (melanin) does mean immortality it creates organismal complexity. This is how we went from the two domains of life to eukaryotes. A better timing mechanism was critical in this transition. This is the real lesson of the Cambrian explosion. Mother Nature linked longevity and health to circadian timing. She used aromatic amino acids and melanin to do it in the eukaryotic tree.

This makes TIME, the ultimate resource in any decentralized network. Mother Nature invented this idea first in evolution. The cell became the ultimate format of the living state because cellular duplication by sex is linked it directly to the ultimate resource of the cosmos: Circadian timing.

Fecundity is controlled by the leptin melanocortin pathways that go from the retina directly to the SCN.

# Thread: STOP ALL ASPIRIN FOR CARDIAC PREVENTION
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stop-all-aspirin-for-cardiac-prevention.28661/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-05T21:26:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stop-all-aspirin-for-cardiac-prevention.28661/post-332240>

The story this week centers on the use of aspirin for prevention of cardiac events in people _without heart disease_. I used italics because this story ONLY applies to people without heart disease—so called primary prevention.

In 2018, the NEJM and the Lancet published three trials that randomized tens of thousands of patients _without_ heart disease to either low-dose aspirin or a placebo. [ASPREE](https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMoa1805819) studied older people; [ASCEND](https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/nejmoa1804988) studied patients with diabetes, and [ARRIVE](https://doi.org/10.1016/S0140-6736\(18\)31924-X)studied patients deemed to be at higher risk of heart disease. The main question of these studies was whether aspirin reduced the rate of future cardiac events, without increasing the rate of bleeding.

The gist of the results is that aspirin used for primary prevention provided no net benefit. ARRIVE and ASPREE did not report statistically significant lower rates of cardiac events. ASCEND reported a 12% reduction in cardiac events but a 29% increase in bleeding.

These three trials strongly argued against using aspirin for the prevention of future cardiac events—in an average patient.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-05T21:29:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stop-all-aspirin-for-cardiac-prevention.28661/post-332241>

Your greatest life asset is your ability to earn. Your best way to earn is via Bitcoin. Your greatest life resource is your time. You parse your time best when you think in decentralized fashion first.

How has Big Pharma set up their ability to earn? They have moved from drugs to vaccines for disease. Why? Jabs are the only product created on Earth that makes more money as it fails. Every time a vaccine fails, the company doubles its profits, because now they can sell a “booster” which is just a new name for the same old vaccine that has failed. The faster it fails you, the more money Big Pharma makes.

The profit center does not end there. Big Pharma knows when the jab fails side effects result. A failing product creates another sales funnel of profits to be made off all the new drugs necessary to treat all the adverse events from the vaccines. This is a great gig if you can build it.

You need help from the public to accomplish it. Telling people to take aspirin for a headache was establihed early on, not tell they to do the same to prevent a heart attack. For the public you should train them apriori to be centralized thinkers in schools before they go to doctors. Make sure they ask for a pill for every ill at every visit. Reenforce that decision making. Pill for every ill works on the placebo effect. Patients learn to ask for drugs when they are ill. Make sure you build electronic medical records systems that list the diagnosis to the treatment codes and treatment options. You train docs to use the EMR. They have to click buttons to finsih their charting. At the end tell MDs in residency and medical school it's best coding to write Rx at the end of their treatment notes. It will lead to better pay. Incentives drive behavior.

Never forget the lesson built into the Allegory of the Cave. That lesson is still operational today for Big Pharma. Most people end up being conformists; they adapt to prison life they are accustomed to. A few become reformers; they fight for better lighting, better ventilation. Hardly anyone becomes a rebel, a revolutionary who breaks down the prison walls. You can only be a revolutionary when you see the prison walls for what it is in the first place.

Dr [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) As you know -> Deuterium depleted water has a greater effect on the heart than aspirin.

Deuterium depleted water stimulates the GLUT4 translocation in the presence of insulin and lowers blood glucose concentrations.

[Checking your browser - reCAPTCHA](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34510301/)

After the 8-week treatment, our results indicate that, in the presence of insulin,

-   deuterium depletion markedly reduced serum levels of glucose, -fructose amine, and -HbA1c, in a dose-dependent manner.

These data suggest that deuterium depletion dose-dependently enhances the effect of insulin on GLUT4 translocation and potentiates glucose uptake in diabetic rats, which explains the lower serum glucose, -fructose amine, and -HbA1c concentrations.

Based on our experimental data, deuterium-depleted water could be used to treat patients with metabolic syndrome (MS) by increasing insulin sensitivity.

CVD has more to do with blood sugar management that a drug thinning therapy.

# Thread: Let‘s talk about „the Hum“
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/let%E2%80%98s-talk-about-%E2%80%9Ethe-hum%E2%80%9C.28662/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2024-03-05T23:26:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/let%E2%80%98s-talk-about-%E2%80%9Ethe-hum%E2%80%9C.28662/post-332247>

I heard it two years ago briefly, and now I‘ve been hearing it for about 2 months every night: the Hum!

It’s not in my head, because I just got back from camping two hours up north. And there was no hum, total quiet.

I researched it a bit and the best I came across is an engineer who overlaid the map of Hum hearing people with a map of forced gas pressure pipes. It’s very striking how similar it is.

However, it might still be a different thing and the gas pipes could also just pick up the resonance from somewhere else.

About 5 percent of people hear it. I‘ve talked to many of them online who I think have a mental illness over everything else. Then there are tinnitus ones.

Then we have regional hums - like the Taos hum - that might be different from the world wide hum. Maybe they are all different.

Animals have been reactive to some homes with hums - scared, and it seems that some homes conduct a hum better than others.

My neighbor got me into it, by constantly asking, if I heard it. Now I can’t unhear it.

Interestingly enough it might be related to my vibrating chest at night, but I’ll put that into my journal.

The hum is just made for conspiracy theory. I sure don’t know what it is, but I can hear it. During the day there is usually too much other noise. It’s a very low frequency droning. Like somebody lets his diesel motor run idle. Sometimes it pulses. The pulse can pick up speed. And multiple people hear that speed difference.

Is it intended? Is it industrial? Is it harmful? Maybe even beneficial? Or if it’s not beneficial, can it be turned into something beneficial?

Oh the mysteries of this planet

### NikkiYork — 2024-03-05T23:29:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/let%E2%80%98s-talk-about-%E2%80%9Ethe-hum%E2%80%9C.28662/post-332248>

Oh, and I tested my hearing. I can’t hear anything below 20hz, so I’m not special. Many people can hear 20hz. I would think that’s about the frequency of the hum.

It’s very base like, if you remember going to clubs and feeling the music in your chest.

### John Schumacher — 2024-03-06T01:37:09+0200

[@NikkiYork](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/28233/),
I'm glad to hear you had a "rest" from the "hum" during you camping trip.
I've never been to a club nor loud concert so I don't know what music in my chest might feel like.

I prefer the quiet sounds Nature provides.
Each morning sunrise (for the past fifty years), barefoot on the moist earth, I take time to notice...

Thank you for your post,
Grandpa John

### NikkiYork — 2024-03-07T22:38:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/let%E2%80%98s-talk-about-%E2%80%9Ethe-hum%E2%80%9C.28662/post-332296>

Same!

So yesterday the hum was so loud, that my chest vibrated.
I went crazy 3 years ago because of chest vibrating at night. Had it for 2 years, then it stopped. I thought it was me. Now I know it’s not. Or possibly tense muscles vibrate more, but initially it’s actually a sound doing this.
3 years ago I was just so surprised that I would wake up at 4 on the dot every night.

And then it stopped on day. It went on, off, on off and stopped.

I have no idea what it is, pumps? Drilling? I‘ll try to find that photo of the gas line map.

### Saichi — 2024-07-15T00:25:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daylight-phone-to-go-with-your-daylight-computer.28664/post-337166>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=337163)
>
> Maybe we should find out if an AM signal from a DC powered device is harmful in any way before condemning a product and freaking people out.

I'm certain EMI of high power density is harmful regardless of source. I haven't condemned anything btw.

I found this quick description online:

> ## How to Reduce Electromagnetic Interference (EMI)​
>
> EMI issues can occur in any type of electronic device, ranging from common items like a cell phone to sophisticated systems such as an avionic electrical system. Implementing certain measures can enhance the design process for any device, with emissions measurements serving as the initial step.
>
> Reducing EMI starts at knowing what the causes are and being able to measure them. A few key factors to accomplishing this is:
>
>
> 1.  Proper grounding and shielding materials as needed.
> 2.  Use of EMI filters, decoupling capacitors, and ferrite chokes.
> 3.  PCB layout with minimal trace lengths.

I just measured my Samsung S5 cell phone (the oldest version Verizon allows me to have and still function) and the EMI within three inches with wifi, bluetooth, mobile data turned off is crazy. There's also an intermittent rapid Russian Woodpecker in the EMI.

# Thread: Dopamine and kids
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dopamine-and-kids.28665/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-06T16:28:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dopamine-and-kids.28665/post-332260>

Managing dopamine is a big issue with the latest generation of kids and it has led to many unusual behaior characteristics now being linked to their generation. I am not interested in sterotyping kids, I am interested in understnading why their choices and behavior has changed compared to other cohorts. I think their environment is the key piece to this puzzle.

Most of the milennials today have grown up in blue lit world and nnEMF everywhere. Most of the nnEMF is RF and microwaves which is particularly known for lowering dopamine and melatonin production in recent research. Dopamine is incredibly important in making choices and in time management. Melatonin is important in sleep quality and duration and mitochondrial energy production.

These frequencies of nnEMF are in their schools, it's on their phones where they get their porn, video games and the internet service. At the Facebook’s scale, behavioral targeting doesn’t just reflect their generations behavior, it actually influences it. This is why malls are closing. Kids no longer like malls because everything is now online. These kids are fine buying their clothes online and not running wild at malls. This is why social media sites like Facebook have "like button's" on threads. It is there to reenforce the light driven processes that make dopamine levels in the eye dynamic and controllable remotely that are going below our perception during our engagement on line.

Over time, a "social media service" which was 'originally' supposed to connect humanity, is actually partitioning us into fractal disconnected bubbles of altenrative realities that are now stereotyping these kids. I think it is obvious why they are different and it is because we've allowed our kids to become blue light toxic from womb to their first job. It follows them home on their laptops, LED TV's, and light's illuminating their homes. Both destroy dopamine levels in the retina and brain.

The collateral damage of a low dopamine level is a low ocular melatonin level in the eye. This generalizes to the frontal lobes as mentioned in my Ubiquitination 24 blog. This is why milennials GLOBALLY have massive spikes in myopia and sleep problems for the age compared to older cohorts. We've never seen this before but we act like we do not know why. We know why but technology companies are using this to build fortunes off our population by controlling the decision making process and suggesting behaviors that are beneficial to their profits. This low level creation of the biogenic amines in the eye has down stream effects in their frontal lobes to affect the reward process and alters their behavior. This is why companies cannot keep these kids in jobs long at all.

This dynamic change in dopamine production is capable of spreading like a prion disease to deeper levels in the brain as these kids age. I also expect to see more mitochondrial disease in younger people and this process has already begun in clinics. Doctors all are reporting massive increases of chronic diseases in young kids which is driving the acuity rates in medical care way up. This is not something physicians were taught to expec, much less explain or understand.

The fluctuating dopamine levels come from light's ability to alter voltage gates in dopaminergic neurons and it can spread like a wild fire into the ventral segmental areas of the brain to cause many diseases in young kids. Strokes now are common in teenagers and twenty year olds. The use of opiates in kids is now at record levels and we act like we have no clue. Some of us a DEEP clue because we're paying attention to how modern life has changed in the last 20 years. Dopamine is the neurohormone made normally from UV/IR light exposure that supports the development of the DC electric current in the retina. The only thing that milennials made sure they went outside to see lately was the 2017 eclipse. Prior to that they were inside texting, Facebooking, googling, and working on their laptops. This is why it is happening: Reality 12 blog.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-06T18:50:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dopamine-and-kids.28665/post-332263>

**Today’s lesson:** Change is excruciatingly difficult for low agency people because their biological system lacks dopamine creation in their frontal lobes due to a lack of sun. A low dopamine frontal lobe consistently overestimates the value of what they presently have while simultaneously underestimating the value of what they may gain by giving that up now. They do not understand opportunity cost in anything. Unless you are prepared to give up something valuable to you right now, you will never be able to change at all. You’ll remain forever in control of the things you cannot give up. Low dopamine = low agency and it binds us to this reality and keeps us unskilled in communicating with ourselves or nature. To become a decentralized thinker requires solar dopamine optimization

### Mat.I — 2025-05-29T12:14:36+0300

No solar light = no dopamine. No dopamine = no frontal time. No frontal time = no agency.

Time is written by dopamine in light-entrained circuits.

# Thread: re: February 2019 ALS vblog:  Observations from a layman
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-february-2019-als-vblog-observations-from-a-layman.28668/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### clemsnwill — 2024-03-07T01:40:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-february-2019-als-vblog-observations-from-a-layman.28668/post-332275>

TLDR: this layman has been living with ALS symptoms for 5+ years and thinks Dr. Kruse is spot on here, as usual

1.) It is interesting to me that Dr. Kruse mentions channelopathies here. Prior to my ALS diagnosis i asked the neurologist about the possibility of channelopathies after noticing the similarities in how, with the slightest bit of anxiety, i would tighten up and fall with how the myotonic goat falls. My exact question to neurologist:

"If statins have been shown to interfere with calcium ion channels (Article A) and myotonias are caused by disruptions (genetic) in calcium ion channels (Article B), then is it possible that statin related interference with calcium ion channels could present similar symptoms to a (genetic) form of myotonia?"
Article A: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5030724/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5030724/)
Article B: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4234136/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4234136/)

The question was intriguing enough to prompt the dr to order a blood test for channelopathies, but the results came back normal
Is it coincidence that farmers treat myotonic goats with taurine and some ALS patients see benefits from taking TUDCA?

2.) MAVS: Mid-December '23 our house caught a particularly bad case of the flu. It was rough but our immune systems kicked in and did their jobs. After recovery i noticed some of my ALS symptoms seemed a bit better, especially my chronic sinus inflammation. It's as if having a proper immune response to the flu righted my ship (or plugged a few holes at least) in a way. Similarly, my future ex-wife regained her annoyingly strong sense of smell that she lost during covid 2 years prior.

3.) Inflammation & Melanin: My neurologists had me taking Theracurmin, as recommended by the top ALS researchers at ALS Untangled. It seemed to help the most with movement and coordination, so I took it daily for ~2 years, until:
1 April '23 we took a family vacation to the beach where I noticed I was getting sunburned rather quickly, and rather deeply. I never had this problem before ALS, so I set out to figure out why?. Took me 3 months to find an answer, but it turns out, curcumin blocks melanin production (fact check request here). I stopped the Theracurmin in August and by October my skin was able to tan again. I started astaxanthin per Dr Kruse and am now able to maintain a decent tan even after weeks of little to no sun. Astaxanthin doesn't seem to have quite the same anti-inflammatory properties of Theracurmin, but I will gladly trade the short term benefits to movement and coordination with the long term healing benefits that increased melanin can provide. Thank you Dr Kruse, i likely would not have realized the significance of this connection without your help.

4.) Does a needle EMG produce nnEMF? I had 2 of these procedures and my ALS seemed to spread to previously unaffected areas within 4-6 weeks of each procedure. This was most obvious in my tongue, which was not tested in the first EMG, and was clearly unaffected prior to the second. But within a month of that needle going into the left side of my tongue, my tongue started locking up on the left side in the exact spot the needle went in. My first idea was the needle was contaminated with dysfunctional mitochondria from another part of my body, but it now seems more probable that the electricity from the needle puts the surrounding mitochondria on tilt, and / or damages them beyond repair?
Does cholesterol / myelin / membranes serve as a protective barrier against nnEMF?

Random:
5.) Why do serious infections (eg. pneumonia, sepsis,) often represent the beginning of the end for ALS patients? Is the infections themselves or is the high dosages of mitotoxic antibiotics that kill the patients?

6.) Why are empathetic people more likely to find yawning contagious?

V/r,
Will Rutherford

### Brent Patrick — 2024-03-12T00:13:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-in-high-latitude-grown-grains.28670/post-332433>

> [Mishamasha said:](/goto/post?id=332407)
>
> I eat grain because I cannot afford pastured meat and wild seafood more than once a week. I eat beans and grain mostly. I thought food didn’t matter so much!
>
> Majority of ancestors in northern latitudes had grain in their diet because of the food storage need for winter
>
> hey Brent, what do you know about the C3 and C4 and CAM plants?

**C3 plants always have one spot on the carbon backbone that is more deuterium depleted.**

C3 plants use C3 photosynthesis which uses CO2 in a 3-carbon compound and C4 plants use C4 photosynthesis which incorporates the CO2 in a 4-carbon compound. C3 plants like cooler weather and C4 plants like a stronger light. 85% of plants are C3 types.

[https://jackkruse.com/reality-7-blood-chlorophyll-types-food/](https://jackkruse.com/reality-7-blood-chlorophyll-types-food/)

[https://www.cropsreview.com/c3-plants/](https://www.cropsreview.com/c3-plants/)

"C3 plants use C3 photosynthesis which uses CO2 in a 3-carbon compound and C4 plants use C4 photosynthesis which incorporates the CO2 in a 4-carbon compound. C3 plants like cooler weather and C4 plants like stronger light. 85% of plants are C3 types. These compounds represent different types of sugar that are made photoelectrically from the joining of CO2 and water with sunlight. Photosynthesis is the process of combining light and nutrients of the soil to create energy plants can use. C3 and C4 photosynthesis go about this process differently. Most plants are C3 types. The advantage with C3 photosynthesis is that it is more efficient than the C4 variety under normal light conditions since it requires fewer enzymes. C3 plants use C2. Carbon Dioxide is used to create the 3-carbon compound G3P in order to produce usable energy. The higher CO2 concentration in C4 plants means that the stomata (water region), the gas exchange part of the plant, do not need to open up to get any more, so less water is lost. C4 uses CO2 to create the 4-carbon compound oxaloacetate instead. C4 plants tend to do better in high oxygen environments. C4 plants also go through photosynthesis faster than C3 plants when there is a lot of light and heat nearby. Additionally, C4 plants have much better water efficient than the C3 variety since they don’t have to keep their stomata open as much.
There are several thousand different types of C4 species with 19 different plant families. Examples include corn, saltbush and many plants that thrive during the summer months.
ADP is an organic compound that is essential for the transfer of energy during photosynthesis. ADP is composed of a five carbon atom compound known as ribose, one adenosine molecule, and two phosphate groups. It is formed by removing one phosphate from adenosine triphosphate, ATP.
The energy needed for photosynthesis is obtained from sunlight. Plants store this energy in the form of ATP and then use it to carry out photosynthesis. Photosynthesis takes place in two distinct stages, the light stage and dark stage. The light stage is the light-dependent stage of photosynthesis, which occurs in the thylakoid membranes of the chloroplast. During this stage, white light is absorbed by the pigments in the chloroplast to form high-energy compounds, such as ATP and Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide Phosphate, NADPH. In order to produce the energy required to drive chemical reactions in photosynthesis, ATP loses one phosphate to become ADP, and the NADPH loses one electron to become NADP+. Phosphorus also is key in animal photosynthesis and make H+ from the water made in mitochondrial matrix which is deuterium depleted.
During the dark stage, the energy-depleted compounds, ADP and NADP+ are converted back to high-energy forms, ATP and NADPH respectively. These high chemical compounds are then stored to drive other chemical reactions necessary for the synthesis of sugar and other carbon-containing compounds. ATP is also used in the oxidation of photosynthetically-produced carbohydrates in the mitochondria during cellular respiration. This can be made with food electrons or with red light alone". **JK

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-blocking-clothing.28672/post-332364>

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-03-10T21:39:30+0200

### Pratima — 2024-03-10T22:17:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-blocking-clothing.28672/post-332369>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=332364)
>
> Can't wear any metal unless it's under leather clothing anywhere around mmWaves.
>

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-10T22:34:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-blocking-clothing.28672/post-332373>

The purpose of nnEMF clothing is to block - RF, not electric or magnetic fields. The metallic content of the fabric reflects the RF protecting the wearer (think light and mirror). If you wear a hoodie made of blocking fabric it will protect you from the sides and back but not the front and what goes in the front is reflected back by the fabric behind your head. Being metallic this clothing will attract and absorb electric fields. So if you are inside in an area with high 60Hz AC electric fields your exposure to this nnEMF could be increased. nnEMF clothing does nothing to protect against magnetic fields.

Would I personally wear nnEMF protective clothing . . . under certain circumstances yes. When traveling, the advantages out way the disadvantages. Also if pregnant.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-11T04:04:20+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-11T04:37:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phd-bias.28675/post-332385>

There is more to consider: The **Congressional Apportionment Amendment** (originally titled **Article the First**) is a proposed amendment to the [United States Constitution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Constitution) that addresses the number of seats in the [House of Representatives](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_House_of_Representatives). It was proposed by Congress on September 25, 1789, but was never ratified by the requisite number of [state legislatures](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/State_legislature_\(United_States\)). As Congress did not set a [time limit](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Article_Five_of_the_United_States_Constitution#Ratification_deadline_and_extension) for its ratification, the Congressional Apportionment Amendment is still pending before the states. As of 2024, it is one of [six unratified amendments](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_amendments_to_the_United_States_Constitution#Unratified_amendments).

Fourteenth Amendment, Section 2: Representatives shall be apportioned among the several States according to their respective numbers, counting the whole number of persons in each State, excluding Indians not taxed.

Note it does not exclude illegal aliens and this is why the democrats are so OK with an open border. Maybe why so many of the illegals wind up in red states to turn them purple before they turn blue.

Recall the spot of 1st amendment goes to the apportionment amendment, which they may have thought was the more important if we use their criminal logic. It shouldn't be forgotten. It follows since Biden wants an open border; recall heads of illegals are counted with Americans to set the amount of people in a state to send to Congress. If you control the number of seats in the House and the executive branch you can run a covert parallel government.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-11T04:38:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phd-bias.28675/post-332386>

In the [1st United States Congress](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1st_United_States_Congress), [James Madison](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_Madison) put together a package of constitutional amendments designed to address the concerns of [Anti-Federalists](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anti-Federalism), who were suspicious of federal power under the new constitution. The Congressional Apportionment Amendment is the only one of the twelve amendments passed by Congress which was never ratified; ten amendments were ratified by 1791 as the [Bill of Rights](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Bill_of_Rights), while the other amendment (Article the Second) was later ratified as the [Twenty-seventh Amendment](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twenty-seventh_Amendment_to_the_United_States_Constitution) in 1992. A majority of the states did ratify the Congressional Apportion Amendment and, by the end of 1791, the amendment was just one state short of adoption. However, no state has ratified the amendment since 1792.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-11T04:51:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phd-bias.28675/post-332387>

The amendment lays out a mathematical formula for determining the number of seats in the House of Representatives. It would initially have required one representative for every 30,000 constituents, with that number eventually climbing to one representative for every 50,000 constituents. However, there is some agreement that the last line contains a [scrivener's error](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scrivener%27s_error).

Since the amendment was never passed in 1792, Congress has set the size of the House of Representatives by statute. This makes the Speaker of the House and the make up of the house a region for attack. I believe the Deep State is now weaponizing this area of the Constitution to stop anyone who they think will block their agenda. Congress regularly increased the size of the House to account for population growth throughout the 19th century until it fixed the number of voting House members at 435 in [1911](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apportionment_Act_of_1911), where aside from a temporary increase to 437 members from 1959 through 1962 after [Alaska](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alaska) and [Hawaii](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hawaii) were [admitted to the Union](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Admission_to_the_Union), it has remained.

The [2020 United States Census](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2020_United_States_Census) recorded a population of 331.4 million; consequently, the minimum number of representatives in the House could have grown to about 6,600 under the terms of the original House version of this amendment, which did not contain the scrivener's error.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-11T04:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phd-bias.28675/post-332388>

In [Federalist Paper No. 55](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federalist_No._55), [James Madison](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_Madison) argued that the size of the House of Representatives has to balance the ability of the body to legislate with the need for legislators to have a relationship close enough to the people to understand their local circumstances, that such representatives' social class be low enough to sympathize with the feelings of the mass of the people, and that their power be diluted enough to limit their abuse of the public trust and interests.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-11T04:59:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phd-bias.28675/post-332389>

With 50 states today, 27 additional state ratifications are necessary to reach the required threshold of 38 ratifications needed for this Apportionment amendment to become part of the Constitution.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-uncle-jack-say-in-the-bitcoin-thread-that-etfs-might-harm-bitcoin.28679/post-333096>

### [YouTube ordered to reveal the identities of video viewers](https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2024/03/youtube-ordered-to-reveal-the-identities-of-video-viewers?utm_source=iterable&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=b2c_pro_oth_20240401_aprilweeklynewsletter_paid_v1_1_171171097931&utm_content=innocent_Youtube_viewer)

Federal authorities have asked Google to reveal the identities of people that watched certain videos in at least two investigations.

 www.malwarebytes.com

**selling Bitcoin for cash**

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### YouTube ordered to reveal the identities of video viewers​

Posted: March 26, 2024 by [Pieter Arntz](https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/authors/metallicamvp)
Federal US authorities have asked Google for the names, addresses, telephone numbers, and user activity of accounts that watched certain YouTube videos, according to unsealed court documents [Forbes](https://www.forbes.com/sites/thomasbrewster/2024/03/22/feds-ordered-google-to-unmask-certain-youtube-users-critics-say-its-terrifying/) has seen.
Of those users that weren’t logged in when they watched those videos between January 1 and 8, 2023, the authorities asked for the IP addresses.
The starting point of one of the investigations is an entity that uses the handle “elonmuskwhm” and is suspected of money laundering by **selling Bitcoin for cash.** As part of the investigation, agents sent the suspect links to tutorials on YouTube about mapping via drones and augmented reality software.Then they asked YouTube to send them data about the people that watched that video.
But those video tutorials were not private and had been watched over 30,000 times by the time the agents asked YouTube’s parent company Google for information about the viewers.
In another case, related to a bomb threat, the authorities asked for information about the viewers of eight selected live streams. One of those live streams has over 130,000 subscribers.
The police received a threat from an unknown male that there was an explosive placed in a trash can in a public area. When the police went to investigate the matter, they found out their actions were broadcasted through a YouTube live stream camera. Apparently similar events had taken place before, so for good reason law enforcement is after the evildoers.
But asking for data of that many viewers, many of which we can assume to be innocent bystanders, goes against what privacy experts believe to be reasonable. This type of digital dragnets go against the fourth amendment: freedom from unreasonable searches.
Albert Fox-Cahn, executive director at the Surveillance Technology Oversight Project (STOP) said:

> “No one should fear a knock at the door from police simply because of what the YouTube algorithm serves up. I’m horrified that the courts are allowing this.”

According to the documents Forbes has seen, the court granted the order but asked Google not to make it public. We don’t currently know if Google complied with the request for information.
Google spokesperson Matt Bryant told Forbes:

> “We examine each demand for legal validity, consistent with developing case law, and we routinely push back against over broad or otherwise inappropriate demands for user data, including objecting to some demands entirely.”

STOP [condemned](https://www.stopspying.org/latest-news/2024/3/23/stop-condemns-youtube-bulk-search-warrant-as-digital-dragnet) the US Department of Justice for securing a bulk warrant to track every YouTube user who watched the completely legal videos about mapping software for drones.
John Davisson, senior counsel at the Electronic Privacy Information Center, told Forbes:

> “What we watch online can reveal deeply sensitive information about us—our politics, our passions, our religious beliefs, and much more. It’s fair to expect that law enforcement won’t have access to that information without probable cause. This order turns that assumption on its head.”

Warrants like these turn innocent people into suspects for no other reason than watching a perfectly legal video. The YouTube warrants are similar to geofence warrants, where court issues a search warrant to allow law enforcement to search a database to find all active mobile devices within a particular area.
These warrants turn the fear that certain online searches or your viewing history is going to put you on some kind of list, into reality. It also encourages users to use a VPN for even the most harmless activities and discourages YouTube visitors from logging in.

* * *

**We don’t just report on privacy—we offer you the option to use it.**
Privacy risks should never spread beyond a headline. Keep your online privacy yours by using [Malwarebytes Privacy VPN](https://www.malwarebytes.com/vpn).

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/led-indoor-lights.28682/post-332491>

Hello thank you for the reply and I don’t have any health issues so I think I will try the candle light.i have blue light blocking glasses and i was considering buying some bulbs that are full spectrum And have different setting so i can use red light or amber light at night if need do you think it’s something worth investing in?

### coho3131 — 2024-03-13T18:08:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/led-indoor-lights.28682/post-332513>

> [Martinlillico said:](/goto/post?id=332491)
>
> Hello thank you for the reply and I don’t have any health issues so I think I will try the candle light.i have blue light blocking glasses and i was considering buying some bulbs that are full spectrum And have different setting so i can use red light or amber light at night if need do you think it’s something worth investing in?

There are people here who could probably give you a better answer than me, but I think you would want to test the bulbs with a spectroscope to confirm the wavelengths you are seeking. I would not be surprised if a dim incandescent bulb is better than any of these new technology bulbs.

Candle lights are good health vise
but
they will make a mess of the ceilings in your flat.
Mine got so much sooth that when my girlfriend moved in, she was scraping sheets of sooth from ceilings. They were falling off in large pieces.
So, if you live in the cave, candles are acceptable.

It is helpful for sick puppies.
It is not that good for healthy people.

People who managed to live very long,
supercentenarians or just old people,
they are usually as thin as you are now,
they do not eat much of animal flesh.
Average American (most of them are sick) they eat 220 pounds of meat yearly.
But supercentenarians eat 20 pounds a year.
That amounts to the weight of on thick slice of bacon a day.

So if supercentenarians do not eat that much of animal flesh, what do they eat? Carbohydrates.

Carbohydrates have a problem.
Many things are called carbohydrates.
For now, think of all kinds of beans as a desirable.

### JanSz — 2024-03-13T17:37:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.28683/post-332510>

> And I will get my BMI and fat percentage, is there anything else like that you suggest?

About PUFA.
All PUFA's are suspect.
Do not worry about too low DHA.
Worry about way way too high LA (Linoleic Acid)
PUFA (Omega-6 and Omega-3)
We used to have about the same amount of both.
That was in the time when people were hungry.
Say, before 1850.
Someone invented a way to squeeze seeds and get oil out of them.
Suddenly farmers were asked to produce 10000x more seeds, maybe more.
Everything eating seeds or seed oil grows, pigs, fish, birds, other animals.
People too.
First, they were thin (like you) but also short, about 5'4".
Now people are not hungry, they are growing up and wide.

We are mammals.
What happen in real world to animals.
End of summer and fall there is lots of food.
Animals eat a lot, the more they eat the hungrier they get (leptin is getting low).
Why do they do that?
They are getting ready to hibernate.
When they are about ready, but not really, they get into torpor.
Refresh your understanding of what torpor is (not pretty).
This is where more than half people on this earth are getting, getting into torpor.
People are getting ready to hibernate.
That is a price of not being hungry.

//////////////////

I got a master's degree, worked there for 8 years, making really good money there.
I look around, I have nothing after all this work.
Can't get a passport to immigrate to USA because they say that I am too valuable.
Screw it, at least they told me what the problem is.
I quit my job, so now I am a bum.
Little over three years later I got passport.
\--
So, I have a longer history of not working.
\---

# Thread: Does turmeric / curcumin interfere with melanin production?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-turmeric-curcumin-interfere-with-melanin-production.28688/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### clemsnwill — 2024-03-14T15:41:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-turmeric-curcumin-interfere-with-melanin-production.28688/post-332538>

i think it might based on personal experience, but find it hard to believe Dr Kruse would recommend it in his book if it did...

### clemsnwill — 2024-03-14T15:57:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-turmeric-curcumin-interfere-with-melanin-production.28688/post-332540>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=332539)
>
> [View attachment 27361](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/27361/)

### [How to Reduce Melanin, Which Controls Pigmentation, for Lighter Skin](https://www.healthline.com/health/how-to-reduce-melanin#:~:text=Turmeric,melanocytes%20to%20create%20more%20melanin.)

Skin lightening treatments can temporarily reduce your skin’s melanin production. Here's how to do it, as well as important precautions and potential side effects.

 www.healthline.com

#### Turmeric​

According to a 2012 study in [Phytotherapy ResearchTrusted Source](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21584871), the active compound in [turmeric](https://www.healthline.com/health/turmeric-for-skin) may reduce melanin synthesis. This compound, called curcumin, works by inhibiting tyrosinase. This suppresses the ability of melanocytes to create more melanin.

### JanSz — 2024-03-14T17:35:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-turmeric-curcumin-interfere-with-melanin-production.28688/post-332541>

# Thread: El Salvador residency question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-residency-question.28689/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael Allred — 2024-03-15T22:11:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-residency-question.28689/post-332579>

We are seriously considering an El Salvador residency. Can anyone help me with the following two questions please:

1\. Is the residency temporary or permanent? If temporary, how long does it last?

2\. Is anything required to maintain your residency? For example, do you have to spend a specific amount of time of the year living there

### jackmck — 2024-03-16T17:25:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-residency-question.28689/post-332596>

Hi Michael,

Here's a few things I've found:

The process for becoming a permanent resident in ES includes becoming a temporary one first. Once a person has been a temporary resident for some time (for applicants from non-Spanish-speaking countries it's three years), they can apply for Permanent Residency. At that time, they can leave the country for up to two years in a single trip without losing their status. Once they become a citizen (which happens for most applicants after five years), they can leave the country for five years at a time, and special conditions regarding their country of origin apply.

During those first three years, Immigration will only allow temporary residents to leave the country for 90 days in a single trip or for 120 days cumulatively over the course of one year. This means that one needs to plan to live in El Salvador for at least eight months out of the year.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

> Hi, Does anyone have a way to dark mode the screen on Optimal Klubs online or the PDF of JK Blogs?

These are phone lines, are they as bad as Powerlines or Cell towers? Like living beside a powerline is horrible same with cell tower, but is phone line the same?

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-03-17T19:49:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phone-lines-emf.28692/post-332609>

Too many variables to say one way or the other.

For example:
How close are you to power lines?
How electrosensitive are you?
What is your redox like?
How much electricity is flowing thru the lines?
What elevation are you at?
What kind of cell towers are you referring to?
Proximity to towers?
Are you holding a device talking to the towers?
If 5G, are you between the device and tower?
Are you near lots of water? In water?

And I'm sure there are other factors I'm not considering

# Thread: Blood dyscrasias and liver enzyme alterations = a lesson in quantum mechanisms
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-dyscrasias-and-liver-enzyme-alterations-a-lesson-in-quantum-mechanisms.28694/>
