══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-18T16:08:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-dyscrasias-and-liver-enzyme-alterations-a-lesson-in-quantum-mechanisms.28694/post-332627>

Evans syndrome is a severe autoimmune illness. One study reported a median survival of 7.2 years with Evans syndrome. Those with secondary Evans syndrome had a 5-year survival rate of 38%. The leading causes of death for those with Evans syndrome are bleeding, infections, and hematological cancer.

**Evans syndrome** is an [autoimmune disease](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autoimmune_disease) in which an individual's [immune system](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Immune_system) attacks their own [red blood cells](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Red_blood_cell) and [platelets](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Platelet); the syndrome can include immune [neutropenia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neutropenia). These immune cytopenias may occur simultaneously or sequentially in blood cell lines.

Its overall phenotype resembles a combination of [autoimmune hemolytic anemia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autoimmune_hemolytic_anemia) and [immune thrombocytopenic purpura](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Immune_thrombocytopenic_purpura).[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Evans_syndrome#cite_note-pmid14782741-1)[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Evans_syndrome#cite_note-pmid31292991-4)[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Evans_syndrome#cite_note-pmid19638626-5) Autoimmune hemolytic anemia is when an autoimmune process destroys the red blood cells that normally carry oxygen. This starves mitochondria of oxygen and induces redox shifts in metabolism and proteome management in the cell. That mismanagement leads to immune cell destruction of bloodlines by the serial attack of cell membrane antigens. This destruction is accomplished by losing control of the proteome time management from nuclear DNA transcription. The time ledger mechanism in cells becomes defective because light frequencies that control the process are absent.
Consequently, the clock genes begin to run freely in immune cells, leading to an altered proteome. This altered proteome response causes missense translation and leads to phenotypes where immune cells begin to destroy normal cells. Autoimmunity is an information defect leading to an altered light spectrum in biophotons. Immune thrombocytopenic purpura is a condition in which an autoimmune process destroys platelets. Platelets are a component of blood that contributes to the formation of [blood clots](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blood_clot) in the body to prevent bleeding.

The initial treatment is glucocorticoid corticosteroids or intravenous immunoglobulin, also used in ITP cases. In children, it can remain well controlled with long-term immunosuppressant therapy that occasionally leads to a spontaneous complete resolution of the condition. Red light increases ATP while lowering blood glucose by 25-35%, forcing cells into new proteome management of the nuclear DNA. People with altered liver enzymes mimic the same symptoms or phenotype when light signals are altered inside tissues. The addition of missing light frequencies can lower liver enzymes very rapidly, and this ability of light shows us how light controls the proteome expression in mammals.

Mammals with blood and gut AI responses will almost always have atrophic skin with evidence of altered melanin biology at some level. This can be at the eye or skin because of altered frequency management by the sense organ's connection to the HPA axis.

Lack of sun and too much blue light/nnEMF is behind the curtain of this disease. How do we know this? Glucocorticoid use helps by raising blood sugar, which affects the key mechanisms behind this disease. Many forget that full-spectrum sunlight contains 43% red light, which creates ATP. In autoimmune disease, glycolysis becomes more thermodynamically efficient in generating ATP in red light-deficient cells. This relativity becomes evident in terms of ATP production per unit mass of protein (that is, faster). However, through quantitative flux analysis and proteomics, science has shown us that mitochondrial respiration is more proteome efficient than aerobic glycolysis. Using glycolysis tells us cells have lost their ability to light controls internally because there is a loss of proteome control needed to control the circadian management of cellular pathways.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-19T18:13:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eyes-see-emfs.28695/post-332666>

A phone line carries about 50V DC along with the phone signal. A TV satellite is receiving / transmitting a signal but not power. Electrical power lines carry power at different AC voltage levels depending where they are in relation to the power plant and consumer. Cell towers all have different frequencies they broadcast depending on the company they belong to. This is all energy at a huge range of frequency from 60Hz (60x per second) electric to a 5G (generation) cell signal that could be 20GHz (20 Billion x per second). # Thread: Information about grounding my laptop - grounding cord
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/information-about-grounding-my-laptop-grounding-cord.28697/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KinaBibiDiop — 2024-03-19T18:40:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/information-about-grounding-my-laptop-grounding-cord.28697/post-332669>

hello hello

I am looking into grounding my laptop but there is something I do not understand:

does the plug of the gronding cord necessarily have to be a 3-prong plug or can it be any type of plug (depending on the types of plugs available in my country) that goes into a wall socket?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-19T19:07:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/information-about-grounding-my-laptop-grounding-cord.28697/post-332671>

For your laptop, or any device, to be grounded while charging, it must be connected to a ground! No need if you use it on battery. You must have a 3 prong plug - the third prong IS the ground OR you can add an external ground cord and attach it to the body of the computer (device) IF it is metal. You can get ground cords that use a USB plug to ground.

### KinaBibiDiop — 2024-03-19T21:34:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/information-about-grounding-my-laptop-grounding-cord.28697/post-332681>

ok, so when it's not charging there's no use grounding it. If I want to use my laptop while it's charging them it's important to ground it...I did not know that, thanks

and a 3 prong plug is necessary. But what if my socket does not support the 3 prong plug?

> For me the jury is out. I'm currently deciding that whilst I like the sunshine I can't take the heat, so I'm going to have to find a place 5000 ft up and near the equator.
>
> Where that will be is TBD

When I first got into cold thermogenesis, I would sit in cold water for 30 minutes to 1 hour (after the intro/gradual acclimation period). I was impervious to both cold in winter and heat in summer for hours afterward.

This experience reminded me of this girl from high school who grew up in Michigan and was wearing a tshirt in 50 degree weather in Louisiana!

**The process for becoming a permanent resident in ES includes:**

[#2 (jackkruse.com)](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-residency-question.28689/post-332596)

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-residency-question.28689/post-332596](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-residency-question.28689/post-332596)

Hi Michael,

Here's a few things I've found:

The process for becoming a permanent resident in ES includes becoming a temporary one first. Once a person has been a temporary resident for some time (for applicants from non-Spanish-speaking countries it's three years), they can apply for Permanent Residency. At that time, they can leave the country for up to two years in a single trip without losing their status. Once they become a citizen (which happens for most applicants after five years), they can leave the country for five years at a time, and special conditions regarding their country of origin apply.

During those first three years, Immigration will only allow temporary residents to leave the country for 90 days in a single trip or for 120 days cumulatively over the course of one year. This means that one needs to plan to live in El Salvador for at least eight months out of the year.

I have been both places. Costa Rica isn’t an ark and has omnipresent government control. But it isn’t recovering from civil war and the water is good. Goods and materials are expensive but labor isn’t. I’m in Southern Costa Rica and drink from some of the rivers it’s so pristine. Wildlife is amazing as is the local food culture. Uvita has over 500 vendors who take bitcoin and a jam packed farmers market with local produce, eggs, dairy and fish that all take bitcoin. Pickleball courts just built. No 5g but cell towers in town to avoid but not as much on the beaches. Electricity is an issue. I am learning most of Central America has ungrounded neutral electric lines with substations quite spread out so the neutral line is almost always contaminated. I have Ethernet there.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-ready-to-change-my-eating-per-recommendations-in-the-leptin-rx.28703/post-332733>

Hi, All! I've already done some of the smaller changes specified in The Leptin Rx, but it's time to take a deeper dive into the actual meals/no snacks/no grains/no processed sugars part. Big habit change for me, but I'm ready. I've told my husband I've followed Dr. Kruse and am reading his content with my usual amount of skepticism (which is a lot whenever I read any health info, since having seen the centralized medicine machine firsthand the last 21 years) and I am cautiously optimistic (and excited), as I cannot see a single thing in Dr. Kruse's content that doesn't make total sense. I wonder if I should start a journal on the forum, I'm kind of nervous to do it. . .not sure. Open to advice, feedback. Glad and appreciative to have found this.

### Saichi — 2024-03-21T20:05:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-ready-to-change-my-eating-per-recommendations-in-the-leptin-rx.28703/post-332740>

Nnemf effects are non-linear so "relatively mitigated" won't suffice for most people.

# Thread: TN First state to fight back against chem trails

### DLO — 2024-03-21T19:45:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tn-first-state-to-fight-back-against-chem-trails.28704/post-332734>

**Legislators in the great State of Tennessee this week passed a bill seeking to ban the spraying of chemicals into the atmosphere, a government weather manipulation technique known as “geo-engineering**.**“**

On Monday, the state Senate passed [SB2 691/HB 2063](https://wapp.capitol.tn.gov/apps/BillInfo/Default.aspx?BillNumber=HB2063), which seeks to “prohibit the intentional injection, release, or dispersion, by any means, of chemicals, chemical compounds, substances, or apparatus within the borders of this state into the atmosphere with the express purpose of affecting temperature, weather, or the intensity of the sunlight.”

### Alexandra — 2024-03-21T19:46:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tn-first-state-to-fight-back-against-chem-trails.28704/post-332735>

### DLO — 2024-04-04T01:54:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tn-first-state-to-fight-back-against-chem-trails.28704/post-333198>

Another TN win
The bill opposing mRNA in food was passed by the Tennessee legislature and awaits the governor’s signature. The bill comes in response to a University of California-Riverside research project looking into whether mRNA which targets pathogens could be implanted into edible plants, which would then be consumed. Local media described the bill as a move targeting “vaccine lettuce,” would classify foods modified to act as vaccines, as pharmaceuticals that essentially require a prescription.

UC Riverside associate professor of Botany and Plant Sciences, Juan Pablo, said “We are testing this approach with spinach and lettuce and have long-term goals of people growing it in their own gardens,” adding “Farmers could also eventually grow entire fields of it.” Another researcher, Nicole Steinmetz, said in the same release that they planned to use nanoparticles or “plant viruses, for gene delivery to plants.” A Kentucky company has already been “infecting growing tobacco plants with a genetically modified coronavirus” to see if it can produce antibodies for a potential vaccine, adding that the company “can already do this right now.” US Representative Thomas Massie sounded the alarm against manipulating food.

# Thread: My Journey to Waking up with the Sun CONSISTENTLY
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Solarmagbro — 2024-03-23T00:53:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-332772>

Hi All,

I used to live in London, England at 51.5072° N, 0.1276° W.

I now live in Tetouan, Morocco at 35.5889° N, 5.3626° W.

In 2023, I spent two months reading the entire Jack Kruse Patreon archive and began experimenting with sunrise waking, Epi Paleo Rx, better blue light management, and EMF mitigation. Although I was already well versed in these things, diving deep into Kruse's work helped me up my level.

To hold myself accountable, I started tracking how often I wake with sunrise on the 1st January 2024.

**So far**

In **January**, I woke up with the sunrise on **17 days (61%).**
In **February**, I woke up with the sunrise **14 days (50%)**

My goal in 2024 is to have an average of 80-90% percent wake with sunrise.

I find it difficult to be consistent with this practice. This journal is a way for me to be held publically accountable. I will share a journal every week detailing how often I woke with the sun, and how I'm getting more consistent, and where I've failed.

I understand that this simple habit is incredibly important. It's the foundation.

And yet, it's been so difficult for me to maintain due to a lifetime of bad circadian management and insomnia.

I appreciate your support.

### Alexandra — 2024-03-23T02:03:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-332773>

Hello! Way to go on your progress so far. I, too, am making changes and understand certain things can be difficult and take time alto adjust. But you can do it. I try to remember how a little…then a little more…becomes a lot.

### Sandra — 2024-03-23T07:43:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-332776>

I've also started to see every sunrise. I had to set my alarm for 5:30am for the first 10 days, but then I started to wake up on time without it. Sometimes an hour before which was not ideal!! But now as the sunrise is getting a little later for me, it's getting easier too. I've seen every one since 23 Feb, so just one month in, but I've made it a priority for me to see if it makes a difference. Any sunrise is better than none so just keep gong in the right direction.

### Ravi Sandhu — 2024-03-23T13:07:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-332778>

I was doing Jack's stuff in my little apartment in Battersea SW LDN back in the day.

Made a lot of it work, and still kinda miss LDN.

May return one day. I was just happier there.

Lets see some progress.

\-Ravi

### Solarmagbro — 2024-03-26T00:46:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-332842>

Managed to wake up once with sunrise this past week. However, I then went back to sleep at 9AM. Since then, my sleep cycle has been screwed. This start of the week, today, I woke up at 10:30 AM and at the very least, stayed up the whole day. I've set up an automated block on both of my computers which will prevent me from ever going on the computer post 12AM for the rest of the year. Of course, I also use f.lux in the evening hours. Typically, I switch off the PC at 10 PM. But sometimes, I need to stay online past that particularly when I'm struggling to fall asleep, or I haven't done enough work in the day.

Tomorrow, my plan is to wake up even earlier than 10:30 AM, and get back in the rhthym.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-26T07:18:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-332848>

Maximize the good, minimize the bad. There are several types of nnEMF: RF from WiFi, Bluetooth, cell phones, smart meters etc, Magnetic fields from motors, bad wiring etc and Electric fields from wires in the walls, floor & ceiling and everything plugged into them. It is not just screen time & blue light at night. Create a EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary to help with optimal circadian re-balance. Visit [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse) and look around, watch the videos - info that might help.

### Solarmagbro — 2024-04-08T17:23:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-333403>

Last week was crap. Woke up for sunrise just once.

This past week I woke up 5x for the sunrise.

It's been incredibly spiritually illuminating to see it rise each time.

My hearts shakes with reverberations of ectasy and love the rest of the day when I manage to glimpse the majesty of that deep orange glow. This sunrise protocol is more than just a circadian rhthym hack designed to optimise health; it's a deep visceral dive into living from my heart and accessing my higher power.

I feel this in my bones. Each day I wake up with the sun and see it rise the rest of my day is enveloped in a higher state of concscious awareness that's inexplicably and tantamountally different to when I don't.

Can you relate?

### Solarmagbro — 2024-05-19T18:52:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-334900>

This past week I woke up for sunrise 6/7 days.... so magical to get the sunrise for 20 mins... This sunday I didn't wake up for sunrise... I woke up at 9AM.... and the difference in how I feel is palpable... I believe eating too much fish yesterday made me more sloggy and it was consequently harder to get up.

### Solarmagbro — 2024-05-27T19:53:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-to-waking-up-with-the-sun-consistently.28708/post-335194>

Only woke up for sunrise 4/7 times last week. Also went back to sleep for a few hours during those days. So that wasn't great. This week will be better. I woke up for sunrise today this Monday but also went back to sleep due to needing extra recovery after 10 days of antibiotics. Tomorrow I will feel enough energy to stay up.

# Thread: Soap and skin health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/soap-and-skin-health.28713/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mishamasha — 2024-03-24T17:21:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/soap-and-skin-health.28713/post-332802>

Does anyone know about how soap and shampoo effect on our largest organ, our beloved skin? Does it distrupt our solar panels ability to transform Sunlight?

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-24T20:57:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/soap-and-skin-health.28713/post-332805>

It can depending on many factors.

### NikkiYork — 2024-03-31T05:31:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/soap-and-skin-health.28713/post-333035>

Obviously there are all kinds of different soaps and shampoos.
One factor is the ph and the change of it.
There are a bunch of theories out there about raising ph or lowering it or evening it out.

In cosmetology you learn that the ph of skin and hair is between 4 and 4.5, but I recently read that’s been taught the wrong way and it is closer to 7 like water, so a scam. I don’t know yet, if that really makes sense.

The next subject would be that tensides work with lipophilic ends and hydrophilic ends to pick up dirt and they dry out the skin…

Another factor with cosmetics is, that the regulations are based on the assumption that skin is non-permeable, which it clearly is.

A lot of positive was achieved by hygiene, but I’m sure it comes with a downside, so I want to say less is more…

I have no idea, how soap would affect melanin production.

### jeremiasbtome — 2024-03-24T21:21:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-jeremias.28714/post-332809>

Have been thinking about doing this for some time now and think its a wise move. It will keep me accountable and will hopefully relate or help some people too. I am writing this just as the sun is about to finish setting.

I want to post as much as possible., to keep a journal of my wellbeing and something to look back on.

I live in Portugal. In a SLAVE box (apartment| 2nd floor . I live with my young sister and dog. I have an online business which helps me live here and provide for me and my sister. We moved from Dublin Ireland, real shitty parent situation so we left and I also thought Portugal would have been better since its warmer.
WRONG

Living in an apartment I think i feel weaker and not too optimal. Sure I get more sun than in Ireland but still not as much as El Salvador or other closer equator countries. Not good enough

I must work hard while juggling all these circadian requirements. I need to make a lot more money so i can move my sister to a house isolated in the countryside so we arent getting BLASTED by wifi from everyone in the apartment.

I have forsaken my body's biology too long. Putting my biz in front of my health thinking God will protect me. But God didnt invent man to live in this ocean of nnEMFs

I mus take better care of my health for my life, because if im not good for myself, I will fail my sister, Ill fail God and those who need me. I have 60 seconds left

When the sunsets I turn off tech and dont use it. No ALANA too. My sister uses it which still affects me but slowly I know she will use it less and less. Its only been 1 year learning about Dr.Kruse and there have already been many changes.

I must persisst and keep planting seeds in her head to get her to change

See you soon

I need to get more sun. I typically start my mornings with no tech for the first 2 hours. I need to prepare my breakfast before hand cuz im always missing the start of sunrise. Must do better Jeremias

I felt GREAT today, and I really noticed it's my sleep and circadian lifestyle. Yesterday I started a new routine where when the sun sets I immediately turn off ALL Lights and no more tech. I used to use red light and be up for a few hours but listening to jack past few months really made me realize that even that has a huge effect

FIRE shrank neanderthals brains. And they were always outside. Red light will have huge effects and probably already do. So starting yesterday Jeremias I MUST be in darkness when the sun is gone. Only use it when i NEED to be awake or do something. I have already wasted 20 eyars on this earth and most of them were spent with light after dark.

I believe I'll be dead in the next decade or two. So if I dont change now I'll only get worse

OCD

LOSER

Jeremias,

Reminds me of me going crazy when working.
Make sure to take care of your eyes.
Think of arranging for high or higher contrast of whatever you are working on, and good lighting.
Double check lighting.
When I got my first office job, it was engineering drafting.,
Grey paper, grey pencil and some light. Overall low contrast.
8 hr work, 4 hrs overtime, take home work, 4 more hours. Every fken day, every day.
I was convinced that the lamp I was using was 150 watts.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-29T19:27:21+0200

Yes ALL technology is (can be) bad BUT a computer/laptop can be set up to be VERY low negative impact. Hard wire internet, turn off Bluetooth, use on battery NOT while charging (unless grounded), adjunct color temp of screen. Reduce the electric field in your work space from circuits and other devices. Have as much natural light as possible.

Increase the positives and reduce the negatives in your life . . . the most negative thing in your life might just be your attitude.

I think Jeremias may have 50hz electricity.
2x50=100 (mitochondrial).

My electrical system has 2 phases plus ground, 200Aps.
One phase plus ground=120V
Two phases=240V
(Other than AC which is 240V, **the rest of the system is 120v and really works thru ground.**
When I was adding new wiring system to my home (and thinking electrically),
I have found that my home had very poor grounding.
So, I have added three very long grounding poles near the ground where electricity was coming to my home.
And one 1" dia 8feet long on the other side of my home, about 60feet away, basically on the other side of the house.

The area where the grounding pols are located is frequently water sprinkled by my lawn sprinkling system.

..

### JanSz — 2024-03-30T00:07:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-jeremias.28714/post-332976>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=332971)
>
> Everyone (house) in the US has 2 phase 60Hz 240V. How do you know you have a bad ground? You can have multiple ground pole but they MUST all be connected to your main panel.

> How do you know you have a bad ground?

Initially I had few GFCI's. They were actually working.
Byt when I extended my electrical system to the other side of my house, initially to have a spa there, the GFCI for the spa did not want to work.
So, I installed that 8 feet long grounding pole and that solved the problem.
So, I realized that grounding in my home was questionable, and started looking how my house was grounded.
One 1/4" or less than 1/2" short pole, barely in the ground (you could easily move that thing with one hand.
So, I replaced that.

All that I did was under permit and on the end inspected by inspector from JCP&L who is my power supplier.

...

I do suggest framing your inner monologue to become more positive, e.g. cut out the loser/lacking approach and replace with a positive, "look what I accomplished today". wanted to do an update post

Spent a few months in Portugal, even at one of "europe's warmest places" for holidays in the summer, this yea wasn't the hottest, we hada good august though

I went to the beach almost everyday in august, and was outside practically all day except when I had to cook and toilet

I noticed some INTERESTING CHANGES

My hair went more blonde than ever. And not in the most obvious places. I got hair everywhere alot more, my stomach area especially.
And 2-3 weeks in i NOTICED something really interesting, my eyebrows started slowly getting more blonde, i had to leave by the end of august, so it didnt fully complete but they were like almost all more blonded

Photo below showing me at the start of year vs last day in Portugal

Its crazy though, Portugal EVERYNODY lives indoors, the balconies in apartments so many have them blocked by windows, its like they hate the outdoors

In ireland, where im in now, id argue people embrace the outdoors more than in portugal

Back in Ireland at the moment cuz some personal family things

But Im on a farm currently for a month and I noticed some things first few days while I was here

Way less radiation here compared to dublin where i lived. In my dublin house I would have 5-10 wifis blasting from each side since we had houses right next to us

Here on the farm, its only 1. Felt better for sure when I first came but failed cuz I have been using wifi due to bad internet at the farm, my fault and need to figure out how to get a better internet provider to use ethernet with

IRELAND atm has 12 hours day 12 hours night,, so ive been going to sleep around 7pm and waking up around 7:30am but its taking ages to sleep, but at the same time i should follow the dark and light cycles. I probably will try do go to go to bed when there is 10 hours until sunrise instead of 12,13,.14 as the months progress and days get shorter

BUT i will aim to only be listening to podcasts in the dark or if i have some work to do non tech, via candles. My plan is to stay in ireland until i get my driver license and then go somewhere warm for 2 months

QE #5

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-28T16:13:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brca.28715/post-332914>

If you understand nuclear genomics, you'll find out BRCA1 & 2 beliefs are a nDNA scam because it never considers how mtDNA turns on or off the epigenome of the genes.

When you go to a geneticist to find out if your cancer might have been caused by a mutation in BRCA1 or BRCA2, the genes that were later made famous by Angelina Jolie, you'll learn a lot. Quick science lesson: we _all_ have these genes, in fact we have two copies, inherited from each of our parents. BRCA stands for BReast CAncer, and these genes **actually _protect_ us** against breast and ovarian cancer.

But sometimes, genes can malfunction. This is called a mutation, and most often, it is due to proton changes in the backbone of DNA. If you watch my Kruse for Dummies lecture, you learn exactly how it happens, and it has NOTHING to do with genes. It is 100% due to how energy is time-stamped in cells. You'll realize that if the "matter" in your DNA protecting you is altered by the environment, you’re at greater risk of that thing. Enter breast cancer risk

It has ZERO to do with the presence or absence of BRCA 1 or 2.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-28T16:25:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brca.28715/post-332915>

Aging and cancer exhibit epigenetic silencing of genes that should NOT be silenced. The environment's light and temperature signals silences things that should not be silenced.

Let me give you a small snippet of how cancer works in the new world of epigenetics.

We now know that in many cases, gene inactivation is NOT due to a bona fide DNA mutation like BRCA1 or 2. Instead, the **non-mutated gene** can be silenced by an epigenetic mechanism that involves histone deacetylation of the histone proteins surrounding active genes (euchromatin) and DNA methylation of a cytosine-rich region of promoter sites (“start location”) on the gene.

This means any gene is capable of becoming a cancer; BRCA 1/2 are not special cancer genes. This idea is buried in the bad science of centralized medicine.

These cytosine-rich areas are called CpG islands, and the cytosine bases can be methylated by the enzyme DNMT. Did you know blue light and nnEMF cause hypermethylation by themselves? Remember, Jolie is an actress and always in that artificial light, lives a nightlife, and rarely in the sun because her dermatologist told her to block it to keep her skin movie star wrinkle-free. Poor thinking led to breast and ovary removal, and surgeons benefited, as women who were lost in 2013.

Thousands of “good genes” must remain in the euchromatin state to allow for gene expression to occur. When histones are acetylated, transcription factors can reach the DNA because of the “open structure” of the histones. When the promoter region has a hypomethylated CpG island, the transcription factor can bind to the DNA promoter region. Transcription factors migrate into the nucleus in response to various cellular signaling pathways. They are “silenced” and cannot be turned on by cellular signals, even if the gene is not mutated.

My bet in 2013 was that Scott Kelly would come back from space and be hypermethylated compared to his brother left behind on Earth because of my understanding of this science. It turns out I was right. Because I was right, it means anyone who followed Jolie's decision to remove her breasts and ovaries was wrong.

In clear language, my opinion is that your cellular terroir dictates your genome and not vice versa. Go re-read the first Levee in the quilt document. There is Jolie's truth bomb. The Kruse for Dummies lecture was expert in laying this all out. Watch it. It was an epic presentation. [https://optimalklubs.com/kruse-for-dummies-general/](https://optimalklubs.com/kruse-for-dummies-general/)

# Thread: Monitor RGB Levels
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monitor-rgb-levels.28717/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JustinGoldberg — 2024-03-25T17:54:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monitor-rgb-levels.28717/post-332822>

I find myself often tweaking Macos night mode, f.lux settings, and my Dell monitor's hardware controls.

The monitor controls have **either** the preset color temperatures, such as (Cool, Warm, Game, Movie, etc...), **or** I can manually tweak the RGB settings; but in this manual mode, I can't choose the color temperature, only Red Green and Blue from 1% to 100%.

In f.lux, if I set the "base" temperature too low, it is strange. So as I get used to the lower temperatures (4400° at noon), I lower it one 100 degree notch every few days as my eyes adjust.

I have Chroma glasses for nighttime, and I put them on after sundown, and I have bought yellow/amber glasses for regular daytime computer use. And I keep the windows open all the time, which is adjacent to my desk.

What setting should I keep the hardware at?

### greentrees — 2024-03-29T04:22:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monitor-rgb-levels.28717/post-332937>

Set the Dell monitor to the warmest colour setting (on mine its 5000k, warmer then 'Standard' or '10000k' etc.) then apply F.lux or preferably Iristech.co

I run mine during the daytime with a window open, no glasses and IRIS set to 900K (nearly full red) to 2700K (balanced red, green, blue). The majority of the time I'm reading the internet set to 1500K (amber). I might watch a video at 1900K (looks yellow, anything above that starts to look green and introduce blue on **my monitor**. You can test your own by using a photo of a RGB colour wheel)

I never run it above 3000K. If your monitor looks like trash when its orange/red its because its cheap and doesn't produce red or green colour's well. The other trick is if it has PWM brightness reduction, set the hardware to 100% brightness (stops the pulsing/PWM) then use IRIS to change the brightness to eliminate PWM based flicker

0K = full red
900K = full red with bits of green light (550nm and above) = red blue blocking glasses
1900K = yellow (mix of red and green light, 480nm and above) = yellow glasses
2700K = white (mix of red, green and blue light, 400nm and above) = vision
6500K = some kind of weird alien hyper blue light
Nighttime I run it at 900K to 1500K, ideally 900K wearing red glasses. Zero blue here

**NOTE: Anything above 900K will damage your eyes day or night**. Retinal apoptosis gets worse as you move from red light to green light then to blue light and the main thing for health is protecting the eyes. You wanna move closer to 900K and away from 6500K at all times.

Some links that might help:

### [The Evolution of LED Backlights - PC Monitors](https://pcmonitors.info/articles/the-evolution-of-led-backlights/)

pcmonitors.info

### [Dell U2717D Review - TFT Central](https://tftcentral.co.uk/reviews/dell_u2717d.htm)

>

### NikkiYork — 2024-08-28T19:49:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-effects.28718/post-339009>

I conducted crystal growing experiments in relation to geometrical shapes in my 20s.

I let salt crystals grow inside
A) a pyramid shape like the one above but made from wood skewers.

B) a cube made from skewers

C) a square drawn on paper

D) a Circle drawn on paper

E) a triangle drawn on paper

F) nothing

G) an attempt of creating orgon energy or however you want to call it by combining layers of metal and organic material. I believe I used tinfoil, the glass my solution was in and something else. Maybe fat. I forgot.

Outstanding was only the pyramid with extremely fast growth.

It was followed by the cube, but the cube was more frazzled. Orgon set up same as cube. Fast.

The two dimensional shapes all three took longer and had similar times, however: the round shape created an even crystal, the square was uneventful and the triangle was distorted.

The nothing was similar to the square. Slow, uneventful, rather broken not very organized.

# Thread: Methylene Blue/Red Light as a Tinnitus treatment
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-red-light-as-a-tinnitus-treatment.28723/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### grillosun — 2024-03-28T12:56:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-red-light-as-a-tinnitus-treatment.28723/post-332907>

Hi Everyone!

I first noticed my tinnitus in 2017, in both ears. It's been a constant presence ever since, never fading away. Despite visiting several doctors and undergoing examinations, there's been no significant issue found in my ear nerves. I do have some minor hearing loss in low-frequency sounds, but that's about it. Doctors have essentially told me, 'Tinnitus is one of those things we can't explain; I can't help you.'

Since 2022, I've been taking a more decentralized approach to my health, following this forum. I've been getting more sunlight (though I had a Basal Cell Carcinoma removed in 2021), more exercise, and eating better.

My lab results look good, with minimal inflammation, yet my tinnitus persists stronger than ever.

I've heard @Dr. Kruse mention that there might be a high quantity of melanocytes in the inner ear. I'm unsure how the connection to tinnitus works, but has anyone tried METHYLENE BLUE to alleviate tinnitus? Red light? What other remedies or approaches could I explore?

Im desperate! If there is someone out here with the same problem, please let me know.

Thanks you very much!

### jules3791 — 2024-03-28T15:35:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-red-light-as-a-tinnitus-treatment.28723/post-332911>

Hello [@grillosun](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29093/) - I always use the search button and dive in that way to find answers. Link to Patreon on this topic as well: [https://www.patreon.com/posts/hypoxia-22-is-41558277](https://www.patreon.com/posts/hypoxia-22-is-41558277)
Red light and low/no tech might be top on your list.
Best! Julia

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-02T16:59:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-red-light-as-a-tinnitus-treatment.28723/post-349964>

Today I give you a gift. It is my mitochondriac anniversary on April 2nd. I post this for my Mom. She used to call me at this time every year. Now i am calling you, my tribe. It is time to wake up.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154)

"The sage doesn't seek to be profound but is useful. A sage himself is a former fool who got tired of themselves."

This insightful quote paints a picture of wisdom born not from an innate desire for grandeur but from a humble journey of self-awareness and growth. A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. Their usefulness emerges from their centralized experience, mistakes made, lessons learned, and a quiet resolve to move beyond the foolishness of their centralized past. It’s a reminder that true depth often comes not from chasing profundity but from the simple, steady act of outgrowing one’s former self.

# Thread: Hi fellow Mitochondriacs, I’m Lewis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-fellow-mitochondriacs-i%E2%80%99m-lewis.28724/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lybogany — 2024-03-28T21:21:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-fellow-mitochondriacs-i%E2%80%99m-lewis.28724/post-332930>

Hello All!!

My name is Lewis and I discovered Dr. Jack Kruse back in November, through a random link on someone’s twitter post to Dr. Max Gulhane’s first interview with Uncle Jack, and it literally has been life changing. I remember listening to it and only understanding 10% of it, but felt this deep sense of truth with what was being said. I felt like I was rewiring my brain and unlearning half-truths I believed about the human body, nutrition, mitochondria, light, water, and magnetism. I ended up relistening to that podcast 11 times and every time I listened to it I felt like I gleaned something new. Needless to say I am on my way to becoming a full-blown Mitochondriac (need to move out of Chicagoland down to the lower latitudes): wearing blue blockers at work (desk job), living by candlelight at night, and have been getting outside and watching the sunrise every morning. I feel transformed with both my mental and physical health these past few months and I believe my reduced exposure to non-native EMF and getting outside in the sun has been a huge factor in that.

Anyone else in the greater Chicago area (city + suburbs) that want to meet up IRL sometime and talk about moving to El Salvador? Hit me up in this post!

Cheers,
L

P.S. - Enjoy the sunrise photo from 12th Street Beach.

It is telling of the psyche just like the **color Pink** that is heavily used in Cancer fund raising

I wish I could remember how I discovered Dr. Jack. It was sometime in 2013 or 2014. Purchased his book at the most pivotal, timely moment, and to this day I have no idea who or what turned me onto him or how I found it.

### lybogany — 2024-03-29T22:45:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-fellow-mitochondriacs-i%E2%80%99m-lewis.28724/post-332970>

Brent and Dr. Jack, appreciate being called out! You’re totally right, the sunglasses photo needs to go, it was an old photo from 2017 too. After November 2023, I have stopped wearing sunglasses. Obviously still have a lot to learn, early on in my journey.

The replacement pic is from me hiking outdoors one summer, trying to get as much sun as possible with my tanktop. Should have been topless, would have been better!

Anyways appreciate the warm welcome. Looking forward to learning more and being a part of this community.

### lybogany — 2024-03-29T23:10:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-fellow-mitochondriacs-i%E2%80%99m-lewis.28724/post-332972>

>
> I wish I could remember how I discovered Dr. Jack. It was sometime in 2013 or 2014. Purchased his book at the most pivotal, timely moment, and to this day I have no idea who or what turned me onto him or how I found it.

Pretty amazing you’ve been in this journey for over a decade. What practices did you remember first adopting and noticing changes your health/life?

Back in 2013, I was completely oblivious. I still thought diet was the key to health, I ignored Bitcoin, and I was completely in the dark (pun intended) about the importance of full spectrum sunlight on one’s health (and the dangers of blue light and nnEMF). I look back a decade and I’m unrecognizable to myself. Watching the sunrise in the middle of Chicago winter while topless outside, putting excess cashflows into Bitcoin, and prioritizing my light environment over my diet, I’d like to think my 2013 self would consider me crazy. Which is good because, and I’m quoting Uncle Jack, that smart muthafuckers look crazy to dumb muthafuckers. I was dumb and just working on getting smarter every day!

Working on getting more skin in the game both figuratively and literally. Getting myself outdoors more often, fewer clothes, and feet to the earth. And also with my mindset and philosophy, if I want to be a mitochondriac, I have to channel those values unequivocally across all areas of my life. Improving day after day.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-30T01:19:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-fellow-mitochondriacs-i%E2%80%99m-lewis.28724/post-332977>

Wounds create wisdom

### markthesunbather — 2024-03-31T16:30:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-fellow-mitochondriacs-i%E2%80%99m-lewis.28724/post-333042>

> [lybogany said:](/goto/post?id=332972)
>
> Pretty amazing you’ve been in this journey for over a decade. What practices did you remember first adopting and noticing changes your health/life?
>
> Back in 2013, I was completely oblivious. I still thought diet was the key to health, I ignored Bitcoin, and I was completely in the dark (pun intended) about the importance of full spectrum sunlight on one’s health (and the dangers of blue light and nnEMF). I look back a decade and I’m unrecognizable to myself. Watching the sunrise in the middle of Chicago winter while topless outside, putting excess cashflows into Bitcoin, and prioritizing my light environment over my diet, I’d like to think my 2013 self would consider me crazy. Which is good because, and I’m quoting Uncle Jack, that smart muthafuckers look crazy to dumb muthafuckers. I was dumb and just working on getting smarter every day!

Well back when I first heard of Jack, I just remember I got real obsessed with moving to Alaska. That was something he was talking about; Alaska. I was thinking maybe I should pack my bags and move there for about a year.

I think the first time I actually saw him talk may have been the clip on YouTube where he's walking through Whole Foods. I remember going to the one in Jacksonville and buying Chilean sea bass for a pretty penny, and also extra virgin coconut oil (which I had never had up to that point).

I definitely got the sun memo from him (I had to have), and started living out at the beach (which I had just moved to a town close to the beach a few months or so prior to me buying his book). I'd say I got the Epi-Paleo book "just in the nick of time", except, I was already almost a goner and probably should've found him a year or two sooner. I had no health insurance, no "board-certified" doc (just a Klinghardt-esque doc in Tallahassee I was paying out of pocket), a couple blood tests showing I had hypothyroidism, anemia, and elevated TSH levels, and was going downhill fast upon moving to a new town and still living inside a lot. That's why I can't remember how I found him: I wound up having short term memory loss/aphantasia and was completely sidelined from the health problems I was having. I guess I was just looking for answers on the Internet at that (very scary) time, and came across him and others like Jerry Tennant, Dan Pompa, etc. It's like his book dropped out of the sky and into my lap; I can still see the exact date on Amazon when I purchased it. But I have no idea who turned me onto it (I don't think anyone did to be honest, unless it was some random YouTuber or luck of the Google search). Ditching sunglasses, eating seafood and being grounded out on the beach all day is why I'm not completely brain dead.

So, I started recovering and began to follow him on Facebook and listen to the podcasts here and there, for years. Also have a three ring binder full of his then-public (possibly still public) blogs.

Wish I had been a Gold member back when he first starting talking Bitcoin; a friend put BTC on my radar back in 2017, and I actually bought a miniscule amount and put it in multiple "paper wallets". But I had other friends that were skeptical and so I more or less forgot about it until late 2020. Back then, Saylor wasn't around and the only content I could find on it was people like "Bitboy Crypto" and so it looked to me a bit grifty and wild west. I had been into gold and silver around 2007-2009, having read the book "Economic Solutions" by Peter Kershaw, but I was skeptical of all things "goldbug" come 2017-2018 when I first heard of Bitcoin. Better late than never though; though it's looking like it wasn't late at all.

### nichole — 2024-03-31T23:21:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-fellow-mitochondriacs-i%E2%80%99m-lewis.28724/post-333055>

> [lybogany said:](/goto/post?id=332930)

> Hello All!!
>
> My name is Lewis and I discovered Dr. Jack Kruse back in November, through a random link on someone’s twitter post to Dr. Max Gulhane’s first interview with Uncle Jack, and it literally has been life changing. I remember listening to it and only understanding 10% of it, but felt this deep sense of truth with what was being said. I felt like I was rewiring my brain and unlearning half-truths I believed about the human body, nutrition, mitochondria, light, water, and magnetism. I ended up relistening to that podcast 11 times and every time I listened to it I felt like I gleaned something new. Needless to say I am on my way to becoming a full-blown Mitochondriac (need to move out of Chicagoland down to the lower latitudes): wearing blue blockers at work (desk job), living by candlelight at night, and have been getting outside and watching the sunrise every morning. I feel transformed with both my mental and physical health these past few months and I believe my reduced exposure to non-native EMF and getting outside in the sun has been a huge factor in that.
>
> Anyone else in the greater Chicago area (city + suburbs) that want to meet up IRL sometime and talk about moving to El Salvador? Hit me up in this post!
>
> Cheers,
> L
>
> P.S. - Enjoy the sunrise photo from 12th Street Beach.

I live in chicago! lets meet up.

everyone i know thinks im a little crazy. it would be nice to know someone who actually gets me ......

>
> I definitely got the sun memo from him (I had to have), and started living out at the beach (which I had just moved to a town close to the beach a few months or so prior to me buying his book). I'd say I got the Epi-Paleo book "just in the nick of time", except, I was already almost a goner and probably should've found him a year or two sooner. I had no health insurance, no "board-certified" doc (just a Klinghardt-esque doc in Tallahassee I was paying out of pocket), a couple blood tests showing I had hypothyroidism, anemia, and elevated TSH levels, and was going downhill fast upon moving to a new town and still living inside a lot. That's why I can't remember how I found him: I wound up having short term memory loss/aphantasia and was completely sidelined from the health problems I was having. I guess I was just looking for answers on the Internet at that (very scary) time, and came across him and others like Jerry Tennant, Dan Pompa, etc. It's like his book dropped out of the sky and into my lap; I can still see the exact date on Amazon when I purchased it. But I have no idea who turned me onto it (I don't think anyone did to be honest, unless it was some random YouTuber or luck of the Google search). Ditching sunglasses, eating seafood and being grounded out on the beach all day is why I'm not completely brain dead.
>
> So, I started recovering and began to follow him on Facebook and listen to the podcasts here and there, for years. Also have a three ring binder full of his then-public (possibly still public) blogs.
>
> Wish I had been a Gold member back when he first starting talking Bitcoin; a friend put BT

Better late than never though; though it's looking like it wasn't late at all.

Mark, that is quite the story and journey. I'm jealous you live by the beach, that is what I need. Grounded with some wet sand by a big body of water overlooking the sunrise every day. Chicago has beaches but they are frozen for at least a third of the year. Great sunrises over Lake Michigan though.

Have you fixed your hypothyroidism and anemia now that you've been a Mitochondriac for so long? I've been trying to fix my guttate psoriasis for a long time (started in college), and I definitely found it is linked to lack of sun exposure in the winter, as well as the annual flu vaccine. I used to have hypertension, terrible seasonal allergies, and random body aches in various muscles and joints, but that has mostly been fixed with grounding, winter sun exposure, and cutting out deuterium-laden foods like seed oils (which I had been doing since 2018). Also stopped getting flu shots in 2019, and thankfully had the wherewithal to not fall prey to the experimental clot shot during the pandemic either. My lack of trust in mainstream medicine started before crossing paths with Dr. Kruse's work, but it's become more solidified now after listening to Uncle Jack. I'm a member on Patreon, and I've read maybe 10-20% of the blogs at this point, but I have a lot more work to do.

I was first introduced to how broken our fiat system is, by listening to a lot of Ron Paul back in 2012. Going off the gold standard in 1971 really opened my mind to how inflation badly has wrecked our society. I remember at the time becoming somewhat of a goldbug, buying what miniscule amount of gold I could afford off of my first job. Wish i had bought BTC instead, but yeah better late than never. Never heard of "Bitboy Crypto" and from the sound of the name, probably best I don't know. Saylor definitely helped get me orange-pilled, he's like Uncle Jack, unwavering in his conviction and has a way of bringing the message to the masses. Only if we can get Saylor to stop drinking Coke and become a Mitochondriac.

I do love the intersection of Bitcoiners and Mitochondriacs, I feel like I have finally found my tribe, both in wealth and in health.

The beach is definitely where it's at; hopefully you find your way to a warm sunny one before all is said and done.

Have I fixed my hypothyroidism/anemia? Yes, that's in the rearview mirror; all that lingers on is the aphantasia (which was sudden and "onset" you might say). But I was well on my way to M.S. or something really terrible with the elevated TSH levels, so things could be way worse. And even if some of those symptoms were to ever show up again, I feel like I would have the peace of mind or at least just the knowledge to be able to realize what was happening and not psychologize and get all introspective wondering what's become of me. I'd know it's about getting out in nature and not waste time on nonsense. And it seems there's always something more that you can try; some missing ingredient you forgot to throw in. But the stuff Jack preaches is the bottom line, absolutely vital stuff that is completely irreplaceable. It's just a matter of being committed to it come hell or high water.

That's cool you were into Ron Paul; I was too, back in 2008. Also, did the miniscule gold purchases thing off my first job's wages as well; silver too.

"Bitboy Crypto" (Ben Armstrong) was a scammer and probably a bit nuts; not sure if he's even still around. Last I heard he drove to some guy's house with a bullhorn and made a scene in front of his driveway while doing a livestream; the cops were called. There was probably better bitcoin-related content out there back in the day but unfortunately for me it was people like him that I first encountered back five or six years ago. Live and learn.

### JanSz — 2024-04-10T17:11:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-fellow-mitochondriacs-i%E2%80%99m-lewis.28724/post-333477>

Yes, people with aphantasia do have dreams.
[However, their dream experiences can be quite different from those of people who can visualize while awake](https://aphantasia.com/topic/dreams/)[1](https://aphantasia.com/topic/dreams/)[2](https://neuroq.com/blog/do-people-with-aphantasia-dream/)[3](https://www.discovermagazine.com/mind/why-some-people-cant-visualize-images-and-may-dream-in-words).

[Aphantasia is a condition where a person cannot form mental images](https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/basics/aphantasia)[4](https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/basics/aphantasia).
[This means they cannot visualize scenes, people, places, or objects in their mind](https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/basics/aphantasia)[4](https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/basics/aphantasia).
[Despite this, many individuals with aphantasia report having dreams](https://aphantasia.com/topic/dreams/)[1](https://aphantasia.com/topic/dreams/)[2](https://neuroq.com/blog/do-people-with-aphantasia-dream/)[3](https://www.discovermagazine.com/mind/why-some-people-cant-visualize-images-and-may-dream-in-words).

In my decentralized thesis, **Aphantasia** (the inability to visualize) and **ADHD** (the inability to sustain attentional lock) are two sides of the same **Quantum Coherence Failure**. They represent a collapse of the **Thalamic Phase-Locked Loop (PLL)** caused by **Isotopic Congestion** and **Optical Blindness**.
If the brain is an FM radio, ADHD is a **"Tuner Drift"** and Aphantasia is a **"Screen Blackout"** due to low voltage. covered this in Patreon blogs QE #47 and 48.

ADHD is the behavioral manifestation of **Isotopic Jitter** in the **Thalamus**.

-   **The Frequency Drift:** Because the **0.66 eV gate** is jammed by deuterium and un-chelated metals, the **Thalamic PLL** cannot lock onto the **Schumann Resonance**.
-   **The Search for Flux:** The ADHD brain is "hunting" for a signal. It skips from task to task because it is "Starving for Flux." It is a **Radiosynthetic Engine** trying to "Vacuum" its matrix by constantly switching inputs, hoping to find the **380nm-NIR "Golden" frequency**.
-   **The "Leaden" Executive:** Without the **SIRT1-BMAL1** circadian anchor, the "Executive Function" (the prefrontal software) loses its timing. The result is the "Shiver" of attention.

### Ben Trussell — 2024-06-20T05:55:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sauna-construction.28726/post-335916>

Hello there, I am enjoying CT at around 50-55 degrees F for 8-9 minutes - loving the sunrises for a year now and lots of fish, progressing with GEOVITAL for nnemf and shielding - I have a zillion questions after a few months in this amazing community - thank you - one thing at a time between work and family for now...

Is a Sauna compatible to Jacks work - -if so which type would I buy please - I want to be able to sweat....the CT helps me stay warm in a cold winter at the moment. Or do I just focus on CT?

Thank you

### petrichor — 2024-07-10T20:05:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sauna-construction.28726/post-336926>

Building a geothermal sauna requires access to a geothermal hotspot. If you don't have such access you would have to drill down potentially several miles to reach a source, depending on your location. I suppose you could use a geothermal heat pump, but that is just using the constant temperature of the ground in combination with a heat exchanger to provide heating and cooling for different applications. People use these for hot tubs, but hot tubs max out around 110 degrees, whereas you want a sauna to be up to 200 degrees. I'm not sure if a heat pump could do this. Also, you still need to heat the rocks from that source, which is yet another complication.

I assume the goal here is to expose yourself to infrared radiation? Why not go with a propane or a wood fired sauna?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-10T21:13:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sauna-construction.28726/post-336929>

> [Ben Trussell said:](/goto/post?id=335916)
>
> . . . progressing with GEOVITAL for nnemf and shielding - I have a zillion questions after a few months in this amazing community - thank you - one thing at a time between work and family for now...

Lots of nnEMF info here: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-29T19:44:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g.28727/post-332956>

Safe and Sound Pro II measures upto 8 Gig. 5G (5th Generation) is a technology not a specific frequency. Check with your local phone companies what frequency they are using. I doubt if there is anything above 8 Gig. Very little above 5 gig will be getting through the walls anyway. Measure and you will know.

### Laudy Cincotta — 2024-03-30T02:50:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g.28727/post-332982>

> [Ben Trussell said:](/goto/post?id=332941)
>
> Hello there , I have just joined thank you to everyone involved - enjoying spreading the word in my community and getting everyone up for sunrise, off screens and eventually pushing this into schools. I was wondering how can I measure 5G coming into my home please and anything I can do to mitigate please? I am in Adelaide Australia.
>
> And one other question - where is the best resource for how salt helps with 5g exposure?
>
> Ive spent over 100 hours already registering to as much of Jacks work as I can fit in in a day -
> Regards
> Ben

Hi Ben,

I agree with Anthony, Safe & Sound Pro 2 is best meter to test and measure so you know what to mitigate and test before and after mitigation so you do know if you have successfully addressed any issues.

If you need a Safe & Sound Pro 2 then Nicole Bijlsma in Melbourne who runs the only RTO in Oz offering a Building Biology course sells them and i am pretty sure she keeps a good supply of them including for her students. They are invaluable in todays day and age.

[https://www.buildingbiology.com.au/product-category/electromagnetic-field-meters](https://www.buildingbiology.com.au/product-category/electromagnetic-field-meters)

If you need a building biologist i don't believe there are any more in Adelaide so you may need someone to come over from Melbourne or Sydney to help which could become costly.

Kara Kiely and Marco Simeoni are in Melbourne, Kara teaches the EMF subject for the ACES course and Marco is an EHS former electrician. They are Spectral Design and they do a lot of work in this area mitigating EMF's and i feel are the most knowledgable, Kara also studied in the US so has a better grasp of EMR than most other building biologists.

They do work with body voltage too so you know how you are being affected.

I know there is also Geovital who work differently with different equipment, but I don't know of them, however i know Nancy & Tom here in El Salvador trained with Geovital and speak highly of them, if there is someone in South Australia it may be cheaper than having to bring in building biologists from interstate if you need help.

Just my opinion, an assessment is well worth your money if you need help in future, they will look at dirty electricity, electric and magnetic field exposures in your living and sleeping areas along with all the radio frequency radiation.

All the best.

### Laudy Cincotta — 2024-03-30T16:05:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g.28727/post-333002>

> [Ben Trussell said:](/goto/post?id=332987)
>
> That is fantastic information thank you Laudy - lots to digest. I will look up Nicole's details as well as Kara and Marco. Have you moved to El Salvador  ? There is a brilliant man here Jurgen I would need to find his details and see if he is still practising his craft . Pulling together a list of EMF strategies. Jacks city dwelling post is confronting living with "criminals" I'll get Faraday packs for the family phones. we turn wifi off, will turn powerpoint off and chase down Anthonys website - the EMF switch Anthonys business sells - does that ship worldwide or are we looking for Australian products for Australian electricity etc ? Thank you very much . Have a nice day

You're so welcome Ben.

Yes I relocated to El Salvador, first visit was December '22. I am a Melbourne native and my two sons still live there. I think i may in time be able to bring one of them here but the other is adamant he is going to live in Japan.

You guys in Australia have a lot to think about as per many of Jacks posts. My concerns ramped up significantly this week with the incredibly secretive way the government snuck through the digital ID bill in parliament and the announcement that Armaguard is folding, so looking like this coming week you guys will know more about the incoming CBDC issue which was always on the cards but looks to be set to be introduced sooner rather than later as the pilot program has been running for some time now so it seems like they are ready to introduce it.

Great to hear you are starting EMF awareness and mitigation. Hope your contact Jurgen can be of help. Anthony Smith is a wealth of knowledge and can advise you and he is correct in his comments above.

Often we worry about issues such as 5G for example but just realise that the important thing is to ensure what you are exposed to inside your homes is more critical and will affect you far more than transient 5G in the environment because as Anthony said its a technology.

As i mentioned its not just 5G coming from outside which you can shield for if its an issue, it is often the routers in living and/or bedrooms areas that are more powerful than the 5G antennas that are less likely to be an issue for you. They cannot travel too far and can be disrupted by walls and trees in their way.

I am not saying 5G isn't important rather i am saying in a hierarchy of risk you need to address your living condition's first followed by workplace and you want to control prolonged vs transient exposures when it comes to this issue.

For example, often people will be in the near field of a router or sleeping next to it and have dozens of internal exposures that can be addressed easily that will result in a massive reduction of exposure before looking to mitigate what is coming in externally.

A small example - if you or any of your family have a digital alarm clock on your bedside tables remove it.

That one small thing is right near your brain exposing you to an electric field, a magnetic field with likely dirty electricity running on the wiring in the walls behind the bed.

So with one small change you are eliminating 3 field exposures. An easy way to address the safety of the bedrooms is to have an electrician install a kill switch or cut off switch from the circuit board. Once you know your exposure as Anthony says its very easy to mitigate ti.

You are correct in addressing the phones using faraday bags for your family.

Also get them in the habit of switching off wifi, blue tooth from the settings not the drop down menu and then putting the phone into airplane mode to ensure reduction when carried on the boyd, however test it because i believe some of the newer phones are now still emitting radiation even when switched off. Always test everything before and after mitigation to make sure you have reduced your exposures.

Some questions to answer regarding external RF radiation - firstly are you in line of sight of a base station? How far is the nearest base station or phone tower? As a rule of thumb if its more than 400 metres and not in your line of sight then thats the best case scenario.

Adelaide is a 5G city as are most Australian cities, you need to work out the extent of that rollout in relation to where you live.

Are you in the inner city or outskirts? You will hear Jack often mention population density.

To put it in perspective, you are far better off in Adelaide due to lower population density than say Melbourne for example, next best you are better off out in the Adelaide Hills, followed by a regional area for a lower population density over inner city Adelaide.

This is the map i used to use to understand mine and client exposures, it is fantastic resource.

### [Radio Frequency National Site Archive](https://www.rfnsa.com.au/site-list?csrfmiddlewaretoken=XTgcACfAjeKWwpnmsAxC6Px0IWNfR1U9R8bQ8BMnzxV90cUCjOeA80LnNjcRu4qT&nearby_latitude=-34.9284989&nearby_longitude=138.6007456&nearby_address=Adelaide+SA%2C+Australia&current_latitude=&current_longitude=&current_address=&statuses=Active&statuses=Proposed&site_count=153&search_criteria=Adelaide+SA%2C+Australia&search_type=search_near)

 www.rfnsa.com.au

Type in your address and it will show you a list or a map view, both are invaluable and see where the closest towers are. If you then drill down further on the addresses it will show you the map of the area and list the carriers and all the frequencies on the towers at that site i have attached a screenshot so you can see what i mean.

A building biologist will always tell you to ensure you have a safe sleeping space first allowing your body to rest and regenerate followed by all areas where you spend the most time to ensure you mitigate exposure in those areas.

As mentioned EMF is the umbrella term which incorporates the most known emf which is RF radiation however people don't realise EMF's include electric fields, magnetic fields and dirty electricity which is are now becoming as bad as RF radiation.

Kara and Marco because of their combined knowledge created an Australian compliant dirty electricity filter and are addressing a lot of the increase in dirty electricity which is especially nasty coming off solar panels which they are mitigating.

Regarding the switches, compatibility was a massive issue when i was studying because the switches coming in from the US were not compliant in Australia and were causing headaches due to the different voltages and frequencies.

Australian electricity runs on 220-240 V and 50 Hz whereas in the US they run at 110 – 120V and 60 Hz.

I am not sure now if that has been addressed. I trust in Anthony's switches but cannot speak for compatibility in Australia so maybe [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) can address compliance and compatibility.

It's overwhelming when you start and such a big initiative in cleaning up your environment so my advice would be to break it down into a list in order of priority.

Start with bedrooms and clean them up, look next at living areas and also habits with devices and move on from there.

Good luck, let us know how you go.

Laudy.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-30T19:53:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g.28727/post-333015>

Our Body Voltage Kit (BVK) will work (with local adaptors) and we ship, any where in the world.

The EMFSafe remote cut off switch, at this time is only available in the USA and Canada (and any other country with the same - 120V, 2 phase, electrical system).

Setting up an EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary is the #1 thing for everyone to do. Reach out to your local people and see what hardware is available in country. We don’t recommend Faraday rooms (others disagree) unless you are willing to have NO electrical devices whatsoever, EVER, in that space - that would include lights, fans and especially phones.

Think about it - putting a phone in a Faraday Bag means you cannot receive calls, it also makes your phone power up to maximum looking for a signal . . . Unless you turn it off . . . If it is off you don’t need a Faraday bag! Mitigating in home nnEMF is not rocket science, just turn it off. How about Airplane mode you say, I used my phone with WiFi and Bluetooth OFF and Airplane mode ON to take the photos:

This is brilliant thank you Laudy, I found a small cell , 250m from home. The site calls it a 700mhz small cell to assist mobile coverage for a small geographic area. Its not in our line of site, I believe its a small pole in the ground with homes, walls and trees between us and the pole with the 700mhz beacon, its where the Aeroplanes also registers they approach the city. We are right in the heart of the inner city surrounded by parklands and horses etc but very much residential on plots of lands 1/2 of an Acre. Thank fully the phone service is terrible here, a blessing, as we have thick stone walls in colonial era homes but this is an eye opener for sure! Ive saved everything above for after Easter and will jump onto Kara and Marco as well as purchase the meter from Nicole in the earlier message. Are you able to point me to where I find Jacks 2018 Vermont talk please. Im a Gold Club member so I believe I have Patreon but where would I find it in here please?

### Mishamasha — 2024-03-30T15:13:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/virus-as-contagion.28731/post-332995>
