# Thread: The RISK OF Artificial intelligence......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risk-of-artificial-intelligence.28732/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-30T16:04:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risk-of-artificial-intelligence.28732/post-333001>

Man must have complete authority over the technocratic machines, the code they use and the algorithms that comes from them. If they do not have this level of control, man will be doomed to idiocy, lies and falsehoods perpetrated by elitest technocrats. AI needs strict regulation and TIGHT enforcement control with steep penalties (death) in a Constitutional Republic because of the risks it brings to the Constitution. Take No Quarter against the treasonists and those who commit tyranny with code.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-31T00:54:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risk-of-artificial-intelligence.28732/post-333023>

The real problem I have as a Bitcoiner with artifical intelligence is that when it is unleashed it is a force to centralize things.

This why data collectors love AI.

You must realize who ever talks AI up a lot is 100% centralized and not for decentralization.

They may give it lip service but do not let them steal your oxygen.

Think google, meta, and X as perfect examples of AI that should worry you.

Where does centralization stop and fascism begin? With google and meta we know there is no boundary.

I think Musk so far is reasonable. But it's early.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-31T00:56:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risk-of-artificial-intelligence.28732/post-333024>

AI began in 1943. The paper was 80 years before ChatGPT: Nothing happened until 2023. Mcollick and Pitts was the paper in 1943.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-03-31T01:11:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risk-of-artificial-intelligence.28732/post-333026>

Deep Blue was older AI and it used custom VLSI chips to parallelize the alpha–beta search algorithm, an example of **_symbolic AI._** The system derived its playing strength mainly from brute force computing power.

Open AI is a different animal. It is the animal that needs to be tightly controlled because it allows for massive centralization associated with totalitarianism, fascism, authoritarism, and extreme danger for society.

In a move that will likely set new standards in the world of artificial intelligence, NVIDIA has just [unveiled its latest marvels](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/technologies/blackwell-architecture/): the Blackwell B200 GPU and the GB200 “superchip.”

The Blackwell B200, with its impressive 20 petaflops of FP4 processing power, and the GB200, a synergistic blend of dual B200 GPUs and a Grace CPU, are poised to redefine AI processing capabilities.

This strategic move by NVIDIA arrives at a time when the hunger for advanced open AI solutions is soaring, placing the company at the forefront of meeting these growing demands.

### NikkiYork — 2024-03-31T01:26:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risk-of-artificial-intelligence.28732/post-333027>

AI is the most worrisome development there is.

AI has no heart or soul and it doesn’t live. Smart people told me it would be an extension of our mind, but they are wrong, because it can compute much faster than a human mind.

Yes, it can only do what it is programmed to, but that is the problem. It can do it a lot faster and better than humans and it DOES NOT FEEL PAIN. Which in my opinion is the reason why there cannot be an ethical programming for AI.

The only way - in my opinion and as a philosophy major in ethics - to create a coherent ethical theory is that humans share the experience of pain.
Thomas Nagel writes about that in his book “the possibility of altruism”.

Most people are ethical relativists. They claim different culture, different values, it’s all relative.

It is until pain comes into play. Add common sense, we need that too. If I stand on somebody’s foot by accident and they say “ouch”, I will, as a healthy person, move off of it. Herein lies the possibility of altruism.

This is my first principle, the start from which I can deduct all coherently. So it’s no longer relative, pain is amongst humans something I can set as an absolute-ish standard.

I’ve thought about Shanahan’s quest to teach AI ethics and I believe we are doomed, because the AI does not know physical pain, no matter how you “fake” pain.

The AI contest or race is also a race of “the winner takes it all” and that includes military power world wide. It’s a bleak outlook. I appreciate the try of those who want to teach to a machine what life is, I can’t believe in it.
We better destroy all those machines, sadly. I pray for it.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-31T01:54:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risk-of-artificial-intelligence.28732/post-333030>

Just maybe it won’t be an EMP (solar or man made) that destroys mankind . . . but saves it . . .

### Jack Kruse — 2024-04-01T03:40:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risk-of-artificial-intelligence.28732/post-333083>

It could be the case.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-02T04:52:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risk-of-artificial-intelligence.28732/post-334196>

Google was backed by InQtel (CIA) very early on; it was never just a search engine. The Patriot Act gave unlimited value to many Silicon Valley behemoths. All of them are merely privatized versions of intelligence agency projects (Oracle, Palantir, Facebook, 23andMe, Ancestry, etc.). This is not a new thing; Google has pretty much always been a data aggregator for the surveillance state. Every single one of them infringes on the Fourth Amendment.

### NikkiYork — 2024-05-02T19:11:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risk-of-artificial-intelligence.28732/post-334222>

AI or a machine or an algorithm will soon decide about who lives and who dies.

As much as those machines and programs can help, they are not human, they have no spark, no intuition, no connection to a higher self or a source or however you want to put it. They are merely quantum entangled, they are like stones - about as much animated as stones.

Everybody gets the self driving car example: 3 outcomes are possible. A) drive into a group of 5 possibly kids b) kill a pedestrian who happens to be Jack c) kill its passenger who is Nikki Stein.

Without AI there are many factors coming in. I might run into the kids because I recognize Jack. I might not be able to think and just kill myself, I might run over Jack… I might be inspired and somehow hit the brakes and Jack jumps.

I saw a self driving car make a u-turn on a 6 lane street. No human would drive like that. It was erratic to a human way of driving, but it was “safe”.

So now, if the car has all Jack’s data, maybe it will decide that it kills the kids, because we can make new ones.

Or maybe it will be programmed that kids are higher valued than adults? Or maybe it will protect the passenger who paid for the “experience”?

I studied philosophy and in particular ethics. Utilitarianism is basically the math way of dealing with ethics. No juju, just equations. In Goettingen philosophy was mostly attended by math and physics students. We did contemporary Anglo-Saxon analytical philosophy there.

What you need for programming AI basically. A lot is possible, but you can’t program something that it is like a human.

It’s really the Frankenstein story. In that sense we are doomed, because the hard drive of computers and the computing performance is “better” than ours already.
In that sense we are doomed.

# Thread: Mason Pearson brush at Costco NOW
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mason-pearson-brush-at-costco-now.28734/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2024-03-30T18:11:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mason-pearson-brush-at-costco-now.28734/post-333009>

Hi tribe people,
From your knowledgable hairdresser the best hairbrush on the planet: the British handmade Mason Pearson brush, is currently available at Costco online.
Most of them are sold out.

Our friendly member Christine Huebner told me yesterday and only the junior brush is left now.

They are about 100bucks off from the original price, that’s why they go so quickly.
If you buy on Amazon you will often get fakes, so not worth the risk.

Why is it so great? I’m not sure how they do it, it has not been beaten by any other brand I tried. I do have cheaper ones that come close, but this is the only brush you’ll ever need for long hair. Short hair, oh well, your hair is never older than a couple of months, so don’t worry.

The important part is the combination of high quality boar bristles with nylon - on an air cushion.

The boar bristle scales lock into the human hair scales and transport the natural oils down the hair shaft which will keep your hair healthy and dust free. That is the brush science. The nylon is for stability.

It seems like no real science, but with long hair after 3-6 you’ll start seeing the results of overall healthier hair.

Obviously hair health starts under the skin, however, once the keratinized proteins that your hair is made of get pushed out of the skin, the hair is exposed to all kinds of environmental influences and brushing does the trick to help the hair self clean via its own sebum.

> The boar bristle scales lock into the human hair scales and transport the natural oils down the hair shaft which will keep your hair healthy and dust free. That is the brush science. The nylon is for stability.
>
> It seems like no real science, but with long hair after 3-6 you’ll start seeing the results of overall healthier hair.
>
> Obviously hair health starts under the skin, however, once the keratinized proteins that your hair is made of get pushed out of the skin, the hair is exposed to all kinds of environmental influences and brushing does the trick to help the hair self clean via its own sebum.

Thanks again for reminding me of them!

### NikkiYork — 2024-04-06T20:20:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mason-pearson-brush-at-costco-now.28734/post-333316>

Avoid brushing wet hair, use a comb instead.
Wet hair is a lot weaker than dry hair, because hair is made up of hydrogen bonds (and sulphur and salt bonds).

And in wet hair those hydrogen or water bonds are partially broken and they re-bond when the hair dries.

This is how you create a curl from wet hair. The hydrogen bonds re-bond in whichever shape you put it, like you roll it on a curler, and after it dries you have a curl.  and the curl comes out when you break the Hydrogens bonds again by making the hair wet. How neat is that!

Anyways, wet hair is more fragile.

# Thread: When in Vegas…
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-in-vegas%E2%80%A6.28736/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2024-03-31T11:56:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-in-vegas%E2%80%A6.28736/post-333040>

I’m in Vegas for the first time after becoming light sensitive. As in waking up for sunrise and not exposing yourself to artificial light after dark.

And I have to say, it’s a trip. Everything becomes different.
People seem so sick. It starts hurting. It starts revolting inside. It’s so beautiful to witness and at the same time it’s painful, because: where do we go from here?
Do I have to leave it all behind?
Who is with me? Like nobody?

I’m not going deeper into this, you know what I mean. Or not.

You can probably only relate, if you were out there like I was.

I have to thank Jack for the science and saving so many!!!! Thank you!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: When the protocols aren't working
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-the-protocols-arent-working.28737/>

### OptimalSkr — 2024-03-31T19:27:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-the-protocols-arent-working.28737/post-333046>

So I’ve been following Jack since 2016. I’ve listened to all his podcasts/books bought in and followed all of his advice. I have done everything he said and it has made very little difference in my health. I have gone from a 3/10 to a 5/10. But nothing near where I was before my injury or where I want to be.

Main symptoms include:

-   Chronic fatigue – mainly brain fatigue, but also physical energy levels
-   Need to take lots of breaks and naps during day to recover my energy
-   Body weakness and inability to heal and recover from new and old soft tissue sports injuries (wrist, chest, back, fingers)
-   Hyper sensitivity to sound and noise (hyper acousis) and tinnitus. Also have miso-phenia (bad reactions to certain sounds)
-   Chemical sensitivity

So what am I supposed to do now? I just spent 1.5 weeks in December and 5 weeks from February in the Yucatan getting more sun and going to the beach more than in my entire life and it made very little difference at all.

Yes high in fish - salmon, shrimp, sardines, sole (I don't have access to oysters)

Sleep has not really been an issue since I adopted the light based protocols. I get to sleep pretty easily and sleep straight through the night. So in that sense the sleep is "good". However I usually wake up a few times a week feeling terrible - body fatigue, brain fatigue, slow to get going.

I did go for an official sleep study 2 times - but they said everything was fine - I think they were also looking for sleep apnea, which was negative -- Although I did notice that keeping my door open has opened up air flow and has improved my energy upon waking.

Are they opened to the outside or to another room that is also AC?
\------------------------
It looks like you should keep those doors open all the time.
\----------------

I am in USA, latitude 40° 51’ 26" N
My home is build to be fully closed to incoming air.
(But I make sure that there is air connection to the outside.)

I used to live in Itaorna Brazil -23.00773 (south)
The way they constructed Air Conditioned homes there
is that there was about 1" gap around all windows.
That gap allowed certain amount of air to get in or go out.
That also allowed air pressure to change simultaneously with the outside, especially in presence of winds.

Fresh air is possibly what you are missing at night.

### Greg Cioth — 2024-04-01T03:51:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-the-protocols-arent-working.28737/post-333084>

Sorry to hear about your situation. I was not as bad as you mentally, but had serious muscular issues and heavy metals and related items. Could not sleep at night for over a year, due to pains etc. I spent years trying to fix and lots of $. Did not change until I spent 4 out of 5 months in MX (outside of San Jose del Cabo) combined with reducing my nnEMF load at my main house. MX emf was very low load. Now I am off all supplements, metals test is massively improved and have not felt this good in over a decade. Sleep great etc. Spent mornings on the beach grounding with shirt off and got tan during the day. I limited blue light by probably 50% reduction. PS- I started feeling the initial change at about 1.5 to 2 months.

### Johan Lindøm — 2024-04-01T10:31:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-the-protocols-arent-working.28737/post-333088>

> [OptimalSkr said:](/goto/post?id=333046)

> So I’ve been following Jack since 2016. I’ve listened to all his podcasts/books bought in hook line and sinker and followed all of his advice. I have done everything he said and it has made very little difference in my health. I have gone from a 3/10 to a 5/10. But nothing near where I was before my injury or where I want to be.
>
> My symptoms started with a blow to the face/head in 2012, and I have never been the same since.
>
> Main symptoms include:
>
> -   Chronic fatigue – mainly brain fatigue, but also physical energy levels
> -   Need to take lots of breaks and naps during day to recover my energy
> -   Body weakness and inability to heal and recover from new and old soft tissue sports injuries (wrist, chest, back, fingers)
> -   Unable to work since 2012 – (I worked in marketing/business on computer all day)
> -   Hyper sensitivity to sound and noise (hyper acousis) and tinnitus. Also have miso-phenia (bad reactions to certain sounds)
> -   Chemical sensitivity
>
> Protocols Utilized:
>
> -   Daily sun exposure from May-October – built solar callus (Live in Southern Ontario, Canada - Detroit area)
> -   Take trips down to Playa Del Carmen/Yucatan area in the winter – Recently 1.5 weeks in December and 1 month in February)
> -   See all the sunrises
> -   Never wear sunglasses and take my prescriptions ones off when I am outside
> -   Grounding while getting sun
> -   Always wear red/orange glasses after sundown
> -   Go to bed at 10pm every night – no exceptions - Wake at 7am – shut screens down 1 hour before bed to relax
> -   Only use incandescent and red lights in house when needed
> -   Hardwired whole house for internet – no WiFi used
> -   Do not use the cell phone much at all – always keep on airplane mode
> -   Checked house for EMF with meters and mitigated most of it – turn power to bedrooms off at night
> -   Use red night light on phone and computer screens
> -   Blackout room for sleeping at night – quiet and temperature controlled environment
> -   2 times a week chiropractic for past 7 years
> -   2 walks a day 10 minutes each and light stretching
> -   Eat paleo style diet with all organic ingredients – never eat out/fast food – no alcohol/drugs/caffeine etc
> -   Only drink distilled water with added trace minerals
> -   Only use all natural organic soaps, cleaners, toothpastes, deodorants, etc
>
> Tech and other things tried:
>
> -   Bought EMR tech red lights – firestorm and firewave – (3 years use)
> -   Deuterium Depleted water - D.D.W. – test (3 months)
> -   Magnetico Sleep pad – (6 years)
> -   Bought EMF meters for Magnetic and Electric and Radio Frequency
> -   Mediation and Kriya Yoga (1.5 years)
> -   Andy Cutler Detox Protocol - 7 rounds using ALA and DMSA
> -   10 rounds of hyper baric oxygen therapy - (pro level)
> -   Tried lots of different supplements over the years but stopped taking them years ago
> -   2 sleep studies which reported no negative findings
>
> So what am I supposed to do now? And Jack always just says “move south” --- well I just spent 1.5 weeks in December and 5 weeks from February in the Yucatan getting more sun and going to the beach more than in my entire life and it made very little difference at all.

Sleep is important, and an improvement that is good for you. You don't know alternative outcomes and these could have been devastating. Regrettably, we never gain insight into these. If you are a Haplogroup L0 or equivalent, I don´t think any of your protocols mean anything.... and Ontario. Firestorm is no magic bullet, it is all fake... fortunately Jack has talked less about this solution. I have not yet destroyed my natural ability to produce vit d or melanin, and this year I have been using Firehawk along with both UVa and UVb and this has been a great help to me. However, it is important that I do not treat myself with too much light therapy, this leads to stress, and the information I receive is fake and out of sync with my local natural electromagnetic environment. Light therapy should be used during the day, depending on natural light cycles and what you are trying to achieve. This is my n=1. I am H2 Haplogroup. However, I know that time in natural light is most important, and important right after artificial light therapy. This is not advice to anyone, just my experience or something I imagine. The most important thing for me is to avoid the phone and blue light at night (and light without red/IR), and to spend as much time as possible outside in nature in natural light. Preferably in strong bright sunlight. No protocols, biohacks, and easy or quick fixes. In addition, it is important to respect the dark and light cycle where you live, and not a man-made clock. Your sleep train runs and it depends on the Earth's changes in light and darkness, and does not follow specific times on your phone.

### Ravi Sandhu — 2024-04-01T11:13:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-the-protocols-arent-working.28737/post-333089>

I empathise, and can also sense your determination.

There was a time, when I was really struggling, that I went to the Canary Islands for 2 months. This is 3 years ago now.

I got a good level of light exposure, but also didn't understand a lot of the healing work we do here. I didn't consume enough DHA. I didn't ground enough. But I was super consistent with sunrise, sunset, and I was getting light.

I was stuck and not improving, and then I recall one day when I lay on the beach, all day, for about 8hrs. And I felt....different.

My mind was clearer, I wasn't in the constant emotional pain I'd been living in for decades, and I kinda knew deep inside, OK, there is something here. There must be something I am missing.

There was honestly a lot I didn't really know them. And I suspect, I just needed a lot more healing.

I returned to the UK, moved around Europe, kinda got a little better by being consistent with the various ideas. But never had a real breakthrough.

Over time, I have improved, but I am aware, to properly get myself up, and get somewhere interesting, I think I need a lot more time in the sun. So I will now be off for 6 months and really focusing on nailing this.

I would like others to chime in with their experience, but I can say, 5 weeks, may not have been enough. And I think we have to be quite disciplined when doing these protocols and find ways to spend ~8hrs outdoors a day getting strong light....I wasn't doing that in the Canary Islands, now I have learned, and when I go to Mexico, I will genuinely be out 8-10hrs a day

\-Ravi

### nichole — 2024-04-01T13:47:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-the-protocols-arent-working.28737/post-333090>

HI! how long have you been on your healing journey? i was sick for almost 20 years. bedridden and in and out of the ER. Once your on the right healing path it does take time! patience, consistency, and dedication to your protocol over Time...is what heals you. Once you've become chronically ill it does take some time to reverse the damage, but it definitely will is you are 100% compliant with the right protocol. you will start to see that u have more better days slowly over time and less worse days. are you meditating? meditation really helps to calm the nervous system down, and help with the healing process. Are you doing cold showers everyday? these are a couple of things that also really helped me. they seem so minor but doing everything in my protocol had a specific purpose and its everything that helped me slowly heal over time. diet is huge, im kaetocarnivre....lots of ancestral foods and organ meats.

it takes some time to reset the body back into balance once its ok of wack, but u can definitely do it if your consistent.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-04-02T04:42:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-the-protocols-arent-working.28737/post-333114>

2G and 5G on a WiFi router is referring to the frequency - 2.4 GigaHertz (GHz) or 5 GHz - Nothing to do with 5G (5th generation technology phone system).
If you put the router into a Faraday bag (that works) you will not be able to pick up a signal, the router becomes useless. If you put your computer into a Faraday bag you cannot use it. If you turn off WiFi and Bluetooth on your computer you don’t need a Faraday bag because there is no signal to shield. If you use ethernet connection and turn off WiFi and Bluetooth you don’t need a Faraday bag because again there will not be a signal to block. If you are not hard wired with ethernet but the WiFi is on you need to protect yourself from the signal with a shield between you and it just like the photo I posted.

# Thread: Informed Consent
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/informed-consent.28739/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DLO — 2024-04-01T05:24:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/informed-consent.28739/post-333086>

Hopefully it's just an April fools joke.
**In a significant blow to patient autonomy, informed consent has been quietly revoked just 77 years after it was codified in the Nuremberg Code.**

On the 21st of December 2023, as we were frantically preparing for the festive season, the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) and the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) issued a [final ruling to amend a provision](https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2023/12/21/2023-27935/institutional-review-board-waiver-or-alteration-of-informed-consent-for-minimal-risk-clinical) of the [21st Century Cures Act](https://www.congress.gov/114/bills/hr34/BILLS-114hr34enr.pdf). This allowed..

> _…an_ **_exception from the requirement to obtain informed consent_** _when a clinical investigation poses no more than a minimal risk to the human subject…_

This ruling went into effect on January 22nd, 2024, which means it’s already standard practice across America.
So, what is the 21st Century Cures Act? It is a controversial Law enacted by the 114th United States Congress in January 2016 with strong support from the [pharmaceutical industry](https://fortune.com/2016/12/07/congress-passes-21st-century-cures/). The Act was designed to

> …accelerate the discovery, development, and delivery of 21st-century cures, **_and for other purposes \[?\]…_**\[emphasis added\]

Some of the provisions within this Act make for uncomfortable reading. For example, the Act supported:

_High-risk, high-reward research \[Sec. 2036\].

Novel clinical trial designs \[Sec. 3021\]

Encouraging vaccine innovation \[Sec. 3093\]._

This Act granted the National Institutes of Health (NIH) legal protection to pursue high-risk, novel vaccine research. A strong case could be made that these provisions capture all the necessary architecture required for much of the evil that transpired over the past four years.

Overturning patient-informed consent was another stated goal of the original Act. Buried under Section 3024 was the provision to develop an

> **_Informed consent waiver_** _or alteration for clinical investigation._

Scholars of medical history understand that the concept of informed consent, something we all take for granted today, is a relatively new phenomenon codified in its modern understanding as one of the critical principles of the [Nuremberg Code in 1947](https://www.encyclopedia.com/science/medical-magazines/nuremberg-code-establishes-principle-informed-consent). It is inconceivable that just 77 years after Nuremberg, the door has once again opened for state-sanctioned medical experimentation on potentially uninformed and unwilling citizens.

> …accelerate the discovery, development, and delivery of 21st-century cures, **_and for other purposes \[?\]…_**\[emphasis added\]

Some of the provisions within this Act make for uncomfortable reading. For example, the Act supported:

_High-risk, high-reward research \[Sec. 2036\].

Novel clinical trial designs \[Sec. 3021\]

Encouraging vaccine innovation \[Sec. 3093\]._

This Act granted the National Institutes of Health (NIH) legal protection to pursue high-risk, novel vaccine research. A strong case could be made that these provisions capture all the necessary architecture required for much of the evil that transpired over the past four years.

Overturning patient-informed consent was another stated goal of the original Act. Buried under Section 3024 was the provision to develop an

> **_Informed consent waiver_** _or alteration for clinical investigation._

Scholars of medical history understand that the concept of informed consent, something we all take for granted today, is a relatively new phenomenon codified in its modern understanding as one of the critical principles of the [Nuremberg Code in 1947](https://www.encyclopedia.com/science/medical-magazines/nuremberg-code-establishes-principle-informed-consent). It is inconceivable that just 77 years after Nuremberg, the door has once again opened for state-sanctioned medical experimentation on potentially uninformed and unwilling citizens.

According to this amendment, the state alone, acting through the NIH, the FDA, and the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), will decide what is considered a _“minimal risk”_ and, most concerning, will determine:

> **…appropriate safeguards to protect the rights, safety, and welfare of human subjects.**

**Notice the term _subjects_, not patients, persons, individuals, or citizens…but _subjects_. In asymmetrical power relationships such as clinician/patient, it is understood that the passive _subject_ will comply with the rulings and mandates of their medical masters. The use of the term _subjects_ also serves to dehumanise. The dehumanisation of populations was a critical component of Nazi human experimentation and, as [Hannah Arendt argued](https://www.google.com.au/books/edition/The_Origins_of_Totalitarianism/UqqqtAEACAAJ?hl=en), is an essential step toward denying citizens _“…the right to have rights.”_**
This ruling also allows researchers and their misguided evangelical billionaire backers to potentially pursue dangerous experimental programs such as Bill Gates’ [mosquito vaccines](https://www.npr.org/sections/goatsandsoda/2022/09/21/1112727841/a-box-of-200-mosquitoes-did-the-vaccinating-in-this-malaria-trial-thats-not-a-jo), [mRNA vaccines](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8211312/) [in livestock](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7332557/), and vaccines in [aerosols](https://insight.jci.org/articles/view/155655). This Act encourages these novel and high-risk programs, with medical studies approved as _‘minimal risk’_ by the regulators no longer requiring researchers and pharmaceutical companies to obtain patient consent. Yet, the histories of pharmacology and medicine are plagued with clinical investigations and interventions that were thought to pose no more than minimal risk to humans but went on to cause immeasurable pain, suffering, and death.

This amendment represents merely a first tentative step as the US government _tests the waters_ to see what it can get away with. Given the lack of attention this ruling received in both the corporate press and independent media, the government is likely to feel emboldened to widen its scope. Thus, this decision represents the beginning of a chilling revisionism in Western medical history, as patient autonomy is again forsaken.

This ruling, to be actioned by potentially corrupt scientists, health bureaucrats, and captured health and drug regulators, is another step toward a dystopian future unimaginable just five years ago. No doubt the infrastructure to implement this decree is already being constructed by the same _groupthink cultists_ responsible for the nightmarish pandemic lockdowns, continuing to place the pursuit of profit and _the greater good_ above individual choice, bodily autonomy, and informed consent.

# Thread: Greetings, everyone!

### Cicero106 — 2024-04-02T16:51:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-everyone.28742/post-333135>

I am a 21 year old med-student in Salzburg, Austria and in search of the truth about health and the correct way to live for a human being, which means following the true laws of nature. Fortunately, I was influenced by my father, a physician, who started to drift away from centralized medicine and introduced me and my mother (physician as well) to a different way of thinking. He started with diet and gradually moved up the hierarchy of importance concerning human health, first studying the work of others, for instance Alexander Wunsch and then discovering the work of Dr. Kruse.

I myself am following a carnivore diet, although I am convinced that diet is ancillary/secondary, because I am not living under optimal conditions (EMF, blue light) and well, maybe I got brainwashed a bit too much by the "meat-heads". The more I follow Dr. Kruse, the more I reorganise my priorities and the more I get a deeper and better understanding of what is fundamental. I am eager to learn and acquire as much knowledge as I can, in order to better explain to the people (my loved ones, patients, other physicians, as many people as I can) how they can reach their maximal state of health and well-being, simply by doing what nature designed us to do. Thus, in other words, I want to teach them to make me jobless.

# Thread: Anne and Blake's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-and-blakes-optimal-journal.28744/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Annemcnulty — 2024-04-03T13:59:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-and-blakes-optimal-journal.28744/post-333157>

I am a 42 year old woman in Ottawa, Canada and have been diagnosed with chronic fatigue, mold illness/CIRS, POTS, mild PCOS, EBV, a variety of chronic infections (including strep and Lyme) plus complex PTSD from childhood. We've always been "health conscious" and worked with functional medicine practitioners, but I only started learning about quantum biology and Dr. Kruse's work about six months ago. Since then we've significantly reduced nnEMF in our home, changed all our lighting, become much more careful about wearing orange glasses after sunset, and see the sunrise almost every day. I seem to feel best on a mostly carnivore diet. I do travel for work a fair amount though which is a nightmare for light, water and magnetism.

In spite of my health issues, I am still functional (i.e. can work and parent). I am currently much more focused on helping my 8 year old son, who has been diagnosed with PANS/PANDAS, ADHD and Pathological Demand Avoidance (an autism spectrum disorder characterized by nervous system activation from any demands). The practitioners we work with feel this is mostly driven by mold exposure (which likely started in utero) and Bartonella. His symptoms have been mood instability, impulsivity, vision issues (needs reading glasses plus has a stigmatism), hyperactivity, chronic fight/flight, many sensory issues, picky eating, occasional depression (he won't leave the house sometimes), severe leg pains at night (about once every two weeks), and keratosis pilaris all over his arms and legs. His symptoms have improved with mold remediation, a mold detox protocol (nystatin, itraconazole, LDN, welchol, ketotifen, H1/H2 blockers and many supplements) plus doing all the nnEMF and light work above. We also use homeopathy and frequency specific microcurrent. I am guilty of using a lot of interventions at once so it's hard to know what has helped the most.

I would say he is 70% improved, but still struggles with nervous system dysregulation, impulsivity (which has social consequences), the leg pains, and reading at school (which is worse when he is in fight/flight). I'm struggling with whether I should stay the course with the treatment modalities above, or try to find a practitioner who is mold/lyme informed but also quantum biology informed. He also has issues with retained primitive reflexes (which seems very common with spectrum kids and we haven't really dealt with that). In general, there are SO MANY suggested causes and interventions for these kids (and many profiteers in the non-neurotypical kids market).

My overarching question is - for conditions driven at least in part by mold/Lyme/etc. - is there still a role for the functional medicine protocols (nutritional supports, anti fungals/anti microbials, mast cell stabilizers, etc.) in healing?

His symptoms have been mood instability, impulsivity, vision issues (needs reading glasses plus has a stigmatism), hyperactivity, chronic fight/flight, many sensory issues, picky eating, occasional depression (he won't leave the house sometimes), severe leg pains at night (about once every two weeks), and keratosis pilaris all over his arms and legs. His symptoms have improved with mold remediation, a mold detox protocol (nystatin, itraconazole, LDN, welchol, ketotifen, H1/H2 blockers and many supplements) plus doing all the nnEMF and light work above. We also use homeopathy and frequency specific microcurrent. I am guilty of using a lot of interventions at once so it's hard to know what has helped the most.

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-04-03T21:54:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coq10-supplementation.28746/post-333177>

I apologize for using the S word

I have found an interestingly massive benefit from Q10 supplementation and am curious what this means.

I am eating full carnivore basically just well cooked red meat, and some seafood (but not as often as I’d like due to cost)

For the most part my circadian cycles have been good so PPP should be working well

I’m curious if there are any other variables that would be making me deficient in Q10. I know [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) has said in his paleo supplementation guide that it’s helpful for mostly meat diets. Meat has CoQ10 so I’m wondering why I am unable to get enough or generate enough for my needs.

Having owned a Tesla for 4 years maybe I am just deep in the hole and need to build it back up?

Very interested to have a conversation here about what depletes it, how to optimize endogenous Q10 creation, etc

I agree no supplements should be needed as humans and that they are almost all useless or dangerous beyond short term aid.

### petrichor — 2024-04-03T23:04:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coq10-supplementation.28746/post-333182>

Blue light depletes it: [https://www.facebook.com/drjackkrus...nown-to-mediate-isomerizatio/626784372145711/](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/the-q-cycle-101-and-optical-biologylight-energy-is-known-to-mediate-isomerizatio/626784372145711/)

> The conversion of energy from carbohydrates and fats to ATP, the form of energy used by cells, requires the presence of coenzyme Q10 in the inner mitochondrial membrane. As part of the mitochondrial electron transport chain, coenzyme Q10accepts electrons from reducing equivalents generated during fatty acid and glucose metabolism and then transfers them to electron acceptors. If CoEnQ10 is destroyed by blue light this has big implications for mitochondrial function at the skin level and could lead to energy balance problems due to elevation of mitochondrial disruption. At the same time, coenzyme Q10 contributes to transferring protons (H+) from the mitochondrial matrix to the intermembrane space, creating a proton gradient across the inner mitochondrial membrane. The energy released when the protons flow back into the mitochondrial interior is used to form ATP.

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-04-04T01:11:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coq10-supplementation.28746/post-333196>

In the case of other things in the body made endogenously though, like glutathione, NRF2 pathway, etc the body responds to the environment. This implies Q10 must be supplemented with any nnEMF exposure.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) needs to discuss Q10 more because I have done everything one can imagine, and alongside strong sunlight, optimizing my environment, consistent carnivore, and seafood, this was the key rate limiting component that finally and immediately has fixed my gut issues. Many others may be inflicted with this bottleneck and I haven’t heard Jack talk much about CoQ10 in podcasts.

When I get sunlight now I feel easily double the amount of optimal. Alongside zero inflammation in my face in the morning.

There must be a way to provoke the body to produce more of its own CoQ10, I hate the idea of supplementing for the rest of my life alongside any nnEMF exposure.

Jack implying that those on a high meat diet should supplement brings up questions as well since meat is the highest source of CoQ10 and for some reason needs to be supplemented primarily with a meat based diet. Is this due to the adaptations required on a multi year basis adapting PPP to a fat burning diet?

Does anyone know if too much Q10 can be bad? Seeing as Jack recommends doses higher than 1000 mg / day for gut diseases I’m curious if the body can cope with high exogenous doses. I haven’t seen any negatives anywhere else and Q10 does act differently than other “antioxidants” which leads me to believe it’s safe compared to the chaos ensued by other exogenous antioxidants.

It seems to me like it may be the exception to the rule of no supplementation, since most other supplements are used on a short term basis, and our modern world imposes this deficiency with a lack of apparent sufficient feedback control in the human body.

### petrichor — 2024-04-05T01:18:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coq10-supplementation.28746/post-333242>

I am cautious about taking supplements myself, including antioxidants, even though I notice the benefits immediately. I do get concerned that I am removing important information from the electron transport chain that occurs when ROS are created. The fact that they help so much indicates to me that I am losing too many electrons from other sources, and my environment still sucks. It's obviously not a zero risk intervention but, like you, I feel much better when I take them, so I do for a period of time, and then I take a break. I feel better about taking like CoQ10 and D-ribose, than NAC or glutathione.

Have you tried molecular hydrogen supplementation in your water? To me this seems like the antioxidant with the lowest risk potential--assuming your water is filtered, remineralized and restructured--as naturally occurring spring water sources are often very high in hydrogen content. You could also find a local spring, and test the water yourself. [https://findaspring.org/map/](https://findaspring.org/map/) Hydrogen is very easy to test for in water. This is worth doing anyway, even if the water isn't very high in H2.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-04-05T03:32:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coq10-supplementation.28746/post-333247>

There is a whole webinar on it. I do not need to say anything else.

The CoQ10 "Isotopic Switch"
CoQ10’s structure, specifically the ten non-conjugated double bonds in its isoprene tail makes it a massive antenna for terrestrial sunlight.

Photoisomerization: When sunlight (or artificial blue light) hits CoQ10, it triggers a rearrangement of the cis-trans positions.

The Radical Signal: This process generates free radicals. In the "Manufactured Dynasty," radicals are always "bad." In the "Quantum Dynasty," these radicals are Information Carriers.

They mediate the geometrical isomerization of monounsaturated fatty acids (MUFAs), providing a "barcode" that tells enzymes like SCD1 and Leptin exactly what the light environment is.

The Frequency Shift: The color change from yellow 570-590nm to orange 590-620 nm is a literal frequency shift in the mitochondrial matrix. It changes the solubility and the "spin" of the Q-cycle, altering how electrons are handed to Complex III.

The Statin/CoQ10 Connection (The Final Trap)
This explains why Statins are so devastating to the eye and the brain.

Cutting the Tail: Statins inhibit the synthesis of the isoprene tail of CoQ10. rockefeller Medcine knew this and did it by design. It is a feature of statins not a bug.

Blind to the Sun: By depleting CoQ10, you aren't just lowering a "lipid"; you are removing the antenna that allows the mitochondria to sense the sun. You are making the cell "blind" to the seasonal light signals, forcing it into a permanent "atavistic" state of low energy and high mass.

CoQ10 is the "Master Tuner" of the mammalian nockchain. It is the molecule that "hears" the biophotons from the RPE and adjusts the mitochondrial "gears" to match.

Yellow State (Coherence): High solubility, efficient electron transfer, healthy SCN timing.

Orange State (Decoherence): Reduced solubility, radical-induced noise, circadian mismatch, and "Relativistic Mass" accumulation.

The Quantum Clinician's move: We don't just "supplement" CoQ10 (which might be in the wrong isomer). We restore the light environment (IR-A and UV-A) to "re-isomerize" the endogenous CoQ10 back into its healthy, "Yellow" functional state.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-15T04:18:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coq10-supplementation.28746/post-348631>

**My Decentralized Synthesis**
I bridge Lane’s work with a decentralized lens: Antioxidants in my system are superfluous as the research shows. There is very littel support in the literature to support the claims that antioxidants do what paid scientists say.

-   Sunlight/Grounding: Supply electrons, stabilize the IMM field, reduce ROS/RNS, and counter Warburg.
-   DDW: Insulates the IMM, preventing “electrocution” and heteroplasmy, reducing cancer risk = big for mRNA turbocancer patients.
-   GOE and Endosymbiosis: Reframe the Warburg effect as an evolutionary response to light/oxidative stress, with modern nnEMF mimicking ancient conditions forcing phenotype and metabolic changes.

Antioxidants in my system are superfluous as the research shows. There is very littel support in the literature to support the claims that antioxidants do what paid scientists say. The major antioxidant made in my framework is melatonin by photo-bio-electric signaling. The rest is markting bullshit.

1.  Antioxidants as Superfluous
    -   Claim: I argue that research shows little evidence supporting the efficacy of exogenous antioxidants (e.g., supplements) as claimed by mainstream science. This aligns with critiques in the literature where high-dose antioxidant supplementation (e.g., beta-carotene, vitamin E) has been linked to **_increased cancer risk_** or no benefit in certain studies (e.g., the Alpha-Tocopherol, Beta-Carotene Cancer Prevention Study, 1994).
    -   Implication: If antioxidants are ineffective or counterproductive, the body’s natural systems (e.g., melatonin via photo-bio-electric signaling) **should be prioritized over marketed solutions.**
2.  Melatonin as the Major Antioxidant
    -   Mechanism: I posit that melatonin, synthesized through photo-bio-electric signaling in mtDNA (e.g., via sunlight, melanopsin, and mitochondrial responses), serves as the primary endogenous antioxidant. Melatonin is known to neutralize ROS/RNS (e.g., •OH, ONOO⁻) directly and indirectly by upregulating antioxidant enzymes (e.g., superoxide dismutase, glutathione peroxidase) and supporting mitochondrial function.
    -   Connection to the Decentralized Framework: This ties into my sunlight/grounding model, where light exposure (AM solar EMF) and electron donation enhance melatonin production, countering nnEMF/ALAN-induced ROS/RNS and stabilizing the IMM’s 30 MV/m field (per Lane).
    -   Outcome: Increased melatonin reduces oxidative stress, inflammation, and the Warburg shift, potentially lowering disease risk (cancer, neurodegeneration).
3.  Photo-Bio-Electric Signaling
    -   Mechanism: Sunlight drives melanin to split water, releasing electrons (Herrera’s work), and Pollack’s EZ water generates a negative charge, both enhancing melatonin synthesis. This photo-bio-electric process, amplified by grounding, replaces the need for exogenous antioxidants by directly neutralizing radicals and maintaining redox balance.
    -   Connection to Warburg Redox Shift: This counters the nnEMF/ALAN-driven collapse of the IMM field, reducing pseudohypoxia and glycolysis dependence, aligning with my light-driven reinterpretation of the Warburg effect. All chronic disease peidemics are associated with this metabolism.
4.  CoEnQ10 cycle = The CoQ10 "Isotopic Switch"
    CoQ10’s structure, specifically the ten non-conjugated double bonds in its isoprene tail makes it a massive antenna for terrestrial sunlight.
    Photoisomerization: When sunlight (or artificial blue light) hits CoQ10, it triggers a rearrangement of the cis-trans positions.
    The Radical Signal: This process generates free radicals. In the "Manufactured Dynasty," radicals are always "bad." In the "Quantum Dynasty," these radicals are Information Carriers.
    They mediate the geometrical isomerization of monounsaturated fatty acids (MUFAs), providing a "barcode" that tells enzymes like SCD1 and Leptin exactly what the light environment is.
    The Frequency Shift: The color change from yellow 570-590nm to orange 590-620 nm is a literal frequency shift in the mitochondrial matrix. It changes the solubility and the "spin" of the Q-cycle, altering how electrons are handed to Complex III.

    The Statin/CoQ10 Connection (The Final Trap)
    This explains why Statins are so devastating to the eye and the brain.

    Cutting the Tail: Statins inhibit the synthesis of the isoprene tail of CoQ10. rockefeller Medcine knew this and did it by design. It is a feature of statins not a bug.

    Blind to the Sun: By depleting CoQ10, you aren't just lowering a "lipid"; you are removing the antenna that allows the mitochondria to sense the sun. You are making the cell "blind" to the seasonal light signals, forcing it into a permanent "atavistic" state of low energy and high mass.
    CoQ10 is the "Master Tuner" of the mammalian nockchain. It is the molecule that "hears" the biophotons from the RPE and adjusts the mitochondrial "gears" to match.
    Yellow State (Coherence): High solubility, efficient electron transfer, healthy SCN timing.
    Orange State (Decoherence): Reduced solubility, radical-induced noise, circadian mismatch, and "Relativistic Mass" accumulation.

    The Quantum Clinician's move: We don't just "supplement" CoQ10 (which might be in the wrong isomer). We restore the light environment (IR-A and UV-A) to "re-isomerize" the endogenous CoQ10 back into its healthy, "Yellow" functional state.

**The Diagnostic Shift:** We stop measuring "Blood Sugar" as a primary marker and start measuring **Pupillary Response** and **Skin Fluorescence** (as a proxy for CoQ10/Melanin redox).

### Anthony Smith — 2024-04-08T00:31:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anthony-smith-emf-posts-photos-for-family-friends.28747/post-333373>

Why we measure . . .
These two rooms were next to each other, why is the reading so much higher in one than the other . . . hidden wires in the floor, walls and ceiling . . . If you turned off the one circuit to the room, it would not fix the problem, if you don’t measure you don’t know.

Main bedroom in the same house. We set up two control zones at the request of the home owner. There were a total of 11 circuits turned off to reduce the reading from 2,401mV down to 13mV.

I haven't been able to see any RFs on airplane mode from my iPhone SE that are clearly distinguishable from background radiation (using Safe and Sound 2 meter), but definitely haven't tested in a Faraday space so thanks for sharing that. I already ramp down the blue light all day and lower the white point at night (and airplane while I sleep), but further reduced EMF plus reducing flicker would be a game changer.

I have definitely noticed I'm less interested in using my phone and computer over the last few months since reducing the blue light output.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-04-08T08:32:25+0300

For home use at EMFSafe we recommend: for electric fields the body voltage method is most accurate and useful (we are partial to our own BVK ). For RF we recommend the Safe & Sound Pro2 and for Mag fields the Alpha Labs UHS2, they are reliable and get the job done.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-04-14T19:35:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anthony-smith-emf-posts-photos-for-family-friends.28747/post-333581>

Walking or horseback - without a phone on your person
I have an older model car (no WiFi/bluetooth) and with my phone off the readings in the car, away from the city are quite low. Turn on the phone and the readings go from <10.00µW/m2 to 20,000µW/m2. If you are in ANY kind of transport with other people multiply this up and if there is WiFi provided (like on many flights these days) expect it to be even worse. ANY new car (ICE or EV) with all the RF built in is a problem, turn it off IF you can and keep your phone OFF.
Follow Jack’s protocols before and after you travel (always) and be as health as possible.

I’m in good health, I had been in El Salvador for 3 days, (lots of sun and beach time) then spent the night at Campo Bello on the side of a volcano. Light, Water, Magnetism . . . Thanks again Uncle Jack for all you do.

I have been looking at buying this meter recently as well, after purchasing a gigahertz solutions ME3030b for Electric and Magnetic fields which has confirmed many suspicions I had around devices etc, but it doesn't seem to flag up issues with the AC in my house (I am in Scotland so 50Hz) and so I'm wondering if It doesn't work as well as the meters you use for detecting these fields. It would appear that I am really significantly affected by nnEMF and whilst I have hard wired everything in my home and disabled bluetooth in the car and carry my phone in airplane and use it wired in the house, judging by these readings it looks like there is much more work to be done. ### Anthony Smith — 2024-06-08T18:38:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anthony-smith-emf-posts-photos-for-family-friends.28747/post-335578>

The Safe and Sound Pro 2 measures upto 8 Giga Hertz. 5G (5th Generation) is a technology NOT a frequency. Here is the US T-Mobile uses 600 Mhz (megahertz) for their 5G service, AT&T uses “C band” which is between 3 Ghz and 4 Ghz. Verizon has some high band 8GHz+ in a few down town areas and stadiums but I believe 90%+ of 5G here (US) is below 8 Giga Hertz. 20 GHz + (mm wave) is FAST but does not travel far from the tower and does not go through trees, let alone walls.

The biggest “bang for the buck” in nnEMF mitigation for most is avoiding blue light AND turning off the 50 hz (60Hz) AC at night. Obviously the RF and magnetic field should not be ignored.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-07T00:07:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anthony-smith-emf-posts-photos-for-family-friends.28747/post-336767>

AC electric fields - The fields from wires depends on the voltage, wire layout, weather etc.
Transmission lines carry high voltage electricity, typically at 345,000 volts
Sub-transmission lines - 69,000V
Local transmission lines - 13,800V
From the transformer:
Light industrial - 220V to 440V
Commercial - 220V
Residential - 120V - 220V

I’ve been under local lines and picked up nothing but have also picked up 50V+ within 20ft. I have also seen 5mG magnetic in a home 30ft from a local transmission line. In any given situation you DO have to measure to know. I’ve measure 250V+ in the middle of a street with buried wires. I normally get very little from the line into a home unless within a few feet.

All in home 120V wires put out an electric field 6ft - 8ft Unless in metal conduit, IF there is a magnetic field it is normally only within 2ft+/-. I have measured a residential on the ground transformer here in Oregon and had to be within 2ft to measure any field.

Electric fields radiate out 6ft to 8ft so never use (hold) a phone that is charging unless it is a grounded power cord. If the computer (desk top, tower) is grounded there should be no electrical transfer so touching the mouse should be ok. If you have everything grounded but are still “feeling it” when touching the mouse then something like a kitchen rubber glove would insulate you from direct contact.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-10T20:46:27+0300

It's basically made of coal, specifically fullerene's that probably attract EMF and suffocate the flow. I would be curious about your thoughts.

The EMFSafe Switch is something you install next to the breaker panel in your home. Not something you take on the road. They are manufactured in America. You need to measure your sleep area to find the offending circuit then order the right switch for your situation and have it installed by an electrician. We are 10x more sensitive to nnEMF at night. To protect yourself from in home electric fields at night turn off the circuits in and around your bedroom. To protect yourself from RF coming into your home at night get a canopy. Lots of info and videos here: [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

In regard to Shungite - it might block RF and electric fields as a type of shield but you would have to cover the whole phone for it to work. If shungite worked to block/absorb the signal, the phone would not work - science is science. The video I watched claimed it was reducing the RF but the meter was set on electric fields and putting your hand in the way would give the same results. If your phone works it is sending out a signal 360º to connect to the nearest tower. It will increase the signal/power until it has a good connection. When you block the antenna you make the phone send out a stronger signal. When talking on the phone you want a shield between you and the phone with the back open. The shield helps protect you and with the back open the phone stays at low power. Shield Your Body (SYB) has a good one: [Www.EMFSafeShop.com](http://Www.EMFSafeShop.com)

That's interesting. Understood. I have to think about my whole home situation anyway, but I have a few practical questions.

1) If my job dictates me to live in the suburb of Chicago, do you know whether being near the water / Lake Michigan is better than not?

2) Is being 2 blocks from the underground Metro a big problem? My house is 4-5 blocks away. I understand NOTHING is better, but if you compare underground 208V power line that connect between my house and the garage, and some overhead power line that also run near the house, generally how bad is it to have an above ground Train or underground Metro lines 2 blocks away that is certainly much further away than any power line I have right around me?

3) I would totally have my house tested, but I am not back until August, and I may sell the house in exchange to a smaller one. Can I generally say that still a house with a yard whose environment I can control is better than an Apartment or Condo? Anyway, is your service available in the Chicago area? Alternatively, I can buy the devices and I can learn how to test the values.

4) The magnetic field can only be bad if they are fluctuating through running currents. Static magnetic field, such as the Magnetico bed, is supposed to help. Just clarifying.

5) You refer to 5G as RF (Radio Frequency) which in my world refers to MHz to 1 GHz (literally from the Radio days) but perhaps now it encompasses MHz to several hundred GHz. Would it be correct to think that the higher the frequency the worse? 5G not only has 3-5 GHz, but it also sends bundles of several hundred GHz pulses, which is the same technology that the Military uses with what is known as Traveling Wavetube Amplifier (TWA). This is used as a crowd control weapon, is also referred to as an "active denial device" and is also the cause of the mysterious "microwave disease" that diplomats got when they traveled to Cuba or Russia. They got zapped by powerful active denial pulses and have their hearing destroyed, brain fog, tinnitus or literally TBI.

6) If I live in a 5G area (which pretty much is the case in the entire Chicago area, but also anywhere with lots of people) that is in the air, independent of our electricity then your EMF mitigation would not help? Yet, 5G pulses might yet be the biggest danger. It's less "measurable" because of the wavefront / pulsed nature, but it might still be more dangerous.

Literally if my cellphone still works at home, this means my 3G or 5G is working. How do your devices mitigate those effects? I can turn on Airplane mode, blue tooth, Wifi, but we are still bathed in the 3G network.

My logical thinking tells me that whenever we have cell phone reception, which is literally everywhere, including in Atami in Maria Theresa's house, this is not a nnEMF that you can switch off, right? Jack told me that you think 5G is not active unless we have a receiver. But the problem is that 5G never comes alone. It still has 2G and 3G as a backup?

Same with Metro, Train ... effects that you cannot turn off with a switch because you don't control those nnEMF?

7) What would be your suggestion for the best portable mitigation strategy, besides being out in sun etc. I am particularly interested in the best strategy to mitigate 5G and Wifi effects because we can literally not avoid them as long as we operate in civilization today, earn our money, have our jobs etc. Maybe put phone inside a Faraday pouch until we need them, take it out, use it, and put back in? My worry is that Wifi is still there, regardless of our devices. Sigh. It's not easy.

Rule #1 measure and you will KNOW the answer and be equipped and empowered to make informed decisions. I would not move to a place I had not measured.

My thoughts:
1- I’m guessing there is no nnEMF coming from the water SO the closer to the water that is 50%+/- of your circle with no nnEMF.

2- You live in Chicago, as Jack would most likely say “a shit hole” so whatever you do will be just making a really bad situation less bad but still bad. I don’t see being 2 blocks from a metro will affect you much either way. Avoid power lines.

3- A detached house gives you more control, an apartment or Condo you have other people with WiFi etc very close to you.

4- Correct

5- “RF” is used today for all radio/TV/cell, radar etc frequencies. 90%+ of 5G is sub-6GHz but there is some 20Ghz+ in Chicago. What you are subjected to depends entirely upon exactly where you live. It can be different a block away. With 5G it is the pulsed modulation as much as the specific frequency. 5G has the potential for the “active denial” frequencies but I doubt they are in general use.

6- The affects of all the different types of EMF are cumulative so any time you can reduce your exposure to ANY EMF the better off you are. That said night time mitigation, when we are 10x more sensitive to the effects of EMF, will have the greatest benefit. The only advantage of Chicago is all the building have metal clad wiring so the electric fields from the walls are normally lower there.
The EMFSafe cut off switch is to reduce the electric fields, magnetic fields and in home origination RF from the circuits in the home. For most people setting up a sleep sanctuary gives them a 70%+ reduction in daily nnEMF exposure. Outside originating RF require shielding and we recommend a bed canopy.

There is no 5G in El Salvador, not sure they even have 4G, I believe it is mostly 3G. In America, again it is my understanding, you only get a 5G signal if there is a 5G device to receive it BUT if the person next door to you has a 5G device you are getting zapped.

7- You are focused on RF, while this is important it is primarily addressed by WHERE you choose to live. The blue light, electric and magnetic fields from IN your home are a far greater nnEMF exposure and addressed by HOW you live. You can reduce what RF exposure you have from the outside with a bed canopy or other shielding. BUT 70%+/- of your daily nnEMF exposure Is from the inside . . . Turn it off. A phone in a Faraday pouch will power up to maximum power looking for a signal and drain your battery. Just turn it off when not in use.

You live in a mud pit and you are asking me how to get clean . . . Get out of the mud pit.

In order to build Noah's arc, most of us still need to make money. I have a duty and wish to spread the words to others. It's not good enough for me to rescue my own skin. I am healthy enough, but my people are not, and my students are not.

I grew up 10000 times more ideal than my kids. Virtually out all day, no TV, no Wifi, no nothing, in the suburbs. Every day, outside bicycling, rollerskating and playing with other kids until dinner time. Every weekend, out in nature and all family vacations spent in the mountains or water. My parents did not believe in sunscreen and they told me to get really tan as an insurance for the winter. I don't think they knew any of Jack's teaching, but it was their intuition. We ate only food from whole ingredients, which also back then meant only seasonal food. My mom did not believe in any food that comes in a package, she only used real butter, even though back then margarine was already broadly used. My mom made regularly liver and forced it on me for some reason. I thought that it was the most disgusting thing ever, but I had to eat it. People my age and older did not even get an email until halfway through College, and even that, I went to the library to read. I still went to the library and copied pages out of publications to read them during my PhD. My Master thesis was written with a typewriter, believe it or not, and I grew up in Germany! My PhD thesis was written with a computer, but the computers were hard wired. My building where I teach did not have Wifi until 7-8 years ago. I had my fortune to grow up very differently, and I probably owe my health to lifestyle, choices my parents made and the state of technology when I was younger. My heart aches thinking how all of this has changed for today's generation of kids.

I have a forum that I need to use. I teach Chemistry undergraduate and graduate students. I have tenure and am unfireable, have a hard salary (as opposed to relying on soft money, i.e. grants), and can use my position to spread the word. I can generate at least one course however I want to design it. So I will use that forum to teach younger students about the power of sunlight. I am still contemplating on the course title. Maybe "Solar Powered Physical Chemistry of Human Health".

Yes, I need to put my own oxygen mask on first (which I am doing) and need to first rescue my son (which I am also trying). My plan will have to adjust due to this, and my plan might take longer, but that's okay. I just wanted to explain why getting myself out of the mudpid is not the only priority for me. ### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-13T22:27:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anthony-smith-emf-posts-photos-for-family-friends.28747/post-337112>

Many things are the way they are today because of a modern day “horses’ ass” . . . But many are because of ancient horses’ asses” . . .

The US standard railroad gauge (distance between the rails) is 4 feet, 8.5 inches. That's an exceedingly odd number. Why was that gauge used?
Well, because that's the way they built them in England, and English engineers designed the first US railroads. Why did the English build them like that?
Because the first rail lines were built by the same people who built the wagon tramways, and that's the gauge they used. So, why did 'they' use that gauge then?
Because the people who built the tramways used the same jigs and tools that they had used for building wagons, which used that same wheel spacing. Why did the wagons have that particular odd wheel spacing?
Well, if they tried to use any other spacing, the wagon wheels would break more often on some of the old, long distance roads in England . You see, that's the spacing of the wheel ruts. So who built those old rutted roads?
Imperial Rome built the first long distance roads in Europe (including England ) for their legions. Those roads have been used ever since.
And what about the ruts in the roads?
Roman war chariots formed the initial ruts, which everyone else had to match or run the risk of destroying their wagon wheels. Since the chariots were made for Imperial Rome , they were all alike in the matter of wheel spacing. Therefore the United States standard railroad gauge of 4 feet, 8.5 inches is derived from the original specifications for an Imperial Roman war chariot. Bureaucracies live forever.
So the next time you are handed a specification/procedure/process and wonder 'What horse's ass came up with this?', you may be exactly right. Imperial Roman army chariots were made just wide enough to accommodate the rear ends of two war horses. (Two horses' asses.)
￼
Now, the twist to the story:
When you see a Space Shuttle sitting on its launch pad, there are two big booster rockets attached to the sides of the main fuel tank. These are solid rocket boosters, or SRBs. The SRBs are made by Thiokol at their factory in Utah . The engineers who designed the SRBs would have preferred to make them a bit fatter, but the SRBs had to be shipped by train from the factory to the launch site. The railroad line from the factory happens to run through a tunnel in the mountains, and the SRBs had to fit through that tunnel. The tunnel is slightly wider than the railroad track, and the railroad track, as you now know, is about as wide as two horses' behinds.
So, a major Space Shuttle design feature, of what is arguably the world's most advanced transportation system, was determined over two thousand years ago by the width of a horse's ass.
And you thought being a horse's ass wasn't important? Ancient horse's asses control almost everything.

> What would be an example source of nnEMF source that is not captured by your safe and sound RF meter (reading 200 MHz to 8 GHz), but captured by the Gaussmeter (13 Hz to 75 kHz), given that the bad source is not the DC magnetic field, but an AC field? Sounds like power lines?
>
> What about electric field fluctuations (which may be caused by magnetic field fluctuations and vice versa) between 75 kHz and 200 MHz that is not captured? Is this band not of concern because power lines / train / metro are in the Gaussmeter regime and the Wifi/Bluetooth/Cell is in the RF regime, butwe don't have much nnEMF polution in the 75 kHz to 200 MHz polution?
>
> A lab for electron microscopy is typically shielded with a Faraday cage. They put in copper or some other metal mesh all round the room, and then have to make sure that all the metal, the door / opening, floor and outlets are connected to the same ground. And then internet is fed in through ethernet. I supposed such an option is too expensive to do for living space, but if everyone was conscientious about nnEMF, in principle for any new construction, bedrooms could be shielded in this way to generate a sanctuary? I guess one difficulty would be doors and windows, but the doors could have a metal mesh and good seal, and same for window, the blinds or the insect mesh could be easily connected to the faraday cage. So even for a new construction home, it would be more of a DIY thing, unless we want to pay exorbitant money.
>
> What amazes me is that we figured out for sensitive measurements that any nnEMF is harmful. So we go to great length mitigating it, even if it costs a huge amount of money. But for humans, nah, nnEMF could not have an effect cause we are not as sophisticated of a machine compared to an electron microscope.
>
>
> If you want to buy a UVB/UVA lamp for humans, it's impossible to do so for a reasonable price. But you can easily get that in the reptile store for very little money. For reptiles we figured out that without UVB/UVA and red light that is alternated between day and night, they become ill and die or they simply won't move. But nah, that's for reptiles. Human's could not be as sensitive as reptiles are. Another source is Halloween stores: you can get blacklight.
>
> If you want to buy all-spectrum LED light for humans the occasions we need to be indoors, it's impossible if we want 300 nm to 900 nm covered. But it's pretty standard accessory for plant growers. Look for plant light in Amazon, and then it's totally possible. We figured out that tomatoes, basil, and all sorts of plants don't grow much indoors without the full solar spectrum. But nah, that's for plants. Humans could not be possibly as sophisticated as plants.
>
> This whole idea that we have our own solar panel, our own live batteries, are run by DC electric current, and if there is AC, run by a cascade of molecular clock is so logical, but so crazy that it did not enter our general consciousness, textbooks, scientific papers, .... nothing. The Rockefellers and the likes were pretty crazy successful in brainwashing the majority of humankind.

I hope you are having a good time in ES, I stayed at the same place and spent many hours on the same beach, enjoy.
Yes, talking will make more sense when you are back in the “pit”.
The Safe & Sound Pro 2 gives actual measurements Peak/Max & Average. as well as the warning lights. It does not give the frequency. I have a meter that gives frequency and it is almost always 2.4Ghz WiFi/phone.
The single largest source of magnetic fields in the home is the 60Hz magnetic field from the wires and appliances etc.
When you find the circuits that affect you in your bedroom and turn them off at night, you creat an EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary and “kill” 100% in home origination electric fields including “DE”, magnetic fields and RF from those circuits. Outside origination RF is a separate issue where a canopy is the simplest, most practical, most cost effective solution.
A faraday room keeps EMF out . . . and in. ANY nnEMF on the inside stays inside and bounces around. They can work IF one knows how to use them, they can also make things MUCH worse if you don’t.

To the rest of what you say . . . I agree . . . We use animals to find what hurts humans and how to treat that BUT at the same time we know what these animals need to be healthy BUT that does not apply to humans - weird! As always, follow the money. I have 850nm NIR (security) lights, a UV (party black light) light and a full spectrum aquarium light. Same frequencies as the “medical” “light therapy lights” at a fraction of the cost. I use these when I cannot be outside.

I look forward to visiting with you and helping with some “pit” management.

All nnEMF has a cumulative affect and for most people, along with blue light, the 60Hz (50Hz) AC field in their home (& office) is the largest source of daily nnEMF - and the easiest to mitigate. LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-31T22:24:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anthony-smith-emf-posts-photos-for-family-friends.28747/post-337891>

> Anthony would an adapter like this work to an external ground if you don't have that luxury of the grounded plug? Or does it cause more issues?

Yes you can add an external ground seeing as a 2 pin plug has no ground even when plugged in to an outlet that’s a ground. A 3 pin plug (with a ground) plugged into an outlet will only be grounded IF there is a ground in the wiring as part of that circuit.

Most of the outlets I tested in EL Salvador, even though they were 3 holes (the round one being for the ground) there was NO ground on the circuit. So in this case even if a cord has a ground pin, the device is not grounded if the circuit or the building has no ground . . . Then an external ground can be brought in.

Only way to know if there is a ground is to check with a circuit tester Like this.

> [pfedorow said:](/goto/post?id=337889)

What I have noticed with many clients over the years is that some (EMF mitigation) techniques help some people that are very sick get less sick BUT these same techniques do not help “less sick” get well. They can even make people who are basically “well” feel worse. Less nnEMF is alway better BUT the best way to go about it can change. What works for one may not work for another depending where you are on the journey.

For example . . . I find it interesting that the same people who tell us to get rid of metal bed frames and metal coils (sometimes to buy their $10,000 cotton and organic foam beds, which may be VERY good by themselves) are ok with metallic grounding sheets or pads which are then connected by wire to the house electrical system or a 50ft antenna. This may help very sick people get less sick but we have MANY reports of “well” people having bad results with in home grounding. Ground outside in Nature with your body in direct contact with the Earth.

I share this because there may be people here on the Forum that are grounding themselves inside and still having problems. Ground appliances NOT you. Grounding inside make you part of the circuit and the 60Hz (50hz) AC fields now have a path of least resistance - through YOU - to ground. At night TURN OFF the AC circuits in your bedroom, get up in the morning and watch the sun rise with your body directly in contact with the Earth . . . It is called “EARTHING” for a reason.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-25T02:31:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anthony-smith-emf-posts-photos-for-family-friends.28747/post-347259>

**\*\*\* Public service announcement \*\*\***
Taking body voltage readings correctly. Understand how the equipment works.
Your body contains water, it is conducive, it acts as an antenna attracting (absorbing) nnEMF.
With a body voltage meter we can measuring the amount of 60Hz (50Hz) AC electric field that is being induced into your body.
This is done by giving the electric field a path of least resistance to ground THOUGH THE METER, in so doing we can measure it.
IF however part of your body is touching the ground, SOME or ALL the field will go to ground not through the meter. When this happens the meter is giving you a false LOW reading.
If you touch a grounded device (ie fridge or grounding pad) the body voltage meter may drop to “0” ZERO - not because the field is no longer there affecting your body but because it is going to ground through the grounded device NOT the meter.
If you were in 2,000mV before you are still in 2,000mV but you are now part of the circuit and the 2,000mV is now going through to ground.

Being in a 60Hz (50Hz) AC electric field (and not grounded) is like being in a bath tub.
Being in a 60Hz (50Hz) AC electric field (and GROUNDED) is like being under a waterfall . . .

Taking a body voltage reading lying on the bed is ok . . . IF there are no grounding pads or sheets etc. BUT
if standing - you must wear insulated (non grounding) shoes and
if sitting - a non metallic chair with shoes on or with feet off the ground.

Have fun measuring. For more info: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse) (follow grounding and measure links).
