### aaronwebb — 2024-05-19T21:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-journal.28772/post-334905>

A little over a month later, here's an updated report and some challenges.

A couple of weeks after the last report, I was feeling really great. I was sleeping well, rising before sunrise eagerly, and continuing my daily routing mostly outside all day. Attached is a pic of the outside work area I have set up. My body voltage sitting here with my laptop is .09. I'm working on mounting an Ethernet outlet out here so I can plug in a long cable instead of the WiFi. My heart palpitations, tinnitus, and dizziness have greatly improved. This lasted a couple of weeks.

About 3 weeks ago I started feeling bad. It was a light sore throat along with very active sinuses and felt like I was fighting something. I handled it ok the first couple of days then the exhaustion started. I laid outside all day and slept. Side note: for most of my life, whenever I get sick with almost anything, it seems to take me a longer to recover than most people I know, a week minimum. After the customary week, I started to feel better like I wasn't actively sick anymore and was much more functional, however, the exhaustion and sinus activity still hasn't quit. It's hard to put words to. The exhaustion is nearly debilitating, my muscles feel weird and like they're being drained, there's kind of an anxious feeling, my appetite has gone way down, and brain fog has been terrible. There's been a few days I nearly slept the whole day.

I've tried to maintain my routine. I've missed maybe a quarter of the sunrises since then. I try to get up for sunrise, get some food in me, then sometimes I'll go back to bed. I've been spending time outside as usual but it's been quite stormy the past week. I've been eating Epi Paleo but it's a real challenge to get as much volume. This morning I'm optimistic. I woke at sunrise, got outside, ate a little, then slept 3 more hours. I'm outside writing this and it's sunny day the the exhaustion isn't as bad today. I'm still sleeping well at night.

I've found through experimentation that charcoal, zeolite, magnesium seems to help a lot with the muscle, heart, and anxious feelings. It's been suggested that my body is healing and needs a lot of extra support right now. I don't really know where else to go with it other than just trying to stick to the routine, experimenting, and hope it resolves.

### aaronwebb — 2024-06-28T17:48:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aarons-journal.28772/post-336239>

As I've been increasing my time in the sun outside the shade, work has been increasingly impacted as I can't see my screen. Box to the rescue  Looking forward to a Daylight Computer laptop someday. Body voltage floats around 7mV here. Black Cooltan shorts.

# Thread: Zone 2 cardio
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zone-2-cardio.28775/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cicero106 — 2024-04-09T11:25:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zone-2-cardio.28775/post-333442>

Hello guys, NEW member here. I know this has probably already been discussed, but if someone found the time and patience to enlighten me, I would be very grateful.
Growing up the only people I heard not recommending cardio were bodybuilders, because it ruins your gains. One year into carnivore I heard ”meatheads” like Bart Kay and Sean O’Mara explain how bad of a tool cardio is for fat loss (not why I want to do it) as well as improving cardiovascular fitness , and that in general it should be avoided because it raises colesterol and causes visceral fat accumulation.
Then I read how low intensity endurance training (for instance Zone 2 cardio) is beneficial for mitochondrial biogenesis, angiogenesis, myogenesis and neurogenesis and I thought to myself “Great, I discovered that I like jogging in nature at a comfortable pace that would allow me to conduct a conversation AND in addition it is great for my mitochondria, vessels etc.” I naively thought: “Dr. Kruse would for sure approve this!”
But as I was reading the Leptin Rx, I saw that he did not recommend it, also for people that were able to gain their LS back. Then reading the forum I found this post and that Dr. Kruse agreed with it: { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Now, I ask myself: “Does this refer to high-intensity cardio for multiple hours, like someone would do trying to win an iron-man or marathon, or to any form of prolonged training? And is it false that low-int. training is great for mitochondrial biogenesis or do the ”side-effects” just outweigh the “benefits”?”

Again, I know it might be redundant for the veterans, but if someone with more experience on the forum or also more experience about this topic in general is willing to clarify and chat, it would be very appreciated.

PS: I also thought about our ancestors or the native americans and how I always assumed, that there were days where they would jog for hours hunting down a prey and that we are “endurance hunters” similar to wolves and dogs, thus designed to jog long distances.

> Growing up the only people I heard not recommending cardio were bodybuilders, because it ruins your gains. One year into carnivore I heard ”meatheads” like Bart Kay and Sean O’Mara explain how bad of a tool cardio is for fat loss (not why I want to do it) as well as improving cardiovascular fitness , and that in general it should be avoided because it raises colesterol and causes visceral fat accumulation.
> Then I read how low intensity endurance training (for instance Zone 2 cardio) is beneficial for mitochondrial biogenesis, angiogenesis, myogenesis and neurogenesis and I thought to myself “Great, I discovered that I like jogging in nature at a comfortable pace that would allow me to conduct a conversation AND in addition it is great for my mitochondria, vessels etc.” I naively thought: “Dr. Kruse would for sure approve this!”
> But as I was reading the Leptin Rx, I saw that he did not recommend it, also for people that were able to gain their LS back. Then reading the forum I found this post and that Dr. Kruse agreed with it:[View attachment 27686](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/27686/)
>
> Now, I ask myself: “Does this refer to high-intensity cardio for multiple hours, like someone would do trying to win an iron-man or marathon, or to any form of prolonged training? And is it false that low-int. training is great for mitochondrial biogenesis or do the ”side-effects” just outweigh the “benefits”?”
>
> Again, I know it might be redundant for the veterans, but if someone with more experience on the forum or also more experience about this topic in general is willing to clarify and chat, it would be very appreciated.
>
>
>
> PS: I also thought about our ancestors or the native americans and how I always assumed, that there were days where they would jog for hours hunting down a prey and that we are “endurance hunters” similar to wolves and dogs, thus designed to jog long distances.

I am not convinced humans ran long distances to hunt.

I think this might be more of a mainstream argument that was created to support the endurance running industry.

I think it is more likely that humans used intellectual tactics and sprinted short distances to hunt.

Endurance training can have consequences unless you have an optimal redox state and excellent mitochondrial health.

When you read the blogs, you might agree.

Endurance running can be satisfying emotionally, but I think there are healthier ways to exercise.

I have converted to mostly walking with occasional sprinting intervals after my heart rate returns to baseline.

### eve001 — 2024-08-10T13:35:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zone-2-cardio.28775/post-338251>

Running makes an awful lot of noise. Even walking makes lots of noise. You can't hunt like that. Wild animals hear you and run away. So sneaking and crawling very silently and slowly - to me - seem much more appropiate for hunting, whereas running seems - to me - more propper to fleeing!  Many sports have something martial about them - which is a thing quite different from hunting to feed your stomach. Try to run or even walk silently in wild nature! It's hard.

### JanSz — 2024-04-09T16:58:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-thread-outing-the-nonsense-of-a-centralized-scientist-on-cold-thermogenesis.28777/post-333446>

My mom lived with us her last 25 years.
Died 95 yo, end of March 2005 (flu season).
Looks like she had flu or cold.
But otherwise had she survived that flu, she might have been still living.
Overloaded with fluids.
So, I think she died of ARDS.
On her own legs she got to car, and I took her to ER.
She died in Emergency Room some hours latter.

She would ALWAYS do right away anything that I ever asked her for.
**Are you saying that she should have taken cold shower?
She had a personal shower next to her room. I build it just for her.**

Now I can do a whole 5min shower in cold from start to finish and feel great. Build up your tolerance slowly. I was also getting in my pool during the winter about 3 times a week for 5min at a time. It was at 55degrees. I tried to get closer to 10min a few times at started to shiver, and thats when I know it's time to get out. The high dopamine levels provide such positive feeling and energy level that lasts for hours.
From what I learned from Jacks protocol, 55 degrees is the magic number and any lower doesn't necessarily provide extra benefits.

# Thread: Help with CIRS/Actinomycetes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-cirs-actinomycetes.28779/>

### TaylorF — 2024-04-10T22:26:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-cirs-actinomycetes.28779/post-333490>

Hello everyone,

I'm looking for some advice.

I am still struggling with my CIRS/Mold journey, and most recently, my apartment tested extremely positive for actinomycetes. The DO I see has a protocol for ridding the apartment of Actinos, so I am willing to put in the effort. However, I am still struggling with brain fog, histamine issues, muscle and joint pain, chronic EBV/viruses, POTS, panic/nervousness/agoraphobia, and I have an allergy to shellfish.

I would say if I started this illness at a 1/10, I am now at a 6.5/10. So, I've made huge improvements in many areas with functional and integrative medicine support. However, I have about 100 lbs to lose and I am really struggling mentally with that fact. I weigh 250 and I need to be around 150 for my height and muscle mass. Most recently, I got to the point mentally where i decided to try Ozempic, and ended up in the hospital with severe stomach pain and nausea -- I never wanted to take it, I've listened to Jack talk about it, but it was almost a last hope weight-wise.

Most recently I was in a fairly remote part of Mexico and I didn't feel any relief. The sun and swimming felt truly amazing, but anytime I went inside or ate any food I was symptomatic. I also experienced low blood pressure and blood sugar whenever I was outside for a touch too long, even with shade.

I'm feeling really overwhelmed and looking for any and all advice you can all give me to get from my 6.5/10 to a true 10/10 - great health like I used to have.

Thanks in advance,
Taylor

### Anthony Smith — 2024-04-10T23:24:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-cirs-actinomycetes.28779/post-333493>

Mold reacts to nnEMF by growing faster so one thing to do is minimize the nnEMF in your home. Turn off WiFi and keep as many of the electrical circuits off as much as you can, especially at night. My wife lost 75+lb with intermittent fasting, she does not watch what she eats but only has one meal a day . . . we are all different but it worked for her.

### JanSz — 2024-04-11T00:14:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-cirs-actinomycetes.28779/post-333496>

get one or two dehumidifiers.

### TaylorF — 2024-04-11T20:54:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-cirs-actinomycetes.28779/post-333522>

I think there is a place for all types of medicine when healing from a 9 year chronic illness, even if integrative medicine is 10% and being a black swan mitochondriac is 90%, like Jack said in the Maria Menounos podcast about acute medicine. A lot of sun, avoiding nnEMF, and healthy RO or mountain valley water has gotten me to a great point from a 0 out of 10 to a 6.5ish, along with mold binders, and following a biotixin protocol. I know light, water, and magnestism in at the 20th latitude will get me to a 10/10, but i currently have $500 in my savings account, and only $500 in bitcoin. Working is essential right now, and I need to ride out my lease, so I'm trying to work with what I have on Long Island. I'm working on a Ramsey Solutions protocol for saving, and then when I get to step 7 (skipping buying a house) I plan to purchase bitcoin with the 3 month of expenses savings.

I just purchased _Prescriptions for A Healthy House by Paula Baker-Laporte and John Banta (4th edition)_ which was recommended to me by my CIRS provider. Working on the actinos will bring my TGF-Beta down which he said is still high. TGF-B is associated with a lot psychological symptoms, I also have a fatty liver with some fibrosis which they say can also be from TGF-B, so I'm hoping that working on Actinos will bring some relief as it seems to be highly associated with Actinos toxicity.

Long Island have lots of places where ground water is very high.
Everywhere it is high, but if you live in any of the red places,
you should have a more than one humidity gauge, and make sure those gauges newer show more than about 30% humidity. Get enough de-humidifiers. How many of them you need, consult those gauges.

Many "good" homes are built over plastic sheets to prevent water incoming to the basements or first floors.
After years of use, when that plastic breaks, the owner of that place get surprise.

[USGS Scientific Investigations Map 3326: Water-Table and Potentiometric-Surface Altitudes in the Upper Glacial, Magothy, and Lloyd Aquifers beneath Long Island, New York, April-May 2010](https://pubs.usgs.gov/sim/3326/)

The USGS, in cooperation with State and local agencies, systematically collects groundwater data at varying measurement frequencies to monitor hydrologic conditions on Long Island, New York. Each year during April and May, the USGS conducts a synoptic survey of water levels to define the spatial...

 eros.usgs.gov

# Thread: AGING AND LONGEVITY = DAMP hypothesis has replaced the ROS hypothesis.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aging-and-longevity-damp-hypothesis-has-replaced-the-ros-hypothesis.28780/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-04-11T05:40:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aging-and-longevity-damp-hypothesis-has-replaced-the-ros-hypothesis.28780/post-333503>

Lessons in Longevity: Aging isn’t driven by ROS. Many now think it is driven by the DAMP Program. I believe sunlight controls all the DAMP molecules

Aging and cancer share some common origins and hallmarks, such as genomic instability. Sunlight exposure creates genomic stability to quiet epigenetic programming and lower protein turnover in cells.

Recent advances indicate that damage-associated molecular pattern molecules (DAMPs) such as high mobility group box 1, histones, S100, and heat shock proteins play location-dependent roles inside and outside the cell.

These provide interaction platforms at molecular levels linked to common hallmarks of ageing and cancer. They can act as inducers, sensors, and mediators of stress through individual plasma membrane receptors, intracellular recognition receptors (e.g., advanced glycosylation end product-specific receptors, AIM2-like receptors, RIG-I-like receptors, and NOD1-like receptors, and toll-like receptors), or following endocytic uptake. Thus, the DAMP Hypothesis is novel and complements other theories that explain the features of ageing. DAMPs represent ideal biomarkers of ageing and provide an attractive target for interventions in ageing and age-associated diseases. DAMP phenotype = a loss of energy at the electronic level in the cell.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-04-17T16:46:16+0300

**“The late-evening snack session resulted in less lipids oxidized than in the breakfast session,” said Kelly. “This confirms that the timing of meals during the daytime and night time cycle affects how ingested food is used versus stored, and that any food ingested prior to bedtime will delay the burning of fat during sleep.”

''the daily timing of nutrient availability coupled with the body’s increased metabolism during sleep (thanks to the body’s circadian rhythms) flipped a switch on fat burning''**

### [Fasting at night or in the morning? Listen to your biological clock, says new research](https://news.vanderbilt.edu/2020/02/27/fasting-at-night-or-in-the-morning-listen-to-your-biological-clock-says-new-research/)

According to a new study published by biological science researchers at Vanderbilt, the answer to eating (or fasting) windows lies in the circadian rhythms of the body’s biological clock.

 news.vanderbilt.edu

# Thread: Hello from the 36° N Latitude
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-36%C2%B0-n-latitude.28786/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KellyMonroe — 2024-04-14T05:11:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-36%C2%B0-n-latitude.28786/post-333570>

Howdy! I’m from Hollister, California—we live in a farming community with about 37,000 people spread out across a large area. I’m 57 years young.

I’m here to trade in my insomniac card for a mitochondriac card. I haven’t slept all night in 15 years, it really sucks. Centralized medicine only offered me pills—I “politely” declined this so-called-lution.

I tripped across Uncle Jack’s body of work and knew he knows a lot of shit that’s super important to my sleep—and sooo much more. I found this info during a nightly learning time (aka during the middle of the night while awake—yeah yeah I know the blue light is a major player in my problem, but I didn’t know that then).

For last 3 weeks, I’ve dutifully witnessed the sunrise—even standing in the rain this morning I’m glad the farm workers weren’t working this morning…they’d have had quite the chuckle at this site.

Cutting iPhone/iPad/giant-ass tv time substantially has been an unexpected win—I feel calmer and very focused. Who knew?

I bought red lights for our bedroom—keeps the neighbors wondering and it’s so relaxing.

Something quite amazing is happening—I’m actually tired and sleepy at 7:30 pm! I fall asleep by 8:30 pm every single night. Definitely no complaints here.

Staying asleep is an issue—but I have faith that if I keep at it, there’s a solution. I’m awake for 1-2 hours nightly but going to sleep early means I feel better with 7.5 hours of totally sleep.

Oh, one more thing, I’m not crazy carb anymore—that’s crazy good in itself!

Another plus, my brain feels about 15 years younger and I’m not searching for words in every conversation. Here’s to more progress as I implement each step. ❤️

### pfedorow — 2024-04-14T17:07:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-36%C2%B0-n-latitude.28786/post-333577>

Welcome,

You will find out studying Jack's information that California is not ideal but if you cannot move consider to measure your environment always for non native emf with an expert like Anthony Smith but . Wish you well on your journey

### KellyMonroe — 2024-04-14T18:06:25+0300

I understand that Cali-wacky isn’t ideal—and I have family ties here (my dad is 86 and thriving—despite blue light all day long must have some weird adaptation that I didn’t get) and I’m responsible if/when he needs care.

Today we live in a rural low population (central coastal region) so it’s bad but not as bad as it could be in large cities.

It’s not forever but it is for now! As I delve deeper I may consult with Anthony—one thing at a time. Sun first.

### JanSz — 2024-04-16T17:23:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ray-peat-ideology-more-centralized-bullshit.28790/post-333624>

Body temperature and pulse rates are critical indicators of metabolic health, reflecting your body's energy production capabilities and overall functionality.
Low body temperature and pulse are indicative of being in “survival mode” rather than thriving.
A thriving metabolism is characterized by higher body temperatures, indicating efficient energy production, which supports a variety of health benefits including better digestion, sleep, and energy levels.

### Glenn A — 2024-04-16T18:47:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ray-peat-ideology-more-centralized-bullshit.28790/post-333625>

> [magnetic said:](/goto/post?id=333613)
>
> Just wondering if anyone could answer why the ray peat followers insist that ketosis or low carb is stressful and that it always raises cortisol and adrenaline to high levels leading to detrimental effects on the body and health. Why do they look unhealthy themselves and people here seem to heal and thrive instead. Do those cultists have any proof for their claims or are they just desperate to cling onto something else just to get by because they either havent heard of jacks teachings or they just didnt do keto the right way(not eating enough protein or adequate nutrients in general). They make many absurd claims regarding health and nutrition. What are your thoughts?

If your first impression is that Peatarians are cultists, go with your gut impression and look elsewhere to learn about health. That's my take after listening to Peat, visiting his forum and commenting probably over 200 times there between 2014 and 2016. You can waste time there, especially trying to reason with the cultists, trying to guide them in almost any other direction. My theory for why he gained such a following is that they are essentially sugar addicts that looked for a savior from what was becoming a keto-worshiping alternative health population. Regardless, no, they never learn. Amazingly, even now, Dr. Mercola has become a "convert" to Peatism in the last year after he had already written 2 books embracing ketogenic diets, and then learned that keto as a long=term diet is actually not that healthy. He now features guest speakers like Georgi Dinkov, Jay Feldman, and Ashley Armstrong, all earlier Peat followers, to push the "Bioenergetic Theory of Health" of these people. For them, maximizing ATP production via a Carbohydrate emphasized diet is the way to gaining optimum energy levels. However, they are ignoring the Circadian Rhythm, the Randle Cycle, and mitochondrial uncoupling which actually provides, (via just Time Restrictive Feeding, TRF), much better health and better energy production because even though carbohydrates truly are needed in higher levels than obtained from a "Keto Diet", you don't actually want [to keep](https://iubmb.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/iub.1627) the body from a cyclical journey into ketosis almost daily, because ketones that come in with the fatty acids after 10-18 hours of fasting are what "uncouple" mitochondria from burning glucose, and let normal multiplication of mitochondria take place, along with removal of the aged ones (mitophagy). ketones are a signaling agent. Without them, yes, the muscles and other tissues will become unnecessarily aged, and unable to take advantage of the glorious glucose that can produce more ATP.

There's a bit of a primer on Mitochondrial Uncoupling here: [https://gundrymd.com/mitochondrial-uncoupling/](https://gundrymd.com/mitochondrial-uncoupling/)

Uncoupling and Longevity: [https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0531556519306722](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0531556519306722)

Benefits of just TRF (Intermittent Fasting): [https://orthomolecular.activehosted...6cc179.322&s=bdc7d8441d9f00d50264c3b740d31448](https://orthomolecular.activehosted.com/index.php?action=social&chash=950a4152c2b4aa3ad78bdd6b366cc179.322&s=bdc7d8441d9f00d50264c3b740d31448)

How TRF affects glucose levels (somewhat regardless of diet macronutrients):
[https://www.nutrisense.io/blog/intermittent-fasting-glucose-levels](https://www.nutrisense.io/blog/intermittent-fasting-glucose-levels)

Studies show that ketone bodies mimic the lifespan extension properties of caloric restriction:
[https://iubmb.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/iub.1627](https://iubmb.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/iub.1627)

Or here is an interview with Dr. Stephen Gundry introducing his book, _Unlocking the Keto Code_

### Glenn A — 2024-04-16T19:23:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ray-peat-ideology-more-centralized-bullshit.28790/post-333626>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=333624)
>
> Body temperature and pulse rates are critical indicators of metabolic health, reflecting your body's energy production capabilities and overall functionality.
> Low body temperature and pulse are indicative of being in “survival mode” rather than thriving.
> A thriving metabolism is characterized by higher body temperatures, indicating efficient energy production, which supports a variety of health benefits including better digestion, sleep, and energy levels.

I'm not even quite 90% convinced of this. There's a few asterisks I'd place on these statements at least.

First, remember these "norms" are averages across entire populations. We shouldn't be trying match (or beat) these numbers, say, for instance body temperature. It takes only a moment to realize that sickness, or even chronic conditions, such as obesity, can raise core temperature. Yet studies that determine "average" are including significant quantities of obese and otherwise sickly people. Do we want to attempt to "match" the average temperature of a population that is to some degree sick, inflamed or infected? Or all three? Notice there are charts that show the difference in core temperatures of obese women/men compared to non-obese. There's a difference of almost 1/2 degree C between the lower, male non-obese temperature and that for an obese female. This was the study, so other factors such as inflammation and age were not included:

[https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1038/oby.2011.380](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1038/oby.2011.380)

Secondly, there is just a strong link between hypothyroidism and body temperature (and also pulse rate) and "hypothyroidism" is often assumed to be a marker of poor health, even when at a subclinical level, because at that level there still may be a lowering of bodily temperature. However, there are also studies that show that hypothyroidism, and also the attendant lower core temperatures are correlated positively with longevity. So studies like this give pause to those who take pains to "cure" are hypothyroid state, or for other reasons to attempt to raise their measured temperature:

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5710266/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5710266/)

So there can be up to a 3.5 year extension to lifespan if you happen to "suffer" from hypothyroidism? (Only 3.2 year average though if you have a cardiovascular condition). This is definitely worth considering if you 1) have lower than "normal" temperature; 2) also have a subclinical hypothyroid condition that may be the sole cause of that sub-normal temperature. Personally, I'm quite content to have symptoms of hypothyroidism such as loss of body hair, and a body temperature that stays in the 96.0-97.0 range, but no apparent loss of energy. To me, this seems like a possible blessing. But I certainly have my prejudice, based on readings about the significance of bodily temperature from many years ago. I'm fairly big on prejudice as a way to avoid extra expense on science, but I'm also big on acknowledging the science when it refutes my prejudice.

> hypothyroidism and body temperature (and also pulse rate) and "hypothyroidism" is often assumed to be a marker of poor health,

Within last about 150 years or so, more accurately since invention of highly accurate grain mills and seed oil presses people are consuming highly altered foods.
Consumption of seed oils which are mostly PUFA and especially Linoleic Acid (O-6) raised LA content (many folds) in the Earth population.
For that, I have seen suggestions that hypothyroidism and lower body temperatures are not really health problems but body's expectations that hibernation time is coming.

.

### Glenn A — 2024-04-16T23:54:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ray-peat-ideology-more-centralized-bullshit.28790/post-333631>

Interesting observation. I don't doubt that PUFA could be a cause, or one cause of hypothyroidism or Hashi's. It may be rather hard to prove it isn't, or to find people with the symptoms, but who are NOT quite polluted with PUFA in storage if not in circulation. I have been aware of, and shunning excess PUFA for over a decade, but if I were high in that toxin before, and it's likely, It may have brought on a hypothyroid condition that I can not shake, regardless of a far better current diet. It will take more study of pristine-diet primitive peoples to see if "low" thyroid occurs naturally on a more primitive diet, and if also manifests with lower body temperature.

I just did some research and cannot find studies of populations that tend to tie hypothyroidism to hardly anything still except iodine/selenium deficiency. I think the science here is lacking in what you are proposing. It would be nice to see some studies linking PUFA to the onset of thyroid issues. Please post if you have anything on this. Possibly the Ray Peat Forum has covered this. Let's check.

I am 84 yo.
So, to me it is interesting what supercentenarians do to live that long.
I think most of them are rather poor, not much money.
They eat what they could afford if it grew in their neighborhood or on the other side of the world.
One interesting information I came across is comparing animal food (meats) people eat.
Average American eats 220 pounds a year. (expensive)
Average super centenarian or centenarian 20 pounds a year.
Those 20 pounds comes to about a weight one thick slice of bacon daily.
Knowing that any meat raised by farmers, "normal" or organic or "wild" is raised on seed oils or the seeds from which most oil was taken out, that meat (animals, birds, fish, "wild") is full of PUFA (or LA O-6).
So, doing that, centenarians must be lower on LA than average population.

The other item is my own personal experience with potassium.
Everybody and their brothers are having GERD.
I had that problem, but no more, after I started seriously supplementing with potassium.

We should not really eat any supplements.
We should eat (correct) food.
Guess why so many peoples are low on potassium.
Because they do not eat carbohydrates.
So, those centenarians, if they practically do not eat many meats, they must be eating carbs.
Not all carbs raise glucose and insulin.

Way back then, before precise mills were invented,
even breads were lover on glycemic index, since they were made from whole grains.
But legumes are also carbs.

How does physics explain K+ ions and light and water? Brownian motion = statistical motions of atoms Einstein realized this the demon Maxwell was talking about in 1867. it took 38 yrs for someone to make sense of it. Do you know, of Einstein's 4 miracle papers of 1905 that the one on Brownian motion is the one most cited? Yet he won nothing for it.

Maxwell was the first to show that the second law of thermodynamics had to be statistical. Einstein showed Brownian motion was the observation of the statistical event predicted by Maxwell. In the 1950's science really upped this game when transistors and semiconductors were found. Why? any trap door that opens in one direction only and requires a specific amount of energy to open it is essentially a Maxwell Demon.

This gave birth to solid state devices we use today. Rectifiers let current pass in one direction and not in reverse, thereby converting AC currents to DC ones. In the 1960's Becker showed bone had a rectifying current built by light in periosteum. The implication is that these semiconducting gates can randomly convert a fluctuating current of electrons in a membrane into a DC current that can be used to physiologic work. The electrical potential of the cell membranes stores energy of light in the electrons it captures photoelectrically. This is done by Coulomb's law. Membranes are designed to capture electric flux and captures magnetic light flux. This, in turn, excites the electron and it becomes a particle only and loses its wave ability. This is part of the photoectric effect. So an excited electron in a semiconductor can only act as a particle. A delocalized electron has more range to act differently, as a wave and/or a particle.

This idea leads us to one conclusion: the electrical potential difference between two sides of a membrane can be harnessed and used to perform cellular work when ever it is needed. Why can energy be harvested in life anytime it wants it? The photoelectric effect is instantaneous, therefore time is no longer an issue. Photons never experience time, unless they are catured by a semiconductor. Even then, they are moving fast just not at the speed of light. The speed of light in a tissue is the rate limiting factor for a cell.

A calculation shows that the electron is traveling at about 2,200 kilometers per second. That's less than 1% of the speed of light, but it's fast enough to get it around the Earth in just over 18 seconds. In silicon (Si) the electron mobility is of the order of 1,000, in germanium around 4,000, and in gallium arsenide up to 10,000 cm2/(V⋅s). Hole mobilities are generally lower and range from around 100 cm2/(V⋅s) in gallium arsenide, to 450 in silicon, and 2,000 in germanium.

Inside your wall copper slows electrons down big time. In the case of a 12 gauge copper wire carrying 10 amperes of current (typical of home wiring), the individual electrons only move about 0.02 cm per sec or about 0.5 inches per minute (in science this is called the drift velocity of the electrons.)

What else is key in this understanding? You begin to see why all eukaryotic membranes are loaded with DHA that have 22 carbons with a huge amount of "electron holes". These holes allow for electrons to delocalizes rapidly. They contain pi electron clouds that are waiting to be excited by incident light from the sun to store this energy for the cells use later on. This explains why DHA has never been replaced one time in 600 million years of eukaryotic evolution, even though evolution is about change over time. Why is it a thermodynamic constant for eukaryotic life? It liberates electrons rapidly to move light energy at rapid speeds. In fact, it is the only lipid capable of turning sunlight into a DC electric current and a DC electric current back to light. What does the high DC electric charge in the inner mitochondrial membrane release when this charge is stored at the electric and vibrational level in a the mitochondrial of a cell? Water. Water than is devoid of deuterium. Water that is designed to operate like a semiconductor. What is its connector to water? Potassium ions are that answer. K+ ions act like glue does in hydrated molecules. K+ is s special when it is married to water and light from the sun hits it. It raises the Coulomb force in youre semiconductor factory called a mitochondria.

### Glenn A — 2024-04-17T05:09:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ray-peat-ideology-more-centralized-bullshit.28790/post-333664>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=333641)
>
> I do not like using names. Either Ray Peat or anybody else.
> That creates distraction, someone will always come with the list of wrong things he did.
> \------
> I am 84 yo.
> So, to me it is interesting what supercentenarians do to live that long.....
>
> Knowing that any meat raised by farmers, "normal" or organic or "wild" is raised on seed oils or the seeds from which most oil was taken out, that meat (animals, birds, fish, "wild") is full of PUFA (or LA O-6).
> So, doing that, centenarians must be lower on LA than average population.....
> ...

Thanks, JanSz.
I use Ray Peat by name because he's a well known, confusing, quantity who hardly ever provides links to the supposed science he preaches. If we provided links, I could sure use them or contradict them with other links. But since he is just spouting thing, I just use his name so people know it's a Peatarian system. For example, as promised, I went to the Peat Forum and searched for "hyperthyroidism + PUFA" and it's mentioned hundreds of times by his followers. But no links. It's just follower-echos. That's my point. It's a religion with repetition but no proof nor even scientific substantiation.
I'm glad to hear you are such a healthy looking, cogent example of humanity at age 84. And I'm totally with you on looking to those who live in supercentenarian societies for examples of how to do the same.

I am 81 and have set no limit on how long I should live. About 8 years ago I studied deeply the different levels of PUFA (more precisely the O6:O3 ratios) in different meats, including comparisons of wild game with free-range beef to CAFO beef and also comparisons of free-range chicken to CAFO chicken. I think you are right that there are high levels of O6 (LA) in beef that is not kept on pasture until slaughter. And even on pasture, beef still ends with a O6/O3 ratio a bit higher than wild grazing venison or bison. But as you say, reducing the percent of the diet dedicated to meat **of any kind** is the real key to health when it comes to longevity. Yet this study will show that _some_ meat adds a bit to longevity. Vegetarians should heed this if they care about longevity. And this study didn't even qualify the meat to be only organic, free-range or anything like that:

[Total Meat Intake is Associated with Life Expectancy: A Cross-Sectional Data Analysis of 175 Contemporary Populations](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8881926/)

I only happened on that study because I was in a "vegetarian/vegan vs carnivore" debate on what might be an optimum diet, and it turned out that this study helped me understand that "a bit of meat" in the diet is better than none.

As to your last statement that I quoted above,

> So, doing that, centenarians must be lower on LA than average population

I think your statement is obviously true, but it might also be stated as: "centenarians must be lower on LA than _almost anyone_ in average populations except those of us who have learned to reduce levels of LA from all the ugly commercial sources, including junk foods, factory farmed meat, eggs, and fish, all bottled seed oils and all restaurant fried foods and salad dressings." Putting that "except" in there attempts to put probably you and I and many other health conscious readers here closer to being equal to the Blue Zone residents in keeping our LA at healthy levels. All we might need to do as you seem to say, to equal their low LA intake would be to reduce the actual _pounds_ of meat we consume annually quite substantially. No? I'm still not convinced that we need to go that low in our meat intake, but yes, to some degree, less could be better. It's probably impossible to have a study on this as the cohorts will always outlive the scientists conducting the trial.

# Thread: The model of how redox chemistry occurs has changed.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-model-of-how-redox-chemistry-occurs-has-changed.28797/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-04-17T22:01:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-model-of-how-redox-chemistry-occurs-has-changed.28797/post-333730>

What becomes of oxygen we breathe?

Oxygen plays a role in a variety of cellular processes, including sterol and fatty acid synthesis, and is critical for oxidative phosphorylation (8). Thus, it is not surprising that changes in oxygen availability can have drastic effects on the function of a cell. Several studies indicate that low-oxygen conditions can induce apoptosis (76, 86). This occurs when oxygen levels decrease to at, or below, 0.5% (anoxia). When oxygen levels are 0.5–3% (hypoxia), cells do not undergo apoptosis. Instead, hypoxia activates a variety of cellular events that can ultimately lead to cell survival depending on the light associated with the hypoxia signal.

The reduction of oxygen to water by mitochondrial respiration may represent the integration of two essential functions for aerobic life: coupling to oxidative phosphorylation for bioenergetic benefits and elimination of the essential ROS substrate for maintaining genomic stability. This may surprise you.

Low oxygen in mammals triggers signal-transduction pathways involved in both cell death and survival. For example, anoxia activates proapoptotic BCL-2 proteins and caspases to initiate apoptosis.
The adaptive cellular events that occur in response to hypoxia are mediated largely by the transcription factor hypoxia-inducible factor-1 (HIF-1).

HIF-1 induces the expression of multiple antiapoptotic BCL-2 proteins to promote cell survival. Interestingly hypoxia increases production of mitochondrial reactive oxygen species (ROS), which serve as signaling molecules to activate HIF-1. This link shows you ROS is not always a bad situation inside a mitochondria. Inside mitochondria, oxygen role has changed because of how experiments have shown us how redox chemistry is really run. It is now seen to give rise to copious amounts of "diffusible reactive oxygen species" known as DRS/DROS.

Implications?

In the field of enzymology, murburn is a term coined by Kelath Manoj that explains the catalytic mechanism of certain redox-active proteins. The term describes the equilibrium among molecules, unbound ions and radicals inside a cell simultaneously.

This process signifies a process of "mild unrestricted redox catalysis".
Murburn is abstracted from "mured burning" (connoting a "closed burning", an oxidative process), and implies equilibriums involving diffusible reactive oxygen species (DRS/DROS/ROS) created inside of mitochondria. Though akin to the oxygen assisted combustion of fuel, unlike the flames produced in the open burning process, the biological reaction occurs in enclosed premises, is mild and generates heat alone without flames. The heat is used by water and by melanin. Heat increases melanin's ability to be an electrical conductor in mammals.

Exogenous dark skin melanin uses this and endogenous melanin makes use of this in uncoupled haplotypes. Such a reaction can also incur selective and specific electron/moiety transfers quantum mechanically that are not acounted for in biochemistry books.
Further, though burning is a reaction that usually involves oxygen (aerobic process), "burning flames" produced by anoxic oxidants are also well-known. Therefore, the enzymes working via murburn scheme (aerobic or anaerobic) can now be called murzymes and the region around the biomolecule where the DRS interacts with the final ‘substrate’ is called ‘murzone’. Melanin and water are key chemicals allowing murzones to operate.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/root-canal-needed-or-zirconia-implant-or-not.28798/post-333759>

During March Q&A I asked Dr Jack about a recommended root canal by my holistic dentist. He suggested to post the xrays on this forum for feedback. I have no pain or swelling or discomfort associated with the tooth in question. The 2022 xray shows the 2nd from the right molar with no issues. In 2023 I had to have the rear molar removed as it had cracked and could not be repaired. I then had a cantilever bridge put on the adjacent molar and it has worked will. The tooth holding the cantilever bridge is the one supposedly needing a root canal. Does the 2024 xray show any sign of a dead tooth that would cause a need for a root canal or implant? # Thread: Hagen's Optimal Journal: A Marathon confronted me with the truth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hagens-optimal-journal-a-marathon-confronted-me-with-the-truth.28799/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mostsaleablegood — 2024-04-18T23:18:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hagens-optimal-journal-a-marathon-confronted-me-with-the-truth.28799/post-333770>

My optimal health journal

Hey there,

I am Hagen and I am 21 years old. I live in Germany/Cologne. I have big dreams and hopes for my life. I want to have the most meaningful life I can possibly have and get the most out of the time that is given to me.

It would be a verdict to myself to not get to optimal health.

What I want to improve:

-   I want to become more energy efficient by becoming fat adapted and improving my metabolic function. (I sometimes deal with lack of energy during the day)
-   Along with the above I still want to run a marathon to see what my body is capable of
-   I want to build up self-confidence and overcome my fear of speaking in front of groups.
-   Since at least 6 years I have Blepharitis and dry eyes and I am hoping to fix it
-   I want to help my family member getting back to optimal health and indepence

Here is what I have integrated from what I learned from Dr. Jack Kruse: (started 23.03.24)

\- morning sunlight after waking and grounding

\- maximize sun exposure, trying to spend at minimum 90 minutes outside each day

\- stopped training in the gym under blue light with a cellular antenna on the roof.
Instead, I started outdoor training around 3pm 2-3 times a week, doing calisthenics and balance training.

\- focusing on consistent bedtimes and sleeping times (going to bed at 22:30 and rising at 6:30)
\- bought blue blocking glasses, a infrared light bulb
\- started avoiding blue light after dark, only using candles and infrared light bulb.

I am applying the leptin rx and eating according to the epi paleo RX.

I switched from tap water to Gerolsteiner water

How did I come here?

I am quite ambitious in sports with the goal of running a Marathon. That has been a journey for me starting in July last year. The preparation for the marathon made me realize problems of my body and health I wasn’t aware before. At first I got medial tibial stress syndrome, because of my bad running form and weak foot muscles. It stopped me from training. I had already payed the 125 € marathon fees, so I still participated in the Marathon. The marathon ended for me quite painfully at kilometer 30. My glycogen stores were depleted. Unfortunately, I wasn’t fat adapted at that point.

It took the failure as a lesson and committed myself to fix problems and to become optimally prepared for the next marathon.

At that time i also had a close family member who got a neolithic disease and fell in the hands of centralized medicine. It made me realize that you need to take responsibility for yourself to avoid becoming a slave of the healthcare system. I think those two things let me here.

I bought barefoot shoes and started to walk in them daily which gave me muscle soreness after short walks in the beginning. I also started doing daily foot strengthening exercises and fixed my shin pain.

I felt ready to train for the next marathon and applied for one 3 months later willing to run barefoot. I started to do 80% Zone 2 20% Zone 5 training increasing the running volume steadily. I also committed to nutrition and started eating low carb and adding ghee and coconut oil and bone broths to my diet. My training went really well with 5 hours Zone 2 training a week 30 HIT training. I also did two heavy lifting sessions a week in the gym.

All went well until i got I got achilles tendonitis in week 12 out of 15 of the marathon training. I fixed it with punctual CT and certain rehab exercises after a few days.

A week later I got slight food poisoning from mal eating in a restaurant. It was followed with a flue. I realized that I was really overtrained. I decided to delay the marathon.
Right at that time I found Dr. Jack Kruse by listening to the "Once bitten" podcast with Daniel Prince. He confronted me with the truth, when he said buying Bitcoin is not enough. Your time is your scarcest asset and you need to maximize it. (Wealth and health are fundamentally linked).

I was already committed to health before, but that’s when it clicked in me to aim for the optimum.

That’s where I am so far after reading the Epi-Paleo-Rx and applying the things mentioned above.

I’ll keep posting updates on my journey here!

Hagen

“Life is no "brief candle" for me. It is a sort of splendid torch which I have got hold of for the moment, and I want to make it burn as brightly as possible before handing it on to future generations.” Georg Bernard Show

I can understand that.

If you complete the marathon now and have leg pain when walking when you are 50 years old, will the mental accomplishment have been worth it, or will you regret it? I think that is the most important question that I ask distance runners. With optimal training, a lot of people may not have future pain from distance running, but unfortunately, I think it affects some people significantly, even with the best training.

There are also mitochondrial heteroplasmy and redox implications that I would recommend researching.

Also, I would not be exercising under artificial light.

Update:

Thanks coho3131 and Jansz for your answers and advice.

I researched mitochondrial heteroplasmy and learned about mitochondrial functions.

It decided to complete my endurance ambitions.

For that I ran a half marathon yesterday near my home with my friends and family supporting me.

Now i am looking ahead to fully optimize my health. I want to focus on improving my redox potential.

Last week I visited a natural movement training in my town. It really helped me with improving my handstand and I am planning to visit it weekly. The next morning, I did CT in a lake with the same guys from the workshop. It’s amazing to see what influence I can have on other people just by setting standards for my own health and life.

I am committed to learn everyday to improve my health and understand more. I also maximize the time i am outside.

Habits I am implementing currently:

-   Training outside HIIT Calistenics in my garden 3 times a week
-   I started working and studying outside, if that’s not possible I am inside with an open window
-   Grounding during sunrise and a couple of times during the day
-   started eating fatty fish every morning
-   Swimming or CT in a lake nearby once a week (on the other days i take a cold shower after seeing the sunrise)

Next week I am going to do lab work to find my n=1:

I start biohacking with:

\- Vitamin D

\- HS- CRP

\- DHEA.

I am thankful to have found Jack Kruse and also to read all the enlightening health stories.
Hagen

My blood work turned out to be:

\- HS-CRP: 0,07 mg/dl

\- Vitamin D3: 37 ng/ml

I don’t really know how I can improve my DHEA. I’ve learned that it’s a proxy for a person’s environment and redox potential. I think I’ll retest it in 2 months, while maintaining the Rx’s and see how and if it changes.

I’ve been at the coast of the Netherlands for 3 days. We spent almost the whole time at the beach or sailing. ### mostsaleablegood — 2024-06-02T21:48:25+0300

Heyy there,

A looot is happening lately... Last week I've been at a local bitcoin conference which went one week in Cologne. The feeling there was incredible. There is collective positive attitude towards a bright decentralised future, which stands completely in contrast to the fiat mindset. I' ve met so many nice openhearted people. And I earned my first good money by selling Crepes value for value for Satoshis. I want to save that money for my future kids or pension. I made a video about it to look back in the next 4 million blocks of my life and see how the purchasing power has increased.

In the last two weeks I've orangepilled almost my whole close family and a lot of friends. And I talked to many new people by handing out flyers for the bitcoin conference.

A lot of future plans and dreams come up again or begin to form, which i've supressed the months and years before. Bitcoin gives me the opportunity for self sovereignty and nature helps me to maximize it. Really excited

Most bitcoiners at the conference don't understand health or value their time yet. Nobody knew about Jack Kruse or Blue Light. There is huge potential to fill the gap of unawareness there.

I am absolutely excited about what is going to happen in the next few weeks to months. I'll be at the Prag conference and am really excited to listen to Jack live. I also hope to see some of you guys

I am studying Mitochondria right know and listen to Jacks podcasts daily.

Aiming up

Hagen

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-18T01:28:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hagens-optimal-journal-a-marathon-confronted-me-with-the-truth.28799/post-339679>

It my understanding the cel towers in ES are all 3G that there is no 4G and definitely no 5G (Yet).
Don’t know the difference on the body between 2.4GHz and 5GHz (microwave ovens use 2.4GHz).
Schumann is 7.83Hz BIG difference from any RF but 60Hz AC fields are much closer.

I believe I remember Jack saying be outside even on cloudy days, the reflected light is still beneficial. Even though you cannot see the sun for 15 mins it is still light and that light is of the frequency you need.

You want to feel really energized plan a few days up in the Lake Coatepeque area.

### Kirsten F — 2024-09-18T04:48:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hagens-optimal-journal-a-marathon-confronted-me-with-the-truth.28799/post-339682>

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-18T17:51:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hagens-optimal-journal-a-marathon-confronted-me-with-the-truth.28799/post-339698>

> [Kirsten F said:](/goto/post?id=339682)
>
> Anthony, Claro in ES is advertising 4.5 G, though I don't know what that actually means.

### Rusty — 2024-09-18T18:38:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hagens-optimal-journal-a-marathon-confronted-me-with-the-truth.28799/post-339700>

> [Kirsten F said:](/goto/post?id=339682)
>
> Anthony, Claro in ES is advertising 4.5 G, though I don't know what that actually means.

4.5G uses carrier aggregation to combine multiple frequency bands (unlike 4G, which uses single frequency band for data transmission)

Also, it uses advanced MIMO configurations. 4G uses 2x2 MIMO (two antennas for transmitting and receiving data)

Basically 4.5G = more wireless bandwidth & speeds = worse for our biology in every way

### Rusty — 2024-09-18T18:48:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hagens-optimal-journal-a-marathon-confronted-me-with-the-truth.28799/post-339701>

> [mostsaleablegood said:](/goto/post?id=339670)
>
> I am also wondering if 5GHz WiFi is affecting you more than the 2.4GHz WiFi? I remember that Robert o becker wrote in his book the body electric that lower frequencies closer to the Schuman resonance might affect you more than higher ones. Technology advanced. Does this still apply? Has anyone sources to research it?

Research on the effects of 5 GHz frequencies is less extensive than that for 2.4 GHz. However, both are bad without a doubt.

I believe becker was specifically talking about ELF-EMF (The AC EMF from the Power grid) when he said lower frequencies closer to the Schuman resonance might affect you more than higher ones.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-09-18T19:52:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hagens-optimal-journal-a-marathon-confronted-me-with-the-truth.28799/post-339703>

When in ES earlier this year I did notice what I thought was an excessive number of cell towers. We avoided the far side (right side looking out to sea) of the beach in Atami because of the tower up on the hill.

Does anyone have any idea what the future plan in ES is for 4G/5G etc?
A map with cell towers on it would be helpful for EHS people to decide were to stay.

### mostsaleablegood — 2024-11-11T00:00:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hagens-optimal-journal-a-marathon-confronted-me-with-the-truth.28799/post-342176>

Hello everyone,

I am two months in El Salvador now and am currently staying on a Permaculture close to Chalatenango. I met lots Mitos and dedicated time to learn more about Health.

The first few weeks I was in El Zonte. Over there I haven't felt good. Deep sleep was lacking and I was in not a good state of being, besides maximizing time in the sun and being grounded on the beach. I wonder if it is caused by nnEmfs in El Zonte.

Here on the permaculture I am also close to a cell tower.
I sleep in a darkened room with no electricity or WiFi.
I feel better, but am still not in an optimal state now.

It's really hard to find a spot with low cell tower radiation, because cell towers are almost everywhere. In the future we are also seeing way more radiation with Starlink etc.

So I am thinking about ways to block nnemfs at night.
\-How much does using a grounded copper cable connected to my body help?
How much do nnEMF blocking blankets help?
Are there good sources of information about how to avoid cell tower radiation at night?

Thanks for your help

Hagen

# Thread: Cholesterol and UV Light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-and-uv-light.28802/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### robtherhino — 2024-04-19T22:09:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-and-uv-light.28802/post-333800>

I'm speaking with a friend of mine who is a doctor.
I shared that UV light regulated blood cholesterol.
He has asked for sources.
I can explain the mechanism from listening and reading the work of JK (cholesterol sulfates when hit with UV light and becomes water soluable).
I've also heard Carrie Bennett share a lot about how UV light hitting the skin dialates blood vessels, has them rise towards the surface, etc.
I just don't have any sources to point at for him to get educated on it.
Looking for help.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-04-19T23:09:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-and-uv-light.28802/post-333802>

### [Post-exposure persistence of nitric oxide upregulation in skin cells irradiated by UV-A - Scientific Reports](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-022-13399-4)

Evidence suggests that exposure to UV-A radiation can liberate nitric oxide from skin cells eliciting vasodilation in-vivo. However, the duration of nitric oxide release in skin cells after UV exposure is not well studied, with emphasis on UV-B mediated iNOS upregulation. The current study...

 www.nature.com

### coho3131 — 2024-04-20T02:38:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-and-uv-light.28802/post-333808>

> [robtherhino said:](/goto/post?id=333800)
>
> I'm speaking with a friend of mine who is a doctor.
> I shared that UV light regulated blood cholesterol.
> He has asked for sources.
> I can explain the mechanism from listening and reading the work of JK (cholesterol sulfates when hit with UV light and becomes water soluable).
> I've also heard Carrie Bennett share a lot about how UV light hitting the skin dialates blood vessels, has them rise towards the surface, etc.
> I just don't have any sources to point at for him to get educated on it.
> Looking for help.

The paper below might be a good start. Dr. Kruse has written on these topics extensively also.

This doctor will need to be able to look beyond the information he was served in medical school in order to grasp the important concepts of Nature that you are trying to teach him.

I wish you luck, but I want you to know that most of us here have wasted too much of our time trying to teach people that were not ready to learn.

### coho3131 — 2024-04-21T08:09:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-and-uv-light.28802/post-333833>

> [robtherhino said:](/goto/post?id=333800)
>
> I'm speaking with a friend of mine who is a doctor.
> I shared that UV light regulated blood cholesterol.
> He has asked for sources.
> I can explain the mechanism from listening and reading the work of JK (cholesterol sulfates when hit with UV light and becomes water soluable).
> I've also heard Carrie Bennett share a lot about how UV light hitting the skin dialates blood vessels, has them rise towards the surface, etc.
> I just don't have any sources to point at for him to get educated on it.
> Looking for help.

### [Sunlight and Vitamin D: A global perspective for health - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24494042/)

Vitamin D is the sunshine vitamin that has been produced on this earth for more than 500 million years. During exposure to sunlight 7-dehydrocholesterol in the skin absorbs UV B radiation and is converted to previtamin D3 which in turn isomerizes into vitamin D3. Previtamin D3 and vitamin D3...

 pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### Jack Kruse — 2024-04-21T15:46:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-and-uv-light.28802/post-333835>

My Twtter feed from last night is littered with this answer to another MD who asked the same question on 4/20/24.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-and-uv-light.28802/post-333908>

# Thread: Question today on my April 2024 Q & A:  How is it possible the human brain can operate at 20 volts?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-today-on-my-april-2024-q-a-how-is-it-possible-the-human-brain-can-operate-at-20-volts.28808/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-04-22T04:56:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-today-on-my-april-2024-q-a-how-is-it-possible-the-human-brain-can-operate-at-20-volts.28808/post-333851>

**Question today on my April 2024 Q & A: How is it possible the human brain can operate at 20 volts?**

ANSWER: Sunlight and water join in unison to create a new form of plasma that is liquid crystalline. This type of water plays the central role in creating and maintaining the coherence of organisms. It allows optimal transformation of energies from the sun to your colony of mitochondria. Liquid crystalline water is the primordial quantum molecular machine built by Mother Nature that transforms solar energy at close to 100% efficiency for cells. Liquid crystalline water allows for delocalization of electrons and the hydrogen bond in water networks in cells. It appears that ferroptosis controls the electrostatic layer in cells that controls regeneration and wound healing but water is where the quantum mechanical actions occur that optimize thermodynamics of living.

Remember that the human brain is surrounded by water. That water is called CSF = cerebral spinal fluid.

The human brain is Nature's most successful quantum computer that runs on 20 volts of power. That one fact should astound you. In liquid water an excited proton can be found simultaneously – delocalized - at the O-H bond distance from either of two neighboring oxygen atoms This implies that the proton can belonging to two different water molecules simulataneously. This delocalization process increases the probability of proton transfer in living systems. This mimics what happens in photosynthesis. The energy of excitation to the delocalized state is less than 20 % of the O-H bond energy. These results show that liquid water has definite quantum properties, and has the ability to be quantum coherent.

The energy transactions in tissues are thermodynamically dense compared to silcon powered technology gear. Energy density in bone is a million to a billion times as dense as the powerplant in your cell phone. As a result, the thermodynamic ledge in organisms has to be a lot more efficient; this is why cells use AMO physics to make this possible. The photolithography used in cells has to be atomically precise to approach this zero-entropy quantum coherent state.

### JanSz — 2024-04-22T15:19:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-today-on-my-april-2024-q-a-how-is-it-possible-the-human-brain-can-operate-at-20-volts.28808/post-333856>

>
> ANSWER: Sunlight and water join in unison to create a new form of plasma that is liquid crystalline. Liquid crystalline water is the primordial quantum molecular machine built by Mother Nature that transforms solar energy at close to 100% efficiency for cells. It appears that ferroptosis controls the electrostatic layer in cells that controls regeneration and wound healing but water is where the quantum mechanical actions occur that optimize thermodynamics of living.
>
> Remember that the human brain is surrounded by water. That water is called CSF = cerebral spinal fluid.
>
> The human brain is Nature's most successful quantum computer that runs on 20 volts of power. That one fact should astound you. In liquid water an excited proton can be found simultaneously – delocalized - at the O-H bond distance from either of two neighboring oxygen atoms This implies that the proton can belonging to two different water molecules simulataneously. This delocalization process increases the probability of proton transfer in living systems. This mimics what happens in photosynthesis. The energy of excitation to the delocalized state is less than 20 % of the O-H bond energy. These results show that liquid water has definite quantum properties, and has the ability to be quantum coherent.
>
> The energy transactions in tissues are thermodynamically dense compared to silcon powered technology gear. Energy density in bone is a million to a billion times as dense as the powerplant in your cell phone. As a result, the thermodynamic ledge in organisms has to be a lot more efficient; this is why cells use AMO physics to make this possible. The photolithography used in cells has to be atomically precise to approach this zero-entropy quantum coherent state.

> **These results show that liquid water has definite quantum properties, and has the ability to be quantum coherent.**

ADH level tells body how much water it is holding in.

Is it better to keep more water in the body (higher ADH)
or better to keep less water (dehydration) (lower ADH)?

How that affects sense of thirst (in healthy body)?

/////////

I remember dr Boros complaining that many of his patients had ADH=~1
So, their bodies were mostly dehydrated?

Drinking, forcing drinking excessive amounts of water, eventually dehydrates body?
In an effort to increase their ADH (and body hydration) he was suggesting drinking much less water.

Would not it be better to just add (supplement) minute amounts of ADH? (That would likely also reduce thirst)?

# Thread: ARE VACCINES CONSTITUTIONAL IN THE USA?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-vaccines-constitutional-in-the-usa.28810/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-04-23T03:19:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-vaccines-constitutional-in-the-usa.28810/post-333873>

Mandatory vaccination is constitutional in the USA. It's something that has been constitutional in our country since the Jacobson v. Massachusetts decision and even before our own constitution dating back to 1738 we've had the upholding of quarantine and other measures to protect the public's health. The legality of compulsory vaccination is not a matter that is in question. 1905 the Supreme Court held that compulsory vaccination laws are constitutional when they are necessary for public health and for public safety. The case was Jacobson v. Massachusetts, a case taught in first year of constitutional law classes. In the years since then the court has affirmed the constitutionality of state compulsory vaccination laws in cases like Zucht v. King which upheld childhood vaccination requirement for entrance to public schools. In fact compulsory vaccination laws have existed in the US in some form since the 19th century.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-light-behind-why-diabetics-get-peripheral-neuropathy-and-nerve-pain.28811/post-333879>

IS LIGHT BEHIND WHY DIABETICS GET PERIPHERAL NEUROPATHY, PAD, AND NERVE PAIN?

Everyone should know by now blue light and nnEMF raise cortisol and raise blood glucose and insulin levels.

It is well know that blue intense light increase cortisol levels, stimulates wakefulness shutting down melatonin production - [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28790405](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/28790405)

Blue light and nnEMF, on a probability basis, is the main etiology for peripheral artery disease (PAD). The reason glucose spikes from light create endothelial dysfunction is that artifical light is ubiquitous day and night now. Because the stimulus is on is destroys the vitamin C levels in cells. As Vitamin C drops so does NADPH from the PPP. Blood glucose rises because it can no longer be oxidized by metabolism or stored as glycogen when humans live under the chronic light stimulus. Due to the chronicity of this process dopamine levels drop because it uses Vitamin C as a cofactor in its creation. As a result the body then degrades melanin to create dopamine because of the artifical light stimulus. The major antioxidant of the cell, glutathione also drops because NADPH is needed to recycle it via the PPP as well. This is where glucose become dangerous. When it cannot be used and it rises your body will use the PPP to convert it to sugar alcohol and that compound causes peripheral neuropathy. I talked about this 15 years ago in the PPP blog in the EMF series.

The main effect of this build up of sorbitol is a decrease in a nutrient called myo- inositol. Myo-inositol is responsible for normal nerve conduction to take place. Inositol is a B vitamin compound. The sorbitol causes build up of free radicals, namely peroxides, hydroxyl, and nitric oxide free radicals. People forget the NOx receptor or the NO cofactor BH4 can be oxidized to cause neuropathic pain and and raise your BP. These are all commonly seen in diabetics. In diabetics or in injured nerves, sorbitol concentrates free radicals to steal electrons from the surrounding tissue to cause dielectric collapse to make a positive feedback loop and worsen nerve function or pain as time goes on. This in turn depletes nerve cells of taurine and worsens mitochondrial redox. (Hansen 2000) Studies done by Terada et al., in 1998 suggested that there was a close relationship between the unregulated polyol pathway and the depletion of carnitine in the mitochondria that leads to neuropathy. Neuropathy is a mitochondrial redox disease. Red light exposure actually help peripheral neuropathy by increasing ATP but because it lowers blood glucose to decrease sorbitol accumulation. Many supplement advocates suggest diabetics use a “carnitine like” analog called R alpha lipoic acid. I do not find it help in those with destoryed melanin. It is thought to balance this depletion and it is still used as a treatment of diabetic peripheral neuropathy today by some functional medicine folks. Living indoor behind glass is a major risk factor for PAD and peripheral neuropathy because of how glass effects the spectrum of sunlight. Red light has an abscopal effect on nerves afflicted by polyol peripheral neuropathy. Few know this. The slide below shows you why.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-13T20:44:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-light-behind-why-diabetics-get-peripheral-neuropathy-and-nerve-pain.28811/post-358162>

**HEALING PERIPHERAL NEUROPATHY?**

HOW?

**When we subtract the sun we subtract the possibility of healing.

UVA-mTOR Interplay and Spectra:** UVA absorption via OPN5/SIRT1 boosts NAD+, activating AMPK to inhibit mTOR, favoring photo repair like mitochondrial biogenesis. Outputs include IR-A emissions (600–1000 nm) from ATP/AMP ratios and clock genes, promoting coherent UPE for DNA repair. Melanin (from tyrosine/phenylalanine) protects DNA by ROS quenching, while melatonin (95% mitochondrial, from tryptophan) switches metabolism seasonally. Interconnections involve quantum mechanisms like electron transfer. Sunscreen, sunglasses, nnEMF, bluelight, and shitty cities/states/countries block all of UV light = THOSE WHO STAY SICK WITH ANY DISEASE [https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-66-135709978](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-66-135709978)

Heme protein renovation is fundamentally tied to AM sunlight, with research confirming that **Ultraviolet A (UVA)** radiation in the **320–380 nm** range is a primary driver of this process. As highlighted in the slide above, this interaction is a critical component of a "photorepair" system that resyncs cellular metabolism.

**The Role of 380 nm UVA in Heme Renovation

Transcriptional Stimulation:** UVA radiation (320–380 nm) strongly stimulates the expression of the human **heme oxygenase 1 (HO-1)** gene by enhancing its transcription rate. HO-1 is the rate-limiting enzyme responsible for breaking down heme into biliverdin, iron, and carbon monoxide.

**Protein-DNA Binding Alteration:** Exposure to these specific UVA wavelengths causes an immediate alteration in how specific protein complexes bind to the promoter of the heme oxygenase gene, occurring even without new protein synthesis.

**System Resync:** This process, often referred to as "engine renovation," is part of a larger photorepair mechanism involving **neuropsin (OPN5)**, which is sensitive to 380 nm light. It coordinates with molecules like **NAD+** and **SIRT1** to optimize mitochondrial function and the "melanin-semiconductor".

**Melatonin and Photoprotection**
While UVA drives the renovation of heme, it also induces oxidative stress. **Melatonin, created inside the engine,** acts as a crucial "buffer" in this system:

**Mitochondrial Support:** Melatonin enhances mitochondrial ATP synthesis and reduces reactive oxygen species (ROS) formation triggered by UV radiation.

**Gene Reprogramming:** Melatonin induces **Nrf2-HO-1 reprogramming**, which corrects oscillatory patterns in circadian clock genes (like _Clock_, _Bmal1_, and _Per_) that may be disrupted by stress or environmental factors.

**Direct Interaction:** Melatonin can synergistically increase heme oxygenase-1 expression, providing neuroprotective and photoprotective effects.
In this decentralized framework, **sunrise** serves as the initial trigger, providing the necessary 380 nm UVA "instruction" to renovate heme proteins and resync the body's internal clocks before higher-energy UV rays arrive later in the day.

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-04-23T22:35:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-in-gainesville-fl-need-resources.28812/post-333898>

I just recently moved to the outskirts of Gainesville FL to optimize sunlight and though my current location doesn't have 5G, every day I see another 5G tower being installed along the main roads of Gainesville.

We need to fight this, so I am going to try and bring awareness to the community with a focus on keeping kids safe (lots of families here). Maybe it can extend further if we create a great professional website (which I can do as a designer and programmer) with a strong emphasis on real threats and avoiding any murky information.

If anyone has any excellent resources for helping people to quickly understand the 5G threat please share them here. I am building my own list but I want to be sure not to miss anything extremely significant that will quickly catch the community's attention.
