### Kendall Toerner — 2024-04-25T21:55:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-in-gainesville-fl-need-resources.28812/post-333971>

Well here's my first attempt that I'm posting in several facebook groups and on Nextdoor:

Hi everyone! I'm an engineer and wanted to share something dangerous in our community that affects us all, especially our kids. Hoping we can all take action to avoid severe issues.

As a former Facebook/Oculus VR engineer, I am extremely worried about the speed at which 5G towers are being installed around Gainesville. I have extensive experience in the tech world and also a deep knowledge of human biology (biophysics), so I'm uniquely educated to discuss the threat 5G poses on our community. I am not someone who backs anything without proof or scientific reasoning so please ask me any questions if you don't know much about these topics.

I've counted 9 towers for 5G along just the roads I travel on around Gainesville. This is what they look like: [https://www.gainesvilletimes.com/news/hall-prepares-new-technology-wireless-ordinance/](https://www.gainesvilletimes.com/news/hall-prepares-new-technology-wireless-ordinance/)

5G is dangerous because of how it affects human/animal cells, in particular their mitochondria. It dehydrates our mitochondria, by preventing them from using oxygen. In case you didn't realize, almost all diseases stem from a lack of oxygen (also called Hypoxia). This also reduced ATP as the output of mitochondria, so your cells can't do their jobs properly or make energy well.

Do you feel tired often during the day? Has it been getting worse? Have you gained weight? These are all signs of a loss of energy due to lack of oxygen to our mitochondria. 5G is especially dangerous to kids because their brains are still developing, and are not fully myelinated yet. It can cause severe problems as you can see in studies I've linked below.

Although other non-ionizing radiation in the microwave and mmwave range is also dangerous long term in high concentrations, 5G is much closer to military radar (20-40 gigahertz). Man-made light like 5G has never existed on our planet, nonetheless been beam-formed at us from 50 feet away. No human has been tested around this. It is a massive experiment and it has consequences on our health.

This man-made light changes how our genes are expressed through changes in protein folding as well, since this important process is governed by charges (electrons) being added or removed to proteins (the main signaling molecules for all aspects of our biology). There are many other consequences to changing our light environment like this and it will be a long time before studies can even be paid for (the cellular companies have zero vested interests in doing this for obvious reasons). I do not want to be a part of this testing.

Let's please start a lively discussion here and maybe we can get a movement going. Happy to help answer any questions or confusion around the topic.

More information and studies:

### [We Have No Reason to Believe 5G Is Safe](https://www.scientificamerican.com/blog/observations/we-have-no-reason-to-believe-5g-is-safe/)

The technology is coming, but contrary to what some people say, there could be health risks

 www.scientificamerican.com

### Saichi — 2024-04-26T01:34:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-in-gainesville-fl-need-resources.28812/post-333987>

> [Kendall Toerner said:](/goto/post?id=333972)
>
> Anyone know if there is a better 5G meter than this one at a somewhat approachable cost? [https://safelivingtechnologies.com/products/safe-and-sound-pro-mmwave-meter.html](https://safelivingtechnologies.com/products/safe-and-sound-pro-mmwave-meter.html)

Unless you want to spend $25k+ on a mmWave meter, then SLT's is the only "approachable" cost.

The greatest exposure anyone not living across an mmWave mast needs to be concerned about are car collision radar systems. When you're driving around town and stopped at red light the car in front of you is beaming up to 1000 µw/m^2 of mmWaves into you. Best to stop at least 15 feet away from it.

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-04-26T23:40:12+0300

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=333985)

### Saichi — 2024-04-26T23:43:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-in-gainesville-fl-need-resources.28812/post-334008>

> [Kendall Toerner said:](/goto/post?id=334007)
>
> I figured such short towers would be the high band frequencies, interesting

The cylinders are usually modulated mid-band which is terrible for your health. Lot of people who didn't get symptoms from regular 4G mid-band are having a tough time with modulated advanced 4G and 5G mid-band.

### petrichor — 2024-04-27T00:21:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-in-gainesville-fl-need-resources.28812/post-334009>

You did a great job here already, but I wanted to add one more. I really like Environmental Health Trust, they do a great job of highlighting these issues without coming off as murky.

### [Environmental Health Trust | Information About Cell Phone, Wi-Fi, 5G, and Bluetooth Radiation Science Facts on Health Effects](https://ehtrust.org/)

Environmental Health Trust (EHT) is a leading global advocate for public health and environmental protection. Discover cutting-edge research, policy developments, and practical guidance on reducing exposure to electromagnetic radiation from cell phones, wireless devices, and other sources.

 ehtrust.org

Their founder, Devra Davis, wrote a book called "Disconnect" 10 years ago and even at that time she was able to make a very convincing case that cell phones were not safe. I believe it has been updated since 5G got added to the mix.

I have had decent success from sending skeptics to EH Trust and recommending her book.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-quick-questions.28813/post-333901>

Hello There, a lot of gratitude for everything I am getting the chance to learn - thank you

Some quick questions if ok as I dissect the Blogs and talks.

Understand Oysters and Scallops - eat raw - thats great and very enjoyable - am I still getting the daily benefit of DHA and Iodine eating (cooked in Duck fat) wild caught Australian ocean fish like Whiting, Flathead, Tommy Ruffs - any Australian folk on here experienced in this would be great please - for international folk - that would be Australian Herring, Cod and Platycephalidae.

The bits about eating for fibre - the bits of seafood like their exo skeleton? is there any advice or blogs on this please ? do we eat the crab shell?

Thoughts on Blood Donations - I was on a Protool to reduce (successfully) Ferritin, Iron Saturation, increase Ceruloplasmin, etc - increase Bio Available Copper - any harm in continuing Blood Donations on a 3 month cycle - this Protocol was high in Cod liver Oil and beef liver (aka WAP Foundational principles) so have stopped that (Thank you )( and in hindsight was having a negative impact on me- skin and vision issues ) and all Supps since reading content so far from Jack

Are there any Australian suppliers of blue light blocking glasses that are 5G compatible - or best bet to use Blue tech (Matt) - mine are from Ra Optics

Thoughts on cold exposure to wet grass with bare feet and top off at sub 4-5 degrees in Adelaide - Australia autumn and winter please ? it feels good and doing something positive -

Thank you -very much - I have a lot more but try to keep contained to a few quick ones to have some daily wins

Regards
Ben

Since we know thyroid hormone and Vitamin A Protect Against Vitamin D toxicity in cows, it also follows that humans who are blue light toxic and liberate Vitamin A would destroy their Vitamin D levels, get serious thyroid problems and develop dental decay. This is the wisdom that was buried in the pregnenolone steal syndrome blog long ago. [https://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/?print=print](https://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/?print=print)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-04-24T22:09:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-want-to-remineralize-your-teeth-start-here.28816/post-333941>

I recently came across several papers published in _The Veterinary Record_ in the 1960s documenting the efficacy and risks of injecting cows with a mega-dose of vitamin D just before calving to prevent “milk fever,” which isn’t a fever at all, but is a mix of clinical symptoms including weakness, loss of appetite, and in the extreme case heart failure. It results from low blood levels of calcium, which sometimes occur in the first few days of lactation, before the cow can adequately adjust its calcium balance to meet the new demands for this mineral. It may be a relatively recent disease resulting from the excessive demand for lactation placed on modern dairy cows. In this post, I will review these papers, which provide evidence that thyroid hormone and vitamin A protect against the harmful effects of the vitamin D injections.

In 1963, JB Tutt published an uncontrolled study showing that nine out of ten cows that had previously developed milk fever remained free of clinical symptoms when injected with ten million IU of vitamin D just prior to calving (_Vet Rec._ 1963;75:469-70). Tutt did not report the weight of the cows, but we should keep in mind that the body weight of cows can run ten or more times the weight of a typical human, so this dose is very high but not as extreme as it sounds, and its human equivalent is probably close to a single dose of one million IU.

JM Payne soon responded (_Vet Rec._ 1963;75:848-9) by publishing the results of his own department’s study wherein this dose of vitamin D was not without harm. In this study, the investigators injected cows with either 2.5 million IU, 10 million IU, or 40 million IU. The highest dose produced symptoms of vitamin D toxicity such as excessive urination and loss of appetite, but the cows recovered within seven days. Apart from this, all cows appeared healthy until they were slaughtered seven months later.

Post-mortem examination revealed the cows that received 10 million and 40 million IU were not so healthy after all. Untreated cows and cows given 2.5 million IU appeared normal in these examinations, but those receiving higher doses of vitamin D had extensive lesions in the heart, the aorta and other arteries, and the kidneys. These included pitting and corrugation of the walls of the arteries, widespread calcification, and blood clots at the bifurcations in the arteries and in the heart. The medulla and the tubules of the kidneys were also calcified, with large stones lodged in the renal pelvices, which are the funnel-shaped cavities through which urine exits the kidney into the ureter.

A few years later, Payne published a second report with R Manston showing that vitamin A and thyroid hormone both protect against these effects (_Vet Rec._ 1967;81:215-6). As in the experiment previously mentioned, while the cows given 40 million IU experienced clinical signs of toxicity including loss of appetite, excessive urination, and even death, those given 10 million IU were free of symptoms. Despite this, 83 percent of them developed extensive calcification of the cardiovascular system and kidneys.

If the cows were also given 100 milligrams of thyroxine (the “T4” form of thyroid hormone) or 5 million IU of vitamin A, the occurrence of these lesions dropped to zero.

Payne’s group also injected cows with 5 million or 7.5 million IU of vitamin D. Two-thirds of them suffered from pathological calcification. Untreated cows and those receiving 2.5 million IU vitamin D did not, but this low dose of vitamin D did nothing to prevent low blood levels of calcium. It was safe, but not effective.

The authors included in the same report several later experiments they conducted using diets that contained less calcium, more phosphorus, and slightly more magnesium. In these experiments, the dose of vitamin D required to produce pathological calcification increased to 20 million IU. Thyroid hormone still proved effective at preventing the calcification, although the investigators did not report additional experiments with vitamin A at this dose of vitamin D. When they experimentally manipulated the mineral content of the diet and the timing of the injections, they found that either increasing magnesium and phosphorus or dividing the dose of vitamin D into several injections could reduce, but not eliminate, the incidence of pathological calcification.

Even though the only treatments that reliably and completely eliminated the pathological calcification were the co-administration of either vitamin A or thyroxine, Payne and Manston concluded that “there seemed to be little point in pursuing this line of work if a ‘natural’ factor was already available awaiting discovery,” by which they meant manipulating the mineral intake of the cows.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-01-31T01:43:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-want-to-remineralize-your-teeth-start-here.28816/post-346013>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=333941)
>
> I recently came across several papers published in _The Veterinary Record_ in the 1960s documenting the efficacy and risks of injecting cows with a mega-dose of vitamin D just before calving to prevent “milk fever,” which isn’t a fever at all, but is a mix of clinical symptoms including weakness, loss of appetite, and in the extreme case heart failure. It results from low blood levels of calcium, which sometimes occur in the first few days of lactation, before the cow can adequately adjust its calcium balance to meet the new demands for this mineral. It may be a relatively recent disease resulting from the excessive demand for lactation placed on modern dairy cows. In this post, I will review these papers, which provide evidence that thyroid hormone and vitamin A protect against the harmful effects of the vitamin D injections.
>
> In 1963, JB Tutt published an uncontrolled study showing that nine out of ten cows that had previously developed milk fever remained free of clinical symptoms when injected with ten million IU of vitamin D just prior to calving (_Vet Rec._ 1963;75:469-70). Tutt did not report the weight of the cows, but we should keep in mind that the body weight of cows can run ten or more times the weight of a typical human, so this dose is very high but not as extreme as it sounds, and its human equivalent is probably close to a single dose of one million IU.
>
> JM Payne soon responded (_Vet Rec._ 1963;75:848-9) by publishing the results of his own department’s study wherein this dose of vitamin D was not without harm. In this study, the investigators injected cows with either 2.5 million IU, 10 million IU, or 40 million IU. The highest dose produced symptoms of vitamin D toxicity such as excessive urination and loss of appetite, but the cows recovered within seven days. Apart from this, all cows appeared healthy until they were slaughtered seven months later.
>
> Post-mortem examination revealed the cows that received 10 million and 40 million IU were not so healthy after all. Untreated cows and cows given 2.5 million IU appeared normal in these examinations, but those receiving higher doses of vitamin D had extensive lesions in the heart, the aorta and other arteries, and the kidneys. These included pitting and corrugation of the walls of the arteries, widespread calcification, and blood clots at the bifurcations in the arteries and in the heart. The medulla and the tubules of the kidneys were also calcified, with large stones lodged in the renal pelvices, which are the funnel-shaped cavities through which urine exits the kidney into the ureter.
>
> A few years later, Payne published a second report with R Manston showing that vitamin A and thyroid hormone both protect against these effects (_Vet Rec._ 1967;81:215-6). As in the experiment previously mentioned, while the cows given 40 million IU experienced clinical signs of toxicity including loss of appetite, excessive urination, and even death, those given 10 million IU were free of symptoms. Despite this, 83 percent of them developed extensive calcification of the cardiovascular system and kidneys.
>
> If the cows were also given 100 milligrams of thyroxine (the “T4” form of thyroid hormone) or 5 million IU of vitamin A, the occurrence of these lesions dropped to zero.
>
> Payne’s group also injected cows with 5 million or 7.5 million IU of vitamin D. Two-thirds of them suffered from pathological calcification. Untreated cows and those receiving 2.5 million IU vitamin D did not, but this low dose of vitamin D did nothing to prevent low blood levels of calcium. It was safe, but not effective.
>
> The authors included in the same report several later experiments they conducted using diets that contained less calcium, more phosphorus, and slightly more magnesium. In these experiments, the dose of vitamin D required to produce pathological calcification increased to 20 million IU. Thyroid hormone still proved effective at preventing the calcification, although the investigators did not report additional experiments with vitamin A at this dose of vitamin D. When they experimentally manipulated the mineral content of the diet and the timing of the injections, they found that either increasing magnesium and phosphorus or dividing the dose of vitamin D into several injections could reduce, but not eliminate, the incidence of pathological calcification.
>
> Even though the only treatments that reliably and completely eliminated the pathological calcification were the co-administration of either vitamin A or thyroxine, Payne and Manston concluded that “there seemed to be little point in pursuing this line of work if a ‘natural’ factor was already available awaiting discovery,” by which they meant manipulating the mineral intake of the cows.

What I've seen in the alt literature and used to great effect is 1000iu per lb of bodyweight for a human per day to get rid of all nascent fever/ infection but that in order to avoid the calcification associated with such high doses that one has to take at least 200 mcg of vit K2 mk7 with the daily dose and some magnésium glycinate or bisglycinate

>
> Niobium is the future of tooth re-mineralization I think:
>
> [https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0109564119309145](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0109564119309145)

I do not see it this way. It might be good to build superconducting nanowire single photon detectors to measure UPEs from dentin but if Niobium was a great choice Nature would already use it for regeneration and it does not. Type II superconductors, especially in thin films, are used in superconducting nanowire single-photon detectors (SNSPDs). These devices detect single photons by exploiting the transition from the superconducting to the normal state when a photon disrupts a supercurrent. This atom is likely going to be used in making unique intracellular photomultipliers some day.

Niobium is not used in cells because it exhibits multiple oxide forms and can readily transitions between them. This transition ability can significantly undermine the metal's effectiveness, particularly in harsh environments when capacitors are involved. This is a problem in a water based environment.

My charger despite functioning 'normally' must've been damaged internally causing it to release immense levels of EMI, probably from dropping it on the floor over time. I remember the charger also started introducing a high pitch noise in my external speakers October of last year. Ironically I attributed my destroyed Bristol to the new microwave levels implemented during FEMA test which also occurred in the same month.

I purchased a new charger today for my laptop and the audible EMI doesn't extend past a few feet now.

A portable AM radio is a must for everyone here along with the rf, e, m, and in-line DE meters. Amazing an Apple charger destroyed my Bristol and not current NYC.

Not sure why 100 foot internet fiberoptic is ridiculously cheap but few feet USB is expensive. But still peanuts compared to what centralized medicine swindles people out of to make them sicker.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-07-31T01:49:28+0300

I like SYB we carry their canopies. That is a $300 gizmo!
All you need to do to ground something is to ground it:
Set up BV meter with its ground cord connected. Hold the BV meter in one hand and touch something with your other hand. . . If BV goes up - it is NOT grounded . . . Connect a ground cord to the thing you are testing - touch it again - If BV then goes down (often to zero) it IS now grounded.

# Thread: What does phosgene toxicity have to teach us about mitochondria?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-phosgene-toxicity-have-to-teach-us-about-mitochondria.28825/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-04-28T16:02:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-phosgene-toxicity-have-to-teach-us-about-mitochondria.28825/post-334039>

Phosgene is a major industrial chemical used to make plastics and pesticides. At room temperature (70°F), phosgene is a poisonous gas. With cooling and pressure, phosgene gas can be converted into a liquid so that it can be shipped and stored.

No one knew how phosgene harmed us until recently. Luca Turin figured out the quantum mechanism. What he found now helps us understand SOD2 better.

It has long been known that phosgene, a deadly war gas and industrial reagent, causes intense oxidative stress, but how it does so remains unclear. Here, we report an accidental discovery: electron spin resonance spectroscopy (ESR) of live fruit flies reveals that phosgene exposure results in a distinctive manganese (II) hyperfine spectrum. The Mn(II) signal correlates with mitochondrial SOD2 expression. We suggest that phosgene acylates a highly conserved SOD2 active site tyrosine. This prevents the Mn redox cycling between ESR-silent Mn(III) and ESR-active Mn(II) that is required for superoxide dismutation. We propose that mitochondrial SOD2 inactivation is responsible for phosgene toxicity.

**NATURE'S MAGNETS ARE IONS EJECTED FROM THE CORE OF STARS.

_They are atoms heavier than iron. Those atoms are used by cells to create other unique magnetic signals that are useful to the clock genes in cells to be accurate flow meters of entropy._**

This implies that all biological magnets used in mitochondria and cells come from things that make light. This is axiomatic and critical in showing how physics trumps biochemistry in controlling how life unfolds below the cell level.

Since manganese has a nuclear spin of 5/2 and a nuclear magnetic moment of 3.4687, spectral lines of Mn II show it has a hyperfine structure (HFS). Manganese has one stable isotope with a mass number of 55. The ground electronic configuration of Mn II is 3d5(6S)4s (Sansonetti & Martin 2005). This specific electronic configuration allows cells to create SOD2 from excess oxygen from mtDNA metabolism.

It reveals the queerest aspects of Nature that remain hidden from the biologists and biochemists. Manganese is not a particularly common element on Earth. It is the 12th most common element in the rocks of Earth. However, where it is concentrated reveals the quantum biological story of the evolution of prokaryotes and their lives before oxygen filled the ionosphere. The most incredible abundance of manganese is ferromanganese nodules and crusts along the ocean floor. Marine chemical processes and microorganisms (prokaryotes) capture dissolved manganese in seawater, which is precipitated on the ocean floor. Once captured, the prokaryotes could use the element to create a spectra and a bunch of chemical magnets (ROS/RNS) to drive biology using light DIRECTLY from matter. It should immediately stimulate your neurons to recall that Prokaryotes emit 5000 times more light than eukaryotes. It would help if you remembered that prokaryotes populated our ocean long before eukaryotes ever appeared on Earth. This mechanism is ancient and it is photonic and electronic. It is not holistic! It is not alternative medicine. In fact, it is native biology, and it reveals that centralized science and medicine are the real alternative versions of events created by the minds of man in his labs. It is directly related to the organizational plans buried in Nature.

Shannon's theory of Information has told us that unusual things make excellent information carriers in computing machines. ROS and RNS production and light spectra are examples of such signals. The ground electronic configuration of Mn II is 3d5(6S)4s, a superb information carrier with evolutionary severe weight. Phosgene is a chemical that puts an acetyl group on the aromatic amino acid tyrosine, and this small biochemical change blocks the electronic transition and blocks the flow of information in mitochondria. It blocks the Mn redox cycling between ESR-silent Mn(III) and ESR-active Mn(II) required for superoxide dismutation. Many people do not know that ESR silent Mn(lll) is the source of many of the critical evolutionary porphyrins of life. High spin Mn(lll) has an electronic configuration 3d4(6S)4s. Nature used the D shell electronic configuration of Mn as her signal for SOD2 production in living creatures. Let that level of complexity sink in while you still think biochemistry is "the main controller" in your cells.

The physical chemistry of the atom of Mn contains the answer.

High spin Mn(III) is the archetypal of such non-Kramers ions. Mono and polynuclear Mn(all) are of central importance in biological systems that use heme-based proteins like cytochrome c oxidase, catalase, and photosystem II. In contrast, Mn(III) porphyrins phthalocyanines have been used as building blocks in the construction of molecule-based magnets. This has vast implications for how life operates below the cell level. Soon you'll learn about that level because it is not in your biology books and not in any medical textbooks. That is how vital Turin's serendipitous finding was for the Ivory Tower. the Ivory Tower of science still has no idea what he uncovered. Some of us already knew biology was driven by changing the electronic configuration of ions using light spectra.

**CITES**

[https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/ja971169o](https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/ja971169o)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-07-14T00:38:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-phosgene-toxicity-have-to-teach-us-about-mitochondria.28825/post-337130>

There is also new data on how VO2max changes with age, which is likely linked to the NO & PAD story:

Previous studies in men suggested decline was mostly central (heart, lungs, circulation) rather than peripheral (muscle, mitochondria).

However, the new data on women finds both central and peripheral contributions. This is because women tend to work out indoors more than men, devoid of sunlight. # Thread: Who can I turn to with hyper-memory or how can I figure this out?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-can-i-turn-to-with-hyper-memory-or-how-can-i-figure-this-out.28826/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NikkiYork — 2024-04-28T16:23:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-can-i-turn-to-with-hyper-memory-or-how-can-i-figure-this-out.28826/post-334041>

I was thinking since this is also a brain forum, maybe somebody has ideas or experiences or even knowledge.

I remember everything. Well, not really, but my memory is unreal and people don’t believe me.
I can remember learning to talk and learning to walk. I have clear memories of age probably 0. Or 1 years old. I also have odd early memories that look different from your classical memory.

We moved shortly before I turned two years so gladly I have proof that I was younger than that with all memories I have from my first house.

I do actually claim that I remember being born, but since it is officially impossible, I’m just some sort of freak who believes it. It’s a very scattered memorie and mostly consists of cold, bright and tile. And humans. It is not a real picture memory.

A very dominant memory is that of falling asleep pre-speech. And I see everything mirrored in my room. At least that’s what my mom tells me. I see that room now. When it turned dark, things started to move in front of my eyes. My mom had put a snake like object on the window sill to protect me from the draft. That snake moved and it scared the shit out of me every night. Horrid!!!

I tried to tell her. I tried to point. I remember that so clearly. And I could not make her understand. I learned talking very early

I remember nursing and my Mom said it’s impossible because she only nursed for 3 months.

Oh well there you go. I’m making it up, but I actually have many proofs that I am not making it up for things that I could not have known. There were not many photos taken back in the day. I do t want to bore you, but I have those.

Anyways. This is a riddle of my life and of consciousness and how it works. We have the internet now and I can impossibly be the only person who claims this type of memory. Maybe we can shed some light onto it here.

Maybe somebody can explain to me why or how it is impossible and then I can go from there?

I don’t have photographic memory. My grandfather had that. He could tell you the number of the page of a book that a specific sentence was written on. I don’t have that. He could recite poems like no other.

My sister remembers long numbers because she sees them as colors and she just reads the color combination in her mind. She is a walking phone book. I don’t have that either.

If you don’t have an answer, but a good story. I like to hear it

### DLO — 2024-04-30T01:38:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-can-i-turn-to-with-hyper-memory-or-how-can-i-figure-this-out.28826/post-334110>

Wake me when you can show up to med school empty handed and pass everything at top of class like Jack. Now thats memory.

### NikkiYork — 2024-04-30T04:14:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-can-i-turn-to-with-hyper-memory-or-how-can-i-figure-this-out.28826/post-334118>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=334110)
>
> Wake me when you can show up to med school empty handed and pass everything at top of class like Jack. Now thats memory.

Nah man, please keep sleeping

### Anthony Smith — 2024-04-30T06:22:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-can-i-turn-to-with-hyper-memory-or-how-can-i-figure-this-out.28826/post-334131>

Life is not a completion with others . . . but with yourself . . . and who you are meant to be VS what other people would have you be.

# Thread: Type 1 diabetes help
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetes-help.28830/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ChelseaBear — 2024-04-29T23:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetes-help.28830/post-334102>

I'm new to Jack Kruse and so grateful to have found him. My 14 year old son was diagnosed with type 1 diabetes in January.

I would say we are pretty old fashioned, eating real food including lots of fat, protein and not a lot of carb. I grow a huge portion of what we eat. I always prepare our food from scratch. No Doritos, margarine, pre-made salad dressings or commercial bread in my house! We also have kept our sugar intake pretty low, including fruit and starch. I've had my boys outside a lot since babyhood because we homeschool and have prioritized fresh air and wild spaces. We have no auto-immune issues on either side of the family so this was pretty shocking. He had covid back at the end of summer. He isn't vaxxed at all except a tetanus/diphtheria shot after stepping on a nail in the barnyard. Curiously, he got a cell phone right around the same time he got sick with Covid, as he was starting a part-time job (at 13!).

Anyway, here it is. My son is the picture of health. 6' tall, well muscled, low body fat, full of energy. Why did this happen? Is there really no hope for a cure from mother nature? I understand that conventional medicine says when the insulin cells "burn out" they don't regenerate. My stubborn spirit says never accept no for an answer.

Our family is now on a high protein, full fat, very low carb diet to help support him on his journey of keeping his blood sugars in the healthy non-diabetic range. The way we eat is amazing, fully sustainable and very enjoyable. He is learning how to watch his blood sugars and keep them in the right range with insulin shots. This is all well and good as long as I don't fall into despair thinking about the heavy burden this will be for him for the rest of his life. Injections in the morning, after meals, at bedtime, any time he's to high. And finger pricks all day long. There is constant concern about taking to much insulin, or having high blood sugar and risking eye damage or blindness, kidney disease, heart disease, amputations, early death and all that awful shit. We understand that the silver lining is how well he can learn about his own metabolism and fine tune it. He might even have the ability to optimize his health and live longer and stronger for it. But still, this is a burden that I pray God will take from him.

Does anyone out there know if JK has some tricks up his sleeve for regenerating insulin cells?

### Jack Kruse — 2024-04-30T00:45:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetes-help.28830/post-334104>

The egg and sperm he came from had high heteroplasmy rate in his beta cells. You lived too high latitude when you were pregnant and in his childhood, he abuses blue light light and technology, and if he was compliant with the jab mandate that would be whipped cream on the shit sundae. That covers it

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-04-30T16:49:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetes-help.28830/post-334133>

Is T1D reversible?

I was arguing with my nurse friend the other day about this. He says it is not reversible. I believe it is possible based on what you teach.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-01T01:55:27+0300

### [Caldera: Crater Formed by Volcanic Collapse or Explosion](https://geology.com/articles/caldera/)

Calderas are massive craters located at the sites of enormous volcanic eruptions. They can form by collapse or by an explosive blast.

geology.com

### lumiereisgood — 2024-11-19T21:07:38+0200

Stop dairy product 100%. ZERO exception. Bovine insulin is probably à trigger. Depending on the stage of the disease, I have had incredible successes, sometimes completely cured.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-11-20T01:30:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/type-1-diabetes-help.28830/post-342666>

Melatonin deficiency in Mom comes from a lack of UV light by Mom prenatally and since we know that melatonin controls DNA methylation and histone modification, a lack of melatonin in Mom and her germline due to creation via AM light exposure prevents changes in gene expression that are directly related to the programming of endocrine pathology in offspring. This is how one creates children with Type 1 diabetes. Mom's with lowered melatonin also are low dopamine and also cannot regenerate many of the visual and non visual photoreceptors too.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-04-30T02:14:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gerd-gut-and-nnemf.28831/post-334114>

Oxygen is a powerful element for the human gut because of what it does to electrons. Oxygen use by life began the initial penetration of solving complexity in tissues and organizational structure. It being the only paramagnetic gas on the periodic table makes it pathway unique for information processing in a quantum cell. O2 has two unpaired electrons.

Tissue complexity is enabled by electrons, and is responsible for producing more electrons in a thermodynamic system. This is why life exploded after the Cambrian event. There is no other reason. We need more electrons to get more oxygen so we can generate more electrons. This is why humans lost their fur. Their skin became a new way to recover more electrons from the sun, in the from of photons. Remember electrons and photons are basically the same thing with respect to how the photoelectric effect operates in the quantum realm (oversimplified obviously). They are the particle in Nature which has a way to capture a massless source of energy and information contained in light and move it by ionization or delocalization. This helps explains why all cells release ultraweak UV light. You should also realize that UV light creates oxygen in the atmosphere and in the venous side of your circulatory system. Big implications for diseases like GERD when the system is awry.

**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DebraGM — 2024-04-30T04:30:32+0300

### [The Bitcoin Power Law Theory](https://giovannisantostasi.medium.com/the-bitcoin-power-law-theory-962dfaf99ee9)

Bitcoin is more similar to a city and an organism than a financial asset.

### NikkiYork — 2024-05-01T18:24:27+0300

Why did you post this? A comment would be helpful to understand where you were heading with this, or what you want your the forum people to do.

I read it and now what?

### markthesunbather — 2024-05-01T18:51:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bitcoin-and-power-laws.28832/post-334168>

The Fred Krueger guy is apparently big on this, whereas British Hodl thinks it's silly.

If I had to venture a guess, the reason Hodl doesn't dig it is probably because people who do see it as a roadmap for getting in and out of Bitcoin at certain intervals (but I'd have to go back and look closer at his tweets to really know if that's where he's coming from).

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-02T07:51:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bitcoin-and-power-laws.28832/post-334204>

Bitcoin follows Kleiber's law to some degree because both are powered by decentralized networks that use time as their key metric. Neither use energy as the critical metric. They both use time as the metric because time is the most immutable aspect of reality. The principle of scale invariance used in making predictions in time is simple and buried in the power laws of biology and physics. Time allows for keeping track of things. Time is needed to give chaos instructions.

In biology, we have punctuated evolution. I think the Cambrian explosion is the most giant VALUE bubble in the history of life. Thirty-two phyla showed up overnight. It is a feature of the biology system, not a bug because buried deep inside cellular machinery, quantum mechanics is using power laws in many ways we are blinded to. Kleiber's law is one of these ways. This is why bubbles are part of Bitcoin's cycles as well. They are the fingerprints of decentralized design. It's a feature, not a bug. If we did not have price volatility, it would be a sign that the thermodynamics of decentralization was weakening.
In the
Money is a time machine used to keep track of who owes what to whom. In the history of man, everything we have used as money fells into two categories: _physical_ artifacts and _informational_ lists. Or, to use more common parlance: tokens and ledgers. It is important to realize the PHYSICAL difference of these categories.
The first method — a physical token — _directly_ represents the state of things.
The second one — a ledger — _indirectly_ reflects the state of things.

The Electromagnetics spectrum mimics a token. This is why photons do not experience time; they are timeless. It follows, tokens in reality are timeless. In the Bible, there is a passage about light, but few will see it in the quantum mechanical way: In Corinthians 4, it says, "For things seen are temporary, but the things unseen are everlasting.

Physical tokens are unique composites of atoms whose assembly is not easily replicable. Think AMO physics. That describes the atomic arrangement of atoms in a mitochondrion. Pure information does not have this property. If you can read the information, you can also copy it perfectly. This represents RNA/DNA. A mitochondrion is trustworthy in biology; RNA/DNA ledgers are fundamentally trustless. Practically speaking, it follows that physical tokens are unique in their thermodynamic abilities. Biological DNA ledgers are initially unique but built to adapt to the mitochondrial signaling on the state of things in the environment. Informational ledgers in the physical world are not unique at all. They can be varied and changed as time goes forward. That is part of their inherent design.

Each comes with advantages and disadvantages. For example, tokens are physical and distributed; ledgers are informational and centralized. **Tokens are inherently trustless; ledgers are not.** In biology, the token is represented by DNA/RNA molecules and telomere length. The ledger is measured by mtDNA, the epigenome program that controls the outcome. Today, centralized science focuses myopically on RNA/DNA, which is the part of the system that is without trust because it can be manipulated easily. Centralized science spends no time on mtDNA dynamics, and it should be because a mitochondrion is a trustworthy, fully decentralized time machine.

When it comes to physical tokens, the time of a transaction does not matter. This is true with money or in cells. You either have the coins or sunlight in your pocket or cell, or you don’t; you can spend them or can’t. It just matters that you have them. The simple act of possession is the only prerequisite for spending or living. The laws of nature take care of the rest. In that sense, physical tokens are trustless and timeless.

Regarding ledgers, the physical possession idea falls by the wayside. Whoever or whatever biological process controls the ledger needs to ensure that things are _in order in RNA/DNA. Just as you cannot_ spend money that you don’t have and you can’t spend money that you have already spent previously, You cannot live life without sunlight, and you cannot regenerate tissues degraded with light you've already used.

In traditional systems, time is an absolute measure governed by atomic clocks. However, in the decentralized world of Bitcoin, time is relative. It's not the clock ticking that matters, but the creation of new blocks. This forms a kind of 'cryptographic time,' where the state of the blockchain determines the past, present, and future. This measurement or application of time is resistant to manipulation. Bitcoin offers a novel perspective on time, one that is intertwined with its operations and immune to external influences. Biological clocks are designed the same way, but the problem is that light from our environment does ruin the circadian cellular clockwork, and this is where diseases come from. When we lose our sense of time, chronic disease is the result.

### JanSz — 2024-05-02T17:20:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bitcoin-and-power-laws.28832/post-334217>

I estimated my yearly spending
and
attempted to keep 5-7 years of future spending in cash

**Subforum:** Beginners Area

### Kendall Toerner — 2024-04-30T19:41:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-change-happen.28834/post-334141>

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-in-gainesville-fl-need-resources.28812/post-334020](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5g-in-gainesville-fl-need-resources.28812/post-334020)

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
I want your message and incredible findings to find as many people as possible. Although helpful for certain people, institutions, pharma, etc, your tone comes across very aggressive and most people cannot handle or do not trust such an approach. I understand that med school is hardcore and we have some serious issues to fix that require brute force, but attacking people who want to help and understand (and who have massive other commitments other than deciphering every message you've ever posted on all of the internet) is counter-productive.

I don't mind being pushed as I enjoy constructive ruthless criticism, but go so far to call people lazy (I have worked my ass off my entire life to get to where I am, including teaching myself 10 programming languages) and be so aggressive when you don't even know them is a bit too far.

“Anger dwells only in the bosom of fools.” - Albert Einstein

I've been digesting and evaluating your teachings and work for 2 years now, and still am trying to find all the pieces to the puzzle, because I have a (self-employed) job that takes much of my time so I can even afford avoiding most EMF and live somewhere with high solar energy. I also didn't go through med school and have had to learn every approach to research and changing my previous mindset along this journey. I and most others have a long way to go in this area. Most people are not as lucky as me with free time, nor as open to aggressive feedback. This is illustrated by the reactions of many people I show your content to (in an effort to get them on board with your concepts and findings), who compare your aggressive tone to the yelling Paul Saladino employs in his videos. It turns a lot of people off to the content.

I believe you grew up in NYC, and I can't imagine you will agree with me on this because of that personal n=1 experience, but my experience has been most of the public will listen to perceived "level-headed" content which comes across most easily as calm and collected (stoicism). I know you're pissed as fuck at our system and I am too, but we can't create large enough change for the people to rise up unless we are approachable to as many of them as possible, while still conveying high quality information and passion. Passion coming across as anger sounds defensive to many people and I don't want your message to be drowned out by that.

I'd be sad to get banned for this post but I want to share this with you because two of your biggest fans and followers (myself and my friend finishing up med school) both see this as the biggest inhibitor to the distribution of this knowledge and your message.

Additionally if you are trying to share your knowledge and mindset approach (nature being the key) on podcasts and social media to get people to see this, share it, and change the world to get rid of our lackluster approach to technology adoption and usage, we don't need every person wasting their time researching all the same stuff. I get it - this stuff is valuable and was hard-found, so you want people to put effort in and work for it. However, most people are not even curious like me and don't want to do all this research because they only care about the end goal, actionable tasks, and over-arching concepts. I am trying to convey as much of this information as possible to other people and my community to get this shit out there, and for you to suggest someone like me is lazy after 2 years of research is pretty frustrating. Your content takes easily 5+ years to absorb, re-read, and fully understand. I have a good grasp on everything, and I wanted to get some additional 5G information from anyone on the forum who can help to spread this message to our communities, as I was requesting.

I personally like your approach in every way except the aggression that I believe should be coming out as a more constructive version of passion.

Everyone’s attitude here is either helping themselves or attacking others for not doing research even though they don’t have enough time to learn everything fast enough on top of their current life duties. # Thread: Another new member, From NW Florida. I'm so EXCITED! Long intro - sorry!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-new-member-from-nw-florida-im-so-excited-long-intro-sorry.28835/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MaggieMiller — 2024-04-30T21:40:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-new-member-from-nw-florida-im-so-excited-long-intro-sorry.28835/post-334144>

72yo retired RN, ER/Trauma the last 3 decades. I chafed at the centralized healthcare system since my very first job. When I was 23, I tried to organize the nurses and the DON called each nurse, one by one, into her office to make sure they would NOT attend the meeting. I was asked to resign. I did and returned working for an agency for more than the hospital paid me. Needless to say, I never rose in the ranks to managerial positions. Management was forced to utilize me when they computerized, b/c I had a masters in computer science, but I didn't keep doing that b/c it was easy to see the EMRs is just a massive billing machine with a little bit of patient care thrown on top. It directs EVERYTHING clinicians do, for the benefit of the profiteers.

I'm a single mom again, raising 2 grandchildren I adopted. I joined here because the past 6 years with the kids has taken a toll on me physically. A recent cross-country move caused some sort of depletion that left me a shell of myself. Labwork from a PCP (who I will never see again, but she did order routine labs) is all okay, but she had no interest in addressing my physical exhaustion. I took bovine adrenals from a health food store one day and woke the next day feeling almost like myself again, so now I'm on a mission to heal myself, b/c obviously my body is out of whack.

I wish I had time to research everything, but in order to raise 2 kids with PTSD in a world where everything is poisoned takes TIME. I cook a lot, drive to farms to get my food, have chickens and am working on my new garden. I have almost eliminated all processed foods from the house. It's been gradual, because the kids did better with a slow transition. Everything costs money, and we're living on my Social Security, so I can only do what I can do. I have amber light bulbs in our bedrooms. I get them off electronics a few hours before bedtime. We go to the beach several times a week, no matter the weather. Today we will probably go in the rain.

I try to rise above supplements and the endless micro-managing of biological processes I don't really understand by using my biggest skill. I have meditated over 50 years and now I find it pretty easy to slip into the God zone, connect with source, the Flow, whatever you call it. I do QiGong Microcosmic orbit every morning as the sun rises, outside with bare feet or socks in Winter. I use the Gateway Tapes a few times a week (the CIA connection leads me to take is slowly so I can listen to my gut before I proceed to the next level).I do breathwork. I attend sound therapy when I can find it (harder here than when we lived in Montana). But I stop to meditate in some fashion 2-3 times a day. I feel like I don't have a choice. It feels mandatory. Energy, Light, Vibration. It's why I was attracted to Jack in the first place - he knows about all of that and he's trying to explain it scientifically. I listen to his podcasts every day, trying to learn more and de-program the nurse part of my brain.

I'm still TIRED by mid-afternoon - more tired than I was when I was a mom in my 30s, and that's the physical thing I'm here to address. I want to find out what happened physically to me in the 6 years since I emergently got custody of my grandchildren. We were in crisis mode for so long that I didn't even notice it happening. I'm well-trained to push through crises because it's ingrained in ER/Trauma. But now we are settled and the kids are both older and mentally better, I'm ready to let the "pushing through" go. I want to rest and still have energy to do all the things I want to do. I'm sure some of it's light/outdoors. I hiked a lot, floated down rivers for hours & did Tai Chi outside almost daily before I got the kids. There HAS to be a way, and I'm here to find it.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-01T01:56:31+0300

But I laugh so hard during your interviews when you get brusque. "Yep! He's a neurosurgeon!" When my mom needed a neurosurgeon, I took her to an asshole. I knew his sister, so I knew he was actually a truly nice man outside of work, but AT work, he was meticulously precise man with zero communication skills. You don't NEED a neurosurgeon to be warm and fuzzy.

# Thread: Justin's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justins-optimal-journal.28839/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Justin Goldberg — 2024-05-02T12:14:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/justins-optimal-journal.28839/post-334210>

Fifth day on the diet.

Messed up and had corn chips on day three and pistachios on day 4.

Is wild caught salmon from China better than farmed salmon from elsewhere?

I'm considering crowd cow or butcher box. Also at my church I met a farmer who raises cows for meat and I told him I would purchase 20 lb of beef. Next time he butchers a cow I'll go in on a quarter. He said that the butcher dry ages the beef, but I can request him to not dry age it. Anyone else from North Georgia wants to split the half cow into a quarter? DM me here or on the nostr primal link in my signature.

On day four I seem to have started to get keto flu. First symptom was a scratch in my throat, like when you first get a cold. Took some airborne. Today's the 5th day and has progressed to a headache. I will be getting some activated charcoal to help. And perhaps some prebiotic powder, (seen it in Fast This Way) but I'm wary of anything Dave Ass-spray says from what Jack says about him.

Bought some grass fed butter, will simmer & filter it to ghee since like most Jewish people I have problems with dairy protein. Butter coffee!

Also on day 4, I woke up at sunrise without an alarm clock, and I wasn't groggy and cranky like usual. First time I watched the sunrise in a very long time (I don't think I've ever watched it really) and stayed out for 45 minutes.

Will confront girlfriend about wifi at some point (they are on timers right now). It's her house and her money. She doesn't understand that I can run a cable in her bed room and kitchen, the common wifi usage areas, and set the signal power level to as low as possible. I'll get a couple of metal mesh covers, but do those dampen or kill the signal?

I've seen coax to Ethernet adapters but DirecTV uses our coax. Afaik coax doesn't work like Ethernet, so it's not just a matter of putting coax splitters between them. I've also seen hpna power line network adapters, although I need to find out if our house has a smart meter before using those. Won't those just radiate the smart meters signal even more? It was built in 2016 so it probably does.

# Thread: ¡Hola! new, but not new--and continue to learn a lot.

¡Hola! everyone. I've been lurking for quite sometime and have been a Gold Member for a few years now and I feel like I know a lot of you already. Thanks to each and everyone of you for your wisdom-- and courage to change.

I'm finally waking up without an alarm. This is big news for me.
I've always been an outdoors kinda girl-- from way back.
Love to swim.
Beach.
Sun.

Here we go!

Sirena

# Thread: High UV triggering autoimmune?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-uv-triggering-autoimmune.28842/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Flynn Jones — 2024-05-02T20:14:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-uv-triggering-autoimmune.28842/post-334224>

Hi,

This may seem a little odd but I am going to put it out there anyway.

I have been following all of JK's work and managed to stabilise my autoimmune disease. I am making it a priority to build my solar callus and get away as much as possible.

What I have noticed is when I spend time in areas of lower latitude, such as Lanzarote, my mouth ulcers start to come back. My theory is that the increased melanocyte stimulation is causing an almost herx/detox reaction. Perhaps It's the deuterium shedding?

Despite flaring up it still makes me feel better, I tan very well and no longer burn.

Wondering if anyone else has experienced similar?

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-02T21:53:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-uv-triggering-autoimmune.28842/post-334227>

When the redox sucks yes. You can added some bicarb use to improve ferroptosis regeneration

### Flynn Jones — 2024-05-03T14:26:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-uv-triggering-autoimmune.28842/post-334247>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=334227)
>
> When the redox sucks yes. You can added some bicarb use to improve ferroptosis regeneration

Great news! I’ll get on the san pellegrino with a bit of baking soda today.

Thanks Doc

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-03T21:18:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-uv-triggering-autoimmune.28842/post-334255>

Why does it work for AI's? It fixes the "Proton problem" in mitochondria. Ya' ever hear of bicarb and carbonic anhydrase? The carbonic anhydrases or carbonate dehydratases enzymes are experts at moving protons to and fro form a family of enzymes that catalyze the rapid interconversion of carbon dioxide and water to bicarbonate and protons (or vice versa). Making water and CO2 is exactly what a HEALTHY mitochondrion does and it is exactly what an AUTOIMMUNE condition comes from. When will we learn? This is a reversible reaction that occurs relatively slowly in the absence of a catalyst. Baking soda is that catalyst. The active site of most carbonic anhydrases contains a zinc ion; they are therefore classified as metalloenzymes. This means nnEMF can affect this transition metal so we have another 5G mitohack.

### [Drinking baking soda could be an inexpensive, safe way to combat autoimmune disease: study](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2018-04-soda-inexpensive-safe-combat-autoimmune.html)

A daily dose of baking soda may help reduce the destructive inflammation of autoimmune diseases like rheumatoid arthritis, scientists say.

 medicalxpress.com

### Flynn Jones — 2024-05-04T13:37:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-uv-triggering-autoimmune.28842/post-334261>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=334255)
>
> Why does it work for AI's? It fixes the "Proton problem" in mitochondria. Ya' ever hear of bicarb and carbonic anhydrase? The carbonic anhydrases or carbonate dehydratases enzymes are experts at moving protons to and fro form a family of enzymes that catalyze the rapid interconversion of carbon dioxide and water to bicarbonate and protons (or vice versa). Making water and CO2 is exactly what a HEALTHY mitochondrion does and it is exactly what an AUTOIMMUNE condition comes from. When will we learn? This is a reversible reaction that occurs relatively slowly in the absence of a catalyst. Baking soda is that catalyst. The active site of most carbonic anhydrases contains a zinc ion; they are therefore classified as metalloenzymes. This means nnEMF can affect this transition metal so we have another 5G mitohack.
>
> ### [Drinking baking soda could be an inexpensive, safe way to combat autoimmune disease: study](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2018-04-soda-inexpensive-safe-combat-autoimmune.html)
>
> A daily dose of baking soda may help reduce the destructive inflammation of autoimmune diseases like rheumatoid arthritis, scientists say.
>
>  medicalxpress.com

Very interesting!

Any idea on dosage? I tried too much last night and was left with mild fever and cold sweats.

Definitely think I overdosed for what my redox can handle.

Already planning my next visit to the canaries.

Planning on El Salvador for first time this December.

Thanks again

# Thread: Fluctuating magnetic field in kitchen?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluctuating-magnetic-field-in-kitchen.28843/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Niki Stanton — 2024-05-03T00:32:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluctuating-magnetic-field-in-kitchen.28843/post-334230>

I kept feeling like garbage every time I was in the kitchen doing food prep. I thought it was just energy tank at the end of the day but it kept being so dramatic a shift in my body that today I took out the trifield.

Hopefully the photos below posts- it’s me standing in front of the stove and the magnetic field fluctuating between about 0.5 to 5.0 and it was cycling back and forth between those two numbers. About every 5-10 seconds.

What is this about?
It’s good to have validation I guess but I kinda need to cook for the family without feeling terrible. Hmm.

### Niki Stanton — 2024-05-03T00:35:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluctuating-magnetic-field-in-kitchen.28843/post-334231>

I feel a little dumb. It’s the burner kicking on and off right? Is that just the nature of all ovens or do I have a special one.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-05-03T07:53:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluctuating-magnetic-field-in-kitchen.28843/post-334241>

Have an electrician check the wiring because as far as I know that should not happen.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-05-03T15:09:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluctuating-magnetic-field-in-kitchen.28843/post-334248>

If the burner did not kick on and off, the oven would not maintain a set temperature

Feedback loops signal the burner to turn off and on to keep the T inside steady

### Niki Stanton — 2024-05-03T16:07:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluctuating-magnetic-field-in-kitchen.28843/post-334249>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=334248)
>
> If the burner did not kick on and off, the oven would not maintain a set temperature
>
> Feedback loops signal the burner to turn off and on to keep the T inside steady

Guess I thought it would be grounded to not put out a high electric and magnetic field when it cycles.

### Saichi — 2024-05-04T15:29:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluctuating-magnetic-field-in-kitchen.28843/post-334263>

> [Niki Stanton said:](/goto/post?id=334249)
>
> Guess I thought it would be grounded to not put out a high electric and magnetic field when it cycles.

It is grounded. All grounded electric appliances put out high fields when cycling. Gas stoves don't though.

# Thread: Is it possible that photons actually have mass?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-05-03T22:04:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334257>

I am reading through the Time series currently for the first time and there is a lot of talk about the momentum of light. I just can't wrap my head around the idea of something having momentum, but no mass. By the equation for momentum, if mass is zero, momentum would be zero.

I've had a feeling that there is a possibility that photons had mass ever since I taught about atomic spectra for the first time many years ago. Thought that maybe we just lacked the instrumentation to detect it bc of how infinitesimally small it likely is (if it exists at all)

Just wondering the thoughts of others. Am I way off base? On to something?

### Brent Patrick — 2024-05-05T03:57:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334291>

### [How does gravity affect photons (that is, bend light) if photons have no mass? | Astronomy.com](https://www.astronomy.com/science/how-does-gravity-affect-photons-that-is-bend-light-if-photons-have-no-mass/)

categories:Science | tags:Astrophysics, Magazine

 www.astronomy.com

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-05T04:31:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334294>

Momentum is an energy concept. Not just a mass content. Consider this slide from Vermont 2018

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-05-06T16:48:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334348>

I see

E² = p²c² + m²c⁴

By this eqn from Einstein, mass can be zero and there can still exist a relationship between energy and momentum

Seems the momentum eqn, p = mv, as it is taught in grade school, is incomplete bc it doesn't account for energy momentum. I was hung up on this basic eqn (p = mv) bc I thought it was complete and true in all cases

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-07T05:54:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334380>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=334348)
>
> I see
>
> E² = p²c² + m²c⁴
>
> By this eqn from Einstein, mass can be zero and there can still exist a relationship between energy and momentum
>
> Seems the momentum eqn, p = mv, as it is taught in grade school, is incomplete bc it doesn't account for energy momentum. I was hung up on this basic eqn (p = mv) bc I thought it was complete and true in all cases

not imcomplete. That is macro physics and mass. Photons are quantum mechanical and quantum mechanics has new ways of dealing with momentum of photons.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2024-05-07T16:49:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334389>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=334380)
>
> not imcomplete. That is macro physics and mass. Photons are quantum mechanical and quantum mechanics has new ways of dealing with momentum of photons.

Ah, okay. So, both eqns are true and complete. They are just intended for use in different scenarios. Thanks for the clarification

### Jack Kruse — 2024-05-09T19:10:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-possible-that-photons-actually-have-mass.28845/post-334524>

[https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1788601083729686923](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1788601083729686923)

# Thread: Prayer request for Wisdom and Grace re pancreatic cancer Dx
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prayer-request-for-wisdom-and-grace-re-pancreatic-cancer-dx.28846/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2024-05-04T16:53:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prayer-request-for-wisdom-and-grace-re-pancreatic-cancer-dx.28846/post-334264>

The month of March my husband developed left abdominal pain radiating around his body an pain that came and went with waves being more intense pain. So far he has chosen care care at two major Virginia hospitals . He had 1st CT scan that revealed 4 cm mass on his pancreas 1 MRI about 12 days later that said WWO contrast that showed 5.5 cm mass on his pancreases another upper body CT wo contrast checking for any spread. On Friday late day 4 26 24 had a endoscopic ultrasound with biopsy of pancreas and we not given paperwork recap when we left the procedure but this biopsy showed up in his “My Chart”.
Went back to emergency department and was admitted to the hospital this past week after pain meds not working and Told stage 1 or 2 cancer on May 3th.

I am in shock trying to adjust to a very strong and able bodied 86 year old becoming so weak and suffering so much pain so quickly. Both my husband and I are Grace Oriented believers in the Lord Jesus Christ and trusting God for his best direction. I am hoping for the best but preparing for the worst. I love and am devoted to my husband but this is long suffering for me as it does not align with my own journey recovering cancer and cancer treatment over the past 15 years.

After visiting my husband in the hospital the last few days in the hospital I personally am very weak physically, emotionally and mentally.
Spiritually claiming : Hebrews 4:16 **Let us therefore come boldly to the throne of grace, that we may obtain mercy and find grace to help in time of need.

Two things Right now

FIRST,** I do not know how to fully interpret the report below especially. **"FINAL DIAGNOSIS COMMENT
[View trends](https://mychart.healthsystem.virginia.edu/Mychart/app/test-results/past-result-details?ecomponentid=WP-24AV1e1Yu-2FixWHf5ku4fet6Q-3D-3D-24aMGYcpsc8xcC1o9qraKSlkzYXKxxaN-2BHRHrk1UMg85k-3D&eorderid=WP-24M3mb2H9JAtqvKsbryoxg7g-3D-3D-24f2I9e0Nt1lm9uH7ACdjPbRn0HGBDfKp6hbFObBhQ1j0-3D)
The cellblock is paucicellular and insufficient material is present for immunohistochemical stains for mismatch repair proteins."

SECOND,

I found this online :
[https://pancan.org › facing-pancreatic-cancer › living-with-pancreatic-cancer › managing-side-effects-palliative-care › symptoms-pain › celiac-plexus-block](https://pancan.org/facing-pancreatic-cancer/living-with-pancreatic-cancer/managing-side-effects-palliative-care/symptoms-pain/celiac-plexus-block/)
[**Celiac Plexus Block - Pancreatic Cancer Action Network**](https://pancan.org/facing-pancreatic-cancer/living-with-pancreatic-cancer/managing-side-effects-palliative-care/symptoms-pain/celiac-plexus-block/)
A celiac plexus block is one way to control the **pancreatic** **cancer** symptom of abdominal **pain**. Seeing healthcare professionals who focus on symptom management and supportive care improves outcomes and is critical for your quality of life. The **Pancreatic** **Cancer** Action Network strongly

\---------------------------------------------------------------------

Hebrews 4:16 **Let us therefore come boldly to the throne of grace, that we may obtain mercy and find grace to help in time of need.
This is our time of need.**

**

### Martha Ray — 2024-05-04T16:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prayer-request-for-wisdom-and-grace-re-pancreatic-cancer-dx.28846/post-334265>

> [Martha Ray said:](/goto/post?id=334264)
>
> The month of March my husband developed left abdominal pain radiating around his body an pain that came and went with waves being more intense pain. So far he has chosen care care at two major Virginia hospitals . He had 1st CT scan that revealed 4 cm mass on his pancreas 1 MRI about 12 days later that said WWO contrast that showed 5.5 cm mass on his pancreases another upper body CT wo contrast checking for any spread. On Friday late day 4 26 24 had a endoscopic ultrasound with biopsy of pancreas and we not given paperwork recap when we left the procedure but this biopsy showed up in his “My Chart”.
> Went back to emergency department and was admitted to the hospital this past week after pain meds not working and Told stage 1 or 2 cancer on May 3th.
>
> I am in shock trying to adjust to a very strong and able bodied 86 year old becoming so weak and suffering so much pain so quickly. Both my husband and I are Grace Oriented believers in the Lord Jesus Christ and trusting God for his best direction. I am hoping for the best but preparing for the worst. I love and am devoted to my husband but this is long suffering for me as it does not align with my own journey recovering cancer and cancer treatment over the past 15 years.
>
> After visiting my husband in the hospital the last few days in the hospital I personally am very weak physically, emotionally and mentally.
> Spiritually claiming : Hebrews 4:16 **Let us therefore come boldly to the throne of grace, that we may obtain mercy and find grace to help in time of need.
>
>
>
> Two things Right now
>
> FIRST,** I do not know how to fully interpret the report below especially. **"FINAL DIAGNOSIS COMMENT
> [View trends](https://mychart.healthsystem.virginia.edu/Mychart/app/test-results/past-result-details?ecomponentid=WP-24AV1e1Yu-2FixWHf5ku4fet6Q-3D-3D-24aMGYcpsc8xcC1o9qraKSlkzYXKxxaN-2BHRHrk1UMg85k-3D&eorderid=WP-24M3mb2H9JAtqvKsbryoxg7g-3D-3D-24f2I9e0Nt1lm9uH7ACdjPbRn0HGBDfKp6hbFObBhQ1j0-3D)
> The cellblock is paucicellular and insufficient material is present for immunohistochemical stains for mismatch repair proteins."
> \-------------------------------------------------
>

### caroline — 2024-05-04T17:57:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prayer-request-for-wisdom-and-grace-re-pancreatic-cancer-dx.28846/post-334269>

### JanSz — 2024-05-04T21:30:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prayer-request-for-wisdom-and-grace-re-pancreatic-cancer-dx.28846/post-334273>

Hi Martha!
I will pray for you and your husband.

I would like to make sure that you know of existence of Medicare program that is available to people on Medicare when doctor signs in paper stating that their life expectancy is less than 6 months.

I have found out about it because of my DW story.
Note, that after doctor's signature, when the doctor signs in, there is no obligation to die within 6 months.
The idea is that
the person is under care of that system, at his home or other place.
The care is to keep him/her comfortable, without pain.
But hi is not being medically attempted to be cured.

When my wife's time came, I was able to take her to our home.
But others, in situation like possibly you are in, (with your cancer), you can tell them to take care of your husband outside, in the care facilities.

My wife got really good daily care, medications were arranged by nurses and coming on time.
I did not have to deal with any financial expenses.

They were even attempting to care for our religious needs.
We refused that part, we are Catholics, local priest was visiting her and sending peoples to talk and pray with her.

God bless you both.
.................

> [Martha Ray said:](/goto/post?id=334264)
>
> I love and am devoted to my husband but this is long suffering for me as it does not align with my own journey recovering cancer and cancer treatment over the past 15 years.

[@Martha Ray](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22506/) , I have been a reader here for a few years and this is my first post. I was compelled. I am also a Christian and know intimately the healing power of our Divine Physician. Recently, when having a tough time, praying, I received a distinct message from the Holy Spirit. It was "Be strong, woman."

I am praying for healing and comfort for you both and saying to you--Be strong, woman.

### Martha Ray — 2024-06-16T23:54:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prayer-request-for-wisdom-and-grace-re-pancreatic-cancer-dx.28846/post-335829>

The happiest day of my husband's life was June 4, 2024.
I am happy for him but it was one sad day of my life.
**_Thank you all for your caring and prayers._**

### caroline — 2024-06-17T03:39:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prayer-request-for-wisdom-and-grace-re-pancreatic-cancer-dx.28846/post-335832>

hugs and prayers to you and your family Martha.

You will get many answers.
Here is mine.
If you are located indoors but windows in your room are open, you get most if not all the UV that you may need.

If you are located indoors with closed windows but the glass in your windows is like 100 or more old, you are getting most of the UV that you may need.

So, if you have pool cage screen (often they are in Florida to fight mosquitos), you are in fully enclosed room, you are not exposed to UV.

**Sirena think of photons of light not as straight lines as others unfornutaley do but as curved. "Gravitational lensing"**

There is only one current correct answer **proven** by Einstein’s general theory of relativity, time and space are fused together in a quantity known as spacetime. Within this theory, massive objects cause spacetime to curve, and gravity is simply the curvature of spacetime.

**As light travels through spacetime, the theory predicts that the path taken by the light will also be curved by an object’s mass. Gravitational lensing is a dramatic and observable example of Einstein’s theory in action.**

### [How does gravity affect photons (that is, bend light) if photons have no mass? | Astronomy.com](https://www.astronomy.com/science/how-does-gravity-affect-photons-that-is-bend-light-if-photons-have-no-mass/)
